name: Roasted Beef Bacon “On The Bone”
text:
Recipe ID: roasted_beef_bacon_“on_the_bone”_merged
Name: Roasted Beef Bacon “On The Bone”

Ingredients:
- INGREDIENTS:
- (For 4 people.)
- The cow bone:
- Beef ribs
- Txuleta fat:
- 2 kg. txuleta fat
- The cow tendons:
- 400 g. cow Achilles tendon
- 1 g. Xanthan gum
- INGREDIENTES:
- (Para 4 personas.)
- El hueso de vaca:
- Costillas de vaca
- La grasa de txuleta:
- 2 kg. grasa de txuleta
- Los tendones de vaca:
- 400 g. tendón de aquiles de vaca
- 1 g. goma Xantana

Steps:
1. PREPARATION:
2. The cow bone:
3. Clean all the meat from the bones.
4. Cut the rib bones to 15 centimeters long with a saw. Place the cleaned bones in a pot with boiling water and vinegar. Remove, dry, and keep in a warm cabinet at 70°C until ready to serve.
5. Txuleta fat:
6. Cut the fat into approximately 5 cm cubes. Place them in an induction pot at power level 7 for 20 minutes, stirring every 3 minutes to completely render the fat.
7. Strain and microfilter using paper towels and a strainer to ensure the fat is clean and free of residue. Reserve.
8. The cow tendons:
9. Serve the tendons in a vacuum cooking bag and seal using cooking program 6.
10. Cook at 92°C for 12 hours.
11. Once cooked, remove from the bag and place on a tray with holes to remove excess collagen.
12. Clean the tendons, separating the meat from the tendon. Place the cleaned tendons in the sorbet processor at speed 8 for 7 minutes at 80°C. Add 1 g of xanthan gum per 400 grams of tendon. Strain through a fine sieve to remove any lumps or impurities.
13. Reserve in the refrigerator.
14. ELABORACIÓN:
15. El hueso de vaca:
16. Limpiar toda la carne de los huesos
17. Cortar los huesos de costilla de 15 centímetros de largo con una sierra, en una olla con agua hirviendo con vinagre colocar los huesos limpios, retirar, secar y hasta que los estén limpios, retirar, secar y reservar en armario caliente a 70°C para el servicio.
18. La grasa de txuleta:
19. Cortar la grasa en dados de 5 cm aprox., ponerlos en una olla de inducción a potencia 7 durante 20 min, revolviendo cada 3 minutos para que la grasa se desgrade totalmente.
20. Colar y microfiltrar usando papeles y un colador, para que la grasa quede limpia y sin residuos. Reservar.
21. Los tendones de vaca:
22. Servir los tendones en bolsa de vacío de cocción y sellar en el programa 6 de cocciones.
23. Cocinar a 92°C durante 12 horas.
24. Una vez cocinados retirar de la bolsa y colocar en una bandeja con agujeros, para retirar el exceso de colágeno.
25. Limpiar los tendones separando la carne del tendón, colocar los tendones limpios en la procesadora de sorbete a velocidad 8 durante 7 minutos a 80°C, agregar 1 g de goma xantana por cada 400 gramos de tendón, colar por un colador fino para retirar cualquier grumo o impurezas.
26. Reservar en cámara.

Other details:
original_language: es

Classifications:
- Dish Type: Main Course
- Cuisine: Other
- Difficulty: Level 3 (Advanced / Michelin)
- Main Ingredient: Meat (Beef, Pork, Lamb, Game)
- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Dairy-Free, Nut-Free

- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy

steps: PREPARATION:
The cow bone:
Clean all the meat from the bones.
Cut the rib bones to 15 centimeters long with a saw. Place the cleaned bones in a pot with boiling water and vinegar. Remove, dry, and keep in a warm cabinet at 70°C until ready to serve.
Txuleta fat:
Cut the fat into approximately 5 cm cubes. Place them in an induction pot at power level 7 for 20 minutes, stirring every 3 minutes to completely render the fat.
Strain and microfilter using paper towels and a strainer to ensure the fat is clean and free of residue. Reserve.
The cow tendons:
Serve the tendons in a vacuum cooking bag and seal using cooking program 6.
Cook at 92°C for 12 hours.
Once cooked, remove from the bag and place on a tray with holes to remove excess collagen.

Clean the tendons, separating the meat from the tendon. Place the cleaned tendons in the sorbet processor at speed 8 for 7 minutes at 80°C. Add 1 g of xanthan gum per 400 grams of tendon. Strain through a fine sieve to remove any lumps or impurities.
Reserve in the refrigerator.
ELABORACIÓN:
El hueso de vaca:
Limpiar toda la carne de los huesos

Cortar los huesos de costilla de 15 centímetros de largo con una sierra, en una olla con agua hirviendo con vinagre colocar los huesos limpios, retirar, secar y hasta que los estén limpios, retirar, secar y reservar en armario caliente a 70°C para el servicio.
La grasa de txuleta:
Cortar la grasa en dados de 5 cm aprox., ponerlos en una olla de inducción a potencia 7 durante 20 min, revolviendo cada 3 minutos para que la grasa se desgrade totalmente.
Colar y microfiltrar usando papeles y un colador, para que la grasa quede limpia y sin residuos. Reservar.
Los tendones de vaca:
Servir los tendones en bolsa de vacío de cocción y sellar en el programa 6 de cocciones.
Cocinar a 92°C durante 12 horas.
Una vez cocinados retirar de la bolsa y colocar en una bandeja con agujeros, para retirar el exceso de colágeno.

Limpiar los tendones separando la carne del tendón, colocar los tendones limpios en la procesadora de sorbete a velocidad 8 durante 7 minutos a 80°C, agregar 1 g de goma xantana por cada 400 gramos de tendón, colar por un colador fino para retirar cualquier grumo o impurezas.
Reservar en cámara.

name: Taro Dumpling
text:
Recipe ID: taro_dumpling_merged
Name: Taro Dumpling

Ingredients:
- 15 g Taro
- 9 g Taiwanese sausage
- Sweet potato 1 x 2L
- Ginger 100 g
- Red Chilli 40 g
- Tong Choi 50 g
- Miso pepper 30 g
- Oil spring onion 70 g
- Spring onion to fry

Steps:
1. Fold
2. Steam for 2.5 min
3. lay them over baking sheets in short boxes NO over laying
4. Brunoise ginger and red chilli, onion
5. In a wok caramelise ginger and red chilli and tong choi and set aside
6. In a wok caramelise ginger and set aside
7. In a wok cook the sweet potato until golden and soften (not too soft) shape should still be in tack
8. Take out the sweet potato, decant into a tray
9. Spread the mix over a tray, pop into the oven then cook
10. At 180C, 0% moisture, roast the mix for 10 min, check and stir, then 5min then check and stir, and roast for 5 more min. Check and take out. The sweet potato mixture should be cooked through, slightly dried on the surface
11. Add chopped caramelised onion, red chilli, tong choi, ginger and spring onion, white pepper to the mix
12. Mix well
13. Once cooled, ready to be used for the dumpling
14. Sweet potato should not be mushy after cooked

Other details:


Classifications:
- Dish Type: Starter / Appetizer
- Cuisine: Chinese
- Difficulty: Level 2 (Intermediate / Professional)
- Main Ingredient: Vegetable-Forward
- Dietary: Nut-Free, Dairy-Free
- Occasion: Family Style / Sharing
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

name: Soy/Eucolyptus Chocolate Ganache
text:
Recipe ID: soy_eucolyptus_chocolate_ganache_merged
Name: Soy/Eucolyptus Chocolate Ganache

Ingredients:
- 3l Soy milk
- 300g Trimoline
- 470g Grue de cacao
- 510g Finely chopped cocoa butter
- 80g eucalyptus leaves
- 1.5kg Manjari chocolate

Steps:
1. Combine all ingredients except for manjari into a small pan and gentle heat up, stiring at all time
2. Spray four sphere moulds with trenspray and place in freezer ready to pipe
3. Once cocoa butter has melted, pore through chinois over chocolate and blend together with hand blender.
4. Pipe mix in moulds while hot and place into blast chiller
5. FOR TEMPERING
6. 1kg Manjari chocolate
7. 500g Finely chopped cocoa butter
8. Weigh into large sous vide bag and gently warm to 55.c
9. Place in Hot Box @ 65°c overnight.

Other details:
Wan Gufh: never abore 70

name: Hot And Sour Soup For Tian Jin Rice
text:
Recipe ID: hot_and_sour_soup_for_tian_jin_rice_parsed
Name: Hot And Sour Soup For Tian Jin Rice

Ingredients:
- knob Ginger
- 300 ml Dashi
- Sesame Oil
- 50 g Carrot
- 25 g Fresh Shiitake Mushroom
- Salt
- 1 tbsp White Pepper
- 3 tbsp Premium Soy
- 2 tbsp Rice Wine Vinegar Kong Yen
- 1 tbsp Ching Kiang Vinegar
- 1 tsp Mirin
- Corn Starch Water

Steps:
1. Infuse dashi with crushed ginger
2. Finely sliced the carrot and shiitake. Really fine like toothpicks
3. In a pan, put in 2-3 tbsp of sesame oil
4. Once the sesame oil is hot, add the carrots and shiitake
5. Season with salt and white pepper
6. Once the carrot and shiitake are nicely sweated, add the dashi without the ginger, discard ginger
7. Bring up to boil
8. Season with premium soy, kong yen rice wine vinegar, ching kiang vinegar, mirin. Taste and see if it needs more salt
9. Take the pan off the heat source. Stir the hot and sour soup in one hand, and pour in the cornstarch water in the other. Keep stirring
10. Bring back to the heat, stir and adjust thickness to a velvety texture

Other details:
Total Time: nan
NOTES: Taste profile should be very peppery from the white pepper
DO NOT PRE MIX: COOK A BATCH JUST BEFORE SERVICE

Classifications:
- Dish Type: Side Dish
- Cuisine: Chinese
- Difficulty: Level 2 (Intermediate / Professional)
- Main Ingredient: Vegetable-Forward
- Dietary: Vegetarian, Nut-Free, Dairy-Free, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Casual / Comfort
- Prep Style: Quick / A la Minute
- Seasonality: Year-Round

name: Puff Pastry Fans
text:
Recipe ID: ventagli_di_sfoglia_merged
Name: Puff Pastry Fans

Ingredients:
- Puff pastry: 1 kg
- Sugar: 100 g

Steps:
1. Prepare the puff pastry according to the recipe on page 288, then roll it out into a rectangle 3-4 mm thick. Roll the longer sides towards the center so that they touch, and press them against each other, so as to flatten them slightly, then cover everything with plastic wrap and put in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour.
2. When the pastry is firm, remove it and cut the roll into slices about 1 cm thick, sprinkle with sugar on both sides and arrange on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Bake at 180° for at least 30 minutes, being careful to turn them halfway through cooking to brown both surfaces.
3. Serve them as soon as they are cold or store them in airtight boxes, away from moisture.

Other details:
Difficoltà:: ★★★
Preparazione:: 80'
Cottura:: 30'
Dosi:: 30 ventagli

name: Drumstick And Turia Sabji
text:
Recipe ID: drumstick_and_turia_sabji_parsed
Name: Drumstick And Turia Sabji

Ingredients:
- 150gr green peas
- 2 big tomato
- 3 drumstick
- 3 Turia ( edge gourd)
- 2 tsp turmeric
- 2 tsp chil powder
- salt
- oil
- 1 tbsp cumin
- 1 tbsp fennel seeds
- 2 tsp of hing
- 1 tsp of soda
- 2 cup of water
- fried thuria muthia ball
- 100gr coconut flour
- 2 tbsp coriander powder
- 2 tbsp fresh chop coriander

Steps:
1. Peel 3 thuria and dice em in big pieces, peel 2 big tomato and dice em in big pieces and season them with 2 tsp turmeric, 2 tsp chil powder and season with salt
2. Heat oil, 1 tbsp cumin, 1 tbsp fennel seeds, 2 tsp of hing and when everything come together nicely roast the veggie with 1 tsp of soda
3. Let cook all together with 2 cup of water, add tomato and turia, once veggie are tender add fried thuria muthia ball
4. At the end Add 100gr coconut flour, 2 tbsp coriander powder, 2 tbsp fresh chop coriander

Other details:

name: Herb Ice Cream
text:
Recipe ID: herb_ice_cream_merged
Name: Herb Ice Cream

Ingredients:
- Part 1
- 1350g whole milk
- 600g whipping cream
- 9 vanilla pods
- 150g trimoline
- 200g caster sugar
- 500g egg yolks
- Part 2
- Blanched herbs
- 100g basil
- 30g chervil
- 20g mint
- 30g fennel

Steps:
1. Warm milk, cream and vanilla pods to 50 celsuis
2. Blend trimoline, caster sugar and egg yolks
3. Mix the milk mix to the eggs, blending together
4. Return back to the heat and cook to 82 celsuis
5. Chill down over ice, pass through a chinois
6. When chilled blend with blanched herbs, and pass again through a fine chinois
7. Pick and weigh out the herbs
8. Blanch the herbs separately, mint takes the longest, 2 minutes
9. Re-fresh in cold water
10. Blend with the adove ice cream base

Other details:


Classifications:

- Cuisine: French
- Difficulty: Level 2 (Intermediate / Professional)
- Main Ingredient: Dairy / Egg
- Dietary: Vegetarian, Nut-Free, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Fine Dining / Plated
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

name: Taiwanese 16 Spice Black Pork Ribs
text:
Recipe ID: taiwanese_16_spice_black_pork_ribs_parsed_2
Name: Taiwanese 16 Spice Black Pork Ribs

Ingredients:
- 300 g Baby Back Ribs
- 1 unit Egg
- 5 g Garlic
- 18 g Michiu Rice Wine or Shaoxing if don't have
- 1 g Baking Powder
- 40 g Corn Flour
- 14 g Superior light soy
- 5 g Sugar
- 2 g White Pepper
- Corn Flour for 2nd Coat

Steps:
1. Mix the Dry Ingredients together so that it blends all nicely.
2. Combine the wet ingredients with the dried ingredients
3. Add the Baby Ribs to the Marinade
4. Let it marinate for 4hours to Overnight
5. Coat it in the Corn Flour before frying it
6. Pre fry the ribs at 160C for 6 Min
7. Let it cool and when one is in order Refry it until Golden and Hot at 180C approx 2-3min depending on Size of the ribs

Other details:

Temperature: 160C, 180C

name: Pickled Shiitake Mushrooms
text:
Recipe ID: shitakes_encurtidos_en_parsed
Name: Pickled Shiitake Mushrooms

Ingredients:
- 1200g dried shiitake mushrooms
- 420g minced ginger
- 1500g sherry vinegar
- 1500g shiitake mushroom water
- 900g sugar
- 60g fish sauce

Steps:
1. a) In a pot, add the dried shiitake mushrooms and water and cook until the shiitake mushrooms are hydrated and tender.
2. b) Let cool in the pot to concentrate the shiitake flavor.
3. c) Reserve the water to make the marinade with the other ingredients; bring to a boil to homogenize the mixture.
4. d) Add the shiitake mushrooms, cut into julienne strips, and marinate for at least 24 hours before use.
5. e) If the shiitake mushrooms are still dry when cutting them, let them cook in the finished marinade.

name: Crispy Leaves
text:
Recipe ID: crispy_leaves_merged
Name: Crispy Leaves

Ingredients:
- For the leaves
- 100g Detander
- 100g Spinach
- 100g Sea Kale
- For wasabi powder
- 15g Wasabi powder
- 100g Seaweed dashi powder
- Extras
- Olive oil spray/ vinaigrette

Steps:
1. Pick leaves so all the the same size (1.5cm).
2. Cut the stems of the leaves.
3. Bring to boil 2l of water with 1% salt.
4. Blanch green and sea leaves for 5 seconds until bright green and soft, remove.
5. Blanch each type of leaf separately.
6. Once the leaves are blanched drain the leaves and place on a silpat disc.
7. Spread the leaves on the silpat disc face up making sure they are flat and not touching each other.
8. Dry in the dehydrator at 55C for 8 hours.
9. Once dry remove and store in airtight container with silica gel.
10. Blend together to form a fine powder.
11. Store in airtight container.
12. Take our leaves and spray them with the oil.
13. Using a shaker, dust the leaves lightly with the wasabi powder.
14. Arrange the leaves neatly with 1 courgette flower and 1 garlic flower inside a napkin on a small white plate.
15. Serve on small white plate.

Other details:
PROCESS: None
FINISHING AND PLATING: None

- Occasion: Fine Dining / Plated

- Seasonality: Year-Round

name: Brioche Lamination
text:
Recipe ID: brioche_lamination_merged
Name: Brioche Lamination

Ingredients:
- batch of dough
- 250g of butter per half
- flour

Steps:
1. Cut the batch in half and roll down to 6cm by hand.
2. Take 250g of butter per half, beat to 2cm thick between cling film and lay in the middle as a diamond (A)
3. Fold the corners to the centre to encase the butter (B)
4. Chill in the freezer for 10 minutes to set the pastry
5. Starting at 30 and decreasing in 2 number intervals, roll the dough down to 11 (be sure to flour sensibly as you go!)
6. Make one book turn
7. Spin the dough to 90 degrees so that the 'seam' faces the rollers
8. Starting at 30 and decreasing in 2 number intervals roll down to 9
9. Make another book turn
10. Spin the dough 90 degrees so that the 'seam' faces the rollers
11. Starting at 30 and decreasing by 2 number intervals, roll down to 8-7. (Please note that due to the temperature in the kitchen and yeast activation, the dough may yield a larger base. Always check with a senior member of staff at this step)
12. Make the final book turn
13. Spin the dough 90 degrees so that the 'seam' faces the rollers
14. Starting at 30 and decreasing in 2 number intervals, roll down to 7
15. Cut the dough into 3 even slabs
16. One at a time with the 'seam' facing the rollers, roll to the width of a gastro tray, approximately 30cm
17. Now spin the dough 90 degrees so that the 'seam' faces the wall
18. Roll down to number 3. This will yield 6 trays per full batch.

Other details:


Classifications:
- Dish Type: Component / Base (Stocks, Sauces, Pastes, Garnishes, Doughs)
- Cuisine: French
- Difficulty: Level 3 (Advanced / Michelin)
- Main Ingredient: Starch/Grain (Pasta, Rice, Bread)
- Dietary: Vegetarian, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Fine Dining / Plated
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

Fold the corners to the centre to encase the butter (B)
Chill in the freezer for 10 minutes to set the pastry
Starting at 30 and decreasing in 2 number intervals, roll the dough down to 11 (be sure to flour sensibly as you go!)
Make one book turn
Spin the dough to 90 degrees so that the 'seam' faces the rollers
Starting at 30 and decreasing in 2 number intervals roll down to 9
Make another book turn
Spin the dough 90 degrees so that the 'seam' faces the rollers

Starting at 30 and decreasing by 2 number intervals, roll down to 8-7. (Please note that due to the temperature in the kitchen and yeast activation, the dough may yield a larger base. Always check with a senior member of staff at this step)

name: To Finish Duck
text:
Recipe ID: to_finish_duck_merged
Name: To Finish Duck

Ingredients:
- Tea Salt
- 200g House tea leaves from XU
- 20g Maldon Salt
- 30g Caster Sugar
- 2g Ground Green Sichuan
- 100g Maltose
- 20 g Light Soy
- 10L Water
- 50g Tea
- 50g Salt
- 30g Sugar
- 20g Dried Orange Peel
- 5g Sichuan Pepper

Steps:
1. Remove stems from tea leave. Fine chop. Combine with salt, sugar and Sichuan pepper.
2. Brush duck with maltose glaze, season with Maldon salt, Sichuan and 'Fragrant' pepper
3. Cook duck in over skin side up at 180C to 48C let Rest.
4. Combine all together and whisk to dissolve salt and sugar.
5. If required Immediately, can take a small measure of water (500g) combine with salt, sugar and tea and bring to a simmer in order to dissolve and brew tea then immediately add to remainder of cold water for brining.
6. Brine duck minimum 4 hrs. maximum 12 hr.
7. Take Brine off and let skin dry

name: Mayonnaise
text:
Recipe ID: maionese_merged
Name: Mayonnaise

Ingredients:
- Egg yolks: 2
- Seed oil: 250 ml
- Lemon: ½ (filtered juice)
- Vinegar: 1 teaspoon
- Salt: to taste
- Ground white pepper (optional)

Steps:
1. In the heat, mayonnaise tends to curdle. It's a shame because, to the connoisseur, the taste is very different from what you find in stores and it's absolutely worth preparing it with your own hands, even if you probably will never buy pre-packaged tubes again. Mayonnaise usually curdles because of the heat or because the oil was added too quickly: remember, to prepare the mayonnaise, oil and eggs must be at room temperature and the yolk must be separated from the white before you finish adding it.
2. To begin, take a bowl with high sides and place it on a damp cloth (so it doesn't move during processing). Pour in the yolks with a pinch of salt and pepper, if you want, then add a teaspoon of vinegar. Start whisking, incorporating the oil a little at a time, mixing and taking care to do it always in the same direction, when you have added all the oil and the sauce will be of the right creaminess you can add the lemon juice and possibly adjust again with salt and pepper. If you don't consume it immediately, you can store the mayonnaise in the fridge covered with plastic wrap.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Preparazione: 30'

raw.name: Mayonnaise
steps: In the heat, mayonnaise tends to curdle. It's a shame because, to the connoisseur, the taste is very different from what you find in stores and it's absolutely worth preparing it with your own hands, even if you probably will never buy pre-packaged tubes again. Mayonnaise usually curdles because of the heat or because the oil was added too quickly: remember, to prepare the mayonnaise, oil and eggs must be at room temperature and the yolk must be separated from the white before you finish adding it.

To begin, take a bowl with high sides and place it on a damp cloth (so it doesn't move during processing). Pour in the yolks with a pinch of salt and pepper, if you want, then add a teaspoon of vinegar. Start whisking, incorporating the oil a little at a time, mixing and taking care to do it always in the same direction, when you have added all the oil and the sauce will be of the right creaminess you can add the lemon juice and possibly adjust again with salt and pepper. If you don't consume it immediately, you can store the mayonnaise in the fridge covered with plastic wrap.

text:
Recipe ID: pear_mousse_merged
Name: Pear Mousse

Ingredients:

- 460g whipped whipping cream
- 2 beans from vanilla
- 8 gelatine
- 100g pear compound

- 50g caster sugar

5. At the same time whip the egg whites, and make an Italian meringue

8. Set in the fridge

name: Walnut Ice Cream
text:
Recipe ID: walnut_ice_cream_merged
Name: Walnut Ice Cream

Ingredients:
- 1550g whole milk
- 400g whipping cream
- 100g walnut oil
- 40g honey
- 5g salt
- 250g sugar
- 50g trimoline
- 400g egg yolk
- 200g roasted walnuts
- 120g walnuts
- 70g caster sugar
- 15g water

Steps:
1. Blend the egg yolks, sugar, honey, salt and walnut oil
2. Warm the milk, cream and walnuts to 50 celsius
3. Add to the yolk mix, and return to the heat
4. Cook till you reach 72 celsius
5. Hold for 10 minutes
6. Chill down on ice
7. Bring the sugar and water to 117 celsius
8. Add the nuts and mix together till they start to cristalise

name: Classic Tiger'S Milk
text:
Recipe ID: classic_tiger's_milk_merged
Name: Classic Tiger'S Milk

Ingredients:
- 1500 gr. Lime juice
- 400 gr. Fish trimmings (sea bass)
- 900 gr. Fish stock
- 225 gr. Figuera onion
- 15 gr. Ginger
- 150 gr. Celery
- 2-2.5 Ud. Habanero pepper
- 95 gr. Salt cilantro stalks
- 90 gr. Garlic
- 450 gr. Ice

Steps:
1. In a container, add all ingredients to a blender, a lot.
2. Blend for 30 seconds per batch without filling it too much, and mix until everything is combined.
3. In the last batch (preferably liquid only), add the cilantro stalks and the habanero pepper without seeds or veins. Strain through a fine-mesh sieve along with the previous mixture, strain well, and reserve the strained liquid for serving.

Other details:

name: Grandma'S Cake
text:
Recipe ID: torta_della_nonna_merged
Name: Grandma'S Cake

Ingredients:
- FOR THE SHORTCRUST PASTRY
- Butter: 200 g
- Flour 00: 400 g
- Salt: 1 pinch
- Egg yolks: 4
- Powdered sugar: 150 g
- FOR THE PASTRY CREAM
- Flour: 80 g
- Fresh milk: 1 liter
- Egg yolks: 8
- Vanilla: 1 pod
- Sugar: 250 g
- FOR THE TOPPING
- Pine nuts: 120 g
- Vanilla-flavored powdered sugar: as needed

Steps:
1. For the shortcrust pastry, refer to the procedure on page 284. When ready, shape it into a low loaf, wrap it in plastic wrap, and let it rest in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour.
2. In the meantime, prepare the pastry cream according to the procedure illustrated on page 280, adhering to the doses in this recipe.
3. While the cream cools in the refrigerator, remove the shortcrust pastry, take about two-thirds of it, and roll it out into a disc that can line a cake tin with a diameter of about 24-26 cm, including the sides. Grease and flour the cake tin and cover it with the pastry. Remove the excess by running a rolling pin over the edges of the cake tin, or by cutting it flush with a small knife with a smooth blade, then prick the shortcrust pastry with the tines of a fork. Finally, pour in the pastry cream.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Cottura: 50'
Preparazione: 30'
Dosi: 10 persone

name: Jabugo
text:
Recipe ID: jabugo_es_parsed
Name: Jabugo

Ingredients:
- 3 kg of ham bones.
- 8 white potatoes.
- 14 carrots.
- 1 pig's foot.
- 3 cow's feet.
- 1kg of knee bones.

Steps:
1. a) We scald the ham bones 3 times; once scalded, we select and check the salt level.
2. b) Blanch the knee bones and the feet.
3. c) Let it cook over medium heat for 8 hours covered.

5. e) Strain and reduce until you get the desired texture.

Ingredients:

- 375 g ‘OO’ flour
- 375 g sugar
- 375 g melted butter
- 200 g corn starch

name: Mushroom Rice
text:
Recipe ID: mushroom_rice_parsed
Name: Mushroom Rice

Ingredients:
- 1 bottle	Sesame Oil
- 50g		Fresh Ginger
- 400g		Rehydrated sliced shitake mushroom
- To Taste	Superior light soy
- Ginger Infused Sesame Oil
- 1300g		Glutinous Rice
- 1200g		Dashi
- 400g		Mushroom for Rice
- 75g		Japanese White Soy
- 30g		Shaoxing Wine
- 5g		Ground White Pepper
- 10g		Maldon Salt

Steps:
1. Heat pan with a small amount rapeseed oil to fry mushroom until golden
2. Add superior light soy to soak into mushrooms.
3. Combine ingredients together.
4. Wrap securely with cling film and steam at 100C for 20--25 minutes
5. Stir, Taste and check with senior member. Adjust with more dashi if needed. Rewrap and steam for another 15-20 minutes.
6. Check and taste again.

name: Korean Lasagna
text:
Recipe ID: lasaa_coreana_es_parsed
Name: Korean Lasagna

Ingredients:
- 20kg ground meat (Lyo)
- 16kg vine tomatoes
- 8 kg red onions
- 1 kg garlic cloves
- 1472kg chili garli (crushed)
- 4 l kimuchi
- 4l fino
- 5kg pickled shiitake mushrooms
- 250g Chinese five spice powder
- 4 pieces of palm sugar

Steps:
1. a) Finely chop all the vegetables and sauté until golden brown.
2. b) Once the sofrito is ready, add the chili garli and fry briefly with the sofrito, then add the wine and let it evaporate.
3. c) Add the meat to the sofrito and mix well, always over the heat, until the meat is loose.
4. d) Finally, season with the pickled shiitake mushrooms, five spice powder, and kimuchi.
5. e) Set aside for later use.
6. f) Do not overcook the meat.

Other details:

name: Mushroom Dumpling
text:
Recipe ID: mushroom_dumpling_merged
Name: Mushroom Dumpling

Ingredients:
- Fresh Shiitake 4500 g
- Oyster Mushroom 750 g
- Shimeji Mushroom 600 g
- Mushroom Water 600 g
- Diced ginger 180 g
- Shaoxing wine 30 g
- Light Soy Sauce 150 ml
- Lotus root grated finely 900 g
- Oil 2400 g
- 1:1 = Sichuan chilli oil. Tiger dressing
- DUMPLING = 10g dough, 20g fillings

Steps:
1. Cut mushrooms to rough 1cm size
2. In a hot pan fry the diced ginger
3. Add mushrooms and cook down nicely
4. Once nicely browned, add blized rehydrated shiitake mushroom
5. Then add chopped fresh shiitake and chopped Oyster Mushroom
6. Add a pinch of salt, then further to cook until wilted
7. Add shao xin wine and taste mushroom
8. Add soy sauce
9. Add grated lotus root and cook to combine
10. Add Mushroom water and let it cook down and form a good consistency

Other details:

Ingredients:

- Transglutaminase Activia PB (Ajinomoto) 0.005 kg
- Bread (15 x 15 cm) 2 slices

Steps:
1. Fillet the mackerel, remove the bones, and dust the flesh side with Transglutaminase Activia PB using a sieve. Overlap the two halves and portion. Slice the bread to measure 1 on a slicer. Place the mackerel on top and trim neatly. Let it rest in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours.

name: Caprese Cake
text:
Recipe ID: caprese_cake_merged
Name: Caprese Cake

Ingredients:
- Butter: 250 g
- Dark chocolate: 250 g
- Peeled almonds: 300 g
- Eggs (large): 5
- Sugar: 200 g
- FOR SPRINKLING
- Powdered sugar: as needed

Steps:
1. If the almonds are not already peeled, blanch them in boiling water for 1 minute, then drain and remove the skin. Toast them lightly in the oven at about 200°, then, when they are cold, crush them not too finely.
2. Break the dark chocolate into pieces and melt it in a bain-marie until it is smooth and without lumps, then let it cool slightly, stirring occasionally.
3. With the help of a food processor, beat the butter (at room temperature) with half of the sugar and the egg yolks, then add the chocolate, mix it and transfer the mixture to a large bowl, where you will pour the chopped almonds. Separately, whip the egg whites until stiff peaks form with the remaining sugar, then gently fold them into the mixture, stirring from bottom to top.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Cottura: 60'
Preparazione: 20'
Dosi: 8 persone

text:

Name: Express Stew

Ingredients:
- Beef tongue
- Water

Steps:
1. a) Place the beef tongues in a large pot and cover them with plenty of water.
2. b) Cover and bring to a boil for 10/11 hours (check for doneness by gently piercing until it no longer resists).
3. c) Once cooked, remove the beef tongues, drain, and wrap in plastic wrap and chill in the blast chiller.
4. d) Save some of the cooking water.

name: Delizie Al Limone
text:
Recipe ID: delizie_al_limone_merged
Name: Delizie Al Limone

Ingredients:
- FOR THE SPONGE CAKE BASE
- Flour 00: 300 g
- Limoni di Sorrento: 1
- Eggs: 300 g of egg whites
- Eggs: 300 g of egg yolks
- Sugar: 300 g
- FOR THE LEMON CREAM
- Butter: 200 ml
- Water: 100 ml
- Limoncello: 50 ml
- Lemons: 2 (grated zest)
- Fresh Cream: 500 ml
- Eggs: 200 g of egg yolks
- Sugar: 200 g
- Powdered sugar: 50 g
- FOR THE LIMONCELLO SYRUP
- Water: 100 ml
- Limoncello: 150 ml
- Lemons: 1 (zest)
- Sugar: 100 g

Steps:
1. 1 Whip the egg whites (which must be at room temperature) with a mixer. When they begin to turn white, add 50 g of sugar, let it whip for a minute, then add another 50 g of sugar and, after another minute, another 50 g. Separately, whisk the egg yolks with 150 g of sugar for at least 7-8 minutes. When the two mixtures are ready, gently mix them together in a large bowl, where you will add the previously sifted flour and the grated zest of 1 lemon.
2. 2 Insert the mixture obtained into a pastry bag and squeeze it into the appropriate silicone mold with a diameter of 7-8 cm. Bake at 180° for about 20 minutes. When cooked, remove from the oven and let it cool slightly before extracting the spheres from the mold and letting them cool completely on a wire rack.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★★
Cottura:: 40'
Preparazione:: 180'
Dosi:: 15 delizie

name: To Assemble
text:
Recipe ID: to_assemble_parsed
Name: To Assemble

Ingredients:
- 1 silicon mold (shaped like a flowerpot)

Steps:
1. Place the mousse at the bottom of the mold, then add the sponge and repeat until you have 2 layers of sponge suspended in 3 layers of mousse adding to the last layer enough strawberry paste to cover the rim of the sponge.
2. Set the cake in the blast chiller.
3. Unmold the frozen mousse and glaze it upside down (with the small end in the air) on a skinny yogurt pot with the terracotta glaze in 1 coating.
4. Return the cake to the freezer as soon as its glazed so it doesn’t melt.
5. Once set, trim down any excess glaze from the rim, flip it over and allow it to defrost in the fridge in a covered container.

Other details:

name: Lemongrass Kombucha
text:
Recipe ID: kombucha_de_lemongrass_merged
Name: Lemongrass Kombucha

Ingredients:
- Lemongrass Kombucha: (For 2 l)
- 30 units lemongrass stalks
- 100 g white sugar
- Piece of kombucha SCOBY (mother)
- 1 spoonful of previously fermented liquid kombucha
- Lemongrass Kombucha: (For 2 l)
- 30 units lemongrass stalks
- 100 g white sugar
- Piece of kombucha SCOBY (mother)
- 1 spoonful of previously fermented liquid kombucha

Steps:
1. Lemongrass Kombucha:
2. Bruise or lightly crush the lemongrass.
3. Boil water and add the lemongrass. Remove from heat, cover with plastic wrap, and let steep for 1 hour. After this time, strain the infusion and cool.
4. When the infusion is cold, add the sugar, the piece of kombucha SCOBY (mother), and a spoonful of previously fermented liquid kombucha. For example, let it ferment for 4 days at room temperature. After this time, taste the fermented liquid, and if it reaches its point of fermentation, bottle it and leave it in the refrigerator for a week before using.
5. Lemongrass Kombucha:
6. Bruise or lightly crush the lemongrass.
7. Boil water and add the lemongrass. Remove from heat, cover with plastic wrap, and let steep for 1 hour. After this time, strain the infusion and cool.
8. When the infusion is cold, add the sugar, the piece of kombucha SCOBY (mother), and a spoonful of previously fermented liquid kombucha. For example, let it ferment for 4 days at room temperature. After this time, taste the fermented liquid, and if it reaches its point of fermentation, bottle it and leave it in the refrigerator for a week before using.

name: Tuna Belly Civet
text:
Recipe ID: civet_de_sangacho_merged
Name: Tuna Belly Civet

Ingredients:
- INGREDIENTS:
(For 4 people)
- The tuna belly:
- 200 g tuna belly
- The finishing:
- 160 ml hare broth (stock batch)
- 30 g hare civet
- 20 g pig's blood
- 6 g kuzu starch
- 12 units tuna belly
- 5% kuzu starch

Steps:
1. ELABORATION:
2. The tuna belly:
3. Thaw the tuna belly. Remove any bones.
Reserve only the loin. Cut into regular sizes and
reserve 3 pieces (20 g. in total) per serving in
refrigeration.
4. FINISHING AND PRESENTATION
5. Mix the hare broth, the civet, and the blood.
Reserve a portion for finishing.
6. Marinate the tuna belly in the previous mixture in the fridge, 
and let it macerate for 1 hour covered with plastic wrap.
7. With the finishing broth, mix the kuzu starch in a
small saucepan while cold, with the finishing broth.
Ensure the starch is dissolved in the liquid in a
homogeneous manner.
8. Bring the mixture to a gentle heat and cook,
stirring constantly with a silicone spatula until a
translucent and slightly gelatinous consistency is achieved. The
mixture should not boil, but it must be ensured that
the starch is perfectly cooked. Reserve covered with
plastic wrap in contact at room temperature and allow
to cool.
9. After this time, drain the tuna belly. Bring the
finishing broth to 80ºC (induction 9). Introduce the
tuna belly and lower to 50ºC (induction 5). For 1
minute and 30 seconds, coat the tuna belly
stirring constantly.
10. On a hot plate, arrange the tuna belly and coat
completely with the broth.

Other details:

- The cheese foam:

- 3 units of nitrogen chargers

name: Cardamom Ice Cream
text:
Recipe ID: cardamom_ice_cream_merged
Name: Cardamom Ice Cream

Ingredients:
- 1200g whole milk
- 300g whipping cream
- 120g egg yolk
- 195g caster sugar
- 75g milk powder
- 15g orange zest
- 7g cardamom
- 115g sour cream

Steps:
1. Bring to the boil milk, cream, zest and cardamom
2. Take of heat and add the milk powder, cool to 50 celsuis
3. Blend the yolks with the sugar and add the milk when at 50 celsuis
4. Return to the heat and cook to 70 celsuis
5. Hold in water bath at 72 celsuis for 10 minutes
6. Chill in ice bath, then pass and blend in sour cream

name: Mackerel And Tomato
text:
Recipe ID: mackerel_and_tomato_merged
Name: Mackerel And Tomato

Ingredients:
- For the Mackerel
- 4 Mackerel
- For the Tomato and marzano
- 1kg Tomato and marzano
- Extras
- Ricotta cheese
- For the Mackerel
- 70g salt
- 30g Sugar
- 2 mackerel
- For the Pickle
- 50g Rice vinegar
- 10g Ginger
- 10g Dried kombu
- For the daikon
- 100g Light soy sauce
- 50g Rice vinegar
- 100g Sake
- 2g Garlic
- 10g Ginger
- 15g fresh wasabi
- Extras
- Pennywort
- Fresh wasabi

Steps:
1. Clean mackerel and remove fillets.
2. Leave in ice water for 20 mins to remove blood.
3. Dry and lightly salt for 20 minutes. Rinse and dry.
4. Using tweezers remove the bones and season on non-skin side with black pepper.
5. Smoked with cherry bark for 5 minutes.
6. Remove skin and trim the skin and store in fridge skin side up.
7. Using a peeler peel the tomatoes and cook in 2% calcium solution for 1 hour.
8. Once cooked rinse in water.
9. Place on a tray with oil and cook in the oven at 130C for 120 mins.
10. Remove and allow to cool. Remove from oil.
11. Place in a dehydrator at 55C for 6 hours turning regularly.
12. Remove and store in fridge.
13. FINISHING AND PLATING
14. Warm a fillet in the holding oven.
15. Place a fillet of the mackerel on the per gastro.
16. Torch the skin side until charred.
17. Place on plate with tomato and a spoon of ricotta cheese.
18. PROCESS
19. 1. Clean mackerel and remove fillets.
20. 2. Leave in ice water for 20 mins to remove excess blood.
21. 3. Dry and lightly salt for 2.5 hours. Rinse and drying tweezers, remove the bones.
22. 4. Remove tweezers, remove the bones.
23. 5. Remove membrane on the skin and pickle in the liquid 45 minutes.
24. 6. Dry and store in fridge.
25. 1. Infuse the ginger and kombu in the vinegar.
26. 1. Add all the ingredients together and infuse 10 minutes then pass.
27. 2. Add the pickle liquid to the daikon and cook at 85C for 2 hours.
28. 3. Chill the remove from bag and slice thin on the mandolin.
29. FINISHING AND PLATING
30. 1. Slice the mackerel into 1.5cm pieces and place on plate.
31. 2. Place 4 strips of the daikon on the plate and garnish with the wasabi and pennywort.

Other details:

- Cuisine: Japanese

- Dietary: Dairy-Free, Nut-Free, Pescatarian, Gluten-Free

Torch the skin side until charred.
Place on plate with tomato and a spoon of ricotta cheese.
PROCESS
1. Clean mackerel and remove fillets.
2. Leave in ice water for 20 mins to remove excess blood.
3. Dry and lightly salt for 2.5 hours. Rinse and drying tweezers, remove the bones.
4. Remove tweezers, remove the bones.
5. Remove membrane on the skin and pickle in the liquid 45 minutes.
6. Dry and store in fridge.
1. Infuse the ginger and kombu in the vinegar.
1. Add all the ingredients together and infuse 10 minutes then pass.
2. Add the pickle liquid to the daikon and cook at 85C for 2 hours.
3. Chill the remove from bag and slice thin on the mandolin.
FINISHING AND PLATING
1. Slice the mackerel into 1.5cm pieces and place on plate.
2. Place 4 strips of the daikon on the plate and garnish with the wasabi and pennywort.

name: Mashed Corn And Hazelnut Sour Cream
text:
Recipe ID: crema_ácida_de_maiz_majado_y_avellanas_merged
Name: Mashed Corn And Hazelnut Sour Cream

Ingredients:
- (For 4 people.)
- Fermented Corn:
- 1 can of corn (212g)
- 50 ml water
- 4 g. koji-kin
- Allspice Pepper Tea:
- 200 g. water
- 20 g. allspice pepper
- Hazelnut Milk:
- 100 g. hazelnut
- 200g. water

Steps:
1. ELABORATION:
2. Fermented Corn:
3. Drain the water from the canned corn.
4. Grind the canned corn and water in a Thermomix for 15 minutes at 40°C. Reserve the mixture in a glass container.
5. Disinfect the molds that will be used for this preparation with alcohol to inoculate the corn.
6. Cut the cheesecloth into 12cm x 12cm squares.
7. Soak the cheesecloth in hot water to disinfect and drain the water from the cheesecloth (per person) into the mold without touching the base (which recovers excess water). It is very important that we leave a space between the molds so that the corn can breathe.
8. Sprinkle the Koji-kin evenly over the corn.
9. Wrap the trays with film and make holes in the film.
10. Ferment the corn in a warm closet at 40°C for 24 hours. After this time, store the corn wrapped in the cloth and on the trays with perforated film in the refrigerator to stop the fermentation.
11. Reserve the corn in the refrigerator for a maximum of 3 days.
12. Allspice Pepper Tea:
13. Infuse the allspice pepper in boiling water for 1 hour, strain and reserve.
14. Hazelnut Milk:
15. In a Thermomix, grind the hazelnuts with the water for 15 minutes. Pass the mixture through superbags and reserve in the refrigerator.

Other details:

translated_from: es

- Seasonality: Year-Round

Drain the water from the canned corn.
Grind the canned corn and water in a Thermomix for 15 minutes at 40°C. Reserve the mixture in a glass container.
Disinfect the molds that will be used for this preparation with alcohol to inoculate the corn.
Cut the cheesecloth into 12cm x 12cm squares.

Soak the cheesecloth in hot water to disinfect and drain the water from the cheesecloth (per person) into the mold without touching the base (which recovers excess water). It is very important that we leave a space between the molds so that the corn can breathe.
Sprinkle the Koji-kin evenly over the corn.
Wrap the trays with film and make holes in the film.
Ferment the corn in a warm closet at 40°C for 24 hours. After this time, store the corn wrapped in the cloth and on the trays with perforated film in the refrigerator to stop the fermentation.
Reserve the corn in the refrigerator for a maximum of 3 days.
Allspice Pepper Tea:
Infuse the allspice pepper in boiling water for 1 hour, strain and reserve.
Hazelnut Milk:
In a Thermomix, grind the hazelnuts with the water for 15 minutes. Pass the mixture through superbags and reserve in the refrigerator.

name: Milk Wafers
text:
Recipe ID: milk_wafers_merged
Name: Milk Wafers

Ingredients:
- 3 EGGS
- 225 SUGAR
- 1 VANILLA
- 120 MILK
- 55 BUTTER MELTED
- 175 FLOUR
- 40G MILK POWDER (BUFFALO)
- 4 G BAKING POWDER

Steps:
1. WHISK EGGS, SUGAR AND VANILLA ON KITCHENAID TO A SABAYON
2. ADD THE MILK
3. WHISK FLOUR, MILK POWDER AND BAKING POWDER TOGETHOR
4. ADD FLOUR MIX TO EGG RIG
5. DO NOT OVERMIX
6. ADD BUTTER AND FOLD THROUGH
7. WEIGH 700G INTO A FOIL LINED LARGE FRAME
8. STEAM @100C FOR 30 MINS
9. REST AND PORTION INTO SLABS.
10. FREEZE HARD AND SLICE ON SLICER TO .5 ON LARGEST FACE OF SLAB
11. BAKE @130C FOR 12 MINS BETWEEN 5 CIGAR RODS - SPRAYED WELL
12. STORE IN TAKEAWAYS- 8 PER BOX

Other details:
MAKES: 1 FRAME

name: Mulled Wine
text:
Recipe ID: mulled_wine_merged
Name: Mulled Wine

Ingredients:
- Full-bodied red wine: 1 liter
- Sugar: 200 g
- Oranges: 1 (zest)
- Lemons: 1 (zest)
- Cinnamon: 2 sticks
- Cloves: 8
- Nutmeg: ½

Steps:
1. Using a potato peeler, cut thin slices of lemon and orange zest, trying to exclude as much of the white and bitter part as possible. Then place the zests in a steel saucepan with not too high edges with the sugar, spices and, lastly, the wine. Turn on the heat and slowly bring to a boil: without stopping stirring, boil for 5 minutes, until the sugar is completely dissolved.
2. At this point, ignite the wine using a long wooden skewer, which will ensure a safe distance: let the fire go out on its own. Then filter the mulled wine with a very fine-mesh strainer and serve hot.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Cottura: 10'
Preparazione: 5'
Dosi: 4 persone

name: Dou Hua
text:
Recipe ID: dou_hua_merged
Name: Dou Hua

Ingredients:
- 100 G Soy bean
- 1000 ML Filter Water
- 1 L Soy Milk
- 5 g Gypsum powder
- 5 g Corn Flour
- 40 ML still water

Steps:
1. Soak soy bean with water overnight (12hrs)
2. filtered water, 100g soy bean (before soak) = 500ml
3. Blitz and squeeze the soy beans
4. Bring to boil, pass through muslin cloth. Cool down and keep it for the next day
5. Can keep soy milk up to 4 days
6. Bring the soy milk to boiled then pass through muslin again
7. Cool the soy milk down to 82C (while waiting mix the gypsum powder and corn flour and still water in a bowl)
8. mix the soy milk and the gypsum solution together and cover it with cling film. Let it set for 30min at room
9. Have soy milk ready, but make dou hua daily for freshness

Other details:
Total Time: 48 min
NOTES: None

name: Shortcrust Pastry
text:
Recipe ID: shortcrust_pastry_merged
Name: Shortcrust Pastry

Ingredients:
- Butter: 250 g
- All-purpose flour (00): 500 g
- Eggs (large): 4 yolks
- Powdered sugar: 200 g
- Lemon: 1 (zest)
- Salt: 1 pinch

Steps:
1. Put the flour, a pinch of salt, and the butter, just taken out of the fridge, into the mixer and blend until you get a sandy and floury mixture, to which you will add the powdered sugar. At this point, form the classic fountain on the pastry board, in the center of which you will pour the lemon zest and egg yolks. Quickly mix everything together, the resulting dough will be compact and elastic.
2. Form a ball, wrap it with plastic wrap and let it rest in the refrigerator for at least half an hour, after which the shortcrust pastry will be ready to be rolled out and used to prepare cookies and pies.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Preparazione: 60'
Dosi: 6 persone

name: Panna Cotta
text:
Recipe ID: panna_cotta_merged
Name: Panna Cotta

Ingredients:
- Gelatin sheets: 3 sheets (6 g)
- Liquid cream: 500 ml
- Vanilla: 1 pod
- Powdered sugar: 150 g

Steps:
1. Panna cotta is a delicious idea to conclude a dinner. It is prepared the night before and is even better if left in the fridge for a whole night; it is very easy to make but, garnished properly, it is super-scenographic and, finally, it will make you look good because it is generally thought that it is an impossible recipe. Instead, just know some peculiarities of the fundamental ingredient, gelatin sheets, and the game is done. You should know that it, the gelatin sheets, must make a sort of thermal journey: it must be soaked in ice water, then squeezed, drained and dissolved in the hottest ingredient you have. Mix everything and the dessert is solved.
2. 1 Soak the gelatin sheets in cold water for 10 minutes; cut the vanilla pod lengthwise and, with the back of a knife, extract the internal seeds.
3. 2 Pour the cream into a saucepan with the split vanilla pod, the internal seeds and the powdered sugar; heat over low heat. When it is about to boil, add the well-squeezed gelatin sheets and stir to dissolve completely. Filter the mixture with a fine-mesh sieve and prepare individual molds, or a single loaf pan.
4. 3 Keep everything in the fridge for at least 5 hours. When serving, immerse the mold in boiling water for a second, then unmold onto a serving plate.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Cottura: 15'
Preparazione: 15'
Dosi: 4 persone

name: Red Gooseberry/Rose Emulsion
text:
Recipe ID: red_gooseberryrose_emulsion_parsed
Name: Red Gooseberry/Rose Emulsion

Ingredients:
- 125 g red gooseberry puree
- 125 g rose oil*
- 2 g leaf gelatin

Steps:
1. Place the two ingredients together in a glass container and sonicate them at 50% amplitude over an ice bath, stirring constantly until the mixture homogenizes completely and goes a pale and opaque pink.
2. Take a portion of the puree out of the batch, and place it in a small pot on the stove.
3. Warm it slightly, and while doing so, bloom 2 g (1 silver gelatin sheet) in ice water.
4. Once bloomed, wring out the excess water and dissolve it in the warmed puree on the stove.
5. Once dissolved, transfer the warm puree back into the rest of the batch.
6. Allow the emulsion to set in the fridge.

Other details:

name: Lovage Puree For Pig'S Head Canape
text:
Recipe ID: lovage_puree_for_pig's_head_canape_merged
Name: Lovage Puree For Pig'S Head Canape

Ingredients:
- 100g picked fresh lovage
- 160g cold water
- 2g gelespressa
- 20g grapeseed oil
- 2g Maldon salt

Steps:
1. Blanch the lovage in boiling water - first for 1 minute, refresh in ice water to set the colour, then back in boiling water for a further 5 minutes. Refresh again.
2. Squeeze all the water from the lovage, the remaining weight should be 40g.
3. Blend the 40g lovage with 160g cold water in vita prep for 2 minutes. Scrape the sides of the jug down with a Maurice.
4. Add salt and gelespressa and blend on high for a further 30 seconds. Scrape down the sides again and then blend, adding the oil for a further 1 minute.
5. Pass and chill over ice.

text:

name: Tania’S Favourite
text:
Recipe ID: tanias_favourite_parsed
Name: Tania’S Favourite

Ingredients:
- chicken thighs
- onion
- ginger
- chilli
- ground coriander
- chilli powder
- turmeric
- salt
- oil
- cardamoms pods
- cloves
- cinnamon sticks
- bay leaves
- green chilli
- onion
- ginger
- garlic clove
- chilli powder
- turmeric
- coriander
- tomato
- coconut
- water
- coconut milk powder
- fresh curry leaves
- butter
- fresh coriander

Steps:
1. Marinate the chicken for 1 hour and half, with chopped onion, chop ginger, 3 chop chilli, 1 tbsp of ground coriander, 1 tsp of chilli powder, 1 tsp turmeric and salt to taste.
2. In this order, heat 4 tbsp of oil in a pan, add 2 cardamoms pods, 3 cloves, 7 gr of cinnamon sticks, 1 bay leaves, 4 green chilli chopped, 2 bug onion finely chop, 10 gr of ginger chop and 9 garlic clove peeled, toast until everything it’s golden brown.
3. Add 250 gr of dice chicken thighs, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1/2 tsp turmeric,3 tsp of coriander,cover with lid and keep cooking and stirring occasionally
4. Chop 1 tomato and blend it until smooth pure
5. Shave half coconut and mix it with 1 cup of water and strain with a kitchen cloth, then add 3 tbsp of coconut milk powder to coconut water and mix all well
6. Once the chicken has cooked for 20 minutes, pour the tomato pure inside the pan add salt to taste, and cook for 2 minutes
7. Add coconut water and bring the stew to simmer
8. Insert a 10  fresh curry leaves bouquet
9. Add 10 gr of butter
10. Once the chicken it's cooked and soft
11. Finish by taking off the bouquet and add fresh coriander

Other details:
servings: 5 people
notes: This recipe can be done with, other types of meat, vegetables or fish
goes well with: Rice and kachumber

- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Nut-Free, Alcohol-Free

- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

name: Dhokla
text:
Recipe ID: dhokla_parsed
Name: Dhokla

Ingredients:
- 2 cup rice
- 1 cup Urad dal
- 1 cup of dry chickpeas
- 2 cup of water
- 1 tbsp of sugar
- 1 tsp hing
- 1 tbsp chili paste
- 2 tbsp of sunflower oil
- Eno fruit salt
- black pepper

Steps:
1. Soak in water overnight 2 cup rice, 1 cup Urad dal and soak for 6 hour before 1 cup of dry chickpeas
2. Strain everything and wash properly, blend by adding 2 cup of water 1 tbsp of sugar 1 tsp hing, 1 tbsp chili paste, 2 tbsp of sunflower oil , until obtain a smooth white semi thick cream ( blended yogurt texture)
3. After add Eno fruit salt mix well and let it rest for 10 minutes
4. Place  dhokla steamer on with water
5. Grease the surface on were you will be putting the DHokla mix to cook, pour the mix in with some black pepper, and proceed to cook for 12-15 minutes
6. Once cook , brush it with same water and oil on top for avoiding dryness

Other details:


Classifications:
- Dish Type: Starter / Appetizer
- Cuisine: Indian
- Difficulty: Level 2 (Intermediate / Professional)
- Main Ingredient: Starch/Grain (Pasta, Rice, Bread)
- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Vegan, Vegetarian, Nut-Free, Dairy-Free, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Family Style / Sharing
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

name: Hazelnut Praline
text:
Recipe ID: hazelnut_praline_parsed
Name: Hazelnut Praline

Ingredients:
- 60 g Sugar Caster
- 50 g Chinkiang vinegar
- 110 g toasted Hazelnut
- 1 Salt
- 10 g water
- 15 g Soy
- 23 g Hazelnut oil

Steps:
1. Put the sugar and Chinkiang vinegar in a pan and make a caramel with it
2. When its dark add the toasted hazelnuts in and coat all the nuts with it, ike making a praline
3. pour the content to a tray lined with parchment paper and let it cool
4. Blitz until smooth paste while adding the other ingredients to make it a bit more loose
5. Reheat before service, you might need to add some water to make it a bit looser

Other details:
Total Time: 45min

name: Sea Cucumber Bestiary
text:
Recipe ID: bestiario_de_pepino_de_mar_merged
Name: Sea Cucumber Bestiary

Ingredients:
- The Black Cucumber:
1 unit of black cucumber
- The White Cucumber:
50 g of white cucumber
- The Garum Jelly:
8 g of cucumber gelatin
42 g of water
0.25 g of agar-agar
- The Roll:
1 unit of black cucumber
50 g of white cucumber
50 g of garum jelly
c/s tablespoon
- The Black Cucumber:
1 unit of black cucumber
- The White Cucumber:
50 g of white cucumber
- The Garum Jelly:
8 g of cucumber gelatin
42 g of water
0.25 g of agar-agar
- The Roll:
1 unit of black cucumber
50 g of white cucumber
50 g of garum jelly
c/s (quantity sufficient) tablespoon

Steps:
1. ELABORATION:
2. The Black Cucumber:
Eviscerate the cucumber. Scrub the cucumbers with salt to remove the viscosity, rinse them very well in cold water. Cook from cold water for 50 minutes from when it starts to boil and lower the heat to medium-low. Cool and empty as much as possible.
3. The White Cucumber:
Eviscerate the cucumber and rinse very well. With a tenderizing mallet, flatten the fibers of the cucumber until it is soft to the touch and feels even softer. Blanch the cucumber for 10 seconds and cool. Cut into pieces of 1x0.3 cm.
4. The Garum Jelly:
Mix the water with the garum and the agar, bring to 80°C. Cool until it sets, and blend until you get a fluid gel. Mix with the white cucumber.
Keep warm to assemble the cucumbers.
5. The Roll:
Put the garum jelly with the white cucumber inside the black cucumber.
Sprinkle delicately with transglutaminase, roll up tightly in a film, so that when cut, it maintains its shape. Refrigerate and temper to a width of 2 cm, keep in the chamber, and temper for serving.
6. ELABORACION:
7. The Black Cucumber:
Eviscerate the cucumber. Scrub the cucumbers with salt to remove the viscosity, rinse them very well in cold water. Cook from cold water for 50 minutes from when it starts to boil and lower the heat to medium-low. Cool and empty as much as possible.
8. The White Cucumber:
Eviscerate the cucumber and rinse very well. With a tenderizing mallet, flatten the fibers of the cucumber until it is soft to the touch and feels even softer. Blanch the cucumber for 10 seconds and cool. Cut into pieces of 1x0.3 cm.
9. The Garum Jelly:
Mix the water with the garum and the agar, bring to 80°C. Cool until it sets, and blend until you get a fluid gel. Mix with the white cucumber.
Keep warm to assemble the cucumbers.
10. The Roll:
Put the garum jelly with the white cucumber inside the black cucumber.
Sprinkle delicately with transglutaminase, roll up tightly in a film, so that when cut, it maintains its shape. Refrigerate and temper to a width of 2 cm, keep in the chamber, and temper for serving.

name: Caramelized Egg
text:
Recipe ID: uovo_caramellato_merged
Name: Caramelized Egg

Ingredients:
- Quail eggs
- White wine vinegar
- Coarse salt
- Nutmeg
- Sichuan pepper
- Bay leaf
- For the caramel
- Glucose
- Fondant sugar
- 8 Quail eggs
- 10 White wine vinegar
- As needed Coarse salt
- As needed Nutmeg
- As needed Sichuan pepper
- Bay leaf
- 100 Glucose
- 200 Fondant sugar

Steps:
1. Break the eggs into a container and set aside.
2. In a pot, boil water with a little vinegar.
3. At the boiling point, add the eggs one at a time, with the help of a spider skimmer, remove all the egg white that rises to the surface, remove the eggs, transfer them to a container with cold water and salt, dry and set aside.
4. Caramel
5. Bring the fondant and glucose to a boil until 160°.
6. Remove from heat and spread on silpat and pull it very thin, cut it into rectangles of about 3 x 4.
7. Place the egg on a baking sheet.
8. Cover it with a sheet of sugar and pass it through the salamander until the caramel softens and wraps the egg; remove from the salamander, turn it and repeat the operation.
9. Place the egg on a spoon, add salt and serve.

Other details:

- Fresh, white, and tender almonds 50

Steps:
1. Clean the anchovies and dry them well

3. Make an anchovy pesto with lemon and almonds
4. Toast the white bread, fill it with the anchovy pesto, press it a little and cut out rectangles of 3cm X 7cm

name: Tania’S Favourite
text:
Recipe ID: tanias_favourite_parsed_2
Name: Tania’S Favourite

Ingredients:
- chicken thighs (250 gr, diced)
- onion (chopped)
- ginger (chopped)
- chilli (3, chopped)
- ground coriander (1 tbsp)
- chilli powder (1 tsp)
- turmeric (1 tsp)
- salt to taste
- oil (4 tbsp)
- cardamoms pods (2)
- cloves (3)
- cinnamon sticks (7 gr)
- bay leaves (1)
- green chilli (4, chopped)
- onion (2, finely chopped)
- ginger (10 gr, chopped)
- garlic clove (9, peeled)
- chilli powder (1 tsp)
- turmeric (1/2 tsp)
- coriander (3 tsp)
- tomato (1, chopped and blended)
- coconut (half, shaved)
- water (1 cup)
- coconut milk powder (3 tbsp)
- fresh curry leaves (10)
- butter (10 gr)
- fresh coriander

Steps:
1. Marinate the chicken for 1 hour and half, with chopped onion, chop ginger, 3 chop chilli, 1 tbsp of ground coriander, 1 tsp of chilli powder, 1 tsp turmeric and salt to taste.
2. In this order, heat 4 tbsp of oil in a pan, add 2 cardamoms pods, 3 cloves, 7 gr of cinnamon sticks, 1 bay leaves, 4 green chilli chopped, 2 bug onion finely chop, 10 gr of ginger chop and 9 garlic clove peeled, toast until everything it’s golden brown.
3. Add 250 gr of dice chicken thighs, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1/2 tsp turmeric,3 tsp of coriander,cover with lid and keep cooking and stirring occasionally
4. Chop 1 tomato and blend it until smooth pure
5. Shave half coconut and mix it with 1 cup of water and strain with a kitchen cloth, then add 3 tbsp of coconut milk powder to coconut water and mix all well
6. Once the chicken has cooked for 20 minutes, pour the tomato pure inside the pan add salt to taste, and cook for 2 minutes
7. Add coconut water and bring the stew to simmer
8. Insert a 10  fresh curry leaves bouquet
9. Add 10 gr of butter
10. Once the chicken it's cooked and soft
11. Finish by taking off the bouquet and add fresh coriander

Other details:
servings: 5 people
notes: This recipe can be done with, other types of meat, vegetables or fish
goes_well_with: Rice and kachumber

name: Curry Gravy
text:
Recipe ID: curry_gravy_parsed
Name: Curry Gravy

Ingredients:
- basmati rice
- eggs (1 per person)
- 2 coconuts
- 28 gr of kashmiri chili
- 1 handful of dry tamarind
- 3 tbsp of sunflower oil
- 1 tsp fenugreek
- 2 1\2 spoon of mustard seeds
- 2 1\2 spoon of cumin seeds
- 8 small cloves
- 1 cinnamon stick
- 4 cardamom
- 6 garlic cloves
- 15 curry leaves
- 1 big red onion
- 1 tsp turmeric
- 4 tsp ground coriander
- 1 coconut shaved
- 1 big tomato
- 4 ladle of sunflower oil
- 50 gr of coconut milk powder
- salt
- 3 tbsp of the tamarind water
- 1 tsp of sugar

Steps:
1. Boil basmati rice for 11 minute ( perfect texture personally)
2. And boil eggs ( 1 per person)
3. Open 2 coconut and shave the pulp, after weight 28 gr of kashmiri chili ( precedently clean it from seed and top)
4. 1 handful of dry tamarind soaked in water and set it aside
5. Heat the pan and put 3 tbsp of sunflower oil and quickly toast the kashmiri chilli and set it aside.
6. On the same pan In this order toast the seguent ingredients:
1 tsp fenugreek
2 1\2 spoon of mustard seeds
2 1\2 spoon of cumin seeds
8 small cloves
1 cinnamon stick
4 cardamom crush open (just the seeds)
6 garlic cloves peeled and roughly chopped
15 curry leaves
1 big red onion dice
1 tsp turmeric
4 tsp ground coriander
1 coconut shaved
7. Toast all together properly and on the end and 1 big tomato dice and keep toasting, after set aside and let it cool down
8. Once the mixture it’ chilled blend together with kashmiri chili and enough water to make a thick smooth paste
9. Heat a pan up and add 4 ladle of sunflower oil and start toasting the paste for 20 minute by stirring frequently
10. Put half of the remaining coconut in the blender together with 4 cups of water and blend it on max speed since you obtain the coconut milk
11. After strain the milk with a kitchen cloth
12. Add coconut milk to the toasting gravy and add 50 gr of coconut milk powder mixed with 1 cup of water
13. Add salt to taste (roughly 2 1\2 tbsp) and let mixture boil for 20 minutes with lid
14. Drain the tamarind and add 3 tbsp of the tamarind water to the gravy together with 1 tsp of sugar
15. When the oil comes to the surface the gravy its ready

Other details:
servings: 5 people

Classifications:
- Dish Type: Component / Base (Stocks, Sauces, Pastes, Garnishes, Doughs)
- Cuisine: Indian
- Difficulty: Level 2 (Intermediate / Professional)
- Main Ingredient: Vegetable-Forward
- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Dairy-Free, Nut-Free, Vegan, Vegetarian, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Family Style / Sharing
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

name: Parmesan Pannacotta
text:
Recipe ID: parmesan_pannacotta_merged
Name: Parmesan Pannacotta

Ingredients:
- 250gr Grated Gruyere
- 500ml Milk
- 11t Whipping Cream
- Salt and Pepper
- 6 Sheets Gelatine
- 3 cloves garlic
- 4 sprigs thyme, stalks removed

Steps:
1. Warm all ingredients except gelatine on No1 for 30 minutes on induction until warm and the cheese has melted into the milk and cream. Season with a little black pepper and sea salt and then pass through a fine chinoise onto ice using tall containers and chill in fridge for 2-3 hours. Skim all fat from top of liquid and pass again. Press but don't force through the solids. Weigh out 1.25kg of mixture.
2. Warm a small amount of the mix and dissolve the gelatine, then mix all together and pass again. Chill over ice until fully set and beat on paddle in kitchen aid place in piping bag and excess in sous vide bag at least 1 hour before service begins.

Other details:

- Seasonality: Year-Round

Ingredients:

- 60g Sugar
- 30g Salt
- 40 g Piece of ginger

name: Tofu Filling
text:
Recipe ID: tofu_filling_merged
Name: Tofu Filling

Ingredients:
- Pressed Tofu (step1)
- Chopped Mustard Green (step2)
- Wilted Spinach (step3)
- Superior Soy
- Aged Soy
- Sesame Oil
- White Pepper
- Maldon Salt

Steps:
1. Press Tofu ( Clean Bean ) for two hours you should have a loss of 20% of the Weight
2. Chop and Fry the Mustard greens for a bit, just to lose some of the water content, you should have a 30% loss of the weight at the end
3. Wash the Spinach, dry it and wilt it in a pan so that its cooked and the water of spinach has sort of evaporated
4. Squeeze all the water out of the spinach with a J-cloth
5. Chop the pressed tofu, Chop the Spinach, mix them together and add the seasonings
6. Add The chopped preserved mustard to the mix, taste it add salt if needed.

Other details:

Warm the 75g tapioca flour and water, till you have a thick paste
Add this to the rice mix
Blend in the robo coup
Spread 250g per tray

name: Club Sandwich
text:
Recipe ID: sandwich_club_es_parsed_3
Name: Club Sandwich

Ingredients:
- 10 liters of HEINZ mayonnaise
- 5 jars of Chili Garlic
- 3kg of ricotta cheese
- 1kg of mascarpone cheese
- 1 Chinese roll (buy from a supplier)
- 2 sprigs of spearmint
- 2 sprigs of basil
- 2 sprigs of cilantro
- julienned spring onion
- 2 quail eggs
- Maldon salt

Steps:
1. a) Put the jars of chili garlic in the Thermomix and grind for 4 minutes until you get a fine paste.
2. b) Mix the chili paste with the mayonnaise until the mixture is homogeneous.
3. a) Mix the cheeses until a homogeneous mixture is obtained.
4. b) Reserve in a container.
5. c) Never use this mixture freshly removed from the cold.

name: Yamaimo Ice
text:
Recipe ID: yamaimo_ice_merged
Name: Yamaimo Ice

Ingredients:
- INGREDIENTS:
(For 4 people.)
- The Yamaimo:
- 100 gr of yamaimo.
- Nitrogen
- For the Clove Konjac Water:
- 6 gr of clove
- 114 mL of water
- 1.2 gr of Konjac.

Steps:
1. ELABORATION:
2. The yamaimo:
3. Peel the yamaimo, crush in a Thermomix and strain over a fine-mesh sieve with lito and let stand for 8 hours with film in contact with the surface.
4. For the Clove Konjac Water:
5. Infuse the water and clove. Add the Konjac and stir with a rod until thickened. Reserve in cold.
6. FINISHING AND PRESENTATION
7. Form a 18 gr quenelle and immerse in liquid nitrogen.

name: Wantou Pasta
text:
Recipe ID: pasta_wantou_en_parsed
Name: Wantou Pasta

Ingredients:
- Wantou pasta
- Water
- Oil
- Salt
- Ice

Steps:
1. a) Put a pot with water, salt, and oil to boil.
2. b) Take the pasta out of the freezer 3 minutes before using it; it is very important to cook all of it, it is not acceptable to defrost it from one day to the next.
3. c) Introduce the sheets for 10 seconds and transfer them to the bowl with ice to stop the cooking process.
4. d) Arrange them in a gastronorm container with parchment paper and oil for later use.
5. e) There are 4 sheets per plate.

Other details:

name: Px Cream
text:
Recipe ID: px_cream_merged
Name: Px Cream

Ingredients:
- 1400g double cream
- 2 vanilla pods
- 8 leaves gelatine
- 8 yolks
- 6 whole eggs
- 200g caster sugar
- 100g PX
- 250g extra PX

Steps:
1. Bloom gelatine on cold water
2. Whisk the egg yolks, whole eggs, 50g PX and caster sugar together
3. Warm the cream with the vanilla
4. Add the warm cream to the egg mix
5. Return to the heat
6. Warm till you reach 75 celsuis
7. Take off the heat and mix in the gelatine till all is dissolved
8. Pass through a chinois
9. Chill in a ice bath
10. Beat in the extra 250g PX

Other details:


Classifications:
- Dish Type: Dessert
- Cuisine: Other
- Difficulty: Level 2 (Intermediate / Professional)
- Main Ingredient: Dairy / Egg
- Dietary: Alcohol-Free, Nut-Free, Gluten-Free
- Occasion: Fine Dining / Plated
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

raw.name: Px Cream

Whisk the egg yolks, whole eggs, 50g PX and caster sugar together
Warm the cream with the vanilla
Add the warm cream to the egg mix
Return to the heat
Warm till you reach 75 celsuis
Take off the heat and mix in the gelatine till all is dissolved
Pass through a chinois
Chill in a ice bath
Beat in the extra 250g PX

name: Thai Seafood Broth
text:
Recipe ID: souquet_thai_es_parsed
Name: Thai Seafood Broth

Ingredients:
- 12 shallots
- 100g garlic + 400g garlic
- 100g ginger
- 8 tomatoes
- 1l white wine
- 1kg small prawns
- 1kg small grey shrimp
- 1lt coconut milk
- saffron
- water
- 300g almond
- 200g tom yum

Steps:
1. a) Confit 400g of peeled garlic until golden brown in olive oil. Reserve.
2. b) Fry almonds until golden brown. Reserve.
3. c) Peel 100g of garlic, the ginger, and the shallots. Cut into julienne and sauté in a pot. Clean the tomatoes, cut into julienne, and once the sautéed vegetables are golden, add the tomato.
4. d) Brown the prawns and small grey shrimp in the oven. When the sauté is ready, add the prawns and small grey shrimp and continue cooking.
5. e) When ready, add the tom yum and a few strands of saffron. Continue cooking, and once homogenous, pour in the wine and reduce.
6. f) Then add the water and coconut milk, simmer gently covered for 20 minutes and uncovered for 25 minutes.
7. g) Strain the broth, and in a Thermomix, blend the broth with the confit garlic, fried almonds, and a couple of strands of saffron until the desired texture is achieved.

Other details:
original_language: es
translated_from: es

Classifications:
- Dish Type: Component / Base (Stocks, Sauces, Pastes, Garnishes, Doughs)

- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Dairy-Free, Nut-Free, Alcohol-Free, Pescatarian
- Occasion: Fine Dining / Plated
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

name: Masterstock Poached Goose, Chinese Olive Green, Burned Spring Onion
text:
Recipe ID: masterstock_poached_goose,_chinese_olive_green,_burned_spring_onion_merged
Name: Masterstock Poached Goose, Chinese Olive Green, Burned Spring Onion

Ingredients:
- On Plate :
- Goose Breast 50-50g
- Goose Legs 30-40g
- Masterstock sauce 50 ml
- Chinese Olive Green
- Spring Onion Goose fat
- Burned Spring Onion
- Fermented Red Chillies
- Aged Soy Sauce
- Finely Chopped Spring Onion
- Goose Fat
- Goose carcasse juice
- Aged Soy Sauce
- Goose Breasts
- Very thinly julienned Ginger
- Goose Fat or emulsions
- Goose Fat
- Sliced shallot

Steps:
1. Remove the seeds and chop the fermented chillies very fine
2. DO NOT pre mix this sauce Aged soy is precious.
3. Thinly sliced the goose breasts to 1-2mm slices, about 9-11 slices
4. Emulsify it by whisky or handheld blender or squeezy bottle
5. Thinly slice ginger by hand
6. Super fine julienne to order
7. Thinly sliced the goose breasts to 1-2mm slices, about 9-11 slices
8. Serve with finely julienned ginger on one side
9. Deep fry the shallot in goose fat until golden
10. Crispy shallot used for lard rice

Other details:
Notes: This dish is composed of 5 slices of Goose Breasts that have been poached and two chopped pieces of Legs that have been poached and Confit In a masterstock. A masterstock sauce that has quince vinegar in it, on top we will put Spring Onion thats been fried in Goose Fat, A homemade Chinese Olive Greens made out of Preserved Mustard greens and Black Olives.

name: Cured Pork Belly
text:
Recipe ID: saam_de_panceta_es_parsed
Name: Cured Pork Belly

Ingredients:
- 3 kg of salt
- 1.5 kg of sugar
- 750 gr of sweet La Vera paprika

Steps:
1. a) Cut the pork bellies to the size of the large vacuum bags.
2. b) Ensure that the pork bellies are thick.
3. c) For the marinade, mix the salt + sugar + paprika homogeneously.
4. d) Once we have the marinade, introduce the pork bellies.
5. e) It is very important that all of the marinade is in contact with the pork belly.
6. f) Marinate for 2 hours, then clean well under water and vacuum seal.
7. g) Cook at 80 degrees Celsius with steam for 12 hours.

Other details:

name: Civet. Civet Sauce
text:
Recipe ID: civet_salsa_de_civet_es_parsed
Name: Civet. Civet Sauce

Ingredients:
- 40 red onions
- 20 carrots
- 1kg garlic
- 1kg ginger
- 18 packages of extra smoked Bacon (Óscar Mayer)
- 2 liters white wine
- 480g curry paste
- 4 palm sugar tablets
- 12 liters coconut milk
- 4 liters chicken broth
- 50ml Bacon essence (soda)

Steps:
1. a) Cut all the vegetables into thin julienne strips.
2. b) Brown all the vegetables in a large pot.
3. c) Add the curry paste and fry it.
4. d) Deglaze with the wine and let it evaporate.
5. e) Add the chicken broth and coconut milk along with the palm sugar and a little salt.
6. f) Bring to a high heat to boil, once boiling let it cook for 5 hours over medium heat.
7. g) To finish the sauce, blend it together with the vegetables in a Thermomix and then strain it through a fine-mesh sieve.
8. h) Cool.

Other details:

name: Shortcrust Pastry
text:
Recipe ID: pasta_frolla_merged
Name: Shortcrust Pastry

Ingredients:
- Butter: 250 g
- Flour 00: 500 g
- Eggs (large): 4 yolks
- Powdered Sugar: 200 g
- Lemon: 1 (grated zest)
- Salt: 1 pinch

Steps:
1. Place the flour, a pinch of salt, and the butter (freshly removed from the refrigerator) in a mixer and blend until you obtain a sandy and floury mixture, to which you will add the powdered sugar. At this point, form the classic well shape on a work surface, and pour the lemon zest and egg yolks into the center. Quickly mix everything together; the resulting dough will be compact and elastic.
2. Form a ball, wrap it with plastic wrap, and let it rest in the refrigerator for at least half an hour, after which the shortcrust pastry will be ready to be rolled out and used to prepare cookies and tarts.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Preparazione: 60'
Dosi: 6 persone

name: Crêpe
text:
Recipe ID: crêpe_merged
Name: Crêpe

Ingredients:
- FOR SAVORY CRÊPES
- Butter: 40 g
- Flour: 250 g
- Milk: 500 ml
- Eggs: 3
- Salt: 1 pinch
- FOR SWEET CRÊPES
- Butter: 40 g
- Flour: 250 g
- Milk: 500 ml
- Eggs: 3
- Sugar: 60 g
- Vanilla extract: 1 packet
- Salt: 1 pinch

Steps:
1. Place the sifted flour in a high-sided bowl (along with sugar and vanilla extract if you are preparing sweet crêpes), the pinch of salt and the half liter of milk. Work the mixture until it is smooth and without lumps. Separately, beat the eggs, then add them to the batter and continue to mix. Once the melted butter has been added, the dough will be ready to rest in the refrigerator, covered, for half an hour.
2. At that point, heat a non-stick pan over the heat. When it is boiling hot, pour a ladleful of batter into the center. By tilting and quickly rotating the pan you will be able to cover the entire available surface. Alternatively, you can spread the batter with a spoon or with the appropriate tool.
3. Let it cook for a minute, shaking the pan from time to time to prevent the crêpe from sticking to the bottom; as soon as it is golden, turn it over and wait for it to take on the same color. As soon as it is ready, slide it onto a plate where you will also pile up the subsequent crêpes.

Other details:
Difficoltà: *
Cottura: 2'
Preparazione: 10'
per: 10 persone

text:

Ingredients:
- Foie gras should be A grade, cut into 2cm cubes
- 1 bottle of sauternes wine reduced by haif
- Place 500g diced foie gras into medium size sous vide bag with 80g reduced wine, sous vide and leave in freezer until required.
- 500g marinaded foie gras
- 8g maldon salt
- x5 leaves bronze gelatine bloomed in cold water
- 180g boiling water

Steps:
1. To marinade foie gras;
2. For Parfait
3. Method
4. defrost foie gras and bring to room temp, foie should be soft to touch (around 30 degrees)
5. place softened foie into vita prep with salt and hot water
6. blend on high, using digital thermometer check temp and bring to 59'c
7. add softened gelatin and blend quickly again
8. pass through chinois and chill over ice
9. whisk until smooth for service

name: Rice Dish
text:
Recipe ID: rice_dish_parsed
Name: Rice Dish

Ingredients:
- 2 ladle of oil
- ½ tsp mustard seeds
- ½ tsp cumin seeds
- 2 red onion thin sliced
- 3 chilli cut in half with clean top
- 3 medium boiled and peeled potato dice
- 1 carrot dice
- 70 gr of peas (both cooked in water with baking soda)
- salt to taste
- 1 lemon
- 1 tsp of turmeric
- 250 gr basmati cooked rice
- 1 cup of water
- fresh chop coriander

Steps:
1. Add 2 ladle of oil in a hot pan and toast in this order: ½ tsp mustard seeds, ½ tsp cumin seeds,2 red onion thin sliced, 3 chilli cut in half with clean top and toast well
2. Add 3 medium boiled and peeled potato dice, 1 carrot dice and 70 gr of peas ( both cooked in water with baking soda) and add salt to taste
3. Squeeze 1 lemon in it, add 1 tsp of turmeric and toast everything properly together
4. Add 250 gr basmati cooked rice with 1 cup of water , mix well and cooking for 3 more minutes
5. Serve with fresh chop coriander

Other details:


Classifications:
- Dish Type: Side Dish
- Cuisine: Indian
- Difficulty: Level 1 (Easy / Rustic)
- Main Ingredient: Starch/Grain (Pasta, Rice, Bread)
- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Vegan, Vegetarian, Nut-Free, Dairy-Free, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Casual / Comfort
- Prep Style: Quick / A la Minute
- Seasonality: Year-Round

name: Curry Paste
text:
Recipe ID: pasta_de_curry_es_parsed
Name: Curry Paste

Ingredients:
- 20 stalks of lemon grass
- 1 head of garlic
- 4 shallots
- 2 bulbs of Ginger
- 4 galangal
- 20g of keffir lime
- 12 units of Basque chili peppers
- 24g of cilantro
- 10g of clove
- 24g white peppercorns
- 24g of cumin
- 12g of nutmeg
- 24g of dry mustard seeds
- 200g of coconut fat

Steps:
1. a) Toast the dry spices separately.
2. b) Hydrate the Basque chili peppers without seeds.
3. c) Chop all the vegetables.
4. d) In the Thermomix, add the dry ingredients and make a powder.
5. e) Remove the spice powder from the Thermomix and add the vegetables.
6. f) Grind at maximum speed until a smooth mixture is obtained.

text:

Name: Brown Butter Emulsion

Ingredients:

- 700 g brown butter
- 350 g / 400 g maltosec

2. b) Melt the butter in a saucepan and bring to a hazelnut brown (beurre noisette) stage
3. c) Combine the powdered milk and 350 g of maltosec in a Thermomix and grind well

name: Pâte Brisée
text:
Recipe ID: pâte_brisée_merged
Name: Pâte Brisée

Ingredients:
- Iced water: 70 ml
- Cold butter: 100 g
- Flour: 200 g
- Salt: 1 pinch
- Acqua ghiacciata: 70 ml
- Burro freddo: 100 g
- Farina: 200 g
- Sale: 1 pizzico

Steps:
1. Put the flour, cold butter cut into pieces straight from the refrigerator, and a pinch of salt in a blender. Blend everything until you get a sandy mixture, that is, with a floury appearance. At this point, arrange it on a surface preferably made of metal, glass, or marble (the colder it is, the better), in the classic fountain shape, and quickly knead everything, adding cold water to the fountain until you obtain a compact, firm, and fairly elastic dough. The pâte brisée will be ready.
2. Wrap it in a sheet of plastic wrap and let it rest in the refrigerator for at least 40 minutes before using it. If you prepare it in advance, know that the pâte brisée can be kept in the refrigerator for one or two days. In this case, remember to take it out at least 1 hour before rolling it out because it needs time to soften.
3. Mettete nel frullatore la farina, il burro a pezzi freddo di frigo e un pizzico di sale. Frullate il tutto fino a ottenere un composto sabbiato, cioè dall'aspetto farinoso. A questo punto, disponetelo su una superficie possibilmente di metallo, vetro o marmo (più fredda è, meglio è), nella classica forma a fontana e impastate il tutto velocemente aggiungendo acqua fredda a fontana ottenuta un impasto compatto sodo e abbastanza elastico la pasta brisée sarà pronta.
4. Avvolgetela in un foglio di pellicola da cucina e lasciatela riposare in frigo per almeno 40 minuti prima di utilizzarla. Se la preparate in anticipo, sappiate che la pasta brisée si conserva in frigorifero per uno o due giorni. In questo caso ricordate di estrarla almeno 1 ora prima di stenderla perché deve avere il tempo di ammorbidirsi.

Other details:
Difficoltà: *
Preparazione: 60'
Dosi: 250 g circa di pasta

name: Kale And Radicchio Salad
text:
Recipe ID: kale_and_radicchio_salad_merged
Name: Kale And Radicchio Salad

Ingredients:
- For the Purple kale straight
- 1 head purple kale
- 200g mushroom stock
- For the Purple kale
- 100g Kale
- For the Onion
- 50g Red onion
- 2g Black pepper
- 5g Elderflower vinegar
- 30g sugar
- 30g Water
- For the Pink radicchio
- 1 Pink radicchio
- 200g Pickle onion juice
- For rosella pink radicchio
- 1 Pink radicchio rosella

Steps:
1. 1. Strip all the leaves of the purple kale.
2. 2. Bring the mushroom stock to the boil the pour over the leaves so the leaves are covered with all stock.
3. 3. Grill slightly on the yakitori.
4. 4. Cool slightly on the yakitori.
5. 5. Place in the dehydrator overnight.
6. 6. Once dry and remove and store.
7. 1. Remove large stalk from the kale and wash and dry.
8. 2. Fry for 5 second until crispy then dry on absorbent paper.
9. 3.
10. 1. Thinly slice the onion.
11. 2. Mix the rest of the ingredients together and bring to the boil.
12. 3. Pour the boil liquid over the onion and allow to cool.
13. 4. Once cooked strain off the liquid and reserve in the fridge.
14. 5. Spread the onion over a silpat and try in the dehydrator for two days the blend into a powder.
15. 1. Place the two ingredients in a vacuum bag and seal at 100%.
16. 2. Store in the fridge.
17. 1. Trim the base, wash the leaves and dry and store in fridge
