name: Duck Neck Broth
text:
Recipe ID: caldo_de_cuellos_de_pato_merged
Name: Duck Neck Broth

Ingredients:
- 10 kg Duck Necks
- 8 kg Onion, julienned
- 15 l Water

Steps:
1. PREVIOUS NOTE
2. The duck fat that melts when toasting the necks adds a lot of flavor to the broth. It should be used when making the broth.
3. Mise en Place
4. Chop the duck necks to facilitate browning.
5. Spread the duck pieces on trays lined with parchment paper.
6. Roast in the oven at 220°C (428°F) until the duck is very golden brown. Approximately 40 min.
7. Sauté the onion in enough oil until completely, uniformly, and intensely caramelized.
8. Development
9. Place the onion and duck in a pressure cooker and cover with water.
10. Close the pot and bring to a boil over a fire.
11. When the pot releases pressure, reduce the heat to low and maintain a gentle simmer for 2 hours.
12. Cool the pot and strain the broth using a chinois. Press the meat well to obtain as much broth as possible.
13. Reduction
14. Place the broth in a container and cover with plastic wrap. Chill in the blast chiller until the fat solidifies on the surface.
15. Remove the fat completely and place the broth in a tall pot to reduce it.
16. Reduce the broth until a concentration of 30° is achieved in a refractometer.
17. Pass through a micro strainer, adjust the salt and reserve in 200 g vacuum bags.

Other details:
Para: 1,5 l.

Development

Close the pot and bring to a boil over a fire.
When the pot releases pressure, reduce the heat to low and maintain a gentle simmer for 2 hours.

Reduction
Place the broth in a container and cover with plastic wrap. Chill in the blast chiller until the fat solidifies on the surface.

name: Raspberry, Herb Sorbet, Yoghurt Meringue
text:
Recipe ID: raspberry,_herb_sorbet,_yoghurt_meringue_merged
Name: Raspberry, Herb Sorbet, Yoghurt Meringue

Ingredients:
- 1000g apple juiceos
- 14g pectin
- 205g fructose
- 40g chervil
- 115g basil
- 10g lovage
- 30g mint
- 100g egg white
- 200g sugar
- 70g water
- 30g yopol- or 20g M.S.K. yoghurt powder
- 80g ewes milk yoghurt

Steps:
1. Mix the fructose and pectin together
2. Bring the apple juice to the boil and whisk in the pectin mix
3. Chill down
4. Blanch the chervil and basil together
5. Blanch the lovage and mint together
6. Add to the apple juice, blend and pass through chinois
7. Bring the sugar and water to 121 celsuis then pour onto semi whipped egg white
8. Add to the meringue and spread out to dry, 180g per tray
9. Spread and dry for 24 hours

name: Lemon And Basil Gelato
text:
Recipe ID: lemon_and_basil_gelato_merged
Name: Lemon And Basil Gelato

Ingredients:
- 300g lemon juice
- 650g sugar
- 750g water
- 20g lemon zest
- 50g green or red basil
- 5 sheets of gelatin

Steps:
1. FOR THE SYRUP: bring water, sugar and lemon zest to a boil, at the first boil remove from the heat and add the basil. Let it rest for 20 min. in infusion.
2. IN THE BLENDER: blend apple and lemon juice for 5 min, add the filtered syrup and the gelatin. Strain everything through a chinois (fine-mesh sieve).
3. Put in pacojet containers, freeze and pacotize.

name: Creamy Polished Rice With Mushrooms
text:
Recipe ID: arroz_pulido_cremoso_seimaibuai_con_setas_merged
Name: Creamy Polished Rice With Mushrooms

Ingredients:
- INGREDIENTS:
(for 4 people)
- The enoki mushroom:
- 400 g. enoki mushroom
- The risotto:
- 12 g. olive oil
- 32 g. raw risotto rice
- 60 g. enoki stems
- 400 ml. chicken broth
(broth batch)
- q.s. Mu-garitz seasoning

Steps:
1. ELABORATION:
2. The enoki mushroom:
3. Cut the enoki heads and reserve 10 g. per portion.
4. Cut the enoki stems to the size of the polished rice and reserve 15 g. per portion.
5. The risotto:
6. (Note: From the moment the rice is wet, cook for 8 minutes.)
7. Mix the chicken broth with the Mugaritz seasoning and adjust to taste.
8. In a saucepan, add a little oil, add the polished rice and sauté well. Gradually moisten the rice with the chicken broth. Spread the rice on the base of the saucepan so that it cooks for 8.5 minutes.
After this time, remove the rice from the heat and spread it on a frozen tray. Let cool and portion 8 g. of cooked rice per person.
9. FINISHING AND PRESENTATION
10. Heat the rice until it boils, moisten with chicken broth and adjust the taste of the rice (15 g. per portion) and keep the rice with the cod broth.
11. Place the enoki heads in a strainer with olive oil and sauté lightly over an incandescent grill.
12. Arrange the rice with the enoki stems on one side of the plate, serve the smoked heads (10 g. per portion) on the other side. Serve hot.

name: Hawthorne Jelly
text:
Recipe ID: hawthorne_jelly_merged
Name: Hawthorne Jelly

Ingredients:
- 250g Dried hawthorn berries
- 500g Water
- Part A
- 250g Reserved hawthorn stock (above)
- 165g Caster sugar
- 50g Liquid glucose
- Part B
- 13g Pectin Jaune
- 15g Caster sugar
- Part C
- 3.5g Citric acid
- 30g Water
- Part D
- 3g Citric acid
- 60g Granulated sugar

Steps:
1. Boil the hawthorne berries and water together for 45 minutes, in a covered pot. Strain and reserve
2. Mix the remaining parts A - D in seperate bowls.
3. Bring part A to a simmer. Whisk in part B then bring to the boil.
4. When the mixture reaches 104°C, add part C and whisk vigorously. Remove from the heat and pour immediately onto a tray.
5. Leave to set at room temperature, then place in the fridge to firm up.
6. Cut the pate de fruit to the desired size. Store in a plastic container lined with greaseproof paper
7. For service, then roll in a bowl with Part D

Other details:
Total Time: None
Yield: 40 portions
Total Batch Weight: None

name: Terracotta Glaze
text:
Recipe ID: terracotta_glaze_parsed
Name: Terracotta Glaze

Ingredients:
- 400 g milk crumbs
- 90 g rose oil
- 50 g butter
- aronia berry reduction

Steps:
1. Blend all ingredients in the thermomix until homogenized.
2. Once blended, remove the contents of the jug and pass the paste through a chinois with ladle.
3. After its passed, add the aronia berry reduction at 6% by weight to the glaze.
4. Reserve this glaze in an airtight container in the fridge until needed. (When glazing a cake with this, it should be reheated to 60°C).

name: French Meringue
text:
Recipe ID: french_meringue_merged
Name: French Meringue

Ingredients:
- Eggs: 100 g egg whites
- Sugar: 220 g
- Salt: 1 pinch
- Lemon: a few drops of juice

Steps:
1. Preheat the oven to 80-100°C (176-212°F). Separate all the egg whites and place them in a container with high sides, along with a pinch of salt and a third of the sugar. If you want to make the mixture shiny and reduce the strong smell of the egg, add a few drops of filtered lemon juice. With an electric mixer, beat the egg whites until stiff peaks form, doubling their volume, then incorporate another third of the sugar. Then, with a spatula, add the last third, mixing gently from the bottom up, as if incorporating air.
2. Put the mixture in a piping bag (pastry bag) fitted with a wide nozzle (smooth or serrated) and, on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, form meringues with a diameter of 4-5 centimeters (1.5-2 inches).
3. The meringues should dry more than cook, so leave them in the oven at a low temperature for at least 2 hours (at temperatures below 80°C (176°F), you could extend the cooking time to 4-6 hours). They must not take on color, so after the first hour, take a look: if they are turning yellow, lower the temperature further and slightly open the door, thus preventing the formation of humidity, the meringues' worst enemy.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Cottura: 120'
Preparazione: 15'
Dosi: 30 circa

name: Rose Terracotta
text:
Recipe ID: rose_terracotta_parsed
Name: Rose Terracotta

Ingredients:
- silicon mold (shaped like a flowerpot)
- mousse
- sponge
- strawberry paste
- terracotta glaze
- 2 x 5 cm long branch of oxalis
- 2 x 5 cm branches of oregano
- 2 x 3.5 cm stalks of verbena
- malt soil

Steps:
1. Place the mousse at the bottom of the mold, then add the sponge and repeat until you have 2 layers of sponge suspended in 3 layers of mousse adding to the last layer enough strawberry paste to cover the rim of the sponge.
2. Set the cake in the blast chiller.
3. Unmold the frozen mousse and glaze it upside down (with the small end in the air) on a skinny yogurt pot with the terracotta glaze in 1 coating.
4. Return the cake to the freezer as soon as its glazed so it doesn’t melt.
5. Once set, trim down any excess glaze from the rim, flip it over and allow it to defrost in the fridge in a covered container.
6. Sprinkle the malt soil onto the top of the cake carefully, stick the herbs in so that they’re standing and look realistic.
7. Serve.

Other details:

text:
Recipe ID: mackerel_sandwich_merged
Name: Mackerel Sandwich

Ingredients:
- Mackerel 1 weighing 0.600kg
- Transglutaminase activia pb (ajinomoto) 0.005 kg
- Bread 15 x 15 2 slices

Steps:
1. Fillet the mackerel, remove bones, sprinkle Transglutaminase activia pb on the flesh side with a sieve, overlap the two halves and portion. Cut the bread with a slicer to size 1, place the mackerel on top and trim without smudging. Let rest in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours.

name: Horseradish Juice
text:
Recipe ID: horseradish_juice_merged
Name: Horseradish Juice

Ingredients:
- 100g Horseradish juice
- Stage 1
- 1200g buttermilk
- 300g milk
- 35g lemon juice
- Stage 2
- 275 milk
- 275 cream
- 36g cornflour
- 15g salt

Steps:
1. Method
2. Add horseradish juice to stage 1 and chill over ice bath
3. For stage 2
4. Mix cornflour and milk in bowl
5. Bring cream to the boil
6. Pour milk and cornflour into cream and cook out for 5 minutes (this will become very thick)
7. Now mix both together ove the ice bath and hand blend in the salt
8. Check with senior chef
9. Store in medium bags @ 500g each
10. Freeze Flat
11. Blitz Fresh everyday

name: Chocolate Cake With Molten Heart
text:
Recipe ID: tortino_di_cioccolato
con_cuore_fondente_merged
Name: Chocolate Cake With Molten Heart

Ingredients:
- Butter: 80 g
- Cocoa powder: 10 g
- Dark chocolate: 150 g
- Flour: 20 g
- Salt: 1 pinch
- Eggs: 2 + 1 yolk
- Vanillin: 1 packet
- Powdered sugar: 90 g
- FOR COATING THE MOLDS
- Butter q.s.
- Cocoa powder: 1 level spoon
- FOR DUSTING
- Powdered sugar: q.s.

Steps:
1. Melt the chopped dark chocolate in a bain-marie, then add the butter cut into cubes. Meanwhile, in a large bowl, beat the eggs with the vanillin and sugar until you have obtained a light, fluffy and thick mixture (so it will take about 10 minutes to incorporate them). Then incorporate the chocolate and finally, add the flour and cocoa, sifted with a pinch of salt.
2. When the ingredients are well blended, butter and coat the aluminum molds with cocoa powder, fill them for ¾ and bake them at 180°. After 12-15 minutes (not one more) remove the cakes from the oven, let them cool slightly and turn them upside down on a serving dish, sprinkle them with powdered sugar and serve them still hot.

Other details:
Difficoltà: *
Cottura: 12-15'
Preparazione: 15'
Dosi: 4 tortini

name: Spring Xian Bing
text:
Recipe ID: spring_xian_bing_merged
Name: Spring Xian Bing

Ingredients:
- Glass Noodle 200 g
- Glass noodle 20 g
- Maldon salt
- Cooked Glass Noodles 40 g
- Roasted fennel 50 g
- Dill 30 g
- Garlic 20 g
- Spring onion 30 g
- Green sichuan oil 0.5 tsp
- Aged White Soy 10 g
- Kow Choi Oil 0.5 tsp
- White Pepper 0.25 tsp
- 500 g Pork mince 30% Fat
- 4 tbsp Shaoxing wine
- 2 tbsp Soy Sauce
- 1/2 tsp Sesame Oil
- 1 tsp Salt
- 3 tsp Sugar
- 10 g Diced Ginger
- 10 g Diced Red Chilli
- 120 g Spring onion white Chopped
- Salted Plum Juice 135 g
- Mirin 450 g
- 500 g Rose Flour
- 0.5 tsp Salt
- 1 tsp Sugar
- 1/16 tsp Yeast
- 300 g Water

Steps:
1. Simmer glass noodles for 5min until cooked but still firm
2. Strain the dashi and keep the liquid
3. Rinse the glass noodles under cold running water to stop it from cooking further
4. Cut the fennel to cube size, oil it gently
5. Roast the cubed fennel in josper for about 5min, keep an eye on it
6. Use a scissors to cut down thecooked glass noodles to 2cm lengths
7. Chop the spring onion to very fine rings
8. Slice the wild garlic really thin, and about 3cm long
9. Chop the dills to 2cm wide, do not include the end stems
10. In a mixing bowl, put in the weighted glass noodles
11. Season the glass noodles with Aged white soy, white pepper, kow choi oil and green sichuna oil
12. Taste, add a pinch of salt if it needs it
13. Pull and pan fry like xian bing and serve with salted plum juice mirin
14. Make fresh batch everyday. DO NOT use any leftovers overnight, it's a spring xian bing, it needs to be fresh!!!!
15. Always make small batch only.
16. glass noodles should always be 30% of the total herbs weight
17. Mix everything together
18. Mix for every service. Don't mix too much as it gets too salty
19. Mix well
20. Serve in ramekin to go with the spring xian bing
21. Measure the water to 45C
22. Put everything together in the kitchen aid to knead
23. Knead 10mins
24. Mix for every service. DO NOT use overnight dough

Other details:
SERVE WITH: UMEBOSHI LIQUID MIX
AND MIRIN MIX
RECIPE ->

- Seasonality: Spring

name: The Apples
text:
Recipe ID: las_manzanas:_merged
Name: The Apples

Ingredients:
- INGREDIENTS: (For 4 people.)
- The Apples:
- 4 units apples
- 1 liter water
- 20 g. Cal MEX
- 4 kg lactose (65%)
- 6 liters water

Steps:
1. ELABORATION:
2. The Apples:
3. Peel the apples, lightly turning them if necessary.
4. Prepare a lime bath, dissolving the Cal MEX in the water. Submerge the apples in the lime bath for 3 hours. After this time, remove them from the bath and wash them under running water.
5. Prepare a syrup with the water and lactose.
6. Cook the apples in the syrup over low heat, preventing them from browning, until they are tender inside, around 30-40 minutes. After this time, remove them from the heat and let the apples cool in the syrup. If they are excessively cooked, they will not hold their shape.
7. Make a hole in the base of the apple and remove the pulp, leaving only the skin formed by the lime. Reserve.

Other details:

name: Mint Kombucha
text:
Recipe ID: kombucha_de_menta_merged
Name: Mint Kombucha

Ingredients:
- Mint Kombucha: (For 2 l)
- 2.5 l water
- 2 bunches of mint
- 100 g white sugar
- Piece of kombucha scoby (mother)
- 1 tablespoon of previously fermented liquid kombucha
- Mint Kombucha: (For 2 l)
- 2.5 l water
- 2 bunches of mint
- 100 g white sugar
- Piece of kombucha scoby (mother)
- 1 tablespoon of previously fermented liquid kombucha

Steps:
1. Mint Kombucha:
2. Boil the water and add the mint leaves. Remove from heat, cover with film, and let steep for 1 hour.
3. After this time, strain the infusion and cool.
4. When the infusion is cold, add the sugar, the kombucha scoby, and a tablespoon of previously fermented liquid kombucha. For example, let it ferment for 4 days at room temperature. After this time, taste the fermented liquid and if it reaches its point of fermentation, bottle and leave in the refrigerator for a week before using.

name: Pasta Sfoglia
text:
Recipe ID: pasta_sfoglia_merged
Name: Pasta Sfoglia

Ingredients:
- FOR THE DOUGH
- Manitoba Flour: 150 g
- 00 Flour: 200 g
- Water: about 250 ml
- Salt: 8 g
- FOR THE BUTTER BLOCK
- Butter: 250 g
- 00 Flour: 75 g

Steps:
1. Start with the preparation of the dough. Mix and sift the two flours, preferably in a stand mixer with a leaf hook, or by hand. Dissolve the salt in the water and knead everything at medium speed for about 8 minutes, you must obtain a softer and more elastic mixture than that of bread and, of course, smooth and homogeneous. Let it rest, covered with a cloth, in a cool place for at least half an hour.
2. In the meantime, take care of the butter block. Remove the butter from the fridge, cut it into cubes and put it in the stand mixer with 75 g of flour: mix until you obtain a blended and lump-free mixture, which you will reduce into a square with a thickness of about 1 centimeter, either with floured hands or by placing it between two sheets of parchment paper and flattening it with the help of a rolling pin. Place the butter block in the lower part of the fridge – the coldest one – for at least half an hour.
3. Roll out the dough: you will obtain a rectangle of about 50 centimeters in length, in the center of which you will place the butter block, now ready (1), which you will cover with the two longest edges of the dough, making them match (2). Fold the other two as well, until you close the butter block on each side (3). Always remember to flour the work surface and the dough to make the rolling easier.
4. Take a rolling pin and apply pressure on the dough, so as to make the butter block thinner (4). You can then start rolling out the pastry, which must take on a rectangular, regular shape. To do this, do not limit yourself to rolling out the pastry by moving the rolling pin back and forth (because you risk obtaining a pastry in the shape of an hourglass), but you must impress oblique movements, holding the rolling pin along the diagonal of your ideal rectangle. Start by widening the center as well. When you get a width of about 25 centimeters it will be time to proceed with the folds.
5. Fold the two shorter sides so that they match in the center of the longer side (5), then fold the pastry like a book, mark it with a finger pressure (which will remind you that you have just made the first fold 6), wrap it in plastic wrap and put it in the fridge for at least half an hour.
6. At that point, take out the pastry, place it in front of you with the opening on the right and repeat the rolling and folding operation. Before wrapping it again in plastic wrap and putting it back in the fridge, mark it twice to remember that you have made the second fold.
7. The entire procedure should be repeated two more times, for a total of four folds.
8. In the end, the pasta should be left to rest in the fridge for at least 1 hour. If you do not intend to use it immediately, you can leave it in the plastic wrap and freeze it.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★★
Preparazione: 130'
Dosi: 1 kg di pasta circa

name: Croquette
text:
Recipe ID: croqueta_es_parsed
Name: Croquette

Ingredients:
- 2kg of croquette dough
- Panko
- Wheat Stars
- 500g of beaten egg

Steps:
1. a) Place the Wheat Stars in one tray and the Panko in another.
2. b) Whisk the eggs in a bowl.
3. c) Make 18g balls which we will introduce into the Wheat Stars, in order to shape them.
4. d) From the Wheat Stars, transfer them to the egg and let them sit for a minimum of 3 minutes before passing them through the Panko.
5. e) It is very important not to leave them made for more than a day.

name: Stew Sauce
text:
Recipe ID: salsa_de_estofado_en_parsed
Name: Stew Sauce

Ingredients:
- 2.5 kg ginger
- 2.5 kg garlic
- 10 kg red onion
- 100 vine tomatoes
- 3.600l cooking water
- 150gr fresh basil
- 32 palm sugar tablets
- 8 jars of sweet chili basil paste
- 14 l beef stock
- 6lt of fino oloroso sherry
- 3 jars of tamarind
- water

Steps:
1. a) Peel the garlic and ginger, slice and sauté in a pot with oil.
2. b) Peel the onion and cut into julienne strips. Once the garlic and ginger are cooked, add the onion.
3. c) Clean the tomatoes and cut into julienne strips. Once the onion is softened and browned, add the chopped tomato and continue cooking the sauce.
4. d) Once everything is cooked, add the palm sugar, the jars of tamarind, and the jars of Thai basil.
5. e) After 10/15 minutes, add the fino oloroso sherry and reduce it. Finally, add the beef stock and approximately 25 liters of water.
6. f) Bring to a boil and let it reduce for approximately 8/10 hours. Once the desired density is achieved, strain through a fine sieve.

Other details:

name: Tur Dal Recipe
text:
Recipe ID: tur_dal_recipe_parsed
Name: Tur Dal Recipe

Ingredients:
- 1 cup of tur dal
- 1 tsp of turmeric
- mustard seed
- fenugreek seed
- cumin
- hing
- 1\2 stick of cinnamon
- 12 curry leaves
- 12 chopped green chili
- 1 tsp turmeric
- 2 finely chopped tomato
- coriander
- 1 cup of water
- salt to taste
- tbsp of jaggery

Steps:
1. Wash well 1 cup of tur dal, put in pressure pot with 1 tsp of turmeric and 2 finger of water, proceed to cook for 3 whistle time, and set aside to rest
2. Slightly crush the cooked dal
3. Toast in this order 1 tsp of mustard seed, 1 tsp fenugreek seed, 1 tsp cumin , 1 tsp hing 1\2 stick of cinnamon 12 curry leaves,12 chopped green chili, 1 tsp turmeric, 2 finely chopped tomato sweet all together, add coriander, 1 cup of water, salt to taste and  tbsp of jaggery
4. Combine all together and let it cook properly until done

name: Shrimp Fritters
text:
Recipe ID: tortilla_de_camarones_es_parsed
Name: Shrimp Fritters

Ingredients:
- 100g Cornstarch
- 50g Shrimp Powder
- 2 Shallots
- 70g Green parts of Chinese Scallions
- 9g Fermented Shrimp Paste
- 250g Water

Steps:
1. a) Peel the shallots. Dice the shallots and scallions into brunoise (small, uniform cubes).
2. b) Place the frozen dried shrimp into a Thermomix (or food processor) and grind until you achieve a light, airy powder.
3. c) In a bowl, add the cornstarch, shallots, scallions, fermented shrimp paste, shrimp powder, and water. Stir with a whisk until the batter is homogeneous.
4. d) In a small frying pan, add two drops of oil and spread with a paper towel. Once the pan is hot, pour in a little of the mixture and spread it across the entire pan to create a thin layer. When it starts to detach from the pan, flip the fritter and repeat the process.

Other details:

name: Neapolitan Pastiera
text:
Recipe ID: neapolitan_pastiera_merged
Name: Neapolitan Pastiera

Ingredients:
- FOR THE FILLING
- Orange blossom water: 25 g
- Candied oranges: 50 g
- Butter: 30 g
- Cinnamon: 1 teaspoon
- Candied citron: 50 g
- Lemons: 1 (grated zest)
- Ricotta cheese: 350 g (½ sheep's milk)
- Eggs: 3 (+ 2 yolks)
- Vanilla: 1 packet
- Sugar: 350 g
- Precooked wheat: 250 g
- Milk: 200 g
- FOR THE SHORTCRUST PASTRY (ABOUT 550 g)
- Butter: 125 g
- Flour: 250 g
- Eggs: 1 + 1 yolk
- Vanilla: 1 packet
- Sugar: 100 g
- Lemon: 1 (grated zest)
- Salt: 1 pinch

Steps:
1. Prepare the shortcrust pastry with the doses indicated here beside, according to the recipe on page 284. Once ready, let it rest in the refrigerator for about 40 minutes, wrapped in plastic wrap.
2. In the meantime, dedicate yourself to the wheat cream: in a saucepan, pour the precooked wheat, milk, butter and grated lemon zest and boil over low heat, stirring. You will obtain a thick cream that you will have to pour into a large bowl and let cool slightly.
3. Meanwhile, in a mixer, blend 2 eggs together with the yolks, sugar, ricotta, orange blossom water, vanilla and cinnamon, until you get a smooth and lump-free cream.
4. Roll out the shortcrust pastry, keeping a small amount aside. Line a round 28 cm diameter baking tin with the obtained pastry, which you have previously buttered.
5. When the wheat cream has cooled slightly, add the ricotta cream and mix, then add the candied citron and orange. Mix well, then pour the filling into the tin and, if the edges of the pastry are higher than the cream, level them.
6. With the pastry set aside, make a not too thin sheet from which you will cut strips of 1.5-2 cm wide with a toothed wheel, which you will use to decorate the top of the pastiera, arranging them in a grid. Brush the strips with a beaten egg and bake the pastiera at 180 °C. After about 1 hour, when the surface is golden brown, remove it and let it cool. At that point, cover it and keep it in the refrigerator overnight, then gently unmold it, put it on a serving dish and sprinkle it with powdered sugar before serving.

Other details:
Difficoltà: media
Cottura: 60'
Preparazione: 30'
Dosi: 10 persone

name: Lentil Recipe
text:
Recipe ID: lentil_recipe_parsed
Name: Lentil Recipe

Ingredients:
- 1 cup tura dal
- ½ cup of red lentil
- 1 dice tomato
- ½ dice onion
- 3 tbsp of ghee
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 2 kashmiri chili ( no seed)
- 10 curry leaves
- 5 garlic finely chopped
- 2 red onion finely chopped
- 20 gr of chopped ginger
- 4 chilli cut in half
- 1 tsp of chilli powder
- ½ tsp turmeric
- salt to taste
- chopped coriander

Steps:
1. Put in pressure pot with 3 fingers of water and 1 dice tomato and ½ dice onion

3. Prepare Tharka:heat 3 tbsp of ghee in a  pan and toast 1 tsp cumin seeds, 2 kashmiri chili ( no seed), 10 curry leaves, 5 garlic finely chopped, 2 red onion finely chopped, 20 gr of chopped ginger, 4 chilli cut in half
4. Toast everything very well
5. Add 1 tsp of chilli powder and ½ tsp turmeric and salt to taste, keep toasting.
6. Slightly crushed the rested lentil and combine with tharka
7. Bring to the boil everything and finish with chopped coriander

name: Buffalo Blue Cheese In Tempura
text:
Recipe ID: buffalo_blue_cheese_in_tempura_merged
Name: Buffalo Blue Cheese In Tempura

Ingredients:
- buffalo blue cheese
- puffed rice 200
- olive oil for frying 200
- for the tempura
- flour 80
- cold water 130
- fine salt 0.1
- sugar 0.1
- brewer's yeast 2.5

Steps:
1. for the tempura
2. dissolve the yeast in very cold water
3. add the flour, salt and sugar, stir until you get a fine and creamy mass
4. seal with plastic wrap and let it rest for at least 4 hours in the refrigerator.
5. Cut the buffalo blue cheese paste into 2x2 squares, place in the freezer for a few minutes without freezing.
6. Remove the tempura and dip each piece of cheese in the tempura, then coat in rice, bread all 4 sides well and fry in hot olive oil (a temperature of 160 ° - 170 ° C); brown the outer surface well, remove and dry.

text:

Name: Prune Tart Custard

Ingredients:
- 2350 UHT WHIPPING CREAM
- 375 CASTER SUGAR
- 690 LIQUID PASTERUISED EGG YOLKS
- 10 VANILLA PODS SCRAPED

Steps:
1. BOIL CREAM AND VANILLA.
2. REST TO 85C
3. ADD SUGAR TO YOLKS AND TEMPER IN 1/3 OF THE CREAM
4. POUR BACK INTO THE PAN AND PASS THROUGH A FINE CHINOIS
5. DISPENSE ONTO TARTS VIA CHINOIS PISTOL OVER A SPOON
6. FILL TO THE RIM.
7. BAKE ON 94C FOR 1.5 HOURS FAN 3.
8. ONCE COOKED REST AT ROOM TEMPERATURE. DEMOULD AND PLACE IN THE FRIDGE
9. BLIND BAKING
10. 175 FULL FAN FOR 10-15 MINUTES OR UNTIL EDGES ARE GOLDEN BROWN
11. TAKE OUT BAKING BEADS

name: Lemon Delights
text:
Recipe ID: lemon_delights_merged
Name: Lemon Delights

Ingredients:
- FOR THE SPONGE CAKE BASE
- All-purpose flour: 300 g
- Sorrento lemons: 1
- Eggs: 300 g egg whites
- Eggs: 300 g egg yolks
- Sugar: 300 g
- FOR THE LEMON CREAM
- Butter: 200 ml
- Water: 100 ml
- Limoncello: 50 ml
- Lemons: 2 (grated zest)
- Fresh Cream: 500 ml
- Eggs: 200 g egg yolks
- Sugar: 200 g
- Powdered sugar: 50 g
- FOR THE LIMONCELLO SYRUP
- Water: 100 ml
- Limoncello: 150 ml
- Lemons: 1 (zest)
- Sugar: 100 g

Steps:
1. 1 Beat the egg whites (which must be at room temperature) with an electric mixer. When they begin to turn white, add 50 g of sugar, let it whip for a minute, then add another 50 g of sugar and, after another minute, another 50 g. Separately, beat the egg yolks with 150 g of sugar for at least 7-8 minutes. When the two mixtures are ready, gently combine them in a large bowl, where you will add the previously sifted flour and the grated zest of 1 lemon.
2. 2 Insert the mixture obtained into a pastry bag and squeeze it into the appropriate silicone mold with a diameter of 7-8 cm. Bake at 180°C (350°F) for about 20 minutes. When cooked, remove from the oven and let it cool slightly before removing the spheres from the mold and letting them cool completely on a wire rack.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★★
Cottura:: 40'
Preparazione:: 180'
Dosi:: 15 delizie

name: Potato Gnocchi
text:
Recipe ID: gnocchi_di_patate_merged
Name: Potato Gnocchi

Ingredients:
- Flour: 300 g
- Potatoes: 1 kg
- Eggs: 1

Steps:
1. I have a sweet tooth, but I can't resist potato gnocchi: for me, this recipe is worth a marriage proposal. Who could remain indifferent to a plate with a balance between flour and potatoes, with the soft but firm consistency of gnocchi?
2. One of the secrets to preparing this very simple dish, which enchants everyone, is to reassure those who will be working in the kitchen, whether they are beginners, is to have the right potatoes. When you buy them, choose those with a floury pulp, that is, the driest ones, such as Dutch or red potatoes.
3. Wash the potatoes and, without peeling them, immerse them in a pot of salted water and let them boil, or cook them in the steam. When they are cooked, peel them while still hot, mash them and place them on a well-floured work surface. Add a pinch of salt, the flour and an egg and work everything together until you obtain a compact mixture that should not be too dry, but moist enough, but at the same time, soft.
4. To make the shape of the gnocchi, detach the dough a little at a time and work it until you obtain many sticks with a diameter of 2-3 centimeters. Divide them into pieces, which you will pass one by one on a fork or on the appropriate board, pressing lightly: the gnocchi will acquire the classic grooves and, on the opposite side, the characteristic hollow will form. Place them on a floured surface or tray and let them rest.
5. To cook them, use a fairly large pot with salted water, in which you will insert them one portion at a time. Drain them as they rise to the surface and season them immediately.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Dosi: 4 persone
Preparazione: 20'
Cottura: 5'

One of the secrets to preparing this very simple dish, which enchants everyone, is to reassure those who will be working in the kitchen, whether they are beginners, is to have the right potatoes. When you buy them, choose those with a floury pulp, that is, the driest ones, such as Dutch or red potatoes.

Wash the potatoes and, without peeling them, immerse them in a pot of salted water and let them boil, or cook them in the steam. When they are cooked, peel them while still hot, mash them and place them on a well-floured work surface. Add a pinch of salt, the flour and an egg and work everything together until you obtain a compact mixture that should not be too dry, but moist enough, but at the same time, soft.

To make the shape of the gnocchi, detach the dough a little at a time and work it until you obtain many sticks with a diameter of 2-3 centimeters. Divide them into pieces, which you will pass one by one on a fork or on the appropriate board, pressing lightly: the gnocchi will acquire the classic grooves and, on the opposite side, the characteristic hollow will form. Place them on a floured surface or tray and let them rest.
To cook them, use a fairly large pot with salted water, in which you will insert them one portion at a time. Drain them as they rise to the surface and season them immediately.

name: Caramelized Egg
text:
Recipe ID: caramelized_egg_merged
Name: Caramelized Egg

Ingredients:
- Quail eggs
- White wine vinegar
- Coarse salt
- Nutmeg
- Sichuan pepper
- Bay leaf
- For the caramel
- Glucose
- Fondant sugar
- 8 quail eggs
- 10 white wine vinegar
- As needed coarse salt
- As needed nutmeg
- As needed Sichuan pepper
- Bay leaf
- 100 glucose
- 200 fondant sugar

Steps:
1. Break the eggs into a bowl and set aside.
2. In a pot, boil water with a little vinegar.
3. When it reaches boiling point, add the eggs one at a time, using a spider strainer, skim off all the egg white that rises to the surface, remove the eggs, transfer them to a bowl with cold water and salt, dry and set aside.
4. Caramel
5. Bring the fondant and glucose to a boil until 160°C (320°F).
6. Remove from the heat and spread on a Silpat mat and pull it very thin, cut into rectangles of about 3 x 4 cm.
7. Place the egg on a baking sheet.
8. Cover it with a sheet of sugar and pass it through the salamander until the caramel softens and wraps around the egg; remove from the salamander, turn it and repeat the operation.
9. Place the egg on a spoon, salt and serve

name: Squid And Thai Papaya. Rabbit Filling
text:
Recipe ID: chipirn_y_papaya_tailandesa_relleno_de_conejo_en_parsed
Name: Squid And Thai Papaya. Rabbit Filling

Ingredients:
- 4 rabbits
- 2 heads of garlic
- 9 white onions
- 6 large carrots
- 12 vine tomatoes
- 4 leeks
- 1 liter of oloroso wine
- 250g of tomato paste
- 3 liters of chicken broth
- 5 Chinese spices

Steps:
1. a) Cut all the vegetables into thin julienne strips.
2. b) In a pan, sear the rabbits that we have previously seasoned.
3. c) Inside a pot, introduce the vegetables until they have a golden color.
4. d) When the vegetables are golden, introduce the chopped rabbit and sauté it with the vegetables and the tomato paste.
5. e) Add the wine and let it evaporate, then pour in the chicken broth.
6. f) Let it cook slowly, covered, until the rabbit is cooked to perfection.
7. g) Remove the rabbit and shred it.
8. h) Reintroduce the bones inside and let it cook uncovered for one more hour.
9. i) Strain the stew and reduce it, then thicken it with the shredded meat.
10. j) Season to taste with the sauce, 5 spices, and chill for use.

Other details:

raw.name: Squid And Thai Papaya. Rabbit Filling
steps: a) Cut all the vegetables into thin julienne strips.
b) In a pan, sear the rabbits that we have previously seasoned.
c) Inside a pot, introduce the vegetables until they have a golden color.
d) When the vegetables are golden, introduce the chopped rabbit and sauté it with the vegetables and the tomato paste.
e) Add the wine and let it evaporate, then pour in the chicken broth.
f) Let it cook slowly, covered, until the rabbit is cooked to perfection.
g) Remove the rabbit and shred it.
h) Reintroduce the bones inside and let it cook uncovered for one more hour.
i) Strain the stew and reduce it, then thicken it with the shredded meat.
j) Season to taste with the sauce, 5 spices, and chill for use.

name: Crème Caramel Ice Cream
text:
Recipe ID: crème_caramel_ice_cream_merged
Name: Crème Caramel Ice Cream

Ingredients:
- 375g milk
- 125g whipping cream
- 6 eggs
- 80g sugar
- 100g egg yolks
- 3g agar agar
- 200g Sugar for caramel

Steps:
1. Make a dry caramel and set in a pan
2. Warm the milk, cream and agar agar till simmer point
3. Beat the eggs, yolks and sugar
4. And milk/cream to the egg mix
5. Mix together
6. Return to a Bain Marie 48
7. Cook till you reach Celsuis 14
8. You may have to blend whilst cooking to make it smooth
9. Pour onto caramel and set in the fridge

name: Apricot Jam Crostata
text:
Recipe ID: crostata_alla_confettura_di_albicocche_merged
Name: Apricot Jam Crostata

Ingredients:
- FOR THE PASTRY
- Butter: 150 g
- Flour 00: 300 g
- Eggs (medium): 2
- Sugar: 130 g
- Lemons: 1 (zest)
- Salt: 1 pinch
- FOR FILLING
- Apricot jam: 600 g
- FOR BRUSHING
- Eggs (medium): 1

Steps:
1. 1 Prepare the pastry with the doses indicated alongside, according to the recipe on page 284. After letting the dough rest in the fridge for at least half an hour, turn on the oven to 180°C (350°F) convection and roll out two-thirds of the pastry into a sheet about 3-4 mm thick, with which you will line a rectangular mold of 20x28 cm (or a round one for pies with a diameter of 20 cm). Prick with the tines of a fork and, with the remaining pastry, create strips using a knife or a pastry wheel. Then pour the jam into the pastry shell and level it with a spoon.
2. 2 Brush the edges of the cake with the beaten egg and place the strips on the surface, forming a grid of diamonds. Brush these as well, then bake for about 45 minutes. Once removed from the oven, let the crostata cool in the mold before serving it on a serving dish sprinkled with powdered sugar.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Preparazione:: 20'
Cottura:: 45'
Dosi:: 6 persone

name: Almond Bavaroise
text:
Recipe ID: almond_bavaroise_merged
Name: Almond Bavaroise

Ingredients:
- 400g Almonds
- 160g Water
- 180g Double cream
- 50g Sugar
- 10g Gelatine, platinum sheets
- 1g Salt

Steps:
1. Soak the almonds overnight in water (not in recipe)
2. Drain the almonds, then add 400g water and the almonds to a vitamix. Blend until warm.
3. Strain the almond milk, through a nut milk / super bag.
4. Weigh out 120g almond milk and warm gently in a pan. Add the bloomed gelatine. Heat until fully dissolved (without cooking the milk for too long).
5. While the almond milk cools back to room temperature, whip the cream with the sugar and salt. Whip to soft peaks
6. Fold the cream through the cool almond milk mixture, and zest in the desired mould.
7. Chill in refrigerator overnight, covered with clingfilm.

Other details:
Yield: 16 portions
Notes: Doris

Classifications:
- Dish Type: Dessert
- Cuisine: French
- Difficulty: Level 2 (Intermediate / Professional)
- Main Ingredient: Dairy / Egg
- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Vegetarian, Alcohol-Free, Nut-Free
- Occasion: Fine Dining / Plated
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

text:
Recipe ID: cucumber_and_lime_jelly_merged
Name: Cucumber And Lime Jelly

Ingredients:
- Cucumber juice 3 lt
- Fresh cucumber juice (from peeled cucumber) 750
- Lime juice 350
- Granulated sugar 80
- Grated fresh ginger 25
- Salt 30
- Gelatin (bronze gelatin sheets)

Steps:
1. Infuse all ingredients (except gelatin) for 1 hour, then strain through super bags.
2. Soak the gelatin in cold water to soften.
3. Take a little cucumber juice, heat it up, and then add the gelatin.
4. Add to the rest of the mixture and set in ice.
5. Once gelatinized, strain through super bags.

name: Shrimp Carpaccio
text:
Recipe ID: carpacio_de_gamba_es_parsed
Name: Shrimp Carpaccio

Ingredients:
- 2 kg frozen large shrimp.
- Oil.

Steps:
1. a) Thaw the shrimp.
2. b) Peel the shrimp and remove the vein.
3. c) Place them in the food processor for 40 seconds at maximum speed.
4. d) Once we have a fine paste, place the paste between plastic wrap with oil and spread it out until you have a thin carpaccio.
5. e) Making sure that the plastic wrap does not break, place them between parchment paper and freeze.
6. f) Once frozen, use a pastry cutter to make circles.

Other details:
ELABORACIONES: CARPACIO DE GAMBA

name: Tempura Fried Buffalo Blue Cheese
text:
Recipe ID: blu_di_bufala_in_tempura_merged
Name: Tempura Fried Buffalo Blue Cheese

Ingredients:
- Buffalo blue cheese
- Puffed rice 200
- Olive oil for frying 200
- For the tempura
- Flour 80
- Cold water 130
- Fine salt 0.1
- Sugar 0.1
- Brewer's yeast 2.5

Steps:
1. For the tempura
2. Dissolve the yeast in the very cold water
3. Add the flour, salt and sugar, stir until you obtain a fine and creamy mixture
4. Seal with plastic wrap and let rest for at least 4 hours in the refrigerator.
5. Cut the buffalo blue cheese into 2x2 squares, place in the freezer for a few minutes without freezing completely.
6. Remove the tempura and dip each piece of cheese in the tempura, then coat in the puffed rice, bread well on all 4 sides and fry in hot olive oil (a temperature of 160°- 170° C); brown the outer surface well, remove and dry.

Other details:

- Seasonality: Year-Round

10. STORE IN AIRTIGHT CONTAINERS

name: Meatballs In Sauce
text:
Recipe ID: polpette_al_sugo_merged
Name: Meatballs In Sauce

Ingredients:
- Ground beef (or pork): 500 g
- Eggs: 3
- Bread crumb: 100 g
- Parmigiano reggiano: 50 g
- Grated Pecorino cheese: 50 g
- Tomato sauce: 1 liter
- Garlic: 3 cloves
- Onion: 1
- Oil: 5 tablespoons
- Parsley: 2 tablespoons
- Basil: 4-5 leaves
- Nutmeg: 1 pinch
- Salt and pepper: to taste

Steps:
1. Remove the crust from a piece of loaf bread and cut 100 g of crumb into squares, which you will crumble in the mixer.
2. Put the ground meat in a bowl (adding, if you like, sausage without the outer skin) with the bread crumbs, Parmigiano reggiano, Pecorino cheese, parsley, eggs, salt, pepper and nutmeg. Knead everything together with your hands and let the mixture rest in a cool place for about 1 hour.
3. In the meantime, dedicate yourself to the sauce. Pour the oil, finely chopped onion and garlic into a large saucepan. When the onion is transparent (it will take about 15 minutes) pour in the tomato sauce and bring gently to a boil.
4. It is time to shape the meatballs: take about 10 g of mixture at a time and shape it with the palms of your hands to give it a rounded shape, until finished.

Other details:
Difficoltà: None
Cottura: 40'
Preparazione: 20'
Dosi: 4 persone
original_language: it
translated_from: it

Classifications:
- Dish Type: Main Course
- Cuisine: Italian
- Difficulty: Level 2 (Intermediate / Professional)
- Main Ingredient: Meat (Beef, Pork, Lamb, Game)
- Dietary: Gluten-Free
- Occasion: Family Style / Sharing
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

raw.name: Meatballs In Sauce
steps: Remove the crust from a piece of loaf bread and cut 100 g of crumb into squares, which you will crumble in the mixer.

Put the ground meat in a bowl (adding, if you like, sausage without the outer skin) with the bread crumbs, Parmigiano reggiano, Pecorino cheese, parsley, eggs, salt, pepper and nutmeg. Knead everything together with your hands and let the mixture rest in a cool place for about 1 hour.

In the meantime, dedicate yourself to the sauce. Pour the oil, finely chopped onion and garlic into a large saucepan. When the onion is transparent (it will take about 15 minutes) pour in the tomato sauce and bring gently to a boil.
It is time to shape the meatballs: take about 10 g of mixture at a time and shape it with the palms of your hands to give it a rounded shape, until finished.

text:
Recipe ID: cake_batter_merged
Name: Cake Batter

Ingredients:
- Eggs
- Egg Yolks
- Powdered Sugar
- Cream
- Flour
- Baking Powder
- Cold Melted Butter

Steps:
1. 1200 gr Weigh the eggs in the mixer
2. 240 gr the yolks and powdered sugar
3. 960 gr Whisk for 2 minutes
4. 720 gr Add the cream and whisk
5. 1300 gr Add the flour and baking powder
6. 60 gr and whisk
7. 600 gr Add the cold melted butter
8. Weigh 850gr per mold
9. Bake at 160°c for 45min

name: How To Cook Jerusalem Artichoke
text:
Recipe ID: how_to_cook_jerusalem_artichoke_parsed
Name: How To Cook Jerusalem Artichoke

Ingredients:
- Jerusalem Artichoke
- salt
- crushed garlic
- rough ginger
- Honey
- Soy Sauce
- Chingkiang vinegar

Steps:
1. Soak the Jerusalem Artichoke for 10 minutes, and then wash it thoroughly
2. Cut the Jerusalem artichoke in half or quarter if big
3. Mix it in a bowl with a bit of salt, crushed garlic and rough ginger
4. Drizzle The jerusalem artichoke with Honey to glaze
5. Roast in the oven at 195C for 15minutes and above until golden and cooked
6. When Golden, Deglaze with Soy Sauce just so give it some taste and a dash of Chingkiang vinegar

name: Cardamom Bechamel
text:
Recipe ID: bechamel_de_cardamomo_es_parsed
Name: Cardamom Bechamel

Ingredients:
- 1 kg of flour.
- 1 kg of butter.
- 10 liters of cow's milk.
- Ground cardamom.
- Salt.

Steps:
1. a) The first step is to put the milk in a saucepan to heat up and prevent lumps from forming.
2. b) In a pot, make a roux, being careful not to brown it too much, as we want it very white.
3. c) Once the roux is ready, add the milk and, with rapid whisking, cook the bechamel.
4. d) Once it is smooth, season with salt and cardamom.
5. e) Finally, strain it through a fine-mesh sieve and cool it down.

Other details:

Name: Whipped Ewes Yoghurt

Ingredients:
- 1kg ewes yoghurt
- 70g ultratex
- 260ml milk
- 300g whipping cream
- 100g icing sugar
- Zest of half a lime
- 1 vanilla pod scraped

Steps:
1. Combine milk and ultratex to form a slurry
2. Place half the amount of yoghurt and the slurry in the robo coupe and blend till smooth and shiney (becareful not to over blend)
3. Place into a bowl with remaining yoghurt
4. Lightly whip the cream icing sugar, zest and vanilla and gently fold in yoghurt
5. Chill straight away

Other details:

name: Potato Fagottini Filled With Ragusano Cheese With Crispy Guanciale And Spring Onion
text:
Recipe ID: potato_fagottini_filled_with_ragusano_cheese_with_crispy_guanciale_and_spring_onion_merged
Name: Potato Fagottini Filled With Ragusano Cheese With Crispy Guanciale And Spring Onion

Ingredients:
- x fagottini
- 1 kg red potatoes
- 250gr flour
- 2 eggs
- parmesan cheese
- nutmeg
- salt and pepper
- x the filling for the roloqbo
- 1lt milk
- 300gr ragusano cheese (450 BATATA (pat)
- 80gr roux (80 BURRO)
- x the guanciale
- 300gr suckling pig guanciale
- vegetable stock in brunoise
- 1l water
- 50gr salt
- bay leaf
- black peppercorns
- x the pork stock
- pork bones and scraps
- concentrated vegetable stock
- x the spring onion
- 10 spring onions

Steps:
1. Prepare the boiled potatoes for the gnocchi, mash them, mince them and knead. Roll out the dough with a rolling pin to a thickness of about half a centimeter, cut out with a square cutter and fill with the ragusano fondue previously prepared in the roloqbo. Then close the gnocchi into fagottini.
2. x the fondue, set the roloqbo to 85° with a serrated knife at a speed of 400, add butter, flour, salt and nutmeg and let it run for 1 minute, then add the milk and as soon as the mixture is ready, immerse the cheese and continue stirring until temperature.
3. Bring the water to a boil with the salt, bay leaf and pepper, let it cool and immerse the guanciale for 20 minutes, meanwhile prepare the brunoise stock which will be used later
4. Prepare a classic pork stock with the bones and as soon as it is ready, bind it and pass it through cheesecloth and add the brunoise vegetables. Put a little sauce together with some guanciale in vacuum cooking bags and cook all night at 42° in steam.
5. Put the spring onions in vacuum cooking bags, seal and cook for about 10 minutes at 85a steam
6. Compose the dish with 4 gnocchi cooked in water and sautéed in butter, the regenerated guanciale always vacuum-packed at 60° in the roner then cut thinly and pour the meat and sauce over the gnocchi, finish with the spring onion browned in a pan.

Other details:
original_language: it

- Dish Type: Main Course
- Cuisine: Italian
- Difficulty: Level 2
- Main Ingredient: Starch/Grain
- Dietary: Nut-Free
- Occasion: Fine Dining / Plated
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Spring

text:
Recipe ID: date_and_tamarind_chutney_parsed

Ingredients:
- ½ kg of frozen deseeded dates
- 10 gr of dry tamarind
- 30 gr of jaggery
- 1 tsp of chilli powder
- ½ tsp of black salt
- 1 cup of water

Steps:
1. Soaked ½ kg of frozen deseeded dates overnight
2. In a pot place 10 gr of dry tamarind , 30 gr of jaggery, 1 tsp of chilli powder. ½ tsp of black salt, and the dates and 1 cup of water

name: Milk Sheet
text:
Recipe ID: foglia_di_latte_merged
Name: Milk Sheet

Ingredients:
- Whole milk 1000
- Liquid cream 70

Steps:
1. Place the milk in a pot approximately 30-32 cm in diameter
2. Bring it to a temperature of 70°C
3. Wait approximately 15 minutes until a solid skin forms on the surface
4. Remove the milk skin, and with the help of your fingers, fold it in half, obtaining a half-moon shape
5. Repeat the operation until you obtain 4-5 semicircles
6. With the help of a brush, coat each milk sheet with the cream, stack the sheets, and store in the refrigerator

Other details:

name: Miso Paste
text:
Recipe ID: pasta_de_miso_es_parsed
Name: Miso Paste

Ingredients:
- 500g reduced mirin
- 500g reduced sake
- 2kg white miso
- 1kg sugar

Steps:
1. a) The first thing we'll do is reduce the mirin and sake separately. When we talk about reducing, I mean going from one liter to half.
2. b) Once we have the reduced liquids, we weigh them and mix everything in a bowl. We then place it in a bain-marie and slowly caramelize it for about 6 hours, until we get a paste.
3. c) Finally, we will introduce it into the Thermomix and leave it as smooth as possible.

Other details:

name: Socarrat
text:
Recipe ID: socarrat_en_parsed
Name: Socarrat

Ingredients:
- 1 head of garlic
- 1 liter white wine
- 3 large white onions
- Choricero pepper
- 8 roasted tomatoes
- Saffron
- 2 liters chicken broth
- 1 liter water
- 2 kg small birds (e.g., sparrows)
- 2 kg arrocera shrimp

Steps:
1. Roast the tomatoes in the oven
2. Peel the garlic and crush in a Thermomix
3. Peel the onion and crush in a Thermomix
4. In a pot, first brown the garlic, then the onion, and finally add the crushed tomato
5. Roast the shrimp and small birds in the oven
6. Once the vegetables are sautéed, add the shrimp, small birds, and the choricero pepper
7. Add the white wine and reduce
8. Add the chicken broth, water, and saffron threads
9. Bring to a boil and cook covered for 25 minutes and uncovered for 25 minutes

Other details:
Elaboracion: Caldo de paella

text:

steps: INSTRUCTIONS

PEEL THE GARLIC CLOVES, REMOVE THE GERM (core), AND BLANCH 10 TIMES, CHANGING THE WATER EACH TIME. FOR THE LAST BLANCH, DO IT IN MILK. AND LET THE GARLIC COOK UNTIL IT IS SOFT. DRAIN AND BLEND IN A MIXER UNTIL YOU GET A SMOOTH CREAM, PASS THROUGH A SIEVE AND SET ASIDE.

name: Potato Chips With Duck Fat
text:
Recipe ID: patatillas_con_pato_merged
Name: Potato Chips With Duck Fat

Ingredients:
- Duck Fat: Duck Fat
- Fried Potato Chips: 1 unit. sour potatoes, Duck Fat, Mandoline
- Duck Fat: Duck Fat
- Fried Potato Chips: 1 unit. sour potatoes, Duck Fat, Mandoline

Steps:
1. ELABORATION:
2. The duck fat: Cut the fat into 5 cm approx. cubes, put them in an induction pot at power 7 for 20 min, stirring every 3 minutes so that the fat is completely degreased.
3. Strain and microfilter, leaving papers and a strainer, so that the fat is clean and without residues.
4. The fried potato chips: Clean the potatoes well, to remove all the excess dirt, cut in a mandoline with a thickness of 0.4 cm , and 9 cm long wavy potatoes.
5. Cut each potato that has stains, they will not work for the dish.
6. Serve the potatoes on a tray with water and wash them until they do not release starch or the water is transparent, changing the water as often as necessary.
7. Arrange the potatoes on a tray with holes next to each other without overlapping and cook in the oven at 99% steam for 3 minutes, remove from the oven and serve in a tray with water to remove any starch that may remain.
8. Once clean, place the potatoes between lithos next to each other without overlapping and dehydrate in the oven at 70°C for 1 hour with ventilation 3.
9. Once dehydrated, fry them in duck fat at 160°C for 3 minutes approx.
10. Remove them from the oil and verify that the center of the potato is crispy (otherwise, we will fry it again to make it crispy).
11. Arrange them in a gastronorm with absorbent paper and inside the hot cabinet at 50°C.
12. Salt.

22. Remove them from the oil and verify that the center of the potato is crispy (otherwise, we will fry it again to make it crispy).
23. Arrange them in a gastronorm with absorbent paper and inside the hot cabinet at 50°C.
24. Salt.

name: Blood Orange Cake Version From Rapeseed Cake
text:
Recipe ID: blood_orange_cake_version_from_rapeseed_cake_merged
Name: Blood Orange Cake Version From Rapeseed Cake

Ingredients:
- BLOOD ORANGE AND TEA BUTTER
- 1 litre blood orange boirion - reduce to 500g
- 7g agar agar
- 20g Darjeeling
- 2 leaves gelatine
- 125g butter
- 2 egg whites
- 300g icing sugar
- 2g orange powder
- MAUC

4. Blend in butter
5. Pass through a chinois
6. Chill
7. Beat on kitchen aid with paddle till smooth
8. Blend to a powder

name: Corn Tostada
text:
Recipe ID: corn_tostada_merged
Name: Corn Tostada

Ingredients:
- For the neutral caramel
- 100g Isomalt
- 100g Glucose
- 100g Liquid glucose
- For the dry coriander
- Bunch of coriander
- 200g Lactose powder
- 500g Lactose powder
- For the powder
- Freeze drying onion
- Tabasco chili powder
- Salt
- For time segments
- 1l edix solution
- 5 limes
- Extras
- Sweetcorn from lab
- Smoked cheese
- Fresh Lime zest

Steps:
1. Mix all the ingredients in a pan and cook to 14.
2. Pour out onto silpat and allow to cool.
3. Once cool blend into a powder and store in airtight container with silica gel.
4. Important caramel is not left out too long as it is very sensitive to moisture.
5. Sieve the lactose powder over the base of a gastro.
6. Pick the coriander leaves to get the nicest leave 2cm tall.
7. Sprue out the lactose powder with out leaves and cover with more lactose powder.
8. Dry at 55C over night.
9. Remove from the lactose and brush off excess powder carefully.
10. Store in airtight container.
11. Take from the lab.
12. Measure and blend together.
13. Using a sharp knife peel limes and remove the segments.
14. Soak the segments at room temperature in the cal x solution for 1 hour, stirring every 15 minutes.
15. Strain and rinse well.
16. Dehydrate for 2-4 hours at 55 degrees until dry. This is important so the tostada doesn't go soggy.
17. Keep in airtight container.
18. Check first with a senior member.
19. Spread obliato disks on silpato evenly apart
20. 10 per tray.
21. Either pre heat the oven to 220 degrees, fan off or use the grill.
22. Dust the obliato disks with neutral caramel powder to coat generously. Ensure no disk is touching another in the section.
23. Put them in in the oven or under the salamander until caramelized.
24. Remove from oven and layer up half of the disks with sweetcorn, dry lime coriander, corn, spring onion and chilli powder.
25. Continue to place the other empty disk on top.
26. Carefully to keep the filling in the centre.
27. Place the sweetcorn tostado on top disk and to enable the two to be closed together.
28. Place the sweetcorn tostado on a folded napkin.
29. Grate the lime and smoked cheese on top
30. Serve

Other details:
FINISHING AND PLATING: None

Classifications:
- Dish Type: Amuse-bouche / Canapé
- Cuisine: Latin American
- Difficulty: Level 3 (Advanced / Michelin)
- Main Ingredient: Vegetable-Forward
- Dietary: Vegetarian, Gluten-Free, Nut-Free, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Fine Dining / Plated
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

Important caramel is not left out too long as it is very sensitive to moisture.

name: Squid Seasoning
text:
Recipe ID: alio_para_el_chipirn_es_parsed
Name: Squid Seasoning

Ingredients:
- 300g chopped scallions
- 200g Giner
- 150g garlic
- 100g sherry vinegar
- 100g white wine
- 50g olive oil

Steps:
1. a) Finely dice all the vegetables (brunoise).
2. b) In a frying pan, add the oil and heat it.
3. c) Add the vegetables to the oil and cook until golden brown.
4. d) Next, add the white wine and let it evaporate slightly.
5. e) Continue with the sherry vinegar and season with salt to taste.
6. f) This preparation can be made in large quantities and stored.

- Sweet potato flour
- 16 Spice Blend mix
- Basil leaves
- Dried Chilies

3. Prefry it at 160C for 6 min
4. Cool
5. Refry at 180C to warm it up
6. Toss in 16 Spice Blend mix
7. Serve with Basil leaves and on a bed of Dried Chilies

name: Chocolate And Cream Gelato
text:
Recipe ID: gelato_al_cioccolato_e_alla_panna_merged
Name: Chocolate And Cream Gelato

Ingredients:
- FOR THE CHOCOLATE GELATO
- Cocoa powder: 50 g
- Carob flour: 5 g (optional)
- Glucose: 15 g
- Milk: 500 ml
- Cream: 150 ml
- Eggs: 6 yolks
- Sugar: 160 g
- FOR THE CREAM GELATO
- Milk: 600 ml
- Cream: 250 g
- Vanillina: 1 packet
- Sugar: 250 g

Steps:
1. Difficulty: ★
2. Cooking: 5'
3. Preparation: 40'
4. Yield: 1 kg of gelato
5. CHOCOLATE GELATO: In a bowl, mix the sugar with the carob flour; add the egg yolks and beat with an electric mixer until light. In a saucepan, heat the milk, cream, and glucose, then pour the liquids over the yolks and mix with a whisk. Return to the heat and cook, stirring constantly, until a temperature of 85° is reached (check with a thermometer): do not bring to a boil, or lumps will form. Remove from the heat and incorporate the cocoa, then cool the mixture quickly by placing the bowl in a larger container containing ice. Then put in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours, then pour the mixture into an ice cream maker. When ready, leave the gelato in the freezer for at least 3 hours before enjoying.
6. CREAM GELATO: Heat the milk with the sugar, cream, and vanillina in a saucepan until a temperature of 85° is reached. Then remove from the

name: Chocolate Lava Cake
text:
Recipe ID: chocolate_lava_cake_merged
Name: Chocolate Lava Cake

Ingredients:
- Butter: 80 g
- Cocoa powder: 10 g
- Dark chocolate: 150 g
- Flour: 20 g
- Salt: 1 pinch
- Eggs: 2 + 1 yolk
- Vanilla extract: 1 packet
- Powdered sugar: 90 g
- FOR COATING THE MOLDS
- Butter: as needed
- Cocoa powder: 1 level spoon
- FOR DUSTING
- Powdered sugar: as needed

Steps:
1. Melt the chopped dark chocolate in a bain-marie, then add the cubed butter. Meanwhile, beat the eggs with the vanilla extract and sugar in a large bowl until you have a light, fluffy, and thick mixture (so incorporate them, it will take about 10 minutes). Then incorporate the chocolate, and finally, add the flour and cocoa sifted with a pinch of salt.
2. When the ingredients are well combined, butter and coat the aluminum molds with cocoa powder, fill them to ¾ and bake them at 180 °C (350°F). After 12-15 minutes (no more), remove the lava cakes from the oven, let them cool slightly and turn them upside down on a serving plate, sprinkle with powdered sugar and serve them still warm.

Other details:
Difficoltà: *
Cottura: 12-15'
Preparazione: 15'
Dosi: 4 tortini

name: Lamb Broth
text:
Recipe ID: caldo_de_cordero_merged
Name: Lamb Broth

Ingredients:
- INGREDIENTS:
- (For 8 l.)
- 6kg lamb bones.
- 1.5kg lamb meat trimmings.
- 5 l. water.
- INGREDIENTS:
- (For 8 l.)
- 6kg lamb bones.
- 1.5kg lamb meat trimmings.
- 5 l. water.

Steps:
1. ELABORATION:
2. Mise en Place
3. - Cut the meat trimmings into regular-sized pieces.
4. - Brown the bones and lamb trimmings on a lit grill with very hot embers. Brown at 220c until you get an intense color.
5. - Put the toasted lamb bones and trimmings in a pressure cooker.
6. Development
7. - Cover the lamb with water.
8. - Bring to a boil and close the pot.
9. - Maintain a gentle simmer for 2 hours.
10. - Strain the broth using a chinois. Press the meat well to obtain as much broth as possible.
11. Reduction
12. - Place the broth in a container and cover with film. Cool in a cold water bath until the fat solidifies on the surface.
13. - Remove the fat completely and strain using a fine sieve.
14. - Place the broth in a tall pot to reduce it.
15. - Reduce the broth until you get 25º in a refractometer.
16. - Adjust the salt and reserve in vacuum bags in portions of 150 g.
17. ELABORATION:

name: Tiger'S Milk Avocado And Jalapeño
text:
Recipe ID: tiger's_milk_avocado_and_jalapeño_merged
Name: Tiger'S Milk Avocado And Jalapeño

Ingredients:
- 100 gr. Lime juice
- 150 gr. Ice
- 150 gr. Small avocados, ripe avocado
- 1 stalk. Celery stalk
- 1 clove. Garlic
- 1 tsp. Ginger (in proportion to the garlic)
- 1 tsp. Xanthan gum
- 80-100 gr. Sunflower oil
- ½ tsp. Water

Steps:
1. Add all the ingredients in the blender (except the oil and xanthan gum) and mix until a homogeneous mixture is obtained.
2. Emulsify with the sunflower oil until creamy, add the xanthan gum and mix.
3. It has to be creamy, if it is too thick add a little more water.
4. REPLACE EVERY 2 DAYS. CORRECT THE TEXTURE AND FLAVOR DAILY

name: Gravy
text:
Recipe ID: gravy_parsed
Name: Gravy

Ingredients:
- 2 ladle of oil
- 1 tbsp of mustard seeds
- ½ tbsp of cumin seeds
- 4 garlic peeled and cut in a half
- 15 curry leaves
- 1 clean kashmiri chilli
- 1 medium onion chop
- 1 tomato diced big
- 1 tsp of turmeric
- 1 tsp of coriander powder
- half shaved coconut
- 1 cup of water
- 3 ladle of oil
- 1 cup of water with 3 tbsp of coconut milk powder
- salt to taste
- 15 gr of butter
- prawns
- fresh coriander
- half lemon

Steps:
1. Heat 2 ladle of oil in a pan and proceed to toast ingredients in this order: 1 tbsp of mustard seeds,½ tbsp of cumin seeds, 4 garlic peeled and cut in a half,15 curry leaves, 1 clean kashmiri chilli, 1 medium onion chop, 1 tomato diced big,1 tsp of turmeric, 1 tsp of coriander powder and mix well
2. Add half shaved coconut, toast all well together and set aside to chill down
3. Add the mixture to blender with 1 cup of water and blend until smooth and lucid paste
4. Put 3 ladle of oil in a pan and start toasting the gravy, for 15 minute until darker colour then add (mixed together) 1 cup of water with 3 tbsp of coconut milk powder, and add to the gravy, add salt to taste and let it simmer for 20 minute
5. Add 15 gr of butter and the prawns until cooked, serve with fresh coriander and half lemon squeeze

Other details:
servings: 2 or 3 people
notes: This recipe can be done with meat, fish and vegetables

Classifications:
- Dish Type: Main Course
- Cuisine: Indian
- Difficulty: Level 2 (Intermediate / Professional)
- Main Ingredient: Fish & Seafood
- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Nut-Free, Alcohol-Free, Pescatarian
- Occasion: Family Style / Sharing
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

name: Smoked Duck Breast
text:
Recipe ID: petto_d'anatra_affumicato_merged
Name: Smoked Duck Breast

Ingredients:
- Cane Sugar 500
- Oranges 3 Units
- Female Duck Breast 4 Units

Steps:
1. Peel the oranges, keeping only the zest without the white part. Dry under a lamp for 3 hours. Blend the cane sugar and the dried zest in a blender until a fine powder is obtained.
2. Line the base of a wide pot with aluminum foil, cover with the sugar obtained, and insert a coarse mesh sieve. Arrange the duck breasts in the sieve, cover everything with film, and put over the heat. Once the sugar starts to burn, lower the flame and leave covered for 8-9 minutes.

Other details:

name: Roasted Victoria Pineapple
text:
Recipe ID: roasted_victoria_pineapple_merged
Name: Roasted Victoria Pineapple

Ingredients:
- Contreau liquor: 150
- Galabé syrup: 200gr
- Galabé sugar: 50gr
- Lemongrass: 4 pcs
- Kaffir lime leaves: 8 leaves
- Vanilla pods: 4pcs
- Java pepper: 4pcs
- Water: 600gr
- Pineapple juice: 500gr
- Ginger juice: 20gr
- Lime juice: 100gr
- Havana dark Rum: 48gr
- Galabé water: 48gr
- Galabé sugar/ syrup, crushed lemongrass, gratted vanilla pods, chopped kaffir lime leave and Java pepper boil
- Leave to infuse 30mins. Add all the juice and the rum. Cool down, and bag with all the spices.
- Store in the fridge. (Label 5 days)
- Victoria Pineapple
- Butter
- Cooking liquor:
- Stock syrup: 600gr
- Water: 300gr
- Ginger juice: 40gr
- Pineapple: 1
- Stock syrup: 250gr
- Water: 250gr
- Picked mint: 300gr
- Picked coriander: 50gr
- Spinach trimmings: 50gr
- Rapeseed oil: 800gr
- Salt: 13gr
- Fresh pineapple juice: 1L
- Galabé sugar: 50gr
- Agar-Agar: 12gr
- Chopped mint: 5gr
- Chopped coriander: 5gr
- Half a large pineapple
- Mint: a bunch
- Small Victoria Pineapple: 1
- Water: 125gr
- Stock syrup: 250gr
- Base (to make at least 6 hours before use):
- Water: 420gr
- Caster sugar: 300gr
- Super neutrose: 33gr
- Atomised glucose: 220gr

Steps:
1. Remove all the eyes from the pineapple, cut it by 2 don't remove the heart. Blow torch it to get the colour out.
2. For half a pineapple get 50gr of butter in a pan with 1 java pepper, 2 kaffir lime leaves, ½ of lemongrass and half a dry vanilla pod.
3. Cook the butter till he boil then add the ¼ of pineapple and baste it with the butter.
4. Put the pan in the oven at 160°C for 5mins.
5. Take it out and add 100ml of cooking liquor, on a stove keep on basting the pineapple.
6. Put again in the oven for another 5 mins.
7. Check if it's cook. Then leave to rest on a cooling rack tray.
8. Portion and fridge till service.
9. Pass the excess of cooking liquor and keep it for service.
10. When you have a Pineapple away 3 portions of pineapple on a garnish tray with some fresh spices (Vanilla, Java pepper, lime leaves and lemongrass) in the oven 3 mins; baste and dry on a jay-cloth. The roasted Pineapple is meant to be sent warm.
11. Mix all together and Vac-pack. (Label 5 day)
12. Remove the skin from the pineapple and slice it at 2mm with the ham-slicer.
13. Deep it into the syrup and drain.
14. Make sure there is not to much syrup on the slice, cling film and spray with Tennspray a dehydrator tray. Place the pineapple slice on it make sure the are not on top of each other.
15. After 4 hours flip them and dehydrate till fully dry.
16. Leave to cool down room temp and blitz to a powder in the Thermomix.
17. Bag and store in the dry store. (Label 2 weeks)
18. In a VitaPrep, mix all together and bring to 63°C.
19. Poor straight away on a gastro on Ice water.
20. Leave to infuse for 12 hours.
21. Strain thought a jay glove on a steamer tray over-night.
22. Bag and keep in the fridge or in the freezer. (Label 1 month)
23. Bring agar agar and pineapple juice to boil.
24. Add agar agar and caster sugar mixed together beforehand.
25. Bring back to boil and cook for 3min in the Blast-chiller.
26. Leave to cool down in a vitamix and pass.
27. Once fully set blitz in a vitamix.
28. Store in the freezer. (Label 1 month)
29. Slice the hair pineapple with the skin with the ham-slicer at 1.5mm.
30. Cut with the ring of 3, 3.5 and 4.3mm.
31. Vac-Pack in the syrup with the chopped herbs.
32. Keep in the fridge. (Label 3 days)
33. Pick the mint and dry it into the oven or the dehydrator at 35°C 6-8 hours till fully dry.
34. Blitz in the Thermomix to a fine powder.
35. Slice the pineapple with the skin with the ham-slicer at 1.5mm.
36. Deep the slices in the syrup.
37. Leave to drain etamine.
38. Remove all the bits from on the slices and put them on a cling filmed and greased dehydrator tray.
39. Dry for 4 hours at 65°C, then flip the slices and place a plastic dehydrator sheet on top and dehydrate till fully dry.
40. Store flat in a plastic box with silica-gel. (Label 5 days)
41. Mix all the dry together, bring the water to 50°C and add the dry, hand blend till fluid.
42. Bring to 90°C, poor in a container and cool down in the fridge. (Label 1 month)

Other details:


Classifications:
- Dish Type: Dessert
- Cuisine: Thai / SE Asian

raw.name: Roasted Victoria Pineapple
ingredients: Galabé sugar/ syrup, crushed lemongrass, gratted vanilla pods, chopped kaffir lime leave and Java pepper boil
Leave to infuse 30mins. Add all the juice and the rum. Cool down, and bag with all the spices.
Base (to make at least 6 hours before use):
steps: Remove all the eyes from the pineapple, cut it by 2 don't remove the heart. Blow torch it to get the colour out.
For half a pineapple get 50gr of butter in a pan with 1 java pepper, 2 kaffir lime leaves, ½ of lemongrass and half a dry vanilla pod.
Cook the butter till he boil then add the ¼ of pineapple and baste it with the butter.
Put the pan in the oven at 160°C for 5mins.
Take it out and add 100ml of cooking liquor, on a stove keep on basting the pineapple.
Put again in the oven for another 5 mins.
Check if it's cook. Then leave to rest on a cooling rack tray.
Portion and fridge till service.
Pass the excess of cooking liquor and keep it for service.

When you have a Pineapple away 3 portions of pineapple on a garnish tray with some fresh spices (Vanilla, Java pepper, lime leaves and lemongrass) in the oven 3 mins; baste and dry on a jay-cloth. The roasted Pineapple is meant to be sent warm.
Mix all together and Vac-pack. (Label 5 day)
Remove the skin from the pineapple and slice it at 2mm with the ham-slicer.
Deep it into the syrup and drain.
Make sure there is not to much syrup on the slice, cling film and spray with Tennspray a dehydrator tray. Place the pineapple slice on it make sure the are not on top of each other.
After 4 hours flip them and dehydrate till fully dry.
Leave to cool down room temp and blitz to a powder in the Thermomix.
Bag and store in the dry store. (Label 2 weeks)
In a VitaPrep, mix all together and bring to 63°C.
Poor straight away on a gastro on Ice water.
Leave to infuse for 12 hours.
Strain thought a jay glove on a steamer tray over-night.
Bag and keep in the fridge or in the freezer. (Label 1 month)
Bring agar agar and pineapple juice to boil.
Add agar agar and caster sugar mixed together beforehand.
Bring back to boil and cook for 3min in the Blast-chiller.
Leave to cool down in a vitamix and pass.
Once fully set blitz in a vitamix.
Store in the freezer. (Label 1 month)
Slice the hair pineapple with the skin with the ham-slicer at 1.5mm.
Cut with the ring of 3, 3.5 and 4.3mm.
Vac-Pack in the syrup with the chopped herbs.
Keep in the fridge. (Label 3 days)
Pick the mint and dry it into the oven or the dehydrator at 35°C 6-8 hours till fully dry.
Blitz in the Thermomix to a fine powder.
Slice the pineapple with the skin with the ham-slicer at 1.5mm.
Deep the slices in the syrup.
Leave to drain etamine.
Remove all the bits from on the slices and put them on a cling filmed and greased dehydrator tray.
Dry for 4 hours at 65°C, then flip the slices and place a plastic dehydrator sheet on top and dehydrate till fully dry.
Store flat in a plastic box with silica-gel. (Label 5 days)
Mix all the dry together, bring the water to 50°C and add the dry, hand blend till fluid.
Bring to 90°C, poor in a container and cool down in the fridge. (Label 1 month)

name: Basil Pesto
text:
Recipe ID: basil_pesto_merged
Name: Basil Pesto

Ingredients:
- Blanched basil 100 GRAMS
- Grated Parmesan cheese 80
- Toasted pine nuts 100
- Grated Pecorino cheese 20
- Olive oil 80

Steps:
1. Previously blanch the basil in plenty of salted water. Cool it in water and ice, drain it and squeeze it well to remove all excess water. Once you have obtained 100 grams of “blanched basil” add all the other ingredients in a bowl, mixing everything well. Fill a container for pacojet and blast chill to -20. Pacotize everything. Blast chill again. Shortly before using, pacotize a second time and pass it through a fine-mesh sieve with the help of a spatula. Store the emulsion in a pastry bag.

Other details:

name: Brownies
text:
Recipe ID: brownies_merged
Name: Brownies

Ingredients:
- 3 egg yolks
- 100g sugar
- 110g cane sugar
- 170g butter
- 100g chocolate
- 40g flour
- 15g cocoa powder
- 80g walnuts
- Almonds to taste
- Chiboust
- 170g milk
- 125g cream
- 8 egg yolks
- 4 sheets of gelatin
- 30g sugar
- 150g glucose
- 8 egg whites
- 25g ciocco.75%
- Sfoglie di glace
- 500g sugar
- 200g glucose
- 80g flour
- 65g cocoa powder
- Salsa cioccolato
- 50g glucose
- 50g sugar
- 210g cream
- 220g ciocc.60%

Steps:
1. Make a crème anglaise + the other ingredients
2. Eggs + sugar + chocolate
3. Melted + butter
4. Then everything else
5. And finally the whipped egg whites. Bake in Pium molds for 25 minutes at 165°C
6. Boil milk + cream, pour over egg yolks and 15g sugar
7. Whip the egg whites with the sugars and pour everything into the chopped cream + gelatin. Combine with the meringue and freeze
8. Mix everything together, spread on silpats and bake at 180°C for 7 minutes
9. Boil everything except the chopped Chocolate. Add at the end.

name: Sauerkraut
text:
Recipe ID: sauerkraut_merged
Name: Sauerkraut

Ingredients:
- The Sauerkraut:
- Curly kale or Cabbage.
- 2% coarse salt
- The Sour Powder:
- Sauerkraut

Steps:
1. ELABORATION:
2. The Sauerkraut:
3. Cut the cabbage very thinly in the slicer.
4. Add 2% salt and massage the cabbage with the salt.
5. Serve on a tray, pressing weight on top.
6. Ferment for a minimum of 4 weeks.
7. The Sour Powder:
8. Extract the fermented cabbage and wash off excess salt with water if necessary.
9. Dry the sauerkraut for 48 hours at approximately 60ºC. Grind the dry sauerkraut in a Thermomix and sieve until you get a sour and salty powder.

name: Burnt Seed Sauce
text:
Recipe ID: burnt_seed_sauce_merged
Name: Burnt Seed Sauce

Ingredients:
- For the seeds
- 120g Dry tomato
- 60g Fresh garlic
- 6g Alexanders seeds
- 4g Clovegano
- 80g Hogweed seed
- 4g Fennel seeds
- 2g Pink negroni pepper
- 30g White wine vinegar
- 4g Salt
- 4g Sweet Cicely seeds
- 5g Yarrow flower dry
- 2g Tansy flower
- 5g Mugwort
- 2g Meadow flower dry
- 500g Water
- For the cod stock

Steps:
1. PROCESS
2. 1. Toast all separately in the yakitori until blackened.
3. 2. Simmer slowly with the water (2 hours)
4. 1. Rinse the cod skins until clear.
5. 2. Scrape or clean the cod skins depending on the quality
6. 3. Put into a large pan with cutlery on the base to prevent sticking
7. 4. Cover with water 3 parts water to 1 part skin
8. 5. Simmer for 2-4 hours on low heat stirring occasionally.
9. 6. Strain the cod stock through a chintose. Discard the skins
10. 7. Place the skins in a clean pan with cutlery on the base. Always check that it doesn't catch on the base
11. 8. The desired texture is 40 Brix or a light golden colour. This will be about 5 times reduced (approx)
12. 1. Cook until desired texture.
13. 2. Put into paco jets (makes three). Fill to 1/3 as it will airate
14. 3. Spin in the paco jet 4 times from frozen.

Other details:
For the paste: 400g reduced cod stock
All the toasted seeds above

2. Scrape or clean the cod skins depending on the quality
3. Put into a large pan with cutlery on the base to prevent sticking
4. Cover with water 3 parts water to 1 part skin
5. Simmer for 2-4 hours on low heat stirring occasionally.
6. Strain the cod stock through a chintose. Discard the skins
7. Place the skins in a clean pan with cutlery on the base. Always check that it doesn't catch on the base
8. The desired texture is 40 Brix or a light golden colour. This will be about 5 times reduced (approx)
1. Cook until desired texture.
2. Put into paco jets (makes three). Fill to 1/3 as it will airate
3. Spin in the paco jet 4 times from frozen.

name: Roasted Jerusalem Artichoke
text:
Recipe ID: tupinambo_asado_merged
Name: Roasted Jerusalem Artichoke

Ingredients:
- The roasted Jerusalem artichoke:
4 units Jerusalem artichoke
- The Jerusalem artichoke filling:
4 units roasted Jerusalem artichoke
2 sprigs thyme
c/s sweet paprika
c/s garlic oil
c/s salt
- The finish:
4 units Jerusalem artichoke
filled

Steps:
1. PREPARATION:
2. The roasted Jerusalem artichoke:
3. Wash the Jerusalem artichoke with water and drain them.
4. On a grill, burn the Jerusalem artichoke directly over a very intense ember, burn until the entire exterior is black. Remove from heat and let rest in a corner.
5. The Jerusalem artichoke will finally have its exterior toasted and its interior juicy and hot (approx. 30 minutes). Reserve covered with film in a hot cabinet at 60°C.
6. The Jerusalem artichoke filling:
7. Remove the thyme leaves and reserve them.
8. Make an incision longitudinally in the Jerusalem artichoke just to extract the flesh.
9. Store the flesh in a warm place. Reserve the burnt skin.
10. Cook the filling in a saucepan and add the thyme leaves and sweet paprika.
11. Cook the filling until boiling. Add a few drops of garlic oil and season with salt.
12. Remove from heat and compactly fill the Jerusalem artichoke skin with the flesh using two plating tweezers. Reserve covered with film at room temperature.
13. FINISH AND PRESENTATION
14. Prepare an oven at 120 °C and heat the Jerusalem artichoke.
15. Serve hot.

name: Croquettes
text:
Recipe ID: croquette_es_parsed
Name: Croquettes

Ingredients:
- 2 kg croquette mixture
- Panko breadcrumbs
- Wheat starch
- 500g whisked egg

Steps:
1. a) Place the wheat starch and Panko breadcrumbs on a tray.
2. b) In a bowl, whisk the eggs.
3. c) Form the croquette mixture into 18g balls and roll them in the wheat starch to shape them.
4. d) Dip the wheat starch-coated balls in the egg, let the excess drip off for at least 3 minutes, and then coat them in Panko breadcrumbs.
5. e) It is very important not to let them sit for more than one day before cooking.

name: Chicken Broth
text:
Recipe ID: caldo_de_ave_merged
Name: Chicken Broth

Ingredients:
- 10kg chicken
- 300g onion
- 200g spring onion
- 100g tomato
- 100g leek
- 100g butter
- 1l red wine
- 500g hydrated chickpeas
- 7l water
- 10kg chicken
- 300g onion
- 200g spring onion
- 100g tomato
- 100g leek
- 100g butter
- 1l red wine
- 500g hydrated chickpeas
- 7l water

Steps:
1. Mise en Place
2. - Reduce the red wine until you obtain 500g.
3. - Chop the chicken pieces.
4. - Brown the chicken in an oven at 180°C until it acquires an intense color.
5. - Clean, peel, and cut the vegetables into mirepoix.
6. Development
7. - In a large pot, sauté the vegetables with the butter.
8. - Add the hydrated chickpeas.
9. - Add the reduced red wine.
10. - Add the water and bring to a boil.
11. - Lower the heat to minimum and cook gently for 90 minutes.
12. - Remove the broth from the heat and strain.
13. - Cool in an ice bath and refrigerate for 8 hours.
14. Reduction
15. - Remove the fat from the surface of the broth.
16. - Package the cold broth in large vacuum bags in 2 kg portions.
17. - Freeze the bags (they should be flat).
18. - Open the bags and thaw the broth blocks on clean cloths (cheesecloth for straining broths). Reserve for 2 days in refrigeration until all the liquid is recovered.
19. - Rectify the flavor of the broth with reduction if necessary.
20. - Portion the broth into bags with 500g each and reserve in the freezer.

Other details:

Para: 1 l

name: Puff Pastry Fan Cookies
text:
Recipe ID: puff_pastry_fan_cookies_merged
Name: Puff Pastry Fan Cookies

Ingredients:
- Puff pastry: 1 kg
- Sugar: 100 g

Steps:
1. Prepare the puff pastry according to the recipe on page 288, then roll it out into a rectangle 3-4 mm thick. Roll the longer sides towards the center so that they touch, and press them against each other so as to flatten them slightly, then cover everything with plastic wrap and put in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour.
2. When the dough is hard, remove it and cut the roll into slices about 1 cm thick, sprinkle with sugar on both sides, and arrange on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Bake at 180°C (350°F) for at least 30 minutes, taking care to turn them halfway through cooking to brown both surfaces.
3. Serve them as soon as they are cold or store them in airtight boxes, away from moisture.

Other details:
Difficoltà:: ★★★
Preparazione:: 80'
Cottura:: 30'
Dosi:: 30 ventagli
