name: Colomba
text:
Recipe ID: colomba_merged
Name: Colomba

Ingredients:
- 1st Refreshment
- Panettone flour 323
- Natural yeast 323
- Water (47.5% of the flour) 153
- 2nd Refreshment
- Panettone flour 800
- Natural yeast 800
- Water 380
- 3rd Refreshment
- Panettone flour 2000
- Natural yeast 2000
- Water 950
- All of the 1st dough
- Panettone flour 5000 833 1000
- Sugar 1900 316 380
- Butter 1900 316 380
- Egg yolks 1400 233 280
- Water 1800 300 360
- Salt 100 16.5 20
- Vanilla 10 1.5 2
- Colomba flavoring 3000 500 600
- Diced orange 3000 500 600
- Diced citron 1000 166 200

Steps:
1. Remove the skin from the natural yeast and use only the inner part. Knead the yeast, water, and flour. Form a loaf, make a cross cut on it, and put it in a suitable container. Then cover with plastic wrap and put it in a proofing chamber at 30°C for about 4 hours. It is ready when it has reached twice its original volume.
2. Remove the skin from the natural yeast and use only the inner part. Knead the yeast, water, and flour. Form a loaf, make a cross cut on it, and put it in a suitable container. Then cover with plastic wrap and put it in a proofing chamber at 30°C for about 4 hours. It is ready when it has reached twice its original volume.
3. Follow the procedure as for the 1st and 2nd refreshments and weigh 3000 g of yeast and try a loaf on which a cross cut will be made and put in a proofing chamber at 30°C for about 4 hours. The remaining yeast must be wrapped in cloth and tied.
4. The amount of water depends on the absorption of the flour. The amount of colomba flavoring depends on the % indicated on the label of the flavoring itself, for example: 1X1000, i.e. 1 g per 1000 g of product; 1X2000, i.e. 1 g per 2000 g of product
5. Knead the 1st dough with flour, egg yolks, salt, vanilla, add a little water with the colomba flavoring and the sugar which can be dissolved in the water or incorporated into the butter. Add the water and butter to the dough, a little at a time and until finished, then let the dough work, until a veil of dough forms between your buttered fingers, at this point incorporate the fruit a little at a time in 1st speed. Place the dough on the buttered table and weigh the pieces, calculating 10% less than the desired weight after cooking, considering the weight of the glaze and the decoration with granulated sugar. Form loaves and place them on boards without cloth. At the end, take the first loaves, cut them in half and place them in the molds, first forming the wings and then the trunk. Place the molds in a proofing chamber at 25 - 28°C with humidity for 8 - 10 hours, the colomba is ready to bake when it reaches the edges of the mold. Spread with the glaze, decorate with granulated sugar, whole raw almonds and dust with powdered sugar. Bake at a temperature of 180 - 200°C for about 25M for pieces of 500 g, while 40-45M for pieces of 1000 g.

Other details:

name: Milk Sheet
text:
Recipe ID: milk_sheet_merged
Name: Milk Sheet

Ingredients:
- Whole milk 1000
- Liquid cream 70

Steps:
1. Put the milk in a pot of about 30-32 cm in diameter
2. Bring it to a temperature of 70° C
3. Wait about 15 minutes until a solid film forms on the surface
4. Remove the milk film, and with the help of your fingers, fold it in half, obtaining a half a
5. Repeat the operation until you get 4 - 5 semicircles
6. With the help of a brush, coat each milk sheet with the cream, add the film and reserve in the refrigerator

name: Oysters And Tiger Pearls
text:
Recipe ID: oysters_and_tiger_pearls_parsed
Name: Oysters And Tiger Pearls

Ingredients:
- 150 g 	Tapioca Pearls
- 20g 	Dark Soy
- 500g	Water
- 200g 	Tiger Dressing
- 1 Oyster
- 10 g Tiger Pearls
- 0.01g  Fine Chives for garnish
- 0.1 g Kow Choi Oil

Steps:
1. Bring together water and dark soy to a boil and whisk in Tapioca Pearls. Cook for 15-20 minutes whisking every so often to prevent tapioca from sticking to the bottom.
2. Strain through a sieve and run quickly under cold water to stop cooking and wash excess starch.
3. Place in container and add Tiger Dressing to absorb.
4. Steam oysters at 100C for 3-4 minutes
5. Place immediately in Blast Freezer to stop cooking.
6. Shuck open oyster.

name: Sea Urchin And Coffee
text:
Recipe ID: sea_urchin_and_coffee_merged
Name: Sea Urchin And Coffee

Ingredients:
- For the sea urchin
1kg sea urchin
- For the custard
350g Mushroom stock
- 25g Espresso
- 375g Cream
- 4 eggs
- Extras
Lemon

Steps:
1. Using a pair of open the urchin from the
bottom in a circular direction.
2. Clean in water and remove the urchin tongues
into water to rinse them.
3. Remove the tongues and store in container on
grease proof paper.
4. Mix all the ingredients together in cold.
5. Fill each ramekin with 30g of mix and place in
gastro.
6. Clingfilm the gastro and steam at 93C for 12
minutes until just set.
7. Allow to cool in the tray the store in the
fridge.
8. Keep for no more that 2 days.
9. In service place in the oven at 64C for 10 minutes.
10. Once warm place 3 tongues of urchin on top of the custard finish with femon zest.

text:
Recipe ID: turnip_custard_merged
Name: Turnip Custard

Ingredients:
- Amount White soy
- 1x Rice wine vinegar
- 10g Vegetable oil
- Three-corner leek
- 300g Savoury egg custard mature

Steps:
1. Peel the turnip and cut in half. Using the tip of a knife, break the turnip into chunks.
2. Coat the turnip with vegetable oil and season with salt. Grill on an oval tray in the Josper. Cook until the edges are black and the turnip is tender.
3. Dress the soy and rice wine vinegar. Check seasoning and adjust if required.
4. For service, top the steamed custard with the turnip and sliced, ice-blanched three-corner leek. Add a little kow and oil.

name: Black Garlic Broth (Garlic Soup)
text:
Recipe ID: caldo_ajo_negro_(sopa_de_ajos)_merged
Name: Black Garlic Broth (Garlic Soup)

Ingredients:
- The vegetables:
- 150 g caramelized onion
- 2 units red bell pepper
- 370 g black garlic paste
- 350 g mushroom
- 350 g shiitake mushroom
- 150 g chard
- 100 ml soy sauce
- 88 g spinach
- 3 liters of water
- 100 g black garlic paste
- The vegetables:
- 150 g caramelized onion
- 2 units red bell pepper
- 370 g black garlic paste
- 350 g mushroom
- 350 g shiitake mushroom
- 150 g chard
- 100 ml soy sauce
- 88 g spinach
- 3 liters of water
- 100 g black garlic paste

Steps:
1. PREPARATION:
2. Mise en Place
3. Clean, peel and cut the vegetables in mirepoix.
4. Development
5. In a large pot, caramelize the onion.
6. Sauté the peppers, mushrooms, shiitake mushrooms and chard.
7. Pour all the vegetables into a pot and add the black garlic paste and soy sauce. Mix.
8. Cook everything for 6 hours over medium heat, stirring every 15 minutes. After this time, strain through a superbag. The broth will weigh approximately 660 ml.
9. Add 100 g of black garlic potato and mix well.
10. Strain if necessary and portion the broth into bags with 100 ml each and store in the freezer.
11. PREPARATION:
12. Mise en Place
13. Clean, peel and cut the vegetables in mirepoix.
14. Development
15. In a large pot, caramelize the onion.
16. Sauté the peppers, mushrooms, shiitake mushrooms and chard.
17. Pour all the vegetables into a pot and add the black garlic paste and soy sauce. Mix.
18. Cook everything for 6 hours over medium heat, stirring every 15 minutes. After this time, strain through a superbag. The broth will weigh approximately 660 ml.
19. Add 100 g of black garlic potato and mix well.
20. Strain if necessary and portion the broth into bags with 100 ml each and store in the freezer.

raw.name: Black Garlic Broth (Garlic Soup)
steps: PREPARATION:
Mise en Place
Clean, peel and cut the vegetables in mirepoix.
Development
In a large pot, caramelize the onion.
Sauté the peppers, mushrooms, shiitake mushrooms and chard.
Pour all the vegetables into a pot and add the black garlic paste and soy sauce. Mix.
Cook everything for 6 hours over medium heat, stirring every 15 minutes. After this time, strain through a superbag. The broth will weigh approximately 660 ml.
Add 100 g of black garlic potato and mix well.
Strain if necessary and portion the broth into bags with 100 ml each and store in the freezer.

name: Butterfish Croquettes
text:
Recipe ID: croquetas_de_pez_mantequilla_en_parsed
Name: Butterfish Croquettes

Ingredients:
- 280g butter
- 360g flour
- 4 liters 45% fat cream
- 8 shallots
- 700g kimuchi base

Steps:
1. a) In a pot, add the butter until it begins to brown.
2. b) Chop the shallots and sauté them with the butter.
3. c) Once the shallots are browned, add the flour and toast it.
4. d) Gradually add the cream and cook over medium heat, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
5. e) Once the dough is well cooked, season it with the kimuchi base and then blend in a Thermomix for 2 minutes.

name: Rabbit Dumpling
text:
Recipe ID: dumpling_de_conejo_en_parsed
Name: Rabbit Dumpling

Ingredients:
- 112g wheat starch
- 75g cornstarch
- 112g glutinous rice flour
- 245g carrot smoothie

Steps:
1. a) In a bowl, add the flours and a pinch of salt.
2. b) Place the smoothie in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
3. c) Pour the hot smoothie into the flour and stir vigorously.
4. d) Knead with your hands and let rest.
5. e) When forming the dumpling, it is very important that the filling is prepared and cold.

name: Milk Chocolate Sponge
text:
Recipe ID: milk_chocolate_sponge_merged
Name: Milk Chocolate Sponge

Ingredients:
- 620g T55 flour
- 5g bicarbonate of soda
- 4g baking powder
- 14g salt
- 450g butter
- 600g caster sugar
- 6 whole eggs
- 500g sour cream
- 60g red wine vinegar
- 350g milk chocolate, biskella, melted
- syrup 50/50 fructose / water
- 200g apple Juice
- 60g fructose
- Vanilla

Steps:
1. Best the butter, sugar and vanilla on the Hobart for 5 mins, making sure to scrape down the sides
2. Add the eggs one at a time
3. Add the all the dry ingredients
4. Add the sour cream
5. Add the milk chocolate
6. And mix until incorporated
7. Weigh off 500g per large BST mold

name: Monkfish And Pistachio
text:
Recipe ID: monkfish_and_pistachio_merged
Name: Monkfish And Pistachio

Ingredients:
- For the monkfish
- 5% salt brine
- For Pistachio base
- 1500ml chicken stock
- 300g pistachios
- 200g almonds
- 40g miso
- 100g rice vinegar
- 20g leeks
- 30g onion
- 200g white onion
- For the pistachio sauce
- 300g base
- 50g pistachio paste
- 140g blanched spinach

Steps:
1. PROCESS
2. 1. Prep monkfish and brine for 20 minutes (approx.)
3. 2. Leave overnight preferably
4. To cook monkfish
5. 1. Set the circulator to 62 degrees with an oil bath inside
6. 2. Temper monkfish and drop in oil
7. 3. Cook for 44 minutes
8. 4. Remove and roll on the yakitori to dry briefly
9. 5. Rest for 2 minutes
10. 6. Carve in half and brush with burnt seed mole
11. 7. There should be a nice rainbow on the fish
12. For Pistachio base
13. 1. Finely chop the garlic, leeks and onion, then brown them in a little olive oil without browning.
14. 2. Add the vinegar and reduce. Once reduced add the miso and cook for a further 2 minutes.
15. 3. Finally add the vegetable stock and pistachio and cook for 4 hours on low heat.
16. 4. Remove from heat and blend to a smooth sauce.
17. 5. Season with salt.
18. For the pistachio sauce
19. 1. Blend base with remaining ingredients
20. 2. Put in paco-jet
21. 3. Blend three times, pass and vacuum pack.
22. 4. Freeze
23. 1. Break down to smaller pieces and ensure cooked thoroughly
24. 2. Season with salt and the burnt lemon puree

Other details:


Classifications:
- Dish Type: Main Course
- Cuisine: Other
- Difficulty: Level 3 (Advanced / Michelin)
- Main Ingredient: Fish & Seafood
- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Dairy-Free, Pescatarian

- Seasonality: Year-Round

For Pistachio base
1. Finely chop the garlic, leeks and onion, then brown them in a little olive oil without browning.

name: Rapeseed Oil Biscuit
text:
Recipe ID: rapeseed_oil_biscuit_merged
Name: Rapeseed Oil Biscuit

Ingredients:
- 450g unsalted butter
- 400g golden caster sugar
- 700g flour
- 12g salt
- 2 vanilla pod seeds
- 6g baking powder
- 150g rapeseed oil
- 180g egg yolks

Steps:
1. Weigh out all the dry ingredients
2. Beat the sugar with the butter in the stand mixer
3. Add all the dry ingredients, CAREFUL NOT TO OVER MIX
4. Finally add the egg yolks and rapeseed oil and mix till it just comes together
5. Roll out between 2 pieces of parchment paper on the dough brake on NUMBER 5
6. Rest in the fridge

name: Cooked Pears
text:
Recipe ID: pere_cotte_merged
Name: Cooked Pears

Ingredients:
- Kaiser pears 60 brix 150
- Thyme honey 30
- Vanilla 2
- favatonka BE bss
- Cane sugar 15
- haway cocktail infusion 30
- POR 5 anni

Steps:
1. Bring the syrup with all the flavorings to a boil and strain everything through a chinois.
2. Bring the flavored syrup to a boil for 2-3 minutes, add the pears, previously cut into small squares, and cook for another 2-3 minutes. Once cooked, place in a blast chiller to cool. Vacuum pack (each bag with 45g of pears and some syrup) and store in the blast chiller until completely frozen.

text:

Name: Taiwanese Beef Noodles Soup… Version 1.0

Ingredients:
- 1kg Beef; Grilled on Josper
- 100g	 Oil
- 100g	Spring onion, Rough Cut
- 50g	Ginger; rough cut
- 100g	Onion; rough cut
- 75g	Garlic; Bruised
- 100g	Tomato Paste

- 5g	5 Spice Powder

name: Presa And Salted Carrots
text:
Recipe ID: presa_and_salted_carrots_merged
Name: Presa And Salted Carrots

Ingredients:
- For the fermented carrots
- 1kg Sandy Carrots (mash)
- 500g Carrot Juice
- 2% Salt
- Kuzu 4.5%
- For the Presa
- To cook
- 2l Grapeseed
- 100g Grand fir
- Extras

Steps:
1. Rinse well in the sink before peeling
2. Peel carrots and remove tops
3. Re-wash again so ensure no sand remains
4. Juice half the carrots and pass through a superbag
5. Weigh the carrots in a vacuum bag and cover with carrot juice. Add 2% of the total weight in salt
6. Vacuum and leave at room temperature for 4-7 days
7. Once fermented remove carrots and slice to desired thickness/ texture. Store vacuum packed in some of the fermented juice in the fridge
8. Remaining juice vacuum in bags needed per service and freeze.
9. For service add 4.5% kuzu and cook slowly until above 85 degrees and the sauce appears translucent
10. Hold temperature above 65 degrees in the oven or a circulator.
11. Defrost Presa the night before
12. Remove fat and sear
13. Portion Presa at 140g per portion
14. Set oil bath to 60 degrees or circulator
15. Place oil bath inside over a circulator
16. Place in oil bath until even caramelisation
17. Place in oil bath until core temperature is 53 degrees
18. Roll on yakitori and smoke over pine
19. Rest and carve

Other details:


Classifications:
- Dish Type: Main Course
- Cuisine: Other
- Difficulty: Level 3
- Main Ingredient: Meat
- Dietary: Dairy-Free, Gluten-Free, Nut-Free, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Fine Dining / Plated
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

Steps:
1. Strawberry Kombucha:
2. Clean the strawberries and remove the stem, crush in a countertop blender until you get a liquid puree, strain, add the sugar and water.
3. Remove 80% of the old liquid from the kombucha tray and replace with new liquid, save the old liquid for reproduction of more trays, gently place the mother on top of the tray and cover with cheesecloth, store in a warm place until ready to use or until it needs to be fed again.

name: Crab Paste
text:
Recipe ID: crab_paste_merged
Name: Crab Paste

Ingredients:
- 1 kg Chopped Tomato
- 500 g Chopped Ginger
- 500 g Garlic
- 500 g Chopped Shallot
- 200 g Tomato Puree
- 200 g Oil
- 200 g Shrimp Paste
- 30 g Sugar
- 70 g Salt
- 200 ml Oyster Sauce
- 200 ml Crab Chilli Oil
- 40 g Red birds eye chilli

Steps:
1. Saute ginger, tomato, garlic, shallot with 300g oil
2. Add tomato puree, saute again until the oil has risen to top
3. Add crab paste, saute again until the oil has separated again
4. Add Chilli, chopped tomato and crab oil
5. Let it simmer for 2hrs
6. Take out after simmering, blitz everything
7. Put into the pot again to caramelise until the oil gets separated
8. Season now with sugar, salt and oyster sauce
9. Vac pack every 1kg in 1kg bags

name: Tropical Pina Colada (Terrazza 2010)
text:
Recipe ID: tropical_pina-colada_(terrazza_2010)_merged
Name: Tropical Pina Colada (Terrazza 2010)

Ingredients:
- Syruped Pineapple
- Diced Pineapple 1
- Water 1000
- Sugar 500
- Cinnamon Stick 1
- Vanilla Bean 1/2
- Star Anise 3
- Almond Milk Foam
- Almond Milk 200
- Cream 100
- Milk 100
- Passion Fruit Gelatin
- Passion Fruit Puree 350
- Glucose 150
- Water 80
- Sugar 5
- Gelatin 2
- Water 25

Steps:
1. Bring water, sugar, cinnamon, vanilla, and star anise to a boil. Add the pineapple and let it infuse until completely cooled.
2. Mix all ingredients together, pass through a chinois strainer, put in a siphon and charge with two gas charges.
3. Bring the puree, glucose, and water to a boil. Add the agar agar and bring back to a boil, add the gelatin, filter everything and blast chill. Store at -18°C.

name: Leek, Chicken And Chive
text:
Recipe ID: leek,_chicken_and_chive_merged
Name: Leek, Chicken And Chive

Ingredients:
- For the leeks (1 portion)
- 1kg leeks (1 portion)
- For the chive oil
- 50g chives
- 100g grapeseed oil
- For the chicken vinaigrette
- 60g Chicken reduced stock
- 20ml Rice vinegar
- 0.25g Xantana
- For rosemary oil
- 3g Rosemary
- 300g Grapeseed oil
- Extras

Steps:
1. Cut leek tops off
2. Break down leeks to remove some of the green and white parts
3. Char leeks on yakitori until black
4. Peel back to expose hearts. (check if they need more cooking)
5. Trim leek hearts to 6cm
6. Vacuum in neutral oil, 6 hearts per portion
7. Blend in the thermos mix for 3 minutes at 70 degrees
8. Pass and ice
9. Blend together to correct consistency
10. Portion at 25g
11. Pick rosemary off stalk
12. Vacuum together and put in hot box overnight
13. Strain

name: Chocolate Chip Muffins
text:
Recipe ID: chocolate_chip_muffins_merged
Name: Chocolate Chip Muffins

Ingredients:
- Baking soda: ½ teaspoon
- Butter: 190 g
- Dark chocolate chips: 100 g
- Flour: 380 g
- Fresh whole milk: 200 ml
- Baking powder: 1 packet
- Salt: 1 pinch
- Eggs (medium): 3
- Vanilla extract: 1 packet
- Sugar: 200 g

Steps:
1. Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F). Soften the butter at room temperature, then beat it vigorously with the sugar (or using a food processor) for a few minutes, until the mixture is creamy. Then add the eggs one at a time, continuing to mix, and the lukewarm milk in a thin stream: the mixture will be ready when it is smooth, fluffy and homogeneous.
2. In a bowl, sift the flour and mix it with the baking powder, baking soda, vanilla extract and salt: add the dry ingredients to the mixture little by little, mixing until everything is creamy and without lumps. It's time to incorporate 80 g of chocolate chips.
3. Fill the paper liners with the dough up to the edge (using a muffin tin, if you have one), then distribute the 20 g of chocolate chips set aside on the surfaces. Bake for about 20-25 minutes: when the muffins are golden, turn off the oven and let them rest for 5 minutes with the door open, then remove them and let them cool completely.

Other details:
Difficoltà: facile
Cottura: 25'
Preparazione: 15'
Dosi: 12 muffin

raw.name: Chocolate Chip Muffins
steps: Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F). Soften the butter at room temperature, then beat it vigorously with the sugar (or using a food processor) for a few minutes, until the mixture is creamy. Then add the eggs one at a time, continuing to mix, and the lukewarm milk in a thin stream: the mixture will be ready when it is smooth, fluffy and homogeneous.

In a bowl, sift the flour and mix it with the baking powder, baking soda, vanilla extract and salt: add the dry ingredients to the mixture little by little, mixing until everything is creamy and without lumps. It's time to incorporate 80 g of chocolate chips.

Fill the paper liners with the dough up to the edge (using a muffin tin, if you have one), then distribute the 20 g of chocolate chips set aside on the surfaces. Bake for about 20-25 minutes: when the muffins are golden, turn off the oven and let them rest for 5 minutes with the door open, then remove them and let them cool completely.

name: The Apples:
text:
Recipe ID: the_apples:_merged
Name: The Apples:

Ingredients:
- INGREDIENTS: (For 4 people.)
- The Apples:
- 4 apples
- 1 l. water
- 20 g. Cal MEX (Lime MEX)
- 4 kg. lactose (65%)
- 6 l water

Steps:
1. ELABORATION:
2. The apples:
3. Peel apples, turning them slightly if necessary.
4. Prepare a lime bath, dissolving the lime in the water. Immerse the apples in the lime bath for 3 hours. After this time, remove them from the bath and wash them under running water.
5. Prepare a syrup with the water and lactose.
6. Cook the apples in the syrup over low heat, avoiding browning, until they are tender inside, around 30-40 minutes. After this time, remove them from the heat and let the apples cool in the syrup. If they are overcooked, they will not hold their shape.
7. Make a hole in the base of the apple and remove the pulp, leaving only the skin formed by the lime. Reserve.

Other details:

Prepare a syrup with the water and lactose.

Cook the apples in the syrup over low heat, avoiding browning, until they are tender inside, around 30-40 minutes. After this time, remove them from the heat and let the apples cool in the syrup. If they are overcooked, they will not hold their shape.
Make a hole in the base of the apple and remove the pulp, leaving only the skin formed by the lime. Reserve.

name: Semolina Pudding
text:
Recipe ID: semolina_pudding_merged
Name: Semolina Pudding

Ingredients:
- 1L MILK
- 250G POSH SEMOLINA FROM NATOORA
- 2 VANILLA
- 100G EGG YOLK
- 150 CASTER SUGAR
- 2 WHOLE EGGS

Steps:
1. WHISK EGGS, YOLKS AND SUGAR ON HIGH SPEED TO A SABAYON
2. COOK SEMOLINA IN THE MILK AND VANILLA FROM COLD STIRRING ALL THE TIME
3. IT WILL REACH A THICK MASH CONSISTENCY
4. LET DOWN WITH A LITTLE EXTRA MILK IF NEED BE
5. COOK OUT FOR TEN MINUTES CONSTANTLY STIRRING WITH A WHISK
6. REMOVE FROM HEAT AND COOL TO 80C
7. WHISK IN THE SABAYON AND BE SURE TO INCORP[ORATE IT VERY WELL
8. SPREAD ONTO A GASTRO TRAY AND CHILL

name: Sunflower Seed Cracker
text:
Recipe ID: sunflower_seed_cracker_merged
Name: Sunflower Seed Cracker

Ingredients:
- 1000g water
- 300g sushi rice (from JFC)
- Toasted and chopped sunflower seeds
- 100g egg whites
- 60g corn flour
- $0.1g powdered saffron

Steps:
1. RICE BASE
2. using pressure cooker on induction hob
3. bring water to boil and add rice
4. fit lid, bring up to full pressure, then turn induction down to 2
5. cook for 8 mins
6. turn heat off and rest for 10 mins to reduce pressure, remove lid
7. some further cooking will be required, on induction, number 2, stirring unti che water is absorbed
8. remove from heat and allow to cool for 30 mins, stirring occasionally
9. rice should now weigh 1100g
10. blend in robo coupe with corn flour and saffron then chill in fridge until cold.
11. CRACKER MIX
12. robo coupe rice base with egg whites
13. spread out 200g areaker mix onto a silpat, spread all the way to the edges, then with the back oil a spoor, divide mix into 4cm x 4cm squares, garnish each square with 5 pieces of chooned sunflower seed, slide silpat onto a cast iron tray and dry above the doves unt the mix starts to lift of the silpat (should be overnight at least, try and stay 1 day in front and store dried crackers in a sealed box)
14. deep fry dried cracies at .00°C in clean veg oil, drain well on kitchen paper and season with alilitle sat

Other details:

name: Shrimp Dentelle
text:
Recipe ID: shrimp_dentelle_merged
Name: Shrimp Dentelle

Ingredients:
- SHRIMP STOCK
- 500g shrimp
- 200g olive oil
- 1500g water
- 1500g langoustine stock
- SHRIMP BATTER
- 2 Litres shrimp stock
- 140g homemade chickpea flour
- 92g cornflour flour
- 240g olive oil

Steps:
1. Warm olive oil and fry shrimp
2. Cook until they start to colour
3. Add water and langoustine stock

9. HOT PAN

11. COOK UNTIL STARTS TO COLOUR

13. WHEN OIL BUBBLES DISAPPEAR RETURN TO HEAT
14. REMOVE WHEN COLOUR IS EVEN

name: Masala Gravy
text:
Recipe ID: masala_gravy_parsed
Name: Masala Gravy

Ingredients:
- 200 gr dried chickpeas
- 3 bay leaves
- salt
- 50 gr of cashew nuts
- 3 kashmir chilli (deseeded)
- 3 bay leaves
- 2 nutmeg skin or 25 gr of grated nutmeg
- 3 cardamom
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 10 cloves
- 1 tsp fennel seeds
- 300gr onion
- 60gr of garlic
- 300gr tomato
- bay leaves
- 1 tsp cumin seed
- 70 gr butter
- 1 tbsp of chaat masala
- 1 tsp chilli powder
- 1 tsp of coriander powder
- 100 gr dry kasuri methi
- fresh chop coriander

Steps:
1. Soak in water 200 gr dried chickpeas 6 hours before use
2. Then put them in a pressure pot with 2 finger of water from the bottom, 3 bay leaves and a pinch of salt.
3. Place the pressure pot lid, and cook at medium high fire for 4 whistle
4. Soak in boiling water 50 gr of cashew nuts, 3 kashmir chilli ( deseeded),3 bay leaves, 2 nutmeg skin or 25 gr of grated nutmeg,3 cardamom, 1 tsp cumin seeds, 10 cloves, 1 tsp fennel seeds.
5. After 30 minutes strain everything and blend with enough water to obtain a thick red smooth paste
6. Cut 300gr onion and 60gr of garlic and blend them until smooth paste
7. Cut 300gr tomato and blend them until smooth paste
8. Heat oil in a pan, toast bay leaves, 1 tsp cumin seed, once everything its nice and roasted add the onion\garlic paste, 70 gr butter and let everything cook together for 20 minutes until the onion paste will turn golden brown and dry.
9. Add the Masala gravy in the pan, 1 tbsp of chaat masala, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp of coriander powderAdd tomato paste and cook the water off, add 100 gr dry kasuri methi,and keep cooking with 1 cup of water.
10. Last add cooked chickpeas the pot with the sauce and cook together for 5 minute, finish with fresh chop coriander

Other details:
servings: 2 people

Classifications:
- Dish Type: Component / Base (Stocks, Sauces, Pastes, Garnishes, Doughs)
- Cuisine: Indian
- Difficulty: Level 2 (Intermediate / Professional)
- Main Ingredient: Vegetable-Forward
- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Vegetarian, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Family Style / Sharing
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

name: Okra Recipe
text:
Recipe ID: okra_recipe_parsed
Name: Okra Recipe

Ingredients:
- 250 gr of okra
- ½ tsp chilli powder
- 3 tsp coriander ground
- 1 tsp turmeric
- ½ tbsp of (ginger chilli garlic)paste
- 1 coconut shaved
- 1 ½ cup of fresh coriander chopped
- 1 tsp of salt ( to taste)
- 1 tsp of sugar
- 1 tsp garam masala
- 2 tbsp chickpeas flour
- ½ lemon squeeze
- 1 tbsp of ghee
- 3 ladle of oil
- 1 tbsp mustard seeds
- ½ cumin seeds
- ½ tspof hing
- 20 curry leaves

Steps:
1. Wash 250 gr of okra and let it dry ( so it won't be sticky)
2. Mix together: “ ½ tsp chilli powder,3 tsp coriander ground,1 tsp turmeric, ½  tbsp of (ginger chilli garlic)paste, 1 coconut shaved, 1 ½ cup of fresh coriander chopped, 1 tsp of salt ( to taste), 1 tsp of sugar, 1 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp chickpeas flour, ½ lemon squeeze  and 1 tbsp of ghee
3. MIx very well until combined, take a ¼ of the mix and blend it until smooth paste and mix together with the original mix.
4. Cut the top of the okra and mark them in the middle until they open ( as a book), and proceed to fill them with the mixture.
5. 3 ladle of oil in a hot pan, 1 tbsp mustard seeds, ½ cumin seeds, ½ tspof hing, 20 curry leaves, and toast the Okra until properly softly cooked

Other details:
servings: 5 people

Classifications:
- Dish Type: Side Dish
- Cuisine: Indian

- Main Ingredient: Vegetable-Forward
- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Vegetarian, Nut-Free
- Occasion: Family Style / Sharing
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

name: Chocolate Salami
text:
Recipe ID: chocolate_salami_merged
Name: Chocolate Salami

Ingredients:
- Dry biscuits: 300 g
- Butter: 150 g
- Dark chocolate: 200 g
- Rum: 2 tablespoons
- Eggs: 2
- Sugar: 100 g

Steps:
1. 1 Roughly crumble the dry biscuits in a large bowl. Then melt the dark chocolate in a bain-marie, mixing until it is creamy and without lumps, then let it cool.
2. 2 Meanwhile, work the butter at room temperature with a mixer, so that it becomes a cream, into which you will gradually incorporate the sugar, eggs, the now cold chocolate and the rum. Pour the mixture into the bowl where you crumbled the biscuits and mix well. You have obtained the dough for the chocolate salami: now it's up to you to decide whether you want to use it all to make a large salami, or divide it into several smaller salamis.
3. 3 To give the dessert the classic cylindrical shape, roll the dough in a sheet of parchment paper which you will then wrap in aluminum foil. After 3 hours in the refrigerator, the salami will be ready to be served, sliced or whole.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Preparazione: 30'
Dosi: 8 persone

2 Meanwhile, work the butter at room temperature with a mixer, so that it becomes a cream, into which you will gradually incorporate the sugar, eggs, the now cold chocolate and the rum. Pour the mixture into the bowl where you crumbled the biscuits and mix well. You have obtained the dough for the chocolate salami: now it's up to you to decide whether you want to use it all to make a large salami, or divide it into several smaller salamis.

3 To give the dessert the classic cylindrical shape, roll the dough in a sheet of parchment paper which you will then wrap in aluminum foil. After 3 hours in the refrigerator, the salami will be ready to be served, sliced or whole.

name: Quince
text:
Recipe ID: quince_merged
Name: Quince

Ingredients:
- Quince
- Sugar (rough prediction )
- Sugar
- Good Rice Wine Vinegar
- Sugar
- Trims of Quince Roughly

Steps:
1. Peel the Quince, KEEP THE TRIMS and cut in quarter, remove and seeds as if doesn't cook
2. Steam the Quince for like 15-20 minutes until its fully soft
3. Blend
4. Cook it / Quince until fine, weight out the weight and Add the same weight in Sugar
5. Cook further down minutes until puree, remove 1/3 of the quince, that will be your quince puree for the filling
6. Cook further until thick like jam. JAM JAM
7. Take the Trims. Put it in the pan and cover it with sugar and vinegar
8. Bring down to simmer for about 30 min just to counter the quince flavor
9. You can pass the Vinegar now and taste it. It should be full of quince flavor, if its not readd the quince in it and let it marinate

Blend

name: Sweet Potato Filling
text:
Recipe ID: sweet_potato_filling_parsed
Name: Sweet Potato Filling

Ingredients:
- 1100 g Sweet potato
- 100 g Ginger
- 150 g Onion
- 40 g Red Chilli
- 90 g Tong Choi (aka magic cabbage)
- 1 tsp White pepper
- 300 g Miso
- 70 g Spring onion
- Oil (to fry)

Steps:
1. Brunoise ginger and red chilli, onion
2. Chop the tong choi fine
3. In a wok caramelise onion, red chilli and tong choi and set aside
4. In a wok, caramelise ginger and set aside
5. In a wok, cook the sweet potato until golden and soften (not too soft) shape should still be in tack
6. Take out the sweet potato, decant into a tray
7. In the tray, mix miso and sweet potato well then spread
8. Spread the mix over a tray, pop into the oven to cook
9. At 180C, 0% moisture, roast the mix for 10 min, check and stir, then 5min then check and stir, and roast for 5 more min. Check and take out. The sweet potato mixture should be cooked through, slightly dried on the surface
10. Add the chopped caramelised onion, red chilli, tong choi, ginger,and  spring onion, white pepper to the mix
11. Mix well
12. Once cooled, ready to be used for the dumpling

Other details:
Total Time: nan min
Total Batch Weight: 1320 g
NOTES: The sweet potato should not be mushy after cooked.

name: Stuffed Zucchini
text:
Recipe ID: zucchine_ripiene_merged
Name: Stuffed Zucchini

Ingredients:
- Round zucchini: 8 (about 250 g each)
- Ground pork: 250 g
- Sausage: 200 g
- Medium eggs: 2
- Parmigiano reggiano: 70 g
- Chopped parsley: 2 tablespoons
- Garlic: 1 clove
- Black pepper: to taste
- Salt: to taste
- Breadcrumbs: 50 g
- Oil: 2 tablespoons
- Vegetable broth: ½ ladle

Steps:
1. Wash and dry the zucchini, then cut off the top part as if to form a lid (cut them at about ¼ of the total height). Score the perimeter, then make a cross in the pulp and extract it by hollowing out the inside, leaving a thickness of about 1 cm. Salt.
2. In a bowl, put the meat, sausage, Parmigiano reggiano, zucchini pulp, eggs, breadcrumbs, salt, pepper, chopped parsley and crushed garlic: mix well, kneading for 5 minutes. Divide the mixture and fill the 8 zucchini, which you will place in a baking tray or dish lightly greased with oil. Add the vegetable broth as well. In a separate tray, moistened with baking paper, place the previously cut lids instead.
3. Bake at 180°C for about 45 minutes (check occasionally that the surface does not become too dark or burn), placing the tray with the lids on the bottom of the oven. After 45 minutes, turn off the oven and leave the zucchini inside for another 10 minutes, then take them out, let them cool slightly, then serve.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★ ★ ★
Cottura: 45'
Preparazione: 60'
Dosi: 4 persone

name: Plum Petite Four
text:
Recipe ID: plum_petite_four_merged
Name: Plum Petite Four

Ingredients:
- For the Cal X
- 20g cal
- 1 litre water
- For the Plums Powder
- 1x Per Pax
- For the Plums
- Sugar 600g
- Water 1000ml
- Extras
- Fine syrup 10 orange blossom
- ORAGE BLOSSOM SUGAR
- 100 gr sugar

Steps:
1. PROCESS
2. For the Cal X
3. 1. Whisk Cal x into measured water
4. 2. Keep mixing during use
5. For the Plums Powder
6. 1. Peel plums with sharp peeler
7. 2. Retain skins and lacto ferment with 2% salt
8. 3. Leave at room temperature for 4-5 days
9. 4. Dry and make powder for Wagu
10. For the Plums
11. 1. Put the peeled plums in cal x bath and rotate every 10 minutes to avoid the cal sitting on the plums.
12. 2. After one hour remove but retain bath to re-use
13. 3. Rinse plums well.
14. 4. Vacuum plums in 60% sugar syrup with fresh grand fir.(check quantity)
15. 5. Cook in oven until soft (approx. 90mins)
16. 6. Stand in fridge to cool
17. 7. Using tweezers remove the stone from the plums.
18. 8. Stand up right and dehydrate until desired texture (2-3 hours at 55 degrees). (check)
19. FINISHING AND PLATING
20. Mix the puree and the agar.
21. Bring to the boil whisking constantly.
22. Allow to cool and set then blend into a pure.
23. Store in piping bag.
24. Start with a roche of the mousse on the centre of the plate.
25. Create and indent in the mousse with the spoon and fill with the passion fruit pure.
26. Place few bits of the caramelized sesame.
27. Pipe dots around the top covering the passion fruit pure.
28. Take a hot cole from the grill and burn the top of the merengue.

Other details:

name: Baci Di Dama
text:
Recipe ID: baci_di_dama_merged
Name: Baci Di Dama

Ingredients:
- Butter: 150 g
- Chocolate: 150 g
- Flour: 150 g
- Peeled almonds: 150 g
- Salt: 1 large pinch
- Vanilla: a few drops of extract
- Sugar: 150 g

Steps:
1. These fragrant almond cookies, which melt into a chocolate kiss, are so good that when I prepare them at home, I have to hide them, otherwise Lauretta sweeps them away. To prepare them, after forming balls with the dough, put the whole baking sheet back in the refrigerator for half an hour, or you risk not being able to obtain the "boat-shaped" profile that allows the two cookies to stick to each other. The result will be beautiful to see; you can do like me, fill some to give as "party favors" at the end of a dinner.
2. Blend the almonds with the sugar in a mixer, then pour them into the bowl where you have kneaded the soft (but not melted) butter cut into pieces with the flour and mix. Then add the vanilla essence and salt. Continue working with your hands until you get a single dough, which you will wrap in plastic wrap and let it rest in the fridge for at least 1 hour. When the dough is firm, divide it into many sticks from which you will cut small portions (about 6-8 g each).
3. Arrange the balls on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and put everything back in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes. Bake in a static oven at 160° for about 20 minutes, or until the base of the cookie is golden brown (if you use a fan oven, the temperature will be 135°).
4. In the meantime, melt the chocolate in a bain-marie or in the microwave. When the cookies have cooled, using a teaspoon or a syringe, pour a drop of melted (but rather thick) chocolate on the flat part of the cookie, and cover with another cookie. When the chocolate has hardened, you can serve the baci di dama.
5. Per personalizzare i vostri baci di dama, potreste realizzare ripieni diversi dal cioccolato fondente: con del cioccolato al latte, per esempio, oppure con delle creme alla frutta (frutta della passione, limone, kiwi), il burro e lo zucchero a velo. Volendo potete sostituire la farina di mandorle con quella di nocciole.
6. To personalize your Baci di Dama, you could create fillings other than dark chocolate: with milk chocolate, for example, or with fruit creams (passion fruit, lemon, kiwi), butter and powdered sugar. If desired, you can replace almond flour with hazelnut flour.

Other details:
Difficoltà: bassa
Cottura: 15'
Preparazione: 60'
Dosi: 40 baci di dama

To personalize your Baci di Dama, you could create fillings other than dark chocolate: with milk chocolate, for example, or with fruit creams (passion fruit, lemon, kiwi), butter and powdered sugar. If desired, you can replace almond flour with hazelnut flour.

name: Strawberry Paste
text:
Recipe ID: strawberry_paste_parsed
Name: Strawberry Paste

Ingredients:
- 40 g koji oil*
- 300 g halved, red strawberries (ripe and topped)
- 2 g rose root spice mix

Steps:
1. In a pan on the stove preheated to 5 on an induction cook top, add the oil, and sear the strawberries face down until very caramelized and almost burnt.
2. Flip them over and season the contents of the pan with the rose root spice blend.
3. Continue to cook the berries until the other side has caramelized as much as the first, and then remove it from the heat.
4. Pass the mixture through a fishnet line fine tamis and allow it to cool.
5. Then transfer it to a piping bag for service.

name: Béchamel Sauce
text:
Recipe ID: besciamella_merged
Name: Béchamel Sauce

Ingredients:
- Milk: 1 liter
- Butter: 100 g
- Flour: 100 g
- Nutmeg, salt: 1 pinch

Steps:
1. Melt the butter in a saucepan, add the sifted flour and cook everything for a few minutes, stirring constantly to prevent the mixture from sticking. In this way you will have obtained what the French call "roux", the base of the sauce.
2. Remove the saucepan from the heat and pour in the warm milk, stirring with a wooden spoon; then bring it back to the heat and cook until the béchamel begins to boil. Now add a pinch of salt and a pinch of nutmeg. At this point, let it cook over low heat as long as necessary for it to thicken, stirring occasionally.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Cottura: 15-20'
Preparazione: 10'
Dosi: 1.2 kg

name: Krapfen
text:
Recipe ID: krapfen_merged
Name: Krapfen

Ingredients:
- FOR THE DOUGH
- 00 Flour: 150 g
- Manitoba flour: 350 g
- Milk: 250 ml
- Yeast: 25 g
- Lemons: (grated zest)
- Salt: 5 g
- Butter: 120 g
- Medium eggs: 1 + 4 yolks
- Vanilla extract: 1 packet
- Sugar: 50 g
- FOR THE FILLING
- Apricot jam: 200 g
- FOR DECORATION
- Powdered sugar: as needed
- FOR FRYING
- Lard (or oil): as needed

Steps:
1. An external observer could easily have thought that my grandmother had a sweets factory, but she cooked out of passion. Continuously, a bit like I do today, she started preparing Christmas sweets in October, making boxes upon boxes that we then gave to friends and relatives. I was the helper in charge of decoration and I sat there spreading colored glazes. Every now and then a krapfen escaped and my palate sparked. As a Trentino purist, I feel the moral duty to say that krapfen is not a bombolone (and vice versa): the filling is jam, the second is cream; the first is fried already filled, the second is not. I love the first one, the second one so-so.
2. 1 Dissolve the brewer's yeast in half a glass of lukewarm milk with a teaspoon of malt (or sugar). Mix with the remaining milk with the granulated sugar, vanilla extract and beaten eggs with a fork.
3. 2 In the meantime, sift the flours and pour them into the bowl of a mixer fitted with a leaf hook; add the grated lemon zest, turn on the mixer and, little by little, pour in the mixture of milk, sugar and eggs. Work all the ingredients until you obtain a homogeneous mixture, then add the softened butter, previously cut into pieces, in two stages. Work the dough until it becomes smooth and elastic.
4. 3 Place it in a container covered with a cloth, where you will let it rise for at least 2 hours (the volume must triple). Then roll it out with a rolling pin into a sheet about 3 mm thick, from which, with a pastry cutter (cookie cutter, glass or cup), you will obtain discs with a diameter of about 6 cm.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★★
Cottura: 5'
Preparazione: 210'
Dosi: 6 persone

Continuously, a bit like I do today, she started preparing Christmas sweets in October, making boxes upon boxes that we then gave to friends and relatives. I was the helper in charge of decoration and I sat there spreading colored glazes. Every now and then a krapfen escaped and my palate sparked. As a Trentino purist, I feel the moral duty to say that krapfen is not a bombolone (and vice versa): the filling is jam, the second is cream; the first is fried already filled, the second is not. I love the first one, the second one so-so.
1 Dissolve the brewer's yeast in half a glass of lukewarm milk with a teaspoon of malt (or sugar). Mix with the remaining milk with the granulated sugar, vanilla extract and beaten eggs with a fork.

2 In the meantime, sift the flours and pour them into the bowl of a mixer fitted with a leaf hook; add the grated lemon zest, turn on the mixer and, little by little, pour in the mixture of milk, sugar and eggs. Work all the ingredients until you obtain a homogeneous mixture, then add the softened butter, previously cut into pieces, in two stages. Work the dough until it becomes smooth and elastic.

3 Place it in a container covered with a cloth, where you will let it rise for at least 2 hours (the volume must triple). Then roll it out with a rolling pin into a sheet about 3 mm thick, from which, with a pastry cutter (cookie cutter, glass or cup), you will obtain discs with a diameter of about 6 cm.

name: Rhubarb
text:
Recipe ID: rhubarb_merged
Name: Rhubarb

Ingredients:
- 800ml rhubarb juice
- 100ml purple carrot (two purple carrots)
- 100ml orange juice (two oranges)
- 200g sugar (quarter sugar to amount of juice)
- 2g gelaspressa
- 8% icing sugar
- lime juice

Steps:
1. Top and tail rhubarb, keep all bases. Discard leaves
2. Cut into 14cm sticks of prime red rhubarb, any thin pale rhubarb put aside for juice
3. Wash the now portioned sticks, pat dry and grade into sous bag.
4. 10 sticks per bag and set aside in fridge
5. Place all trim in juicer and set aside with 1g of ascorbic acid
6. Juice rhubarb, purple carrot and oranges
7. Place in pan with sugar and bring to 70c until liquid starts to split
8. Skim foam and Pass through muslin twice
9. Cool down over ice and blend gelaspressa and re-pass
10. Season rhubarb with 8% icing sugar for 1½ hours
11. Place rhubarb back in sous vide bag(10) with 150ml of the finished rhubarb stock
12. Steam at 70c for 10-15mins
13. Let cool at room temp for 30mins then chill in fridge
14. Strain rhubarb in a large colander, keeping all juices
15. Place in take aways for service
16. Adjust rhubarb seasoning with lime juice for service

Other details:

- 1g ground clove
- 200g sugar
- MULLED WINE GUM
- 250g red wine

- ¼ Grated nutmeg
- 1 clementine squeezed and added
- Reduce this all to
- Part 1

- 200g glucose
- 150g caster sugar
- Part 2
- gelatine, need to use powder at 30g
- 2g tartaric acid
- 70g reduced wine
- Part 3
- 55g wine (RAW)

2. Warm in a water bath to melt the gelatine
3. Part 1

name: Egg Cream Liqueur
text:
Recipe ID: egg_cream_liqueur_merged
Name: Egg Cream Liqueur

Ingredients:
- Pure alcohol: 75 ml
- Cognac: 25 ml
- Whole milk: 300 ml
- Dry Marsala: 25 ml
- Very fresh eggs: 1 yolk
- Vanilla extract: 1 packet
- Powdered sugar: 100 g

Steps:
1. In a bowl, whisk the yolks with the powdered sugar and vanilla extract until you obtain a thick and smooth cream. Then, add the alcohol little by little, the cognac and the Marsala, continuing to mix. Finally, add the milk.
2. Thicken the cream by heating it in a bain-marie, continuing to stir, until you no longer feel the consistency of the sugar (it will take at least 10 minutes). Pay attention to the water in the bain-marie: it should only simmer, not boil. Otherwise, you could cook the eggs, ruining everything.
3. Let the liqueur cool and bottle it, storing it in a cool and dark place, for example in the refrigerator: the eggs have been "cooked" and the alcohol helps with preservation but, unlike commercial products, our liqueur does not contain preservatives, so caution is never too much. Before enjoying it, remember to shake the bottle to mix it properly.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Cottura: 10'
Preparazione: 10'
Dosi: 1 litro

Thicken the cream by heating it in a bain-marie, continuing to stir, until you no longer feel the consistency of the sugar (it will take at least 10 minutes). Pay attention to the water in the bain-marie: it should only simmer, not boil. Otherwise, you could cook the eggs, ruining everything.

Let the liqueur cool and bottle it, storing it in a cool and dark place, for example in the refrigerator: the eggs have been "cooked" and the alcohol helps with preservation but, unlike commercial products, our liqueur does not contain preservatives, so caution is never too much. Before enjoying it, remember to shake the bottle to mix it properly.

name: J Choke And Razor Clam Sauce
text:
Recipe ID: j_choke_and_razor_clam_sauce_merged
Name: J Choke And Razor Clam Sauce

Ingredients:
- 750G j chokes peeled and sliced on the mandolin
- 820g razor clam stock
- 2 cl garlic sliced on the mandolin
- 100g butter
- 50g grapeseed oil

Steps:
1. don't soak the sliced j chokes in water, have them peeled in slightly acidulated water,
but once you slice them don't go back into the water
2. heat a small rondel on the solid top
3. add the oil and then the slicedj chokes and garlic, quickly sweat down, moving
constantly to avoid any colour
4. add some of the stock to create some steam and cover the pan with a lid
5. now cook the j chokes quickly, keep adding a little stock at a tirne until the j chokes are
soft and break up easily
6. transfer to the vita prep
7. add the remaining stock and blend on high for 2 mins
8. add the butter
9. season with a little salt
10. pass through a chinois and chill over ice

Other details:

name: Shrimp Carpaccio
text:
Recipe ID: carpacio_de_gamba_en_parsed
Name: Shrimp Carpaccio

Ingredients:
- 2 kg frozen prawns.
- Oil.

Steps:
1. a) Defrost the prawns.
2. b) Peel the prawns and remove the intestines.
3. c) Put them in the food processor for 40 seconds at maximum speed.
4. d) When we have a fine paste, we place the paste between plastic wrap with oil and stretch it out until we have a thin carpaccio.
5. e) Try not to tear the film and place them between parchment paper and freeze.
6. f) Once it is frozen, we will make circles with the help of a pastry cutter.

Other details:
ELABORACIONES: CARPACIO DE GAMBA

name: Kadi
text:
Recipe ID: kadi_parsed
Name: Kadi

Ingredients:
- 2 cup of yogurt
- 2 tbsp of gram flour
- 1 tbsp of maida flour
- 1 cup water
- 3 tbsp of ghee
- 3 round chilli
- 12 cloves
- 1\2 cinnamon stick
- 4 cardamom pods
- 1 tbsp of chili powder
- 1 tsp of mustard seed
- 1 tsp hing
- 1 tsp cumin seed
- 1\2 tsp of fenugreek
- 12 curry leaves
- 2 tsp of salt
- 1 tbsp of sugar
- salt
- fresh coriander

Steps:
1. Mix together 2 cup of yogurt, 2 tbsp of gram flour, 1 tbsp of maida flour and 1 cup water
2. Heat a pan , add 3 tbsp of ghee, toast 3 round chilli, after 12 cloves , 1\2 cinnamon stick, 4 cardamom pods, 1 tbsp of chili powder, 1 tsp of mustard seed. 1 tsp hing, 1 tsp cumin seed, 1\2 tsp of fenugreek, 12 curry leaves, and 2 tsp of salt
3. Add kadi mix to the spices pan, with 1 tbsp of sugar and adjust the salt
4. Boil for 3 minutes, and take out the big seeds ( cinnamon, cardamom, and cloves if possible)
5. Serve with fresh coriander

name: Rice Cake
text:
Recipe ID: pastelillo_de_arroz_merged
Name: Rice Cake

Ingredients:
- Fermented Rice:
- 300 g. Yume Nishiki rice
- 270 ml water
- 6 g. koji-kin
- The sourdough starter:
- q.s. glutinous rice flour
- For feeding:
- 350 g. glutinous rice flour
- 500 ml. water
- 30 servings of fermented rice
- 6 g. butter
- The hazelnut cream:
- 50 g. inulin
- 100 g. hazelnut milk
- 20 g. allspice tea

Steps:
1. Elaboration:
2. Fermented Rice:
3. Clean the rice in cold water 8 times, changing the water each time, then soak the rice for about 30 minutes. Drain the rice in the automatic rice cooker, adding the corresponding water.
4. Cook for 7 minutes in "White Rice" program
5. Disinfect the trays that will be used for this preparation with alcohol to inoculate the rice. Place the rice on a surface with water holes. It is very important that we leave a space between the 2 trays so that the rice can breathe.
6. Sprinkle the Koji-kin evenly over the lukewarm rice, which will be around 33-35 °C, and very carefully so that all the grains are impregnated with the koji.
7. Soak a cheesecloth and drain it without pressing. Fold over a tray with holes and serve the rice on top.
8. Cover the rice with the cheesecloth and another tray without pressing.
9. Make holes in this film.
10. Ferment the rice in a hot cabinet at 29°C-30°C for 12 hours.
11. Remove the film and let it breathe for 1 minute. Make holes in this film again and ferment until color is opaque.
12. Note that the rice is wrapped in the cloth and in the trays with perforated film in refrigeration to stop the fermentation.
13. Cut rectangular portions of rice of 4.5 cm x 2 cm in this way.
14. Store the rice in refrigeration for a maximum of 5 days.
15. Elaboration:
16. The sourdough starter:
17. In a glass jar, combine 1:1 cooked white rice and water.
18. Let ferment at room temperature for 3 days or until fermentation generates gas and becomes spongy with bubbles.
19. For feeding:
20. In a glass jar, mix approximately 350 g. of glutinous rice flour and approximately 500 ml. of water (until a firm dough is achieved). Mix this dough with the sourdough starter and let ferment in a glass jar.
21. The sourdough starter acts as a 'starter' for the fermentation cycle in cycles of approximately 48 hours depending on the room temperature.
22. If the dough generates gas and becomes spongy with bubbles, it will be ready for the preparation of the dish. That is, observe the sourdough.
23. If the dough does not generate gas, let it ferment for 24 more hours.
24. It is very important that when we generate a quantity of doughs, feeding each sourdough starter acts on its own.
25. Cut the butter into small cubes and keep them at room temperature.
26. In a non-stick frying pan, melt the butter and sear the rice on one side.
27. Serve the toasted rice cake on a warm plate.
28. The hazelnut cream:
29. In a Thermomix. Add the milk, the inulin and the tea and blend for 10 minutes.
30. Put in a container, cover with plastic wrap and let cool in the refrigerator for 2 hours until it obtains the texture of softened butter.

raw.name: Rice Cake
steps: Elaboration:
Fermented Rice:
Clean the rice in cold water 8 times, changing the water each time, then soak the rice for about 30 minutes. Drain the rice in the automatic rice cooker, adding the corresponding water.
Cook for 7 minutes in "White Rice" program

Disinfect the trays that will be used for this preparation with alcohol to inoculate the rice. Place the rice on a surface with water holes. It is very important that we leave a space between the 2 trays so that the rice can breathe.
Sprinkle the Koji-kin evenly over the lukewarm rice, which will be around 33-35 °C, and very carefully so that all the grains are impregnated with the koji.
Soak a cheesecloth and drain it without pressing. Fold over a tray with holes and serve the rice on top.
Cover the rice with the cheesecloth and another tray without pressing.
Make holes in this film.
Ferment the rice in a hot cabinet at 29°C-30°C for 12 hours.
Remove the film and let it breathe for 1 minute. Make holes in this film again and ferment until color is opaque.
Note that the rice is wrapped in the cloth and in the trays with perforated film in refrigeration to stop the fermentation.
Cut rectangular portions of rice of 4.5 cm x 2 cm in this way.
Store the rice in refrigeration for a maximum of 5 days.
Elaboration:
The sourdough starter:
In a glass jar, combine 1:1 cooked white rice and water.
Let ferment at room temperature for 3 days or until fermentation generates gas and becomes spongy with bubbles.
For feeding:

In a glass jar, mix approximately 350 g. of glutinous rice flour and approximately 500 ml. of water (until a firm dough is achieved). Mix this dough with the sourdough starter and let ferment in a glass jar.
The sourdough starter acts as a 'starter' for the fermentation cycle in cycles of approximately 48 hours depending on the room temperature.
If the dough generates gas and becomes spongy with bubbles, it will be ready for the preparation of the dish. That is, observe the sourdough.
If the dough does not generate gas, let it ferment for 24 more hours.
It is very important that when we generate a quantity of doughs, feeding each sourdough starter acts on its own.
Cut the butter into small cubes and keep them at room temperature.
In a non-stick frying pan, melt the butter and sear the rice on one side.
Serve the toasted rice cake on a warm plate.
The hazelnut cream:
In a Thermomix. Add the milk, the inulin and the tea and blend for 10 minutes.
Put in a container, cover with plastic wrap and let cool in the refrigerator for 2 hours until it obtains the texture of softened butter.

text:

name:
Grilled Pineapple
With Honey And Cinnamon
text:
Recipe ID: grilled_pineapple
with_honey_and_cinnamon_merged
Name: Grilled Pineapple
With Honey And Cinnamon

Ingredients:
- Pineapple: 4 thick slices
- Honey: 4 teaspoons
- Brown sugar: 4 teaspoons
- Fresh mint: a few small leaves
- Ground cinnamon: to taste

Steps:
1. Clean the pineapple and cut it into slices approximately 2 cm thick, carefully removing all the dark eyes and the core.
2. Place the slices on a very hot grill: turn them a couple of times on each side, leaving them on the heat for at least 10-12 minutes.
3. When they have browned, remove them from the grill and, on a serving dish, sprinkle them with a teaspoon of brown sugar, one of honey and, finally, a light dusting of cinnamon.

Other details:

Cottura: Cottura: 10-12'

name: Squid And Thai Papaya Stuffed With Rabbit
text:
Recipe ID: chipirn_y_papaya_tailandesa_relleno_de_conejo_es_parsed
Name: Squid And Thai Papaya Stuffed With Rabbit

Ingredients:
- 4 rabbits
- 2 heads of garlic
- 9 white onions
- 6 large carrots
- 12 vine tomatoes
- 4 leeks
- 1 liter of fortified wine
- 250g of tomato paste
- 3 liters of chicken broth
- 5 Chinese spices

Steps:
1. a) Cut all the vegetables into fine julienne strips.
2. b) In a pan, sear the rabbits that have been previously seasoned.
3. c) In a pot, add the vegetables until they have a golden color.
4. d) When the vegetables are golden, add the chopped rabbit and sauté it with the vegetables and the tomato paste.
5. e) Add the wine and let it evaporate, then pour in the chicken broth.
6. f) Cook slowly, covered, until the rabbit is tender.
7. g) Remove the rabbit and shred it.
8. h) Return the bones to the sauce and let it cook uncovered for one more hour.
9. i) Strain the stew and reduce it, then bind it with the shredded meat.
10. j) Adjust the seasoning with the sauce and 5 spices, and cool for use.

Other details:

name: Peanut Powder
text:
Recipe ID: peanut_powder_merged
Name: Peanut Powder

Ingredients:
- 4 kg Blanched peanut
- 800 g Caster sugar
- 58.55 g Peanut
- 50 g Chilli powder
- 50 g Onion powder
- 50 g Salt
- 100 g Paprika

Steps:
1. Oven 175 C (Rational 0 moisture)
2. In two 1/1 shallow gastro, roast peanuts for 15 minutes, turning every 5 minutes (in Rational**) 2KG shallow 1/1 gastro
3. Cool peanuts
4. When cooled, using the Thermomix lid cup, add 3 cups of roasted peanuts to the Thermomix (should be below the blade)
5. Blitz at Speed 8 for 6 seconds
6. Using a spatula, make sure to get all peanuts
7. After blitzed, add sieved sugar
8. Mix thoroughly to make sure mixture is uniform
9. Keep in refrigerator all time
10. Roast blanched peanut to light golden
11. Rational, 0% moisture, 175C for 6min, 6min, 6min
12. Once completely cool down
13. Blitz the peanut at speed 8 for 4 sec
14. Take them completely out and repeat
15. Add the spices at the end and make sure its mixed well

Other details:
Notes: ***In Convotherm, might need to roast a further 5 minutes

name: Mooga Dal
text:
Recipe ID: mooga_dal_parsed
Name: Mooga Dal

Ingredients:
- 2 cups Mooga dal
- 1 tsp turmeric
- 1 tsp salt
- 1 tomato
- 2 tbsp ghee
- 1 tbsp fenugreek seed
- 3 sliced green chilli
- 15 curry leaves
- 2 tsp hing
- 1 tsp turmeric
- 1 tsp of salt
- fresh coriander

Steps:
1. In a pan put 2 cups of Mooga dal, precedently washed , with 1 tsp turmeric and 1 tsp salt.
2. Cover with boiling water and let it cook until ready
3. Finely chop 1 tomato which it will go in when the dal are at half cooking
4. While it’s cook proceed to prepare THARKA by heating up 2 tbsp of ghee, toasting 1 tbsp fenugreek seed, 3 sliced green chilli ,15 curry leaves, 2 tsp hing, 1 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp of salt, oce everything toasted properly add a little bit of water and pour the THARKA with mooga dal once cooked
5. Finish with fresh coriander

- ½ tsp. Water

Steps:
1. Add all the ingredients to the blender (except the oil and xanthan gum) and mix until it is a homogeneous mixture.
2. Emulsify with the sunflower oil until creamy, add the xanthan gum and mix.
3. It should be creamy, if it is too thick add a little more water.
4. REPLACE EVERY 2 DAYS. CHECK THE TEXTURE AND FLAVOR DAILY

name: Caprese Cake
text:
Recipe ID: torta_caprese_merged
Name: Caprese Cake

Ingredients:
- Butter: 250 g
- Dark Chocolate: 250 g
- Peeled Almonds: 300 g
- Eggs (large): 5
- Sugar: 200 g
- FOR SPRINKLING
- Powdered Sugar: q.b.

Steps:
1. If the almonds are not already peeled, blanch them in boiling water for 1 minute, then drain them and remove the skins. Toast them lightly in the oven at about 200°C, then, when they are cold, crush them not too finely.
2. Break the dark chocolate into pieces and melt it in a double boiler until it is smooth and without lumps, then let it cool slightly, stirring occasionally.
3. With the help of a food processor, beat the butter (at room temperature) with half of the sugar and the egg yolks, then add the chocolate, mix it in, and transfer the mixture to a large bowl, where you will pour in the crushed almonds. Separately, beat the egg whites until stiff peaks form with the remaining sugar, then gently fold them into the mixture, stirring from bottom to top.

Other details:

Cottura: 60'
Preparazione: 20'
Dosi: 8 persone

2. 1. Wrap the oysters in greaseproof paper to prevent tearing when sealed in the vacuum bag.

name: Pastry Cream
text:
Recipe ID: crema_pasticcera_merged
Name: Pastry Cream

Ingredients:
- Flour: 50 g
- Milk: 500 ml
- Eggs: 6 yolks
- Sugar: 150 g
- Vanilla: 1 pod
- Flour: 50 g
- Milk: 500 ml
- Eggs: 6 yolks
- Sugar: 150 g
- Vanilla: 1 pod

Steps:
1. Set aside a glass of milk and bring the rest to a simmer in a large saucepan with the vanilla pod, then remove from the heat and let it infuse for 10 minutes.
2. Separately, whisk the yolks with the sugar, add the glass of milk in a stream, then gradually incorporate the sifted flour. Remove the pod from the saucepan and pour in, little by little and while constantly whisking, the mixture of eggs, flour, sugar and milk.
3. Bring everything to a boil and let it simmer over low heat for a few minutes, until the cream has thickened. Continue to whisk to avoid the formation of lumps. When the cream has reached the right density, let it cool in a low, wide and cold container, covering it on the surface with plastic wrap, which will prevent the formation of a darker crust on the surface.
4. Set aside a glass of milk and bring the rest to a simmer in a large saucepan with the vanilla pod, then remove from the heat and let it infuse for 10 minutes.

When the cream has reached the right density, let it cool in a low, wide and cold container, covering it on the surface with plastic wrap, which will prevent the formation of a darker crust on the surface.

name: Pear And Chocolate Cake
text:
Recipe ID: torta_di_pere
e_cioccolato_merged
Name: Pear And Chocolate Cake

Ingredients:
- Amaretti cookies: 100 g
- Butter: 170 g
- Dark chocolate: 200 g
- Flour: 200 g
- Baking powder: 1 packet
- Pears (Coscia variety): 7 (approximately 700 g)
- Eggs: 4
- Moscato wine: Approximately 350 ml
- Sugar: 100 g
- FOR DUSTING
- Powdered sugar: As needed

Steps:
1. Peel the pears, quarter them, remove the seeds and core, then let them marinate in the wine for at least 20 minutes, or place them in a large pan without overlapping, drizzle with the Moscato and cook over high heat until the cooking liquid has completely evaporated. Meanwhile, chop the dark chocolate and melt it in a double boiler, adding 100 g of butter in pieces. Stir to melt everything and let it cool slightly.
2. In a mixer, beat the remaining butter with the sugar and egg yolks until you obtain a nice smooth and light cream, into which you will incorporate the melted chocolate, now lukewarm. Pour the mixture into a large bowl and mix in the finely crumbled amaretti cookies, the flour and the sifted baking powder. Beat the egg whites until stiff peaks form: once ready, mix in the rest of the sugar, beating for a few seconds. Gently fold the egg whites into the mixture, moving the spoon from the bottom up, to incorporate air.
3. Then divide the dough into two parts. Pour the first half into a buttered and floured cake tin with a diameter of 22-24 cm, continue with a layer of pears and finally, cover everything with the rest of the preparation. Bake the cake at 180°C for at least 40 minutes. Once ready, let it cool in the cake tin for about 10 minutes so that it does not collapse, then remove it from the tin and dust it with powdered sugar. Serve warm or cold.

Other details:
Difficoltà: *
Cottura: 40'
Preparazione: 30'
Dosi: 8 persone

name: Shrimp Fritters
text:
Recipe ID: tortilla_de_camarones_en_parsed
Name: Shrimp Fritters

Ingredients:
- 100g cornstarch
- 50g shrimp powder
- 2 shallots
- 70g green part of Chinese scallions
- 9g fermented shrimp paste
- 250g water

Steps:
1. a) Peel the shallots. Dice the shallots and scallions into brunoise.
2. b) Put the frozen dried shrimp in the Thermomix and grind until you get a fluffy powder.
3. c) In a bowl, add the cornstarch, shallots, scallions, fermented shrimp paste, shrimp powder, and water. Stir with a whisk until the mixture is homogeneous.
4. d) In a small frying pan, put two drops of oil and spread with a paper towel. Once the pan is hot, pour a little mixture and spread over the entire pan to create a thin layer. When it starts to come loose, flip the fritter and repeat the process.

Other details:

raw.name: Shrimp Fritters
steps: a) Peel the shallots. Dice the shallots and scallions into brunoise.
b) Put the frozen dried shrimp in the Thermomix and grind until you get a fluffy powder.
c) In a bowl, add the cornstarch, shallots, scallions, fermented shrimp paste, shrimp powder, and water. Stir with a whisk until the mixture is homogeneous.

d) In a small frying pan, put two drops of oil and spread with a paper towel. Once the pan is hot, pour a little mixture and spread over the entire pan to create a thin layer. When it starts to come loose, flip the fritter and repeat the process.

name: Duck Crackers
text:
Recipe ID: duck_crackers_merged
Name: Duck Crackers

Ingredients:
- Broth for crackers
- Chicken broth 350 grams
- Water 350 grams
- Star anise 15 grams
- Cardamom 15 grams
- Szechuan pepper 12 grams
- Duck crackers
- Duck fillets 260 grams
- Tapioca starch 600 grams
- Fine salt 24 grams
- Broth for crackers 420 grams

Steps:
1. Combine the broth and water and bring to a boil, remove from the heat and add the spices and let it steep for 10 minutes. Then pass through cheesecloth.
2. Blend the duck fillets in a cutter and pass through a fine sieve, bring the broth to 70°C (158°F).
3. In a bowl, place the duck meat, tapioca starch and salt, slowly add the broth and mix with a spatula, when the mixture takes shape, finish the processing on the table so that it is not homogeneous.
4. Weigh 325 grams of mixture and form a roll 10 cm (4 inches) long.
5. Simmer for 1 hour, turning only once, let them cool overnight in the fridge.
6. Cut slices of 0.5 cm (1/5 inch), dry in the oven on baking paper at 65°C (149°F).
7. Fry at 180°C (356°F).

Other details:

name: Quiche
text:
Recipe ID: quiche_merged
Name: Quiche

Ingredients:
- 1000g MILK
- 300g RICOTTA CHEESE
- 6 EGGS
- 200g PARMESAN CHEESE
- 10g SALT
- 350g TYPE 00 FLOUR
- 600g WATER
- 30g FRESH YEAST
- 50g OIL
- 5g SALT
- 450g TYPE 00 FLOUR
- 100g BUCKWHEAT FLOUR
- 100g EGG YOLK
- 15g BAKING POWDER
- 375g FRESH CREAM
- 200g MELTED BUTTER
- 100g SUGAR
- 20g SALT
- 250g FLOUR
- 150g BEER
- 50g SUGAR
- 2 EGGS
- 1 EGG YOLK
- 250g PARMESAN CHEESE
- 250g SOFT BUTTER
- 250g FLOUR
- 2 EGG YOLKS
- 100g BLANCHED BASIL
- 80g PARMESAN CHEESE
- 100g PINE NUTS
- 20g PECORINO CHEESE
- 1 CLOVE BOILED GARLIC
- 80g OLIVE OIL
- 100g SUGAR
- 100g APPLE CIDER VINEGAR
- 40g WHITE WINE VINEGAR
- 250g PEELED PEPPERS
- 50g BOILED POTATO
- 30g OLIVE OIL

Steps:
1. Whisk everything together and fill the quiches after adding the desired toppings
2. Dissolve the yeast in lukewarm water
3. Add flour and oil
4. Knead for 10 minutes with the hook attachment
5. Add the salt
6. Rest for 2 hours in the refrigerator
7. Knead cream, butter, sugar, salt, and yeast
8. Add the rest
9. Fill the molds
10. Bake at...
11. Whisk egg yolks, sugar, and flour
12. Shape into art with beer
13. Add whipped egg whites
14. Knead everything together
15. Form cylinders of 2.5 cm
16. Let rest for 2 hours in the refrigerator
17. Cut 1 cm thick, bake at 170 degrees
18. Mix everything together
19. Put in the pacojet beaker
20. Freeze overnight
21. Pacojet

name: Croquette
text:
Recipe ID: croqueta_en_parsed
Name: Croquette

Ingredients:
- 2kg of croquette dough
- panko
- wheat stars
- 500g of beaten egg

Steps:
1. a) Place the wheat stars on one tray and the panko on another.
2. b) In a bowl, beat the eggs.
3. c) We will make 18 gr balls that we will introduce into the wheat stars, to be able to shape them.
4. d) From the wheat stars, we pass them to the egg and leave them for a minimum of 3 minutes before passing them through the panko.
5. e) It is very important not to leave them made for more than one day.

name: Stew Sauce
text:
Recipe ID: salsa_de_estofado_es_parsed
Name: Stew Sauce

Ingredients:
- 2.5 kg ginger
- 2.5 kg garlic
- 10 kg red onion
- 100 vine tomatoes
- 3.600l cooking water
- 150gr fresh basil
- 32 palm sugar tablets
- 8 botes de sweet chili basil paste
- 14 l beef stock
- 6lt de fino oloroso
- 3 botes tamarindo
- water

Steps:
1. a) Peel the garlic and ginger, slice thinly, and sauté in a pot with oil.
2. b) Peel the onion and cut into julienne strips. Once the garlic and ginger are cooked, add the onion.
3. c) Clean the tomatoes and cut into julienne strips. Once the onion is softened and golden brown, add the cut tomato and continue cooking the sauce base.
4. d) Once everything is cooked, add the palm sugar and the tamarind paste, the sweet chili basil paste
5. e) After 10-15 minutes, add the fino oloroso and reduce it. Finally, add the beef stock and approximately 25 liters of water.
6. f) Bring to a boil and let reduce for approximately 8-10 hours. Once the desired density is achieved, strain through a fine sieve.

Other details:

translated_from: es

name: Laver Bread Cracker
text:
Recipe ID: laver_bread_cracker_merged
Name: Laver Bread Cracker

Ingredients:
- 285g black rice
- 1500g water
- 75g tapioca flour
- 10g salt
- 90g dried seaweed (blended to fine powder)

Steps:
1. Cook With 1000g water
2. Cook WITH 500g WATER (UNTIL CLEAL
3. Using a pressure cooker, add the rice, water and tapioca flour together gently heat up stirring all the time so the tapioca doesn't catch on the bottom once mixture changes from being cloudy its ready to go under pressure.
4. Bring up to pressure hold for 8 minutes on number 2. Then turn the heat off and hold for 10 minutes.
5. Place rice mixture, salt and blended seaweed in robot coupe and blend until smooth.
6. Spread 250g of mix per silpat mat. Then transfer to heavy black tray, then using a palate knife spread mixture out, using a long ruler mark 4cm x 4cm squares.
7. Allow to dry on top of rational oven for 36hrs, they are done when they lift themselves of the silpat.
8. Deep fry @ 190c

name: Lemon And Basil Gelato
text:
Recipe ID: gelato_al_limone_e_basilico_merged
Name: Lemon And Basil Gelato

Ingredients:
- 300g of lemon juice
- 650g of sugar
- 750g of water
- 20g of lemon zest
- 50g of green or red basil
- 5 sheets of gelatin

Steps:
1. FOR THE SYRUP: boil water, sugar and lemon zest, when it first boils remove from heat and add the basil. Let it rest for 20 min. to infuse.
2. IN THE BLENDER: blend apple and lemon juice for 5 min, add the filtered syrup and the gelatin. Pass everything through a fine-mesh sieve.
3. Put in Pacojet containers, freeze and Pacotize.

name: New York Cheesecake
text:
Recipe ID: new_york_cheesecake_merged
Name: New York Cheesecake

Ingredients:
- FOR THE CRUST
- Digestive biscuits: 250 g
- Butter: 150 g
- FOR THE CREAM
- Cornstarch: 2 tablespoons
- Lemons: ½ (juice)
- Fresh cream: 100 ml
- Philadelphia cream cheese: 400 g
- Eggs: 2 + 1 yolk
- Vanilla extract: 1 packet
- Sugar: 100 g
- Salt: to taste
- FOR THE TOPPING
- Sour cream: 200 ml
- Vanilla extract: 1 packet
- Powdered sugar: 2 tablespoons

Steps:
1. As soon as I had the confirmation that I would go to New York, defying the diet, I booked (months in advance!) a table at the Cheesecake Factory. The temple of cheesecake, of all the United States, but also more than thirty variations only "The Original", the best cheesecake, a place. I chose my favorite with strawberry sauce, of an almost moving goodness. Not to sin of presumption, but even my recipe, super tested, is not bad. If you want to make the strawberry sauce yourself, you will only have to blend them with sugar and lemon, a delicacy!
2. Put the digestive biscuits in the mixer, adding the brown sugar and chop them, then mix in the butter after melting it in a saucepan. Grease a cake tin (preferably springform, 22-24 cm in diameter). Cut out a circle of parchment paper of the same diameter as the bottom of the cake tin and two strips of the same height as the edges, then line it. Cover the bottom and sides of the cake tin with the mixture, leveling it well with the help of a spoon, then put the cake tin in the fridge for 1 hour (or in the freezer for half an hour). Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F).
3. In a large bowl, beat the eggs with the vanilla extract and sugar. When you have obtained a homogeneous mixture, add the cheese (if you want, use Philadelphia cream cheese, you can add up to 150 g of mascarpone) and mix until the mixture is creamy and without lumps. Without stopping stirring, incorporate the lemon juice, cornstarch, 2 good pinches of salt.
4. Pour the cream into the cake tin that you have removed from the refrigerator (or from the freezer), level it and bake at 180°C (350°F) for 30 minutes, then lower the temperature to 160°C (320°F) and let it cook for another 30-40 minutes. If after the first half hour you notice that the surface of the cheesecake is getting too dark, cover it with a sheet of aluminum foil. When cooked, turn off the oven and let the cake rest in the oven for 30 minutes with the oven door slightly open.
5. When it is cold, mix the sour cream with the two tablespoons of powdered sugar and the packet of vanilla extract: spread the mixture on the top of the cheesecake and decide whether to bake again at 180°C (350°F) for 5 minutes to glaze the cream, then put everything in the refrigerator for at least 6 hours or put the cake directly in the fridge. The optimal solution would be to prepare the cake the night before consumption and let it cool all night.

Other details:
Difficoltà: media
Cottura: 60'
Preparazione: 30'
Dosi: 8 persone

name:
Basic Recipes
Choux Pastry
text:
Recipe ID: basic_recipes
choux_pastry_merged
Name: Basic Recipes
Choux Pastry

Ingredients:
- Water: 200 ml
- Butter: 100 g
- Flour: 130 g
- Eggs: 4
- Sugar: 1 teaspoon (for sweet preparations)
- Salt: 1 pinch

Steps:
1. Pour 200 ml of water into a saucepan (you can also combine 100 ml of water and 100 ml of milk) with the butter, sugar and a pinch of salt. As soon as the water begins to boil, remove the saucepan from the heat and pour in the sifted flour, stirring well and quickly to avoid the formation of lumps.
2. Put the saucepan back on the heat and, over low heat, stir the mixture vigorously. It will soon become consistent and soft and will come away from the sides, forming a ball. When a white film has formed on the bottom of the pan, remove the dough from the heat and let it cool completely on a plate. To speed up this process, you can immerse the saucepan in cold water.
3. Break the eggs - which in the meantime you will have taken out of the fridge and therefore will be at room temperature - and insert them into the mixture one at a time, waiting for each egg to be completely absorbed by the dough before moving on to the next. This operation can be carried out by hand, with a wooden spoon, or in a planetary mixer equipped with a leaf hook.
4. When you have finished incorporating the eggs into the dough, the choux pastry will be ready. It is perfect if, when letting it fall from a wooden spoon, it forms a kind of ribbon.
5. Put it in a pastry bag with a smooth nozzle, grease the baking sheet with butter (or cover it with parchment paper) and form small round mounds of dough (for the cream puffs), or sticks 5 centimeters long (for the éclairs).
6. Bake for 15 minutes at 220°C (428°F) in a static oven (with a fan-assisted oven, keep the temperature at 200°C (392°F)) avoiding opening it, then lower the temperature to 190°C (374°F) and bake for another 10 minutes, after which you can turn off the oven and let the cream puffs rest inside for another 15 minutes with the door slightly open, to let out any humidity. You can also pierce them with a toothpick, so that the air dries them internally, avoiding seeing them soften once extracted from the oven. When they are completely cold, you can use them for your preparations.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Cottura: 25-30'
Preparazione: 30'
Dosi: 60 bignè

name: Sichuan Chili Oil
text:
Recipe ID: sichuan_chili_oil_parsed
Name: Sichuan Chili Oil

Ingredients:
- 3000g (3L) 		Rapeseed Oil
- 180g			Garlic
- 200g			Ginger
- 30g			Star Anise
- 18g			Toasted Coriander Seed
- 600g			Whole Dried Red Chili
- 150g			Red Sichuan Pepper Corn
- 44g			Salt
- 20g			Sesame seeds

Steps:
1. Toast Dried chilli, Sichuan peppercorn and blitz
2. In a pot on low heat combine oil with bruised garlic, bruised ginger and toasted coriander seeds. Confit until  garlic and ginger is golden brown.
3. Remove from heat and strain, discard garlic, ginger, coriander seeds.
4. Heat oil to 100-120C and add blitzed chilis.
5. Let steep for minimum 2 days.

name: Pear And Chocolate Cake
text:
Recipe ID: pear_and_chocolate_cake_merged
Name: Pear And Chocolate Cake

Ingredients:
- Amaretti cookies: 100 g
- Butter: 170 g
- Dark chocolate: 200 g
- Flour: 200 g
- Baking powder: 1 sachet
- Pears (coscia variety): 7 (approximately 700 g)
- Eggs: 4
- Moscato wine: approximately 350 ml
- Sugar: 100 g
- FOR SPRINKLING
- Powdered sugar: as needed

Steps:
1. Peel the pears, cut them into quarters, remove the seeds and core, then let them marinate in the wine for at least 20 minutes or place them in a large pan without overlapping, pour the Moscato wine over them and cook over high heat until the cooking liquid has completely evaporated. Meanwhile, chop the dark chocolate and melt it in a double boiler, adding 100 g of butter in pieces, mix to melt everything and let it cool.
2. In a mixer, beat the remaining butter with the sugar and egg yolks until you get a nice smooth and light cream, into which you will incorporate the melted chocolate, which has now cooled. Pour the mixture into a large bowl and mix in the finely crumbled amaretti cookies, flour and sifted baking powder. Beat the egg whites until stiff peaks form: once ready, mix in the rest of the sugar, beating for a few seconds. Gently fold the egg whites into the mixture, moving the spoon from the bottom up to incorporate air.
3. Then divide the mixture into two parts. Pour the first half into a greased and floured cake tin with a diameter of 22-24 cm, continue with a layer of pears and finally, cover everything with the rest of the preparation. Bake the cake at 180°C (350°F) for at least 40 minutes. Once ready, let it cool in the cake tin for about 10 minutes so that it does not collapse, then remove it from the tin and sprinkle it with powdered sugar. Serve warm or cold.

Other details:
Difficoltà: *
Cottura: 40'
Preparazione: 30'
Dosi: 8 persone

raw.name: Pear And Chocolate Cake
steps: Peel the pears, cut them into quarters, remove the seeds and core, then let them marinate in the wine for at least 20 minutes or place them in a large pan without overlapping, pour the Moscato wine over them and cook over high heat until the cooking liquid has completely evaporated. Meanwhile, chop the dark chocolate and melt it in a double boiler, adding 100 g of butter in pieces, mix to melt everything and let it cool.

In a mixer, beat the remaining butter with the sugar and egg yolks until you get a nice smooth and light cream, into which you will incorporate the melted chocolate, which has now cooled. Pour the mixture into a large bowl and mix in the finely crumbled amaretti cookies, flour and sifted baking powder. Beat the egg whites until stiff peaks form: once ready, mix in the rest of the sugar, beating for a few seconds. Gently fold the egg whites into the mixture, moving the spoon from the bottom up to incorporate air.

Then divide the mixture into two parts. Pour the first half into a greased and floured cake tin with a diameter of 22-24 cm, continue with a layer of pears and finally, cover everything with the rest of the preparation. Bake the cake at 180°C (350°F) for at least 40 minutes. Once ready, let it cool in the cake tin for about 10 minutes so that it does not collapse, then remove it from the tin and sprinkle it with powdered sugar. Serve warm or cold.
