name: Black Seseme Custurd Bun
text:
Recipe ID: black_seseme_custurd_bun_merged
Name: Black Seseme Custurd Bun

Ingredients:
- For the brioche
- 500g plain flour
- 160g milk
- 40g sugar
- 180g whole egg
- 50g butter
- 25g yeast
- 10g salt
- 3g carbon black
- For the sesame cream
- 90g yolk
- 55g sugar
- 150g milk
- 15g cornflour
- 200g ruby chocolate
- 40g sesame paste

Steps:
1. Warm the milk to 37C, add the yeast and sugar and milk.
2. In the mixing bowl with the hook, mix the flour and the milk.
3. Once a dough has formed add the eggs.
4. Cut the butter into dice and store in fridge.
5. Add the cold butter to the dough a little bit at a time.
6. Once all the butter has been added, turn the speed to mix on medium speed 5 minutes until the dough is smooth.
7. Leave to rest in the fridge over night.
8. The next day weight out 30g balls.
9. Cover the frozen portioned sesame cream with 60% humidity.
10. Prove in a warm oven at 30c for 30 minutes with 60% humidity.
11. Once proved set the oven to 160C and cook for 8 minutes.
12. Remove cool and store in the fridge.
13. Whisk together the sugar, yolks and corn flour.
14. Warm the milk and combine with the yolk mixture
15. Cook out to 82C.
16. Once cooked pour the mixture over the chocolate and sesame puree. Emulsify with hand blender and allow to cool in the fridge.
17. Then pipe into semi sphere moulds at 25g each.
18. Freeze and demold and store in freezer.

Other details:

- Cuisine: Other

- Main Ingredient: Dairy / Egg
- Dietary: Nut-Free, Alcohol-Free

text:

- Water 1300

- For the lemon puree

5. In a Thermomix at 80 °C, combine the lemon flesh, a little water, and the sugar

name: Duck Crackers
text:
Recipe ID: cracker_di_anatra_merged
Name: Duck Crackers

Ingredients:
- Broth for crackers
- Chicken broth 350 grams
- Water 350 grams
- Star anise 15 grams
- Cardamom 15 grams
- Szechuan pepper 12 grams
- Duck tenderloins 260 grams
- Tapioca starch 600 grams
- Fine salt 24 grams
- Broth for crackers 420 grams

Steps:
1. Combine the broth and water and bring to a boil, remove from the heat and add the spices and leave to infuse for 10 minutes. Then strain through cheesecloth.
2. Blend the duck tenderloins in a food processor and pass through a fine sieve, bring the broth to 70°C.
3. In a bowl, place the duck meat, tapioca starch and salt, slowly add the broth and mix with a spatula, when the mixture takes shape, finish processing on the table so that it is not homogeneous.
4. Weigh 325 grams of mixture and form a roll 10 cm long.
5. Simmer for 1 hour, turning only once, let them cool overnight in the fridge.
6. Cut slices of 0.5 cm, dry in the oven on parchment paper at 65°C.
7. Fry at 180°C.

Other details:

name: Chocolate Profiteroles
text:
Recipe ID: chocolate_profiteroles_merged
Name: Chocolate Profiteroles

Ingredients:
- FOR 60 CREAM PUFFS
- Water: 200 ml
- Butter: 100 g
- Flour: 130 g
- Salt: 1 pinch
- Eggs (medium): 4
- Sugar: 5 g
- FOR THE CHANTILLY CREAM
- Fresh cream: 500 ml
- Powdered sugar: 2 level tablespoons
- FOR THE COVERING
- Dark chocolate: 400 g
- Fresh milk: 20 ml
- Fresh cream: 500 ml

Steps:
1. 1 To make the cream puffs, follow the recipe on page 286, with the quantities indicated here. When they are cold, make a hole on the base of each one with a thin object (the handle of a teaspoon will work fine) so that the nozzle of the pastry bag can penetrate it.
2. 2 Then prepare the Chantilly cream by putting 500 ml of well-chilled fresh cream in a large bowl; whip with the mixer until the cream remains attached to the whisk without dripping, then add the powdered sugar and beat for a few more seconds. Fill all the cream puffs and close them in an airtight container that you will put in the refrigerator.
3. 3 In the meantime, dedicate yourself to the chocolate coating: put the cream and milk in a saucepan, when they are about to boil add the chopped chocolate. Stir until you get a smooth, lump-free mixture that you will thicken in the refrigerator. At that point, dip the cream puffs, one at a time, in the cream, cover them completely, let them drain and place them on a plate
4. 4 If you do not want to enjoy the profiteroles immediately, put them in the refrigerator and take them out 15 minutes before serving.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★★
Cottura: 30'
Preparazione: 60'
Dosi: 12 persone

name: Limoncello
text:
Recipe ID: limoncello_merged
Name: Limoncello

Ingredients:
- Sorrento Lemons: 10
- Sugar: 1.2 kg
- 95° proof pure alcohol: 1 liter
- Water: 1.5 liters

Steps:
1. Wash the lemons in lukewarm water and scrub them to remove any residue, then peel them with a potato peeler, being careful not to remove the bitter white pith. Place the peels on a cutting board and cut them into small strips. They should macerate for a month along with 750 ml of alcohol in a tightly sealed glass container (jar or pitcher), in a dark and cool place.
2. After a month, filter the alcohol and add the water and sugar, stirring until dissolved. Let the syrup cool and then pour it, along with the other 250 ml of alcohol, into the container with the infused peels. Seal again and let it rest for another 40 days, always in the dark. After this period, the limoncello is ready: all that remains is to filter it, bottle it, and store it in the freezer before enjoying it.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Cottura: 5'
Preparazione: 30'
Dosi: 2 litri

raw.name: Limoncello
steps: Wash the lemons in lukewarm water and scrub them to remove any residue, then peel them with a potato peeler, being careful not to remove the bitter white pith. Place the peels on a cutting board and cut them into small strips. They should macerate for a month along with 750 ml of alcohol in a tightly sealed glass container (jar or pitcher), in a dark and cool place.

After a month, filter the alcohol and add the water and sugar, stirring until dissolved. Let the syrup cool and then pour it, along with the other 250 ml of alcohol, into the container with the infused peels. Seal again and let it rest for another 40 days, always in the dark. After this period, the limoncello is ready: all that remains is to filter it, bottle it, and store it in the freezer before enjoying it.

name: Shrimp Broth Toasted
text:
Recipe ID: caldo_de_gamba_tostadas_merged
Name: Shrimp Broth Toasted

Ingredients:
- 1 kg shrimp
- 1/2 onion
- 5 cloves garlic
- 400 g white wine
- 2 bay leaves
- Salt
- 1 kg shrimp
- 1/2 onion
- 5 cloves garlic
- 400 g white wine
- 2 bay leaves
- Salt

Steps:
1. In a pot, cook the julienned onion and sliced garlic until the onion is caramelized. Add the bay leaves and a little salt. Deglaze with the wine, continue cooking until the alcohol evaporates.
2. In another pot with plenty of olive oil, toast (DO NOT BURN) the shrimp on both sides. Finish by crushing the shrimp heads to release their juices. Deglaze with white wine. Add the caramelized vegetables and integrate all the elements, stirring with a spatula.
3. Cover with water just to the level of the ingredients and bring to a boil. Skim off any foam and cook over low heat with a cartouche on top for 2 hours. Microfilter and reduce to ¿.ºBrix. Strain, cool, degrease, portion, and store.
4. In a pot, cook the julienned onion and sliced garlic until the onion is caramelized. Add the bay leaves and a little salt. Deglaze with the wine, continue cooking until the alcohol evaporates.
5. In another pot with plenty of olive oil, toast (DO NOT BURN) the shrimp on both sides. Finish by crushing the shrimp heads to release their juices. Deglaze with white wine. Add the caramelized vegetables and integrate all the elements, stirring with a spatula.
6. Cover with water just to the level of the ingredients and bring to a boil. Skim off any foam and cook over low heat with a cartouche on top for 2 hours. Microfilter and reduce to ¿.ºBrix. Strain, cool, degrease, portion, and store.

name: Classic Tiger'S Milk
text:
Recipe ID: leche_de_tigre_clasica_merged
Name: Classic Tiger'S Milk

Ingredients:
- 1500 gr. Lime Juice
- 400 gr. Fish Trimmings (corvina)
- 900 gr. Fish Stock
- 225 gr. Figuera Onion
- 15 gr. Ginger
- 150 gr. Celery
- 2-2.5 Ud. Habanero
- 95 gr. Salt with Cilantro Leaves
- 90 gr. Garlic
- 450 gr. Ice

Steps:
1. In a container, add all the ingredients to a blender.
2. Blend for 30 seconds per batch, without overfilling, and mix until everything is combined.
3. In the last batch (preferably only liquid), add the cilantro stems and the habanero without seeds or veins. Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve, pressing well, and set aside the portion intended for service.

name: Chickpea Curry
text:
Recipe ID: chickpea_curry_parsed
Name: Chickpea Curry

Ingredients:
- ½ kg of chickpeas
- salt
- 4 tbsp of oil
- 1 cinnamon stick
- 4 bay leaves
- 4 chop garlic
- 10 gr of chopped ginger
- 3 chopped green chilli
- 3 chopped blonde onion
- 2 tsp chilli powder
- 1 ½ tsp turmeric
- 3 tsp of coriander powder
- 3 tbsp of chana masala powder ( or masala powder)
- 4 big tomato
- 1 tbsp of garam masala powder
- 1 tsp of cumin powder
- fresh coriander

Steps:
1. In a pressure pot cook until 5 whistles ½ kg of chickpeas with a little bit of salt
2. Heat 4 tbsp of oil and toast 1 cinnamon stick, 4 bay leaves, 4 chop garlic , 10 gr of chopped ginger, 3 chopped green chilli, 3 chopped blonde onion; toast everything together
3. Add 2 tsp chilli powder, 1 ½ tsp turmeric, 3 tsp of coriander powder,3 tbsp of chana masala powder ( or masala powder)
4. Blend 4 big tomato for create a smooth paste, and add in the pan and bring to the boil and add salt to taste
5. Add 1 tbsp of garam masala powder and and the cook chickpeas and slightly crush them in the pot and let them cook all together for 10 more minutes
6. Finish with 1 tsp of cumin powder and fresh coriander

Other details:
servings: 8 people

- Dish Type: Main Course
- Cuisine: Indian
- Difficulty: Level 1

- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Dairy-Free, Nut-Free, Vegan, Vegetarian, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Casual / Comfort

- Seasonality: Year-Round

name: Beetroot With Yuzu
text:
Recipe ID: beetroot_with_yuzu_merged
Name: Beetroot With Yuzu

Ingredients:
- For the salt dough
- 500g flour
- 250g egg white
- 25g olive oil
- 150g table salt
- For the Beetroot
- 2kg Cylindra beetroots
- 1kg salt dough
- For the Ponzu
- 350g Water
- 15g Ginger
- 10g Garlic
- 40g Yuzu juice
- 40g Mirin
- 100g Soy sauce
- 5g Powdered dashi
- 10g Lemon juice
- For Hollandaise
- 50g Butter
- 120g egg yolk

Steps:
1. PROCESS

text:

Name: Squid Dressing

Ingredients:
- 300g chopped spring onion
- 200g Ginger
- 150g garlic
- 100g sherry vinegar
- 100g white wine
- 50g olive oil

Steps:
1. a) Chop all the vegetables into a brunoise (small dice).
2. b) Place the olive oil in a frying pan and heat it.
3. c) Add the vegetables to the oil and cook until they take on a golden color.
4. d) Then add the white wine and let it evaporate slightly.
5. e) Continue with the sherry vinegar and season with salt.
6. f) This preparation can be made in large quantities and stored.

name: Shrimp Recipe
text:
Recipe ID: shrimp_recipe_parsed
Name: Shrimp Recipe

Ingredients:
- 1 ladle of oil
- 2 tsp of fennel seeds
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 6 kerala chilli
- 2 kashmiri chilli
- 400gr of shaved coconut
- ½ tsp of peppercorn
- ½ red onion sliced thin
- 1 tomato dice
- 1 tsp of turmeric
- 1 cup of water
- 2 pz kokum
- 2 cups of water (for kokum water)
- 1 tsp of black salt
- 2 cup of kokum water
- 2 cup of normal water
- ½ kg of shrimps
- 1 ladle of oil (for tarka)
- 1 tbsp of ghee (for tarka)
- 1 chop onion (for tarka)
- 4 curry leaves (for tarka)
- 1 tsp of kashmiri chilli powder (for tarka)

Steps:
1. Heat 1 ladle of oil, then add 2 tsp of fennel seeds, 1 tsp cumin seeds, 6 kerala chilli , 2 kashmiri chilli, 400gr of shaved coconut and ½ tsp of peppercorn, keep toasting for 1 minute and add ½ red onion sliced thin, 1 tomato dice
2. Keep toasting and after putting 1 tsp of turmeric, blend everything together with 1 cup of water
3. Prepare kokum water ( 2 pz and 2 cups of water) or imli water until the right acidity to 1qtaste.
4. After obtain a smooth paste add 1 tsp of black salt, and bring to simmer and add roughly 2 cup of kokum water and 2 cup of normal water for semi/liquid texture
5. After the ingredients comes up together and the sauce it's nice and tasty insert  ½ kg of shrimps and bring to the boil
6. Prepare tarka by heating up in a pan 1 ladle of oil,1 tbsp of ghee tost 1 chop onion, and 4 curry leaves , and finish with1 tsp of  kashmiri chilli powder and add it back to the masala

Other details:


Classifications:
- Dish Type: Main Course
- Cuisine: Indian
- Difficulty: Level 2 (Intermediate / Professional)
- Main Ingredient: Fish & Seafood
- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Dairy-Free, Nut-Free, Pescatarian, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Family Style / Sharing
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

name: Socarrat
text:
Recipe ID: socarrat_es_parsed
Name: Socarrat

Ingredients:
- 1 head of garlic
- 1 liter white wine
- 3 large white onions
- Choricero pepper
- 8 roasted tomatoes
- Saffron
- 2 liters chicken broth
- 1 liter water
- 2 kg small grey shrimp
- 2 kg mantis shrimp

Steps:
1. Roast the tomatoes in the oven
2. Peel the garlic and blend in a Thermomix
3. Peel the onion and blend in a Thermomix
4. In a pot, brown the garlic first, then the onion, and finally add the crushed tomato
5. Roast the shrimp and mantis shrimp in the oven
6. Once the vegetables are sautéed, incorporate the shrimp, mantis shrimp, and the choricero pepper.
7. Add the white wine and reduce
8. Add the chicken broth, water, and saffron threads
9. Bring to a boil and cook for 25 minutes covered and 25 minutes uncovered

Other details:

text:

Ingredients:

Steps:
1. Custard:
2. Bring milk, cream to 50°C and add sugar and pectine then cook to 90°C
3. Pour a third of the milk over the yolks.
4. Return everything to the pan
5. Cook to 85°C.
6. Pass.
7. Crèmeux:
8. Cut and melt the custard with the chocolate and the soaked gelatine and blend with hand blender.
9. Add salt.
10. Pass and poor into a plastic container.
11. Wrap with Clingfilm on contact.
12. Leave to set in the fridge for 1 day before use. (Label 5 days)

name: Rock Sugar Syrup And Granita
text:
Recipe ID: rock_sugar_syrup_and_granita_parsed
Name: Rock Sugar Syrup And Granita

Ingredients:
- 454 g Rock Sugar
- 2 L Warm water

Steps:
1. Preheat a large surface pot
2. Put the Rock sugar into the pot to melt, spread them out evenly
3. Keep turning the pan so it doesn't burn
4. DO NOT leave the pan whilst doing
5. Once all sugar's melted, add the measured warm water
6. Bring the syrup up to boil, make sure all sugar's dissolved
7. Decant into a containers to chill
8. Take what you need to freeze, the rest keep as sugar syrup
9. DO NOT leave the pan during the process
10. Freeze in small topperware
11. Once frozen, take out of container to blitz in thermomix quickly
12. Put back to freeze, otherwise the sugar sink to the bottom

name: Miso Paste
text:
Recipe ID: pasta_de_miso_en_parsed
Name: Miso Paste

Ingredients:
- 500g reduced mirin
- 500g reduced sake
- 2kg white miso
- 1kg sugar

Steps:
1. a) The first thing we will do is reduce the mirin and sake separately. When we talk about reducing, I mean going from one liter to half.
2. b) Once we have the reduced liquids, we weigh them and mix everything in a bowl. We place it in a bain-marie and caramelize it slowly for about 6 hours, until we get a paste.
3. c) Finally, we will introduce it into the Thermomix and leave it as smooth as possible.

Other details:

name: Crispy Chicken Skins:
text:
Recipe ID: crispy_chicken_skins:_merged
Name: Crispy Chicken Skins:

Ingredients:
- Crispy Chicken Skins:
600 g. chicken skins
- Las pieles de pollo secas:
600 g. pieles de pollo

Steps:
1. PREPARATION:
2. Crispy Chicken Skins:
3. Remove excess fat from the chicken skins with a triangular spatula, being careful not to tear them.
4. Spread the skins completely flat at 65ºC for 12 hours.
5. Dehydrate the skins, trim the corners and store in a dry place.
6. The Empty Breast:
7. In a pot, heat the oil to 180ºC.
8. Place the breast mold in the hot oil.
9. Using two tongs, place the chicken skin in the hot oil, stretching it as it cooks. Discard any skins that do not stretch.
10. Mochuga: Press the breast with the counter-mold to shape the skin, pressing the counter-mold firmly.
11. Remove the skin from the mold and trim the tips with scissors. Store in a hot cupboard.
12. Chicken Glaze:
13. Dissolve the kuzu in the cold broth. Kuzify over medium heat, stirring constantly with a spatula.
14. Cover with plastic wrap in contact to prevent the formation of starchy skins.
15. FINISHING AND PRESENTATION
16. Serve a drop of chicken glaze.
17. Cover with the crispy empty breast.
18. ELABORACIÓN:
19. Las pieles de pollo secas:
20. Quitar el exceso de grasa a las pieles de pollo con un a espátula triangular teniendo cuidado de no romperlas.
21. Estirar las pieles completamente estiradas a 65ºC durante 12 horas.
22. Deshidratar las pieles, recortar las esquinas y reservar en un lugar seco.
23. La pechuga vacía:
24. En una olla calentar el aceite a 180ºC.
25. Introducir el molde de la pechuga en el aceite caliente.
26. Con ayuda de dos pinzas introducir la piel del pollo en el aceite caliente, estirándola a medida que va sufriendo. Descartar pieles que no estiren.
27. Mochuga: la pechuga pisándola con el contramolde de la dar forma a la piel presionando fuertemente el contramolde.
28. Retirar la piel del molde y recortar las puntas con unas tijeras. Reservar en armario caliente.
29. Glaseo de gallina:
30. Disolver el kuzu en el caldo frío. Kuzificar a fuego medio sin parar de remover con una espátula.
31. Reservar con un plástico film en contacto para evitar la formación de pieles almidonosas.
32. ACABADO Y PRESENTACIÓN
33. Servir una gota de glaseo de gallina.
34. Cubrirla con la pechuga vacía crujiente.

Other details:
La pechuga vacía:: Pieles de pollo secas
Aceite de girasol
Glaseo de gallina:: Caldo de gallina 25ºBrix
(partida de caldos)
6% Kuzu
El acabado:: 4 u. pechuga vacía
Caldo de gallina

name: Rice Chips
text:
Recipe ID: rice_chips_merged
Name: Rice Chips

Ingredients:
- Japanese sushi rice 200g
- Mirin 50g
- Soy sauce 30g
- Mineral water 150g

Steps:
1. Wash the rice, leave it in a chinois under the pressure of cold water making sure that the water rises with a certain transparency from the chinois; drain well
2. Put the rice in a steam cooking bag together with all the listed ingredients and vacuum pack (completely)
3. Steam in the oven at 100°C or in boiling water for about 25 minutes, then cool
4. Open the bag and cut into slices
5. Fry in oil at 140°C, giving it a golden color, dry and serve

name: Cardamom Bechamel
text:
Recipe ID: bechamel_de_cardamomo_en_parsed
Name: Cardamom Bechamel

Ingredients:
- 1 kg flour
- 1 kg butter
- 10 liters cow's milk
- Ground cardamom
- Salt

Steps:
1. a) The first step is to put the milk in a saucepan to heat up and prevent lumps from forming.
2. b) In a pot, we make the roux without letting it brown too much, as we want it very white.
3. c) Once the roux is ready, we add the milk and with a quick whisking motion, we cook the béchamel.
4. d) Once it is smooth, we season it with salt and cardamom.
5. e) Finally, we strain it through a fine-mesh sieve and chill it.

Other details:

name: Sichuan Red Oil
text:
Recipe ID: sichuan_red_oil_merged
Name: Sichuan Red Oil

Ingredients:
- Rapeseed oil 3000 ml
- Garlic 180 g
- Ginger 200 g
- Star anise 30 g
- Coriander seed 60 g
- Whole dried chilli 600 g
- Sichuan pepper corn 150 g
- Salt 2 tbsp
- Sesame seeds 2 tbsp

Steps:
1. Toast coriander seeds for 3 mins
2. On upstairs induction, infuse oil, garlic, ginger (don't burn), star anise and coriander seeds on low heat for 30 mins
3. When garlic is browned, strain and throw solids
4. Heat the strained oil up on high heat to 100-120°C
5. Toast dried chilli and Sichuan pepper corn
6. Blitz dried chilli and pepper corn
7. Pour oil into the chilli/Sichuan mix
8. Leave to sleep for minimum of 2 days

Other details:
Notes: Temp for oil needs to be right. NOT to burn anything. Watch when infusing

name: Blood Orange Jam
text:
Recipe ID: blood_orange_jam_merged
Name: Blood Orange Jam

Ingredients:
- 1kg blood orange boirion juice
- 660g caster sugar
- 200g glucose
- 30g yellow pectin
- 15g water
- 15g citric acid
- 8 blood orange zested

Steps:
1. Mix the pectin with the suagr
2. Bring the lime juice to the boil
3. Whisk in the sugar, pectin and glucose mix Continue to boil/simmer till you reach 107 Celsius
4. Then add the acid mix and set in a pacojet beaker
5. Chill till set and blend in the pacojet
6. Stir in the microplaned zest of 8 blood orange

text:

Ingredients:

- 20g Maldon Salt
- 5g Sugar
- 10g Ginger
- 10g Garlic
- 1g Chilli

Steps:
1. Rule of thumb: Weigh kohlrabi and for each: 1 kg kohlrabi season with 2% Maldon Salt, 0.5% sugar, 30g piece of peeled sliced ginger, 10g garlic, 1 pc of seedless long chilli (check variety of chili with senior team)

name: Barley Cooked In Syrup
text:
Recipe ID: barley_cooked_in_syrup_parsed
Name: Barley Cooked In Syrup

Ingredients:
- Pearl Barley
- Water
- Rock Sugar

Steps:
1. The night before, cover barley with water and bring to boil
2. Once boiled, skim and discard impurities
3. Drain and vac pack the barley. Then put to FREEZE
4. Next day, 1:4 pearl barley to water ratio
5. Put into the pressure cooker to cook until soften
6. The pre boil and freezing process help shortening the cooking time
7. Once all soft, reduce, or drain the liquid to it just covers the barley
8. Add rock sugar, taste
9. Store in fridge. Do not pre cook a big batch
10. Can pre cook and freeze the first process big batch
11. Cook daily or every other day for the second half of the process

Other details:
Total Time: nan

name:
Basic Recipes
Puff Pastry
text:
Recipe ID: basic_recipes
puff_pastry_merged
Name: Basic Recipes
Puff Pastry

Ingredients:
- FOR THE DOUGH
- Manitoba flour: 150 g
- 00 flour: 200 g
- Water: about 200 ml
- Salt: 8 g
- FOR THE BUTTER BLOCK
- Butter: 250 g
- 00 flour: 75 g

Steps:
1. Start with the preparation of the dough. Mix and sift the two flours, preferably in a stand mixer with a leaf hook, or by hand. Dissolve the salt in the water and knead everything at medium speed for about 8 minutes: you must obtain a mixture that is softer and more elastic than that of bread and, of course, smooth and homogeneous. Let it rest, covered with a cloth, in a cool place for at least half an hour.
2. In the meantime, take care of the butter block. Take the butter out of the fridge, cut it into cubes and put it in the stand mixer with the 75 g of flour: mix until you get a blended and lump-free mixture, which you will reduce to a square about 1 centimeter thick, either with floured hands or by placing it between two sheets of parchment paper and flattening it with the help of a rolling pin. Place the butter block in the lower part of the fridge - the coldest - for at least half an hour.
3. Roll out the dough: you will get a rectangle of about 50 centimeters in length in the center of which you will place the butter block, now ready (1), which you will cover with the two longest edges of the dough, making them match (2). Fold the other two as well, until you close the butter block on each side (3). Always remember to flour the work surface and the dough to facilitate the rolling process.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★★
Preparazione: 130'
Dosi: 1 kg di pasta circa
original_language: it

Classifications:
- Dish Type: Component / Base (Stocks, Sauces, Pastes, Garnishes, Doughs)
- Cuisine: French
- Difficulty: Level 2 (Intermediate / Professional)
- Main Ingredient: Starch/Grain (Pasta, Rice, Bread)
- Dietary: Vegetarian, Nut-Free, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Family Style / Sharing
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

Basic Recipes
Puff Pastry

Dissolve the salt in the water and knead everything at medium speed for about 8 minutes: you must obtain a mixture that is softer and more elastic than that of bread and, of course, smooth and homogeneous. Let it rest, covered with a cloth, in a cool place for at least half an hour.

In the meantime, take care of the butter block. Take the butter out of the fridge, cut it into cubes and put it in the stand mixer with the 75 g of flour: mix until you get a blended and lump-free mixture, which you will reduce to a square about 1 centimeter thick, either with floured hands or by placing it between two sheets of parchment paper and flattening it with the help of a rolling pin. Place the butter block in the lower part of the fridge - the coldest - for at least half an hour.

Roll out the dough: you will get a rectangle of about 50 centimeters in length in the center of which you will place the butter block, now ready (1), which you will cover with the two longest edges of the dough, making them match (2). Fold the other two as well, until you close the butter block on each side (3). Always remember to flour the work surface and the dough to facilitate the rolling process.

name: Yellow Bean Garlic
text:
Recipe ID: yellow_bean_garlic_merged
Name: Yellow Bean Garlic

Ingredients:
- 750 g Garlic
- 500 g Rapeseed oil
- 10 tsp Yellow bean
- 1.25 tsp Sugar
- 2.5 tsp Salt

Steps:
1. Robo peeled garlic (keep an eye, it will cut to size v quick)
2. Run under water and drain well
3. Place a layer of J cloth on a rack, put tray underneath and spread garlic over - let dry for a good time
4. Insert butterfly attachment to Thermomix
5. Put oil and drained garlic into Thermomix
6. Keep lid off
7. Turn heat to 120°C, speed Stir, for at least 10 mins. Taste
8. Add yellow bean paste, keep stirring
9. Season with salt and sugar

Other details:
Total Time: 32 min
Notes: Garlic size very important - no bigger than the dot
The consistency should be confit, but not mushed.
Add more rapeseed oil if not quite well covered
Use: Use 2L/week
Make 1L at a time

name: Duck Foie Gras
text:
Recipe ID: duck_foie_gras_merged
Name: Duck Foie Gras

Ingredients:
- Duck Foie Gras
- Butter 1200
- Whole Eggs 400
- Pink Salt 960
- The Marinade
- Red Port 600
- Biara Port 600
- White Port 300
- Brandy 200
- Very Fine Shallots 400
- Very Fine Garlic Cloves n. 4
- Thyme 1 bunch

Steps:
1. PROCEDURE:
2. 1. Put a tray in the oven at 100°C (212°F) for a water bath.
3. 2. Clean the livers and the foie gras, cut them into equal parts, add the pink salt and place everything on a baking sheet with parchment paper and cover with plastic wrap, keeping it in a warm place and mixing occasionally. The temperature of the livers should be around 40°C (104°F).
4. 3. Melted butter and eggs at room temperature.
5. 4. Using a deep container and using an immersion blender, blend the livers with the reduction, then the eggs, and then the butter slowly until you get a homogeneous mixture.
6. 5. Blend in the Thermomix at 65°C (149°F) for 2 minutes at maximum power and then pass through a double sieve.
7. 6. Put the mixture in the terrines in the preheated tray, cook for 35-40 minutes and reach a core temperature of 62°/64°C (144°/147°F).
8. 7. Do not use the blast chiller at all and let it rest for 24 hours.

Other details:
original_language: it

Classifications:
- Dish Type: Starter / Appetizer
- Cuisine: French
- Difficulty: Level 3 (Advanced / Michelin)
- Main Ingredient: Poultry (Duck)
- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Nut-Free, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Fine Dining / Plated
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

raw.name: Duck Foie Gras
steps: PROCEDURE:
1. Put a tray in the oven at 100°C (212°F) for a water bath.

2. Clean the livers and the foie gras, cut them into equal parts, add the pink salt and place everything on a baking sheet with parchment paper and cover with plastic wrap, keeping it in a warm place and mixing occasionally. The temperature of the livers should be around 40°C (104°F).
3. Melted butter and eggs at room temperature.
4. Using a deep container and using an immersion blender, blend the livers with the reduction, then the eggs, and then the butter slowly until you get a homogeneous mixture.
5. Blend in the Thermomix at 65°C (149°F) for 2 minutes at maximum power and then pass through a double sieve.
6. Put the mixture in the terrines in the preheated tray, cook for 35-40 minutes and reach a core temperature of 62°/64°C (144°/147°F).
7. Do not use the blast chiller at all and let it rest for 24 hours.

name: Cucumber, Coconut And Caviar
text:
Recipe ID: cucumber,_coconut_and_caviar_merged
Name: Cucumber, Coconut And Caviar

Ingredients:
- For the Coconut granita
- 1kg Coconut puree
- 1,5 Leaves gelatine
- 12g Salt
- For the Horseradish Oil
- 1kg Horseradish Root
- 500 ml Neutral Oil
- For the Cucumber Balls
- 5 Cucumbers
- For the Cucumber Compresse
- 250g Cucumber Skin Juice
- 50g Horseradish Vodka
- For the Sauce
- 300ml Light Fish Stock
- 30g Celercumber
- Xanthum Gum
- 200ml horseradish/celerecumber

Steps:
1. PROCESS
2. For the Coconut granita
3. 1. Hydrate gelatine in iced water.
4. 2. Warm the coconut puree in a shallow containers
5. 3. Add salt, pass card, blitz a little at a time in thermos to obtain a fine powder
6. For the Horseradish Oil
7. 1. Peel horseradish and blitz in Thermomix
8. 2. Add oil and cook in thermo at 90 degrees for 10 minutes
9. 3. Pass through a chinoix into an iced container.
10. For the Cucumber Balls
11. 1. Peel cucumbers and keep skins
12. 2. Use a Parisienne scoop to ball the cucumbers
13. For the Cucumber Compresse
14. 1. Juice the skins
15. 2. Mix the juice with the horseradish vodka
16. 3. Compress cucumber balls in vac pac machine
17. For the Sauce
18. 1. Blend fish stock with cucumber and celery
19. 2. Pass through a coffee filter overnight in fridge
20. 3. Add 0.2% xanthum gum
21. 5. Bottle and chill

Other details:


Classifications:
- Dish Type: Amuse-bouche / Canapé
- Cuisine: Nordic
- Difficulty: Level 3 (Advanced / Michelin)
- Main Ingredient: Vegetable-Forward
- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Dairy-Free, Nut-Free, Alcohol-Free, Pescatarian
- Occasion: Fine Dining / Plated
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Summer

raw.name: Cucumber, Coconut And Caviar

For the Coconut granita
1. Hydrate gelatine in iced water.
2. Warm the coconut puree in a shallow containers
3. Add salt, pass card, blitz a little at a time in thermos to obtain a fine powder
For the Horseradish Oil
1. Peel horseradish and blitz in Thermomix
2. Add oil and cook in thermo at 90 degrees for 10 minutes
3. Pass through a chinoix into an iced container.
For the Cucumber Balls
1. Peel cucumbers and keep skins
2. Use a Parisienne scoop to ball the cucumbers
For the Cucumber Compresse
1. Juice the skins
2. Mix the juice with the horseradish vodka
3. Compress cucumber balls in vac pac machine
For the Sauce
1. Blend fish stock with cucumber and celery
2. Pass through a coffee filter overnight in fridge
3. Add 0.2% xanthum gum
5. Bottle and chill

name: Peanut Powder
text:
Recipe ID: peanut_powder_parsed
Name: Peanut Powder

Ingredients:
- 4 kg Blanched peanuts
- 800 g Caster sugar

Steps:
1. Oven 175°C (Rational 0 moisture)
2. In two 1/1 shallow gastro, roast peanuts for 15 minutes, turning every 5 minutes (in Rational***) 2KG peanut in a shallow 1/1 gastro
3. Cool peanuts
4. When cooled, using the Thermomix lid cup, add 3 cups of roasted peanuts to the Thermomix (should be below the blade)
5. Blitz at Speed 8 for 6 seconds
6. Using a spatula, make sure to get all peanuts
7. After blitzed, add sieved sugar
8. Mix thoroughly to make sure mixture is uniform
9. Keep in refrigerator at all time

Other details:
Total Time: nan min
Notes: BE CAREFUL when blitzing, risk of turning into peanut butter
NOTES: ***In Convotherm, might need to roast a further 5 minutes

text:
Recipe ID: parmesan,_herb_and_anchovy_merged
Name: Parmesan, Herb And Anchovy

Ingredients:
- For the Anchovy oil
- 30g Anchovy fillets
- 300g Neutral oil
- For the parmesan cream
- 625g Whole milk
- 200g Parmesan
- 25g Egg whites
- 60g Anchovy oil
- 1.0g Xantana
- 1 leaf Gold Gelatin
- For the anchovy bones
- 10 Salted whole anchovies
- For crispy leaves
- 100g Yellow archangel
- For Herbs
- Penny wort
- Micro yarrow
- Chervil
- Nasturtium
- Micro Leaves from Cornwall

Steps:
1. 1. Vac the two together and cook at 60C for 8 hours.
2. 2. Pass and reserve in the fridge.
3. 1. Blitz Parmesan to a fine crumb
4. 2. Heat the milk to the boil and pour over the cheese
5. 3. Add the bloomed gelatina and the oil, allow to cool in an ice cold bain marie.
6. 4. Season with salt and pass
7. 5. Fill sifon gun and charge with 2 gas charges.
8. 6. Reserve in the fridge.
9. 7. Serve very cold
10. 1. Carefully remove the fillets from the bones with out breaking the spine.
11. 2. Scrape clean the spine.
12. 3. Deep fry in oil at 190C and transfer to absorbent paper.
13. 4. When cool store in airtight container.
14. 1. Pick the largest leaves and toast on the yakitori.
15. 2. Once toasted dry at 35C for 8 hours.
16. 3. Store in airtight container.
17. 1. 8g herbs per portion

name: Olive Oil Biscuit
text:
Recipe ID: olive_oil_biscuit_merged
Name: Olive Oil Biscuit

Ingredients:
- 450g unsalted butter
- 400g golden caster sugar
- 700g flour
- 12g salt
- 2 vanilla pod seeds
- 6g baking powder
- 150g extra virgin olive oil
- 180g egg yolks
- 90g ORIGO, CHOPPED SALL CLING

Steps:
1. Weigh out all the dry ingredients
2. Beat the sugar with the butter in the stand mixer
3. Add all the dry ingredients, CAREFUL NOT TO OVER MIX
4. Finally add the egg yolks and olive oil and mix till it just comes together
5. Roll out between 2 pieces of parchment paper on the dough brake on NUMBER 5
6. Rest in the fridge
7. Remove the top sheet of parchment before baking, @165 celsuis 12 minutes
8. Bake until golden brown

name: Focaccia Alla Genovese
text:
Recipe ID: focaccia_alla_genovese_merged
Name: Focaccia Alla Genovese

Ingredients:
- Water: 400 ml
- Manitoba (or flour rich in gluten): 600 g
- Brewer's yeast: 25 g
- Oil: 140 ml
- Malt (or sugar): ½ teaspoon
- Salt and coarse salt: as needed

Steps:
1. Dissolve 40 ml of oil, salt, and malt (or sugar) in lukewarm water, then pour everything into the planetary mixer (or any other bowl if kneading by hand) with half of the flour. Mix until you get a smooth and rather liquid batter. Then add the crumbled brewer's yeast and knead for another 3 minutes. Then add the remaining flour and knead again: you will obtain a homogeneous mixture that will be quite sticky.
2. Grease a baking sheet with 50 ml of oil. Sprinkle very little flour on top, a work surface, and place a loaf of dough. Then place it on the baking sheet and brush it with the remaining oil in the baking sheet itself. At this point, let it rise at a temperature of about 30° (the closed oven with the light on will be fine) for about 1 hour. Now it's halfway, until it has doubled in volume. Then take the baking sheet and spread the dough over its entire surface, making sure that there is still plenty of oil underneath it. Brush the surface of the focaccia with oil and sprinkle it with coarse salt before baking it again (in the off oven) to let it rise for another 30 minutes.
3. At that point, press the dough with your fingertips, firmly imprinting the characteristic holes that distinguish focaccia alla genovese. Drizzle the focaccia with the remaining 50 ml of oil and put everything back to rise (for the last time) for about 30 minutes.
4. It's time to bake the focaccia at 200° for at least 15 minutes. First, however, you must spray it with room temperature water. When it is cooked, be careful to let it cool on a wire rack, so as to preserve its fragrance.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★ ★
Cottura: 15'
Preparazione: 180'
Dosi: 6 persone

name: Pasta Per La
text:
Recipe ID: pasta_per_la_merged
Name: Pasta Per La

Ingredients:
- Water: 600 ml
- Flour: 1 kg + a sprinkling for the work surface
- Brewer's yeast: 50 g
- Oil: 6 tablespoons
- Salt: 20 g
- Sugar: 2 level teaspoons

Steps:
1. The satisfaction of preparing pasta at home is unmatched, especially because the pasta transforms, rises and swells, amazing even the most incredulous of children. Working it is a joy to the touch: smooth, soft and elastic, it takes shape and consistency under our hands. This recipe is one of the most appreciated by Giallozafferano users: of course it is only one of the many possible versions, but it is delicious and quick.
2. Arrange the flour in a fountain on a work surface (or in a large bowl) and make a hole in the center. Crumble the two cubes of brewer's yeast (or the dried yeast) and the sugar in a glass bowl where you have poured a glass of lukewarm water: mix until everything is dissolved. Pour the mixture into the flour. Separately, add the 20 g of salt to another glass of lukewarm water and add the oil, then add to the flour.
3. Start kneading, keeping some flour and the remaining lukewarm water close at hand, which you will integrate into the dough little by little, until you reach the desired consistency.
4. When the dough is smooth, soft and consistent, form a ball and place it in a large bowl adequately sprinkled with flour on the bottom. Cover the bowl with a cloth and place it in a warm place away from drafts, such as the off oven.
5. Wait for the dough to double in volume (it will take 1 hour, 1 hour and a half), then proceed to rolling out the pizza dough.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Preparazione: 15'

name: Grilled Pineapple With Honey And Cinnamon
text:
Recipe ID: ananas_alla_piastra
con_miele_e_cannella_merged
Name: Grilled Pineapple With Honey And Cinnamon

Ingredients:
- Pineapple: 4 thick slices
- Honey: 4 teaspoons
- Cane sugar: 4 teaspoons
- Fresh mint: a few sprigs
- Ground cinnamon: to taste

Steps:
1. Clean the pineapple and cut it into slices about 2 cm thick, carefully removing all the dark 'eyes' and the core.
2. Place the slices on a hot grill pan: turn them a couple of times on each side, leaving them on the heat for at least 10-12 minutes.
3. When they are browned, remove them from the grill and, on a serving plate, sprinkle with a teaspoon of cane sugar, a teaspoon of honey and, finally, a light dusting of cinnamon.

name: Pork Cheek And Onion
text:
Recipe ID: pork_cheek_and_onion_merged
Name: Pork Cheek And Onion

Ingredients:
- For the Pork cheek
- 1kg Pork cheek
- For the sauce
- 1.5l chicken stock
- 250g Pork juice
- 150g Smoked maple syrup
- For the onion
- 150g Red onion
- 5g Salt
- 50g Brown sugar
- 100g Vinegar
- 20g Water
- 2g Dry oregano

Steps:
1. 1. Clean the cheeks well. Making sure to remove all the hair.
2. 2. Salt for half an hour with grey salt.
3. 3. Wash, dry and vac without anything.
4. 4. Cook at 75 for 12 hours.
5. 5. Strain and keep juice.
6. 6. Potion in 100g slices then vac and store for service.
7. 1. Reduce chicken stock but half.
8. 2. Add rest of the ingredients and blend together.
9. 3. Put in bottle and store in the water bath.
10. 1. Thinly slice the red onion.
11. 2. Add the salt and massage until the onion becomes watery then add the oregano.
12. 3. Mix the vinegar and the sugar and the water.
13. 4. Heat until the sugar dissolves.
14. 5. Allow to cool then add to the onion.
15. 6. Leave to rest in the fridge over night.
16. FINISHING AND PLATING
17. 1. Warm one portion of the pork in the water bath.
18. 2. Remove in the bag and torch until it has charred.
19. 3. Cover with sauce and finish with 20g of onion on the side.

name: Mousse
text:
Recipe ID: mousse_parsed
Name: Mousse

Ingredients:
- 60 g lavender cream
- 168 g whipped cream
- 9 g sheet gelatin (silver)
- 365 g 18% crème fraiche
- 168 g Italian meringue
- Red gooseberry and rose emulsion

Steps:
1. Bloom the gelatin in ice water.
2. Heat the 60 g of lavender cream on the stove.
3. Once up to temperature, dissolve the gelatin into the cream.
4. Take two spoonfuls of the crème fraiche and whisk it into the lavender cream to temper it, then pass it through a chinois, into the rest of the crème fraiche.
5. Then fold in 1/3 of the Italian meringue until well incorporated, then the rest.
6. Then proceed to fold in the whipped cream. Mixing gently until well mixed.
7. Move it to the fridge to set for 10-15 minutes.
8. And after that, take the Red gooseberry and rose emulsion and 2 runs, transfer it to the mousse, folding it well, but briefly each time so as not to collapse the mousse, while ensuring everything is well mixed.
9. Transfer the mixture to a piping bag.

Other details:
setting_time: 10-15 minutes

Classifications:
- Dish Type: Dessert
- Cuisine: French
- Difficulty: Level 2 (Intermediate / Professional)
- Main Ingredient: Dairy / Egg
- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Nut-Free, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Fine Dining / Plated
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

raw.name: Mousse

Once up to temperature, dissolve the gelatin into the cream.
Take two spoonfuls of the crème fraiche and whisk it into the lavender cream to temper it, then pass it through a chinois, into the rest of the crème fraiche.
Then fold in 1/3 of the Italian meringue until well incorporated, then the rest.
Then proceed to fold in the whipped cream. Mixing gently until well mixed.
Move it to the fridge to set for 10-15 minutes.

And after that, take the Red gooseberry and rose emulsion and 2 runs, transfer it to the mousse, folding it well, but briefly each time so as not to collapse the mousse, while ensuring everything is well mixed.
Transfer the mixture to a piping bag.

name: Classic Pork Belly
text:
Recipe ID: classic_pork_belly_parsed
Name: Classic Pork Belly

Ingredients:
- 15 kg Pork belly
- 720 ml Light soya
- 630 ml Dark soya
- 900 ml Shaohsing wine
- 30 Spring onion
- 30 g Garlic (crushed)
- 280 g Ginger
- to fill Water
- 30 g Star anise
- 262.5 g Rock sugar
- 15 g Garlic powder
- 44.5 g Whole dried chilli
- 62.5 g Cinnamon stick

Steps:
1. Weigh pork belly
2. Cut to pieces. Roughly 9x18cm (short side to be against grain)
3. Measure liquid
4. Set Rational to 200. Braise until soft overnight
5. Place all pork in the gastro, pour in liquid
6. Into Rational, bring to boil and turn temp down to 95°C for overnight
7. 9am: Take pork out and rest
8. Pour the lard into separate container and sauce into another
9. Chop pork 5-10mm into small ice cream containers (against grain)
10. Reduce sauce by 1/2 upstairs at setting
11. Skim lard and store away
12. Cool the sauce and store away

Other details:

text:

Name: Butterfish Croquettes

Ingredients:
- 280g of Butter
- 360g of Flour
- 4 Liters of 45% Cream
- 8 Shallots
- 700g of Kimuchi Base

Steps:
1. a) In a pot, add the butter and heat until it begins to brown.
2. b) Dice the shallot and sauté with the butter.
3. c) Once the shallot is golden brown, add the flour and toast it.
4. d) Gradually add the cream and cook over medium heat, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
5. e) Once the dough is well cooked, season it with the Kimuchi Base and then blend in a Thermomix for 2 minutes.

name: Grape, Bee Pollen And Yogurt
text:
Recipe ID: grape,_bee_pollen_and_yogurt_merged
Name: Grape, Bee Pollen And Yogurt

Ingredients:
- For the Grapes
- 200g Grapes
- For the Sugar
- 50g Glucose
- 100g sugar
- For the Yogurt espuma
- 350g Yogurt 5%
- 150g Cream 35%
- 2 leaves of gelatine 2½g
- For Bee pollen caramel
- 50g Bee pollen powder
- 40g Crumiel

Steps:
1. PROCESS
2. Peel the grapes and refrigerate
3. Portion at 20g
4. Blend sugar with spice
5. Hydrate the gelatine in ice water.
6. Once hydrated dissolve in the cream.
7. Mix in the yogurt and pass.
8. Fill the siphon and charge with one charge.
9. Mix together and store in airtight container.
10. Using a sieve cover a silpat with the mixture.
11. Heat the oven to 180C.
12. Cook until the crumiel melts.
13. Once melted turn out onto another silpat.
14. Place again in the oven and pull to make shards.
15. Store in air tight container with silica gel.

Other details:

text:
Recipe ID: chocolate_and_cream_gelato_merged
Name: Chocolate And Cream Gelato

Ingredients:
- FOR CHOCOLATE GELATO
- Cocoa powder: 50 g
- Carob flour: 5 g (optional)
- Glucose: 15 g
- Milk: 500 ml
- Cream: 150 ml
- Eggs: 6 yolks
- Sugar: 160 g
- FOR CREAM GELATO
- Milk: 600 ml
- Cream: 250 g
- Vanilla extract: 1 packet
- Sugar: 250 g

Steps:
1. Difficulty: ★
2. Cooking time: 5'
3. Preparation: 40'
4. Yield: 1 kg of gelato
5. CHOCOLATE GELATO: In a bowl, mix the sugar with the carob flour; add the egg yolks and beat with an electric mixer until light and fluffy. In a saucepan, heat the milk, cream and glucose, then pour the liquids over the yolks and mix with a whisk. Then return to the heat and cook, stirring constantly until it reaches a temperature of 85° (check with a thermometer): do not bring to a boil, or lumps will form. Remove from the heat and incorporate the cocoa, then cool the mixture quickly by placing the bowl in a larger container containing ice. Then refrigerate for at least 2 hours, then pour the mixture into an ice cream maker. When ready, leave the gelato in the freezer for at least 3 hours before enjoying.

name: Potato Fagottini Filled With Ragusano Cheese, Guanciale, And Crispy Spring Onion
text:
Recipe ID: fagottino_di_patata_ripieno_di_ragusano_con_guanciale_e_cipollotto_croccante_merged
Name: Potato Fagottini Filled With Ragusano Cheese, Guanciale, And Crispy Spring Onion

Ingredients:
- For the fagottini
- 1 kg red potatoes
- 250g Flour
- 2 eggs
- Parmesan cheese
- Nutmeg
- Salt and pepper
- For the Roloqbo filling
- 1 liter milk
- 300g Ragusano cheese (450 BATATA (pat)
- 80g Roux (80 BUTTER)
- For the guanciale
- 300g Suckling pig guanciale
- Vegetable stock, brunoise cut
- 1 liter water
- 50g salt
- Bay leaf
- Black peppercorns
- For the pork stock
- Pork bones and scraps
- Concentrated vegetable stock
- For the spring onion
- 10 spring onions

Steps:
1. Prepare the boiled potatoes for gnocchi, mash and mince, and knead. Roll out the dough with a rolling pin to a thickness of about half a centimeter, cut out squares with a cookie cutter, and fill with the Ragusano cheese fondue previously prepared in the Roloqbo. Then close the gnocchi into fagottini.
2. For the fondue, set the Roloqbo to 85° with a serrated blade at a speed of 400. Add butter, flour, salt, and nutmeg and let it run for 1 minute, then add the milk and as soon as the mixture is ready, immerse the cheese and continue to stir until temperature is reached.
3. Bring the water to a boil with the salt, bay leaf, and pepper. Let it cool slightly and immerse the guanciale for 20 minutes. Meanwhile, prepare the brunoise-cut vegetable stock, which will be used later.
4. With the bones, prepare a classic pork stock, and as soon as it is ready, strain it through cheesecloth and combine with the brunoise-cut vegetables. Put a little sauce together with some guanciale in vacuum-sealed cooking bags and cook overnight at 42° steam.
5. Put the spring onions in vacuum-sealed cooking bags, seal, and cook for about 10 minutes at 85° steam.
6. Compose the dish with 4 gnocchi cooked in water and sautéed in butter, the regenerated guanciale, always vacuum-sealed, at 60° in the Roner. Then slice thinly and pour meat and sauce over the gnocchi, finish with the pan-fried spring onion.

Other details:

translated_from: it

name: Mimosa Cake
text:
Recipe ID: mimosa_cake_merged
Name: Mimosa Cake

Ingredients:
- FOR THE SPONGE CAKE
- Flour: 200 g
- Potato starch: 40 g
- Eggs: 4 + 8 yolks
- Sugar: 220 g
- Salt: as needed
- Vanilla: 1 pod
- FOR THE PASTRY CREAM
- Flour: 55 g
- Fresh whole milk: 300 ml
- Fresh liquid cream: 300 ml
- Eggs: 8 yolks
- Vanilla: ½ pod
- Sugar: 200 g
- FOR THE LIQUEUR SYRUP
- Water: 100 ml
- Cointreau: 50 ml
- Sugar: 50 g
- FOR THE WHIPPED CREAM
- Fresh liquid cream: 200 ml
- Icing sugar: 20 g
- Butter: 300 g
- 00 Flour: 150 g
- Potato starch: 150 g
- Baking powder: 1 sachet
- Lemons: 1 (grated zest)
- Eggs: 8 yolks + 4 egg whites
- Vanillin: 1 sachet
- Sugar: 300 g
- Salt: 1 pinch
- FOR SPRINKLING
- Icing sugar: 2 tablespoons

Steps:
1. To make this cake you will need two sponge cake layers about 22 cm wide. Therefore, follow the doses indicated on the side, halving them if you only have one oven and are therefore forced to cook one sponge cake at a time. Then repeat the procedure indicated on page 282 twice. While the sponge cakes are cooling, dedicate yourself to the pastry cream, according to the recipe on page 280. When it is ready, let it cool in a low, wide and cold container, covering it with plastic wrap to prevent a skin from forming.
2. Prepare the syrup, dissolving the sugar together with the water and liqueur in a saucepan, then let it cool. Whip the very cold cream with a whisk; as soon as it begins to swell, add the icing sugar and finish whipping, then put everything in the refrigerator. When the pastry cream is cold, put it in a bowl, soften it by working it with a spatula and gently add the whipped cream, except for two tablespoons that you will set aside.
3. The sponge cakes should be cold enough to remove the dark outer part. With a long-bladed knife, cut one of the two horizontally, obtaining three slices of equal thickness, while from the other cut slices of 1 cm thickness, which you will then cut into 3 strips to be subsequently reduced into cubes.
4. It's time to assemble the cake - if you have one, use an adjustable steel ring. Soak the first sponge cake layer with the syrup, then spread a layer of sweetened whipped cream and, finally, a layer of about 1 cm of pastry cream mixed with cream. Then place the second layer and repeat everything from the beginning. Once you reach the third layer, remove the steel ring and cover the surface and sides of the cake with the remaining cream. Then move on to the decoration, making the sponge cake cubes adhere to the cream, which will act as a glue. Then put the cake in the refrigerator, preferably covered with a glass or plastic cover.
5. Turn on the oven to 180°C. Separate the yolks from the egg whites, keeping 4 of the latter aside.
6. Put the butter, half of the sugar and the vanillin in a large bowl or in a mixer and mix until creamy. Then mix in the 8 yolks one at a time, taking care to add the next only when the previous one has been completely incorporated (in the mixer you can put everything together). When you have obtained a light, creamy and lump-free mixture, add the grated lemon zest, the flour sifted with the potato starch and the baking powder.
7. In another tall-sided container, beat the 4 egg whites until stiff with a pinch of salt, then, continuing to beat, add the remaining half of the sugar. Gently incorporate the egg whites into the previous mixture, mixing from bottom to top to incorporate air.
8. Grease and flour a cake tin (if you have one, a springform pan) with a diameter of 28 cm and pour in the dough. Bake at 180°C for at least 50 minutes. Check the cooking with a toothpick: if you remove it completely dry, the cake will be cooked and ready to be taken out of the oven. When it has cooled, place it on a serving dish and sprinkle it with plenty of icing sugar.

Other details:
Difficoltà: Media
Cottura: 50'
Preparazione: 20'
Dosi: 10 persone

Therefore, follow the doses indicated on the side, halving them if you only have one oven and are therefore forced to cook one sponge cake at a time. Then repeat the procedure indicated on page 282 twice. While the sponge cakes are cooling, dedicate yourself to the pastry cream, according to the recipe on page 280. When it is ready, let it cool in a low, wide and cold container, covering it with plastic wrap to prevent a skin from forming.

Prepare the syrup, dissolving the sugar together with the water and liqueur in a saucepan, then let it cool. Whip the very cold cream with a whisk; as soon as it begins to swell, add the icing sugar and finish whipping, then put everything in the refrigerator. When the pastry cream is cold, put it in a bowl, soften it by working it with a spatula and gently add the whipped cream, except for two tablespoons that you will set aside.

The sponge cakes should be cold enough to remove the dark outer part. With a long-bladed knife, cut one of the two horizontally, obtaining three slices of equal thickness, while from the other cut slices of 1 cm thickness, which you will then cut into 3 strips to be subsequently reduced into cubes.

It's time to assemble the cake - if you have one, use an adjustable steel ring. Then move on to the decoration, making the sponge cake cubes adhere to the cream, which will act as a glue. Then put the cake in the refrigerator, preferably covered with a glass or plastic cover.
Turn on the oven to 180°C. Separate the yolks from the egg whites, keeping 4 of the latter aside.

Put the butter, half of the sugar and the vanillin in a large bowl or in a mixer and mix until creamy. Then mix in the 8 yolks one at a time, taking care to add the next only when the previous one has been completely incorporated (in the mixer you can put everything together). When you have obtained a light, creamy and lump-free mixture, add the grated lemon zest, the flour sifted with the potato starch and the baking powder.

In another tall-sided container, beat the 4 egg whites until stiff with a pinch of salt, then, continuing to beat, add the remaining half of the sugar. Gently incorporate the egg whites into the previous mixture, mixing from bottom to top to incorporate air.

Grease and flour a cake tin (if you have one, a springform pan) with a diameter of 28 cm and pour in the dough. Bake at 180°C for at least 50 minutes. Check the cooking with a toothpick: if you remove it completely dry, the cake will be cooked and ready to be taken out of the oven. When it has cooled, place it on a serving dish and sprinkle it with plenty of icing sugar.

text:

Name: Citrus Curd

Ingredients:
- 270G SEA BUCKTHORN JUICE PASSED THOUGH MUSLIN
(OR KALAMANSI JUICE OR BERGAMOT)
- 200G CASTER SUGAR
- 6G AGAR AGAR
- 6 WHOLE EGGS-HAND BLENDED TOGETHOR
- 250G BUTTER-DICED AND PLACED IN THE VITAMIX

Steps:
1. BOIL AGAR, SUGAR AND JUICE AND TEMPER IN THE EGGS

4. PASS THROUGH A CHINOIS ONTO A FLAT STAINLESS TRAY AND
5. CHILL WITH PARCHMENT ON THE SURFACE
6. BEAT ON THE KITCHEN AID WITH PADDLE ATTATCHMNENT UNTIL SMOOTH THEN
7. WITH THE WHISK ATTATCHMENT FOR A FURTHER MINUTE OR SO TO GIVE IT VOLUME
8. STORE IN PIPING BAGS

name: Mapo
text:
Recipe ID: mapo_parsed
Name: Mapo

Ingredients:
- 13.5 kg Diced Onion
- 180 g Sichuan Pepper
- 1350 g Oil
- 216 g Ginger Diced
- 432 g Garlic Diced
- 2625 g Doubanjiang
- 15 l Kombu Stock
- 2100 ml Light Soy
- 450 ml Rock Sugar
- 1050 ml Shaohsing Wine

Steps:
1. Infuse Sichuan pepper corns into the oil disgarding the pepercorns after.
2. Caremelise the onions in the brat pan with the oil.
3. When brown add garlic and ginger until cooked.
4. Add the rest of the ingredients and bring to boil.
5. Cook the Mapo down slowly until the right consistency
6. Chill the mix down completely
7. Blend in the thermo at speed 5 for 4 seconds each batch.

Other details:

NOTES: Some dark soy may need to be added for the right colour

name: Whipped Custard
text:
Recipe ID: whipped_custard_merged
Name: Whipped Custard

Ingredients:
- 1400g Whipping Cream
- 1 Vanilla
- 6 Whole Eggs
- 200g Sugar
- 8 Leaves Gelatine
- Verbena - 20 g fresh from cold, 4 drops oil to finish
- Sauternes or mead reduction and lime juice
- 30g pink peppercorns, washed
- Richmond park honey 5%

Steps:
1. Soak gelatine in iced water
2. Whisk #2 on kitchen, high speed until thick sabayon
3. Heat #1 to near boiling
4. Add soaked squeezed gelatine
5. Temper in sabayon and whisking constantly bring to 74c
6. Pass into a gastro tray chill in the blast chiller
7. Once cold whip on kitchen with paddle attachment (gently)
8. No more than 10% liquid when seasoning

name: Sichuan Chili Oil
text:
Recipe ID: sichuan_chili_oil_merged
Name: Sichuan Chili Oil

Ingredients:
- 3000g (3L) Rapeseed Oil
- 180g Ginger
- 200g Garlic
- 30g Star Anise
- 18g Toasted Coriander Seed
- 60g Whole Dried Red Chilli
- 150g Red Sichuan Pepper Corn
- 4g Salt
- 20g Sesame seeds

Steps:
1. Toast Dried chilli, Sichuan peppercorn and blitz
2. In a pot on low heat combine oil with bruised garlic, bruised ginger and toasted coriander seed. Confit until garlic and ginger is golden brown.
3. Remove from the heat and strain, discard garlic, ginger, coriander seeds.
4. Heat oil to 100-120C and add blitzed chillis.
5. Let steep for minimum 2 days.

name: New York Cheesecake
text:
Recipe ID: newyork_cheesecake_merged
Name: New York Cheesecake

Ingredients:
- FOR THE BASE
- Digestive biscuits: 250 g
- Butter: 150 g
- FOR THE CREAM
- Cornstarch: 2 tablespoons
- Lemons: ½ (juice)
- Fresh cream: 100 ml
- Philadelphia: 400 g
- Eggs: 2 + 1 yolk
- Vanilla extract: 1 packet
- Sugar: 100 g
- Salt: as needed
- FOR THE TOPPING
- Sour cream: 200 ml
- Vanilla extract: 1 packet
- Powdered sugar: 2 tablespoons

Steps:
1. As soon as I had confirmation that I would go to New York, despite the diet I booked (months in advance!) a table at the Cheesecake Factory. The temple of cheesecake, of all the United States, but also more than thirty variations only "The Original", the best cheesecake, a place. I chose my favorite with strawberry sauce, of an almost moving goodness. Not to be presumptuous, but even my recipe, super tested, is not bad. If you want to make the strawberry sauce yourself, you will only have to blend them with sugar and lemon a delicacy!
2. Put the digestive biscuits in the mixer, adding the brown sugar and crumble them, then mix in the butter after melting it in a saucepan. Grease a cake tin (preferably with a removable bottom, with a diameter of 22-24 cm). Cut out a disc of parchment paper of the same diameter as the bottom of the cake tin and two strips of the same height as the edges, then line. Cover the bottom and sides of the cake tin with the mixture, leveling it well with the help of a spoon, then put the cake tin in the fridge for 1 hour (or in the freezer for half an hour). Preheat the oven to 180°.
3. In a large bowl, beat the eggs with the vanilla extract and sugar. When you have obtained a homogeneous mixture, add the cheese (if you want, use Philadelphia more high, you can add up to 150 g of mascarpone) and mix until the mixture is creamy and without lumps. Without stopping stirring, incorporate the lemon juice, cornstarch, 2 good pinches of salt.
4. Pour the cream into the cake tin that you have removed from the refrigerator (or freezer), level it and bake at 180° for 30 minutes, then lower the temperature to 160° and let it cook for another 30-40 minutes. If after the first half hour you notice that the surface of the cheesecake is darkening too much, cover it with a sheet of aluminum foil. When cooked, turn off the oven and let the cake rest for 30 minutes in the oven off and with the door slightly open.
5. When it is cold, mix the sour cream with the two tablespoons of powdered sugar and the packet of vanilla extract: spread the mixture on the top of the cheesecake and decide whether to bake again at 180° for 5 minutes to glaze the cream, then put everything in the refrigerator for at least 6 hours or store the cake directly in the refrigerator. The optimal solution would be to prepare the cake the night before consumption and let it cool overnight.

Other details:
Difficoltà: media
Cottura: 60'
Preparazione: 30'
Dosi: 8 persone

name: Piadina Romagnola
text:
Recipe ID: piadina_romagnola_merged
Name: Piadina Romagnola

Ingredients:
- Water: 160 ml
- Baking soda: 2 g
- Flour: 300 g
- Salt: 10 g
- Lard: 75 g

Steps:
1. In my house, everything has always been cooked, including piadina. My grandfather prepared it, who came from Ferrara and had spent dozens of summers on the Romagna Riviera. He made it seem like magic: he put the same joy and careless disregard into it. Piadina is one of those dishes that should always be made at home, without skimping. You get a food with a flavor infinitely more delicious than any industrial product. Among other things, if friends happen to come to dinner, maybe on a Sunday evening, when you improvise, even just half a loaf, in a very short time a great impression is assured.
2. In a bowl, pour the flour and baking soda, add the lard and lukewarm water (or milk, if you prefer a softer piadina) in which you have dissolved the salt. Knead the dough for about ten minutes to mix all the ingredients well, then flour the bowl, cover it with a damp cloth and let the dough rest for half an hour.
3. At that point, divide it into four equivalent balls, which will allow you to obtain 4 piadinas with a diameter of about 25 centimeters. Roll out the discs with a floured rolling pin, until the thickness does not exceed 4-5 millimeters. To obtain a smooth edge, you can use a circular pastry cutter or a smooth-bladed pastry wheel, to be run, for example, along the contour of a plate.
4. Heat a non-stick pan (ideally cast iron or terracotta) and heat the piadinas on both sides, quickly and over high heat. Cooking should not exceed two minutes per side. If bubbles form on the surface, crush them with the tines of a fork. The piadinas are ready for filling.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Dosi: 4 piadine
Preparazione: 30'
Cottura: 10'

name: Croquette
text:
Recipe ID: croquette_en_parsed
Name: Croquette

Ingredients:
- 2kg croquette mass
- panko breadcrumbs
- wheat starch
- 500g whisked eggs

Steps:
1. a) Put the wheat starch and panko breadcrumbs on a tray.
2. b) In a bowl, whisk the eggs.
3. c) Form 18g balls of the croquette mass and introduce them into the wheat starch to shape them.
4. d) Coat the wheat starch-covered balls in the egg, let them sit for a minimum of 3 minutes, and then coat them in panko breadcrumbs.
5. e) It is very important not to keep them for more than one day.

Other details:

name: Shiitake Stock
text:
Recipe ID: shiitake_stock_merged
Name: Shiitake Stock

Ingredients:
- white onion 1 unit
- Dried shiitake slightly washed 25 unit
- bean sprouts 150 g
- Roasted Soybeans 20 g
- Carrots 3 pieces
- Star Anis 4 units
- Rock Sugar 50 g
- Dried and Rehydrate Chestnut (1 Hr.) 6 units
- sea salt 2 teaspoon

Steps:
1. Put all the above ingredients in a pot with water and bring it to a boil
2. As soon as it comes to a boil, skim it, bring it down a bare simmer
3. let the stock cook for 40 minutes, you should cover it to let all the volatiles ingredients safe
4. Season it to taste, ideally with soy sauce, and salt at this stage
5. Remove the Chestnut, keep it for braising your tofu in with
6. Pass the stock, add 32g of fresh ginger to the stock, and blast chill it

name: Vegetarian Dashi
text:
Recipe ID: vegetarian_dashi_merged
Name: Vegetarian Dashi

Ingredients:
- filtered water x 10L
- kombu x 100g
- shiitake mushroom x 10
- Soy bean x 100g
- Chinese date x 10 pieces
- dried pear x 10 pieces
- dried mandarin skin x 10 pieces

Steps:
1. The night before
2. 1 soak kombu and dates over night in 10L of filtered water and keep it in the cold fridge, make sure the tuppawear is clean and not greasy nor smelly.
3. 2 soak shiitake mushroom in separate water over night. ( You discard this water next morning because Shiitake produce unwanted taste in initial water.)
4. The day you make Dashi
5. 3 put soy bean in the sauce pan and gently toast till it gets coloured and tasty. Careful not to burn. If you toast them with high heat, it easily gets burnt. And do not pre-toast batch of soy beans as it's easier for kitchen staff to do so. You have to toast it very time fresh when you make Dashi.
6. 4 prepare stainless stock pot. Do not use aluminium one. Put the filtered water together with kombu and dates. Add Shiitake soaked in water over night, but discard the water you use to soak Shiitake. Add toasted soy beans, dried pear and mandarin skin.
7. 5 put the stock pot on the small heat, slowly bring it to 60 degree and keep it 60 degree for one hour. Be careful not to boil it.
8. 6 after an hour, you take only kombu out and brink the heat slowly up to boiling temperature. keep it simmer while skimming the scam carefully for 30 minutes.
9. 7 stop the heat and filter the dashi using clean cloth.

Other details:


Classifications:
- Dish Type: Component / Base (Stocks, Sauces, Pastes, Garnishes, Doughs)
- Cuisine: Japanese
- Difficulty: Level 2 (Intermediate / Professional)
- Main Ingredient: Vegetable-Forward
- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Dairy-Free, Nut-Free, Vegetarian, Vegan, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Fine Dining / Plated
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

2 soak shiitake mushroom in separate water over night. ( You discard this water next morning because Shiitake produce unwanted taste in initial water.)
The day you make Dashi

3 put soy bean in the sauce pan and gently toast till it gets coloured and tasty. Careful not to burn. If you toast them with high heat, it easily gets burnt. And do not pre-toast batch of soy beans as it's easier for kitchen staff to do so. You have to toast it very time fresh when you make Dashi.

4 prepare stainless stock pot. Do not use aluminium one. Put the filtered water together with kombu and dates. Add Shiitake soaked in water over night, but discard the water you use to soak Shiitake. Add toasted soy beans, dried pear and mandarin skin.
5 put the stock pot on the small heat, slowly bring it to 60 degree and keep it 60 degree for one hour. Be careful not to boil it.
6 after an hour, you take only kombu out and brink the heat slowly up to boiling temperature. keep it simmer while skimming the scam carefully for 30 minutes.
7 stop the heat and filter the dashi using clean cloth.

name: Tandoor Bread
text:
Recipe ID: tandoor_bread_parsed
Name: Tandoor Bread

Ingredients:
- 200 gr of 00 flour strained
- 125 gr of liquid ( ½ water , ½ milk)
- 1 tsp of salt
- 1 tsp of sugar
- 1 ½ tbsp of oil
- cheese
- chilli
- onion
- tomato sauce

Steps:
1. Mix together 200 gr of 00 flour strained , 125 gr of liquid ( ½ water , ½ milk), 1 tsp of salt, 1 tsp of sugar, 1 ½ tbsp of oil
2. Kneed for 8 minutes and rest for minimum 1 hour, outside with oil on top and cover with film
3. Roll little ball about 40 gr, and roll them in little wraps , once the tandoor its hot proceed to cook the bread by sticking ( with the apposite tool) it in the tandoor wall until cook and golden brown for them take them out
4. You can put all the garnish ( cheese, chilli, onion, tomato sauce, ecc)that you want when you roll the ball before to cook

Other details:
Ratio Dry ingredients: 1 : 0,70 wet ingredients

name: Creamy Bull Testicles
text:
Recipe ID: creamy_bull_testicles_merged
Name: Creamy Bull Testicles

Ingredients:
- INGREDIENTS:
(For 20 people.)
- Chia Eggs:
- 1 lt. water
- 10 g. gelatin sheets
- 20 units chia eggs
- Chia Seeds
- Almorta (Grass Pea) Foam:
- 4.5 g. Methocel
- 375 ml. water
- 75 g olive oil
- 30 g garlic
- 75 g sweet pine nuts
- 375 g Chicken broth 25º
brix (broth batch)
- 4.5 g. xanthan gum
- 150 ml. water
- The bull testicles:
- 100 g. bull testicles
- INGREDIENTES:
(Para 20 personas.)
- Los huevos de chia:
- 1 lt. agua
- 10 g. gelatina hojas
- 20 uni. huevos de chia
- Semillas Chia
- Espuma de Almortas:
- 4.5 g. Methocel
- 375 ml. agua
- 75 g aceite oliva
- 30 g ajo
- 75 g piñón dulce
- 375 g caldo Gallina 25º
brix (partida de caldos)
- 4.5 g. xantana
- 150 ml. agua
- Las criadillas:
- 100 g. criadillas

Steps:
1. ELABORATION:
2. Chia Eggs:
3. Hydrate gelatin sheets in ice water and dissolve the
gelatin sheets in hot water and let cool.
4. Dip the balloons in the gelatin bath and coat
them in the seeds, forming a layer of seeds
uniformly. Place on the surface of the balloon.
5. Germinate for 6 days, watering frequently.
Cover with damp paper when the roots start to sprout.
6. The ideal germination temperature is 30-35ºC.
7. Almorta (Grass Pea) Foam:
8. Hydrate Methocel for 12 hours in 375 ml. of
water.
9. Sauté the garlic in the oil, sauté the garlic,
Prepare a roux with the almorta in fat. Remove
cooking the paprika flour and stir until it is
cooked, avoiding burning.
10. Bring to the heat and add the Methocel and lighten with the
50 ml of water. Add the HOT chicken broth,
to avoid lumps, and leave for 2 minutes
to finish cooking the flour.
11. Add the xanthan gum and beat with the hand blender.
Serve in a siphon with 2 charges. Keep warm in
a bain-marie at a controlled temperature of 80ºC.
12. The bull testicles:
13. Scald the bull testicles and cut them into 1 cm
thick slices.
14. Grill them on the grill and cut into 0.5 x 1 cm cubes.
15. FINISHING AND PRESENTATION
16. Carefully remove the balloon from inside the sprout
(the paper covering the sprouted balloon must be dry),
obtaining some chia sprout eggs.
17. Arrange on the plate with 5 g. of foam and
apply 5 g. of diced bull testicles to the base and
apply the base. Serve with the egg as a base.
18. ELABORACION:
19. Los huevos de chia:
20. Hidratar hojas de gelatina en agua con hielo y resolver las
hojas de gelatina en el agua caliente y dejar templar.
21. Bañar los globos en el baño de gelatina y rebozarlos
en las semillas formando una capa de semillas
uniformemente. Poner sobre la superficie del globo.
22. Germinar durante 6 días, regándola con frecuencia.
Cubrir con papel húmedo cuando empiecen a brotar
las raíces.
23. La temperatura ideal de germinado es de 30-35ºC.
24. Espuma de Almortas:
25. Hidratar Methocel durante 12 horas en 375 ml. de
agua.
26. Sofreír los ajos en el aceite, sofreír los ajos,
Preparar un roux en la almorta en grasa. Retirar
cocinando la harina del pimentón y remover hasta que
se cocine evitando que se queme.
27. Llevar al fuego y añadir el Methocel y aligerar con los
50 ml de agua. Añadir el caldo de gallina CALIENTE,
para evitar que formen grumos, y dejar 2 minutos
para terminar de cocinar la harina.
28. Añadir la xantana y batir con la batidora de mano.
Servir en sifón con 2 cargas. Reservar caliente en
baño maría a temperatura controlada a 80ºC.
29. Las criadillas:
30. Escaldar las criadillas y cortarlas en laminas de 1 cm
de grosor.
31. Asarlas en la parrilla y cortar en dados de 0.5 x 1 cm
32. ACABADO Y PRESENTACIÓN
33. Quitar con cuidado el globo de dentro del germinado
(el papel que cubre el globo de germinado debe estar seco),
obteniendo unos huevos de germinado de chia.
34. Disponer en el plato con 5 g. de espuma y
aplicar con 5 g. de criadillas en cubos la base y
aplicar la base. Servir con el huevo como base.

Other details:
Mugaritz: 2017
original_language: es

Classifications:
- Dish Type: Starter / Appetizer
- Cuisine: Other
- Difficulty: Level 3 (Advanced / Michelin)

raw.name: Creamy Bull Testicles
ingredients:
375 g caldo Gallina 25º
brix (partida de caldos)
steps: ELABORATION:
Chia Eggs:

Hydrate gelatin sheets in ice water and dissolve the
gelatin sheets in hot water and let cool.

Dip the balloons in the gelatin bath and coat
them in the seeds, forming a layer of seeds
uniformly. Place on the surface of the balloon.

Germinate for 6 days, watering frequently.
Cover with damp paper when the roots start to sprout.
The ideal germination temperature is 30-35ºC.
Almorta (Grass Pea) Foam:

Hydrate Methocel for 12 hours in 375 ml. of
water.

Sauté the garlic in the oil, sauté the garlic,
Prepare a roux with the almorta in fat. Remove
cooking the paprika flour and stir until it is
cooked, avoiding burning.

Bring to the heat and add the Methocel and lighten with the
50 ml of water. Add the HOT chicken broth,
to avoid lumps, and leave for 2 minutes
to finish cooking the flour.

Add the xanthan gum and beat with the hand blender.
Serve in a siphon with 2 charges. Keep warm in
a bain-marie at a controlled temperature of 80ºC.
The bull testicles:

Scald the bull testicles and cut them into 1 cm
thick slices.
Grill them on the grill and cut into 0.5 x 1 cm cubes.
FINISHING AND PRESENTATION

Carefully remove the balloon from inside the sprout
(the paper covering the sprouted balloon must be dry),
obtaining some chia sprout eggs.

Arrange on the plate with 5 g. of foam and
apply 5 g. of diced bull testicles to the base and
apply the base. Serve with the egg as a base.
ELABORACION:
Los huevos de chia:

Hidratar hojas de gelatina en agua con hielo y resolver las
hojas de gelatina en el agua caliente y dejar templar.

Bañar los globos en el baño de gelatina y rebozarlos
en las semillas formando una capa de semillas
uniformemente. Poner sobre la superficie del globo.

Germinar durante 6 días, regándola con frecuencia.
Cubrir con papel húmedo cuando empiecen a brotar
las raíces.
La temperatura ideal de germinado es de 30-35ºC.
Espuma de Almortas:

Hidratar Methocel durante 12 horas en 375 ml. de
agua.

Sofreír los ajos en el aceite, sofreír los ajos,
Preparar un roux en la almorta en grasa. Retirar
cocinando la harina del pimentón y remover hasta que
se cocine evitando que se queme.

Llevar al fuego y añadir el Methocel y aligerar con los
50 ml de agua. Añadir el caldo de gallina CALIENTE,
para evitar que formen grumos, y dejar 2 minutos
para terminar de cocinar la harina.

Añadir la xantana y batir con la batidora de mano.
Servir en sifón con 2 cargas. Reservar caliente en
baño maría a temperatura controlada a 80ºC.
Las criadillas:

Escaldar las criadillas y cortarlas en laminas de 1 cm
de grosor.
Asarlas en la parrilla y cortar en dados de 0.5 x 1 cm
ACABADO Y PRESENTACIÓN

Quitar con cuidado el globo de dentro del germinado
(el papel que cubre el globo de germinado debe estar seco),
obteniendo unos huevos de germinado de chia.

Disponer en el plato con 5 g. de espuma y
aplicar con 5 g. de criadillas en cubos la base y
aplicar la base. Servir con el huevo como base.

name: Sambher Gravy
text:
Recipe ID: sambher_gravy_parsed
Name: Sambher Gravy

Ingredients:
- Tura Dal
- Kashimiri chilli
- sunflower oil
- mustard seeds
- cumin seed
- fenugreek seed
- White Urad Dal
- Tur Dal
- Chana Dal
- curry leaves
- Hing
- turmeric
- coconut pulp
- tamarind
- red onion
- tomato
- Sambher masala powder
- Drumstick
- pumping
- aubergine
- mustard
- cumin seed
- fenugreek seed
- Sambher powder
- chili powder
- hing
- green chilli
- curry leaves

Steps:
1. Open coconut and shave the pulp
2. Soak 1 cup of Tura Dal in water
3. Toast 25 gr of kashimiri chilli ( clean and de seeded)  slightly toasted in 3 ladle of sunflower oil in a hot pan, remove the chili and set it aside
4. In the same pan toss 2 tbsp of mustard seeds, 1 tbsp of cumin seed and 1\2 tbsp of fenugreek seed, after the spice are popping add 2 tbsp of White Urad Dal,2 tbsp of Tur Dal and 2 tbsp of Chana Dal, 15 curry leaves 2 tsp of Hing and 1\2 tsp turmeric. Toast everything and add all the shaved coconut mix and toast everything well for 2 minute, then set aside to cool down
5. Wash well the Tura dal precedently soaked and put’em in the pressure pot with 2 finger of water above the dal level
6. Soak 1 handful  of tamarind in water for 10 minutes and strain for obtain the tamarind water
7. Dice 2 red onion and 2 big tomato and put everything in the pressure pot, close it and place it over medium heat fire, cook until 2 whistle and turn off fire and let it rest in the close pressure pot
8. Meanwhile place the coconut mixture in the blender with 3 tbsp of Sambher masala powder, the Kashmiri chili and little bit of water enough to make a smooth and thick paste (GRAVY)
9. Peel and cut 2 inch 3 Drumstick, cube 250gr of pumping and wedge 250 gr of aubergine soak all in water, and put the vegetable with 1 cup of water  in the pressure pot after have been crush slightly the lentil in the pressure pot, without the lid mix well and simmer until vegetable are tender ( but not overcook and mushi)
10. Add 1 ladle of gravy and salt to taste ( approx 3 spoon) and tamarind water
11. In a little pot toast in 2 ladle of oil the spices in this order: 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin seed and 1\2 tsp of fenugreek seed toast 30 second and then add 1 tbsp Sambher powder, 1 tsp chili powder, 1 tsp of hing, 2 green chilli and 10 curry leaves.
12. Once everything it’s nicely toasted add it         all back in the Sambher gravy, and for Mr jai add 4 chilli chopped

Other details:
servings: 2 people

Classifications:
- Dish Type: Component / Base (Stocks, Sauces, Pastes, Garnishes, Doughs)
- Cuisine: Indian
- Difficulty: Level 2 (Intermediate / Professional)
- Main Ingredient: Vegetable-Forward
- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Nut-Free, Vegan, Vegetarian, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Family Style / Sharing
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

text:
Recipe ID: beef_shortrib_xian_bing_parsed
Name: Beef Shortrib Xian Bing

Ingredients:

- 350 g Veal Kidney

- 125 g Black Pepper Sauce

4. Take the kidney out of the wok, add shao xin wine in to deglaze

name: Puff Pastry
text:
Recipe ID: ricette_di_base
pasta_sfoglia_merged
Name: Puff Pastry

Ingredients:
- FOR THE DOUGH
- Manitoba Flour: 150 g
- 00 Flour: 200 g
- Water: about 200 ml
- Salt: 8 g
- FOR THE BUTTER BLOCK
- Butter: 250 g
- 00 Flour: 75 g

Steps:
1. Start by preparing the dough. Mix and sift the two flours, preferably in a stand mixer with a paddle attachment, or by hand. Dissolve the salt in the water and knead everything at medium speed for about 8 minutes: you should obtain a mixture that is softer and more elastic than bread dough, and, of course, smooth and homogeneous. Let it rest, covered with a tea towel, in a cool place for at least half an hour.
2. In the meantime, take care of the butter block. Remove the butter from the refrigerator, cut it into cubes, and place it in the stand mixer with the 75 g of flour: mix until you get a smooth and lump-free mixture, which you will reduce to a square about 1 centimeter thick, either with floured hands or by placing it between two sheets of parchment paper and flattening it with the help of a rolling pin. Place the butter block in the lower part of the refrigerator - the coldest - for at least half an hour.
3. Roll out the dough: you will get a rectangle about 50 centimeters long in the center of which you will place the butter block, now ready (1), which you will cover with the two longer edges of the dough, making them match (2). Fold the other two as well, until you close the butter block on each side (3). Always remember to flour the work surface and the dough to facilitate the rolling process.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★★
Preparazione: 130'
Dosi: 1 kg di pasta circa

name: Caesar Sauce
text:
Recipe ID: caesar_sauce_merged
Name: Caesar Sauce

Ingredients:
- 250 g egg yolks 120
- 8 dl mustard oil
- 4 dl peanut oil 1/2
- 24 g garlic 100
- 2 dl white vinegar
- 90 g anchovies
- 240 g Parmesan Rope 30
- 60 g lemon juice
- 2 g pepper
- 8 g salt
- 10 g tarragon
- 250 g de jaunes 120
- 8 dl d'huile de moutarde
- 4 dl d'huile d'arachide 1/2
- 24 g d'ail 100
- 2 dl de vinaigre blanc
- 90 g d'anchois
- 240 g de parmesan Rope 30
- 60 g de jus de citron
- 2 g de poivre
- 8 g de sel
- 10 g d'estragon

Steps:
1. In a mixing bowl, put: the yolks, mustard, garlic, vinegar, anchovies, Parmesan, lemon juice, salt and pepper. Mix everything and add the peanut oil and olive oil like a mayonnaise, then at the end the chopped tarragon.
2. grand finale
3. Dans un cul de poule mettre : les jaunes, la moutarde, l'ail, le vinaigre, les anchois, le parmesan, le jus de citron, le sel et le poivre. Mixer le tout et ajouter l'huile d'arachide et l'huile d'olive comme une mayonnaise, puis à la fin l'estragon haché.
4. grande finale
