name: Apple Crio-Concentrate
text:
Recipe ID: crio-concentrado_de_manzana_merged
Name: Apple Crio-Concentrate

Ingredients:
- 20 kg Granny Smith apples.
- 10 l water.
- 50 g ascorbic acid.
- Pectinex Ultra SP-L
- Granulated Xanthan Gum.
- 20 kg Granny Smith apples.
- 10 l water.
- 50 g ascorbic acid.
- Pectinex Ultra SP-L
- Granulated Xanthan Gum.

Steps:
1. Mise en Place
2. Prepare a bath of cold water and ascorbic acid.
3. Remove the core from the apples, cut them into quarters and immerse them in the ascorbic acid bath for a few minutes to prevent oxidation of the apple juice.
4. Development
5. Liquefy the apples.
6. Package the smoothie in vacuum bags using the maximum vacuum program to prevent air bubbles from remaining in the bag.
7. Freeze.
8. Chop the frozen juice in the ice chopper to get an "apple slush".
9. Serve 4 or 5 tablespoons of apple slush in the drum of the Robot Coupe Prescouli cl40. Activate the blender and collect the juice that is extracted by centrifugation. Stop centrifugation when you observe that the apple slush has stopped being green and has become white.
10. Discard the white ice.
11. Clarification
12. Add 30 drops of Pectinex Ultra SP-L per liter of crio-concentrate.
13. Let the enzymes act for 48 hours, without shaking or stirring so that the impurities settle.
14. Extract the crio-concentrated and clarified juice, being careful not to cloud it.
15. Texturize with granulated xanthan gum using 0.2 g per liter of crio-concentrate.
16. Vacuum pack in 150 ml bags.

Other details:
Para: 1 l.

name: Dal
text:
Recipe ID: dal_parsed
Name: Dal

Ingredients:
- 2 cup of mura dal
- 1 cup chana dal
- 2 tsp turmeric
- 1 tsp salt
- 1 cup of boiling water
- 1 tomato finely dice
- 1 pinch of green chopped chilli
- 10 curry leaves
- 3 boriamichi chili ( round dry chili)
- half cinnamon stick
- 13 cloves
- 1 tsp mustard seed
- 1 tsp of cumin seeds
- 1 tsp fenugreek seed
- 4 tbsp of oil
- 2 kashmiri chili (de-seeded)
- 1 tsp of cumin seeds
- 1 green chili chopped
- 4 sliced garlic
- 2 sliced red onion
- 1 tomato finely chopped
- 1 tsp of turmeric
- salt to taste
- little bit of water
- 100 gr fresh chopped coriander
- 50 grams of ghee

Steps:
1. Soak 10 minutes in water 2 cup of mura dal and 1 cup chana dal
2. Wash 3 times the soaked dal, add them to a pressure pot together with 2 tsp turmeric and 1 tsp salt and 3 finger of water, proceed to boil for 3 whistle period time ( until dal are crashed) set aside and let it rest
3. Once rested add 1 cup of boiling water in the pan with 1 tomato finely dice, 1 pinch of green chopped chilli 10 curry leaves, 3 boriamichi chili ( round dry chili), half cinnamon stick, 13 cloves,  and 1 tsp mustard seed, 1 tsp of cumin seeds and 1 tsp fenugreek seed bring to the boil and turn the fire off
4. Proceed to prepare dal fry masala by heating a pan 4 tbsp of oil in a pan, toast 2 kashmiri chili (de-seeded), 1 tsp of cumin seeds, 1 green chili chopped, 4 sliced garlic and 2 sliced red onion, sweat all together for 3 minutes add 1 tomato finely chopped and 1 tsp of turmeric and roast for 2 minutes
5. Mix the Dal with Masala add salt to taste, little bit of water, 100 gr fresh chopped coriander and 50 grams of ghee, and cook everything together for 2 more minute until ingredients are combined properly

Other details:


Classifications:
- Dish Type: Main Course
- Cuisine: Indian
- Difficulty: Level 2 (Intermediate / Professional)
- Main Ingredient: Vegetable-Forward
- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Nut-Free, Vegetarian, Alcohol-Free

- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

Once rested add 1 cup of boiling water in the pan with 1 tomato finely dice, 1 pinch of green chopped chilli 10 curry leaves, 3 boriamichi chili ( round dry chili), half cinnamon stick, 13 cloves,  and 1 tsp mustard seed, 1 tsp of cumin seeds and 1 tsp fenugreek seed bring to the boil and turn the fire off

Proceed to prepare dal fry masala by heating a pan 4 tbsp of oil in a pan, toast 2 kashmiri chili (de-seeded), 1 tsp of cumin seeds, 1 green chili chopped, 4 sliced garlic and 2 sliced red onion, sweat all together for 3 minutes add 1 tomato finely chopped and 1 tsp of turmeric and roast for 2 minutes

Mix the Dal with Masala add salt to taste, little bit of water, 100 gr fresh chopped coriander and 50 grams of ghee, and cook everything together for 2 more minute until ingredients are combined properly

name: Elderflower Sponge
text:
Recipe ID: elderflower_sponge_parsed
Name: Elderflower Sponge

Ingredients:
- 230 g sugar
- 3 eggs
- 1 egg yolk
- 84 g lemon juice
- 168 g elderflower oil*
- 155 g OO flour
- 14 g baking powder

Steps:
1. Whip the eggs with the sugar in a stand mixer to soft peaks.
2. Then emulsify the oil in by drizzling it in slowly.
3. Follow with the lemon juice.
4. Stop the mixer, remove the bowl and then sift in the flour and baking powder.
5. Replace the bowl, reduce the mixer’s speed and turn it on to incorporate the dry ingredients until everything is homogenized, then stop the mixer.
6. Transfer the mixture to 2 full sized gastro trays, lightly oiled then lined with a sheet of parchment each.
7. Bake the sponge in a preheated combination oven at 160°C for 15-20 minutes at 50% fan.
8. Once cooked, remove it from the from the oven and allow it to cool in its pan.
9. Punch out rounds of the sponge, Brush them lightly with elderflower oil and grill them on the BBQ until lightly colored on both sides.
10. Cut the discs of sponge in half, width wise, and smear the elderflower paste on each of the cut faces.

Other details:
temperature: 160°C
cook_time: 15-20 minutes

Classifications:
- Dish Type: Petit Fours / Mignardise
- Cuisine: British
- Difficulty: Level 2 (Intermediate / Professional)

- Dietary: Vegetarian, Nut-Free, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Fine Dining / Plated
- Prep Style: Baking / Pastry
- Seasonality: Spring

name: Peanut Crunch:
text:
Recipe ID: peanut_crunch:_merged
Name: Peanut Crunch:

Ingredients:
- Chopped peanuts: 120gr
- White bread: 120gr
- Water: 75gr
- Glucose: 25gr
- Icing sugar: 40gr
- Caster sugar: 20gr
- Cornflour: 5gr
- Egg: 1
- Butter, melted: 40gr

Steps:
1. In a pan, dissolve the water, glucose and caster sugar. Heat to 160°C.
2. Mix in the chopped peanuts and pour onto baking parchment. Place another sheet of parchment over the top and roll out as thinly as possible. Leave to cool.
3. When fully hardened, chop the biscuit finely.
4. Finely dice the chopped bread and bake at 140°C, until golden brown.
5. Combine the cornflour and icing sugar in a large bowl and stir in the chopped peanuts caramel and toasted bread.
6. Add the melted butter, egg into the mixture (above).
7. Sprinkle the dough onto a baking sheet and bake at 160°C for 15 minutes. Break into pieces.
8. Bag and keep in the dry store. (Label 1 month)

Other details:

name: Crème Brûlée
text:
Recipe ID: crème_brûlée_merged
Name: Crème Brûlée

Ingredients:
- Milk: 125 ml
- Fresh Cream: 500 ml
- Eggs: 8 yolks
- Vanilla: 1 pod
- Sugar: 130 g + 3 tablespoons

Steps:
1. 1 Preheat the oven to 180°C. Pour the milk, cream, vanilla seeds (extracted from the pod) and the pod itself into a saucepan, then slowly bring to just below boiling point.
2. 2 Meanwhile, gently mix the egg yolks and 130 g of sugar in a bowl with a wooden spoon, without whisking or creating foam. When the milk is about to boil, turn off the heat, remove the vanilla pod and slowly pour the hot mixture into the egg yolks, stirring constantly until you obtain a smooth mixture.
3. 3 Divide the cream into six single-serving ovenproof ramekins, which you will place in a baking tray containing boiling water that should cover them by a third. Bake for about 50-60 minutes at 180°C. When the surface of the cream is firm and golden brown, remove the ramekins from the oven and let them cool.

Other details:
Difficoltà:: ★
Cottura:: 60'
Preparazione:: 10'
Dosi:: 6 persone

- 20 ml Rice Vinegar
- 20 ml Sesame Oil
- 4 Egg White

Steps:
1. Defrost the Prawns and Squid.
2. Dice down the squid to help it blend.
3. Blend the prawns and squid down in batches with a little water if needeed to help get it smooth.
4. Place a little amout of the puree back into the thermo with the rest of the ingredients apart from the egg whites with a little more puree on top to help blend.
5. Place everything into Kitchen aid mixing bowl and mix untill combined.
6. More squid ink may need to be aded to get the right colour

Other details:
Total Time: nan min
NOTES: Make sure the mix doesn’t get to warm during the blending process

name: Mushroom, Pine Nut And Garlic
text:
Recipe ID: mushroom,_pine_nut_and_garlic_merged
Name: Mushroom, Pine Nut And Garlic

Ingredients:
- For the Mushroom stock
- 80g Dried ceps
- 80g Shiitake flakes
- 200g Burnt onion
- 20g Dried kombu
- 4l Water
- For the Pine nut sauce
- 200g Roasted Pine nuts
- 200g Roasted Peanuts
- 20g White miso paste
- 50g Cooking Sake
- 50g Leeks
- 50g Garlic
- 100g Onion
- For the Mushrooms
- 200g King oyster mushrooms
- Extras
- Wild garlic oils
- Wild garlic flowers
- Garlic capers

Steps:
1. Add all ingredients in stockpot bring to the boil then simmer gently for one hour.
2. Pass through a fine sieve and reserve.
3. Reduce the stock further if necessary and season.
4. Used the leftover mushrooms to be freeze-dried and turned into power.
5. Finely chop the garlic, leeks and onion, then brown them in a little olive oil without browning.
6. Add the sake and reduce. Once reduced add the miso and cook for a further 2 minutes.
7. Add the mushroom stock and pine nuts and bring to the boil.
8. Remove from heat and blend to a smooth sauce.
9. Season with salt.
10. Using a meat slicer or mandolin, slice the mushroom into inner thick discs.
11. Next cut the slices into thin strips resembling spaghetti.

Other details:

- Main Ingredient: Vegetable-Forward
- Dietary: Vegetarian, Vegan, Gluten-Free

- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

name: Apple Strudel
text:
Recipe ID: apple_strudel_merged
Name: Apple Strudel

Ingredients:
- FOR THE DOUGH
- Lukewarm water: 100 ml
- Flour: 250 g
- Oil: 2 tablespoons
- Salt: 1 pinch
- Eggs (large): 1
- FOR THE FILLING
- Butter: 100 g
- Melted butter: 100 g
- Ground cinnamon: 2 teaspoons
- Lemons: 1 (grated zest)
- Golden Apples: 1.5 kg
- Breadcrumbs: 150 g
- Pine nuts: 50 g
- Rum: 4 tablespoons
- Sultanas: 100 g
- Sugar: 120 g
- FOR THE TOPPING
- Powdered sugar: as needed

Steps:
1. When I decided to leave the desk to move - me and my kitchen - into that space that lies within our lines, Asdrì was happy. And terrified. One night I woke up in a panic: what if it went wrong? What if I wasn't up to it? To the decline "what if..." I had to engage my head in something constructive, so I started making strudel: with puff pastry. Late in the morning I finally cut myself a slice inside there was my childhood, my land, my grandmother's teachings, years of dedication to learn how to do it like her. There was my passion for things that change shape, my love for food. There was me. Then yes, I understood that that was my way: my choice, and that it was time to make it.
2. Difficulty: ★★
3. Preparation: 70'
4. Cooking: 40'
5. Serves: 6 people
6. 1 Pour the flour in a fountain shape on a pastry board (or in a bowl) and add the egg, salt and oil. Knead vigorously, slowly adding water as needed to make the dough firm, smooth and elastic. When you are done, form a ball, grease it with oil, cover it with plastic wrap and let it rest for half an hour in a cool place.
7. 2 Meanwhile, soak the raisins in rum or, if you prefer, in lukewarm water and toast the breadcrumbs in butter.
8. 3 Then peel the apples, remove the core and cut them first into four wedges, then into thin slices. In a large bowl, mix them with the squeezed raisins, ground cinnamon, sugar, pine nuts and grated lemon zest. When the ingredients are mixed, let it rest.

I had to engage my head in something constructive, so I started making strudel: with puff pastry. Late in the morning I finally cut myself a slice inside there was my childhood, my land, my grandmother's teachings, years of dedication to learn how to do it like her. There was my passion for things that change shape, my love for food. There was me. Then yes, I understood that that was my way: my choice, and that it was time to make it.
Difficulty: ★★
Preparation: 70'
Cooking: 40'
Serves: 6 people

1 Pour the flour in a fountain shape on a pastry board (or in a bowl) and add the egg, salt and oil. Knead vigorously, slowly adding water as needed to make the dough firm, smooth and elastic. When you are done, form a ball, grease it with oil, cover it with plastic wrap and let it rest for half an hour in a cool place.
2 Meanwhile, soak the raisins in rum or, if you prefer, in lukewarm water and toast the breadcrumbs in butter.

3 Then peel the apples, remove the core and cut them first into four wedges, then into thin slices. In a large bowl, mix them with the squeezed raisins, ground cinnamon, sugar, pine nuts and grated lemon zest. When the ingredients are mixed, let it rest.

name: Salmon Belly Candy, Home Cured
text:
Recipe ID: bombón_de_salmón,_curado_en_casa_merged
Name: Salmon Belly Candy, Home Cured

Ingredients:
- The salmon belly:
- Salmon belly
- Isomalt
- The sauerkraut powder:
- Fermented cabbage
- The salmon belly:
- Salmon belly
- Isomalt
- The sauerkraut powder:
- Fermented cabbage
- 4 units cured tuna belly
- Sauerkraut powder
- 4 units cured tuna belly
- Sauerkraut powder

Steps:
1. The salmon belly
2. Verify that the belly does not have bones, and
3. remove them with de-boning tweezers.
4. Cut strips of 3 cm wide, and then make
5. cubes of 15 to 17 grams, it is important that the
6. cubes have skin.
7. Serve the belly cubes in a 3% brine
8. for 5 minutes, drain and dry well with a cloth.
9. In a heat-sealing box, place a layer of
10. isomalt, put the salmon cubes, with a
11. separation of 2 cm and cover with isomalt, it is
12. important that the top layer is greater than 5 cm.
13. Place the trays inside a vacuum bag for
14. preservation and seal on program 1, leave the bags
15. in the chamber for 12 hours.
16. Once this time has elapsed, remove the
17. belly from the isomalt, serve them on a tray with
18. water to remove all excess isomalt, drain
19. well and let dry with a cloth, reserve in the chamber.
20. The sauerkraut powder:
21. Serve the sauerkraut on grill trays and smoke with
22. vine shoots for 10 minutes.
23. After smoking, dry in the oven at 80 degrees
24. for 10 hours with ventilation at speed 3
25. (Cover the ventilation with a tray so that the
26. sauerkraut doesn't fly away)
27. When the sauerkraut is well dry, grind it in a
28. tabletop processor at speed 9 for 5
29. minutes, to obtain a very fine powder.
30. Vacuum pack in 100 gram bags.
31. The salmon belly
32. Verify that the belly does not have bones, and
33. remove them with de-boning tweezers.
34. Cut strips of 3 cm wide, and then make
35. cubes of 15 to 17 grams, it is important that the
36. cubes have skin.
37. Serve the belly cubes in a 3% brine
38. for 5 minutes, drain and dry well with a cloth.
39. In a heat-sealing box, place a layer of
40. isomalt, put the salmon cubes, with a
41. separation of 2 cm and cover with isomalt, it is
42. important that the top layer is greater than 5 cm.
43. Place the trays inside a vacuum bag for
44. preservation and seal on program 1, leave the bags
45. in the chamber for 12 hours.
46. Once this time has elapsed, remove the
47. belly from the isomalt, serve them on a tray with
48. water to remove all excess isomalt, drain
49. well and let dry with a cloth, reserve in the chamber.
50. The sauerkraut powder:
51. Serve the sauerkraut on grill trays and smoke with
52. vine shoots for 10 minutes.
53. After smoking, dry in the oven at 80 degrees
54. for 10 hours with ventilation at speed 3
55. (Cover the ventilation with a tray so that the
56. sauerkraut doesn't fly away)
57. When the sauerkraut is well dry, grind it in a
58. tabletop processor at speed 9 for 5
59. minutes, to obtain a very fine powder.
60. Vacuum pack in 100 gram bags.
61. FINISHING AND PRESENTATION
62. With lively and intense embers, on the level of height closest to the embers, place the tuna belly first on the meat side for 1 minute and turn them on the skin side for 2 minutes.
63. The success of this recipe depends on the amount of heat from the embers, so that the salmon is crispy on the outside and fatty on the inside.
64. Coat them in the sauerkraut powder and serve very hot.

Other details:
INGREDIENTES: (Para 4 personas.)
ELABORACIÓN: None

name: Orange Marmalade
text:
Recipe ID: la_mermelada_de_naranja:_merged
Name: Orange Marmalade

Ingredients:
- 15 oranges
- 60% sugar (of the orange's weight)
- 8g cardamom
- 5g pink peppercorns
- 2 g star anise
- 1 g clove

Steps:
1. Orange Marmalade:
2. Peel the oranges, removing all the white pith.
3. Lightly chop the segments to extract some juice. Serve or in a saucepan adding 60% of its weight in sugar and the spices.
4. Cook first over high heat until the liquid evaporates, and then lower the heat and cook lightly until the sugar caramelizes.
5. Process in a tabletop processor until you get a fine marmalade.

name: Black Olive Togo
text:
Recipe ID: black_olive_togo_merged
Name: Black Olive Togo

Ingredients:
- For the base dough
- Softened butter 120g
- Wheat flour 120g
- Fine salt 0.3g
- Egg white 100g
- For the olive powder
- Black olive puree 150g
- For the togo
- Base dough 200g
- Olive puree 50g
- Glucose 60g

Steps:
1. Spread the olive puree between two silpats, let it dry next to the oven for at least 24 hours, crumble to obtain a sort of black powder.
2. For the base dough
3. Combine the softened butter with the salt, add the flour, mix well and finally add the egg whites.
4. For the togo
5. Add the previously heated glucose to the base dough, add the olive puree and let it rest for at least 2 hours.
6. Roll out the dough between two silpats, cut out 10 x 5 cm rectangles, sprinkle with the dried olives and bake in the oven at 160° C for at least 4-5 minutes.
7. Remove from the oven and wrap the rectangles on 1 cm diameter steel tubes, thus obtaining small togos, warm them by hand before serving.

Other details:
Ricetta di: Andrea

name: Chocolate, Passion Fruit And Summer Savory
text:
Recipe ID: chocolate,_passion_fruit_and_summer_savory_merged
Name: Chocolate, Passion Fruit And Summer Savory

Ingredients:
- For the Summer savoury oil
- 50g Summer savory
- 500g Neutral oil
- For the Summer savoury merengue
- 200g Sugar
- 100g Egg white
- 100g Winter savoury oil
- For the Chocolate mousse
- 154g Milk
- 154g Yolks
- 27g Trimoline
- 12g Gelatine
- 100g Passion fruit pure
- 100g Chocolate
- For the Caramelized sesame
- 60g Black sesame
- 60g Sugar
- Water

Steps:
1. PROCESS
2. Pick all leaves from the stalk.
3. Blanch the leaves in boiling water then stop in ice water.
4. Blanch water blanched leaves with the oil in the thermomix at 70C for 5 mins.
5. Pass through muslin onto an ice bath.
6. Store in fridge.
7. Add a little water the sugar and cook to 121C.
8. While the sugar is cooking start whipping the egg whites in the machine.
9. Once the sugar has reached 121C add it slowly to the whites to form an Italian merengue.
10. Once the merengue is stable and stiff add the cold oil a little at a time.
11. Store in piping bag.
12. Bring the milk to the boil, yolks.
13. Mix together the yolks and the trimoline whisking continually.
14. Add the Boiling milk to the yolk and trimoline
15. Cook the mixture to 84C.
16. Once cooked pass on to the chocolate.
17. Using a hand blender blend the emulsify the mixture.
18. Blend in the passion fruit pure.
19. Semi whip the cream and fold into the chocolate mix.
20. Store in Container in the fridge.
21. In a pan bring the water and the sugar up to 117C.
22. Once it reaches temperature add the black sesame and mix until it is completely covered.

Other details:

- Seasonality: Summer

name: Coffee Cake
text:
Recipe ID: torta_al_caffè_merged
Name: Coffee Cake

Ingredients:
- FOR THE BASE
- Sugar: 220 g
- Hazelnut Flour: 150 g
- Flour 00: 75 g
- Potato Starch: 75 g
- Butter: 250 g
- Eggs (medium): 6
- Instant Coffee: 10 g
- Baking Powder: 1 packet
- FOR THE COFFEE CREAM
- Milk: 500 ml
- Butter: 250 g
- Granulated Sugar: 200 g
- Eggs (medium): 4 yolks
- Gelatin Sheets: 20 g
- Instant Coffee: 20 g
- Fresh Cream: 500 ml
- Powdered Sugar: 50 g
- FOR THE COFFEE SYRUP
- Instant Coffee: 10 g
- Hot Water: 100 ml
- Sugar: as needed

Steps:
1. Start with the base, creaming the butter and egg yolks, one at a time, without stopping beating. Then add the instant coffee, which you will have previously dissolved in a tablespoon of boiling water. Beat the egg whites until stiff peaks form and, while still beating, add the remaining sugar a little at a time.
2. Pour the butter and yolk mixture into a large bowl where you will add the toasted hazelnut flour, Flour 00, potato starch and sifted baking powder, soften everything with two tablespoons of beaten egg whites, then gently fold in the rest of the egg whites. Pour everything into a 24 cm diameter cake tin that has been previously buttered and floured and bake at 180°C for 45-50 minutes (test with a toothpick to check if it is cooked). Remove the base from the oven when it is cooked and remove it from the mold once it is lukewarm.
3. In the meantime, prepare the coffee cream. Soak the gelatin sheets in very cold water until softened and pour the milk and egg yolks into a saucepan. Bring to just before boiling, then add the granulated sugar, instant coffee and cubed butter, then mix, simmer for 2 minutes, turn off the heat and pour the cream into a wide, shallow container. Cover the cream with plastic wrap, making it adhere to the surface, this will prevent it from hardening. Put it in the refrigerator to cool and thicken for 3-4 hours. When the cream is cold, gelatinous and compact, process it with the blades of a mixer to make it creamy again (if there are lumps, use a sieve) and gently mix it with the cream, whipped with the powdered sugar, then put the mixture in the refrigerator for at least half an hour.
4. Then dissolve the instant coffee in 100 ml of boiling water (or prepare 100 ml of coffee with a moka pot) and sweeten it with sugar (amount to taste). Let the syrup cool.
5. Cut the cake base in half and soak the lower surface with half of the syrup, then spread plenty of cream on it and cover with the other half of the base, the surface of which you will soak with the remaining syrup. Cover the entire cake with cream. With what is left over, you can fill a pastry bag and decorate the cake, which you will have to leave in the refrigerator until ready to serve.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★★★
Preparazione:: 240'
Cottura:: 60'
Dosi:: 10 persone

name: Korean Lasagna
text:
Recipe ID: lasaa_coreana_en_parsed
Name: Korean Lasagna

Ingredients:
- 20kg ground meat (Lyo)
- 16kg vine tomatoes
- 8 kg red onions
- 1 kg garlic cloves
- 1472kg chili garlic (crushed)
- 4 l kimchi
- 4l sherry
- 5kg pickled shiitake mushrooms
- 250g Chinese five spice
- 4 units palm sugar

Steps:
1. a) Chop all the vegetables very small and sauté until golden brown.
2. b) Once the sofrito is ready, add the chili garlic and fry a little with the sofrito, then add the wine and let it evaporate.
3. c) Add the meat to the sofrito and mix very well, always over the heat, once the meat is loose.
4. d) Finally, season with the pickled shiitake mushrooms, 5 spice, and kimchi.
5. e) Set aside for later use.
6. f) The meat does not need to be cooked for long.

name: Puff Pastry
text:
Recipe ID: puff_pastry_merged
Name: Puff Pastry

Ingredients:
- FOR THE DOUGH
- Manitoba flour: 150 g
- 00 flour: 200 g
- Water: about 250 ml
- Salt: 8 g
- FOR THE BUTTER BLOCK
- Butter: 250 g
- 00 flour: 75 g

Steps:
1. Start with the preparation of the dough. Mix and sift the two flours, preferably in a stand mixer with a leaf hook, or by hand. Dissolve the salt in the water and knead everything at medium speed for about 8 minutes. You need to obtain a softer and more elastic mixture than bread dough, and, of course, smooth and homogeneous. Let it rest, covered with a cloth, in a cool place for at least half an hour.
2. In the meantime, take care of the butter block. Take the butter out of the fridge, cut it into cubes and put it in the stand mixer with the 75 g of flour: mix until you get an amalgamated and lump-free mixture, which you will reduce into a square about 1 centimeter thick, either with floured hands or by placing it between two sheets of parchment paper and flattening it with the help of a rolling pin. Place the butter block in the lower part of the refrigerator - the coldest - for at least half an hour.
3. Roll out the dough: you will get a rectangle of about 50 centimeters in length in the center of which you will place the butter block, now ready (1), which you will cover with the two longest edges of the dough, making them match (2). Also fold the other two, until you close the butter block on each side (3). Always remember to flour the work surface and the dough to facilitate the rolling out process.
4. Take a rolling pin and apply pressure on the dough, so as to make the butter block thinner (4). You can then start rolling out the puff pastry, which must take on a regular rectangular shape. To do this, do not limit yourself to rolling out the puff pastry by moving the rolling pin back and forth (because you risk getting an hourglass-shaped puff pastry), but you must imprint oblique movements, holding the rolling pin along the diagonal of your ideal rectangle. Start by widening the center as well. When you get a width of about 25 centimeters it will be time to proceed with the folds.
5. Fold the two shorter sides so that they match in the center of the longer side (5), then fold the puff pastry like a book, mark it with a pressure of your finger (which will remind you that you have just made the first fold 6), wrap it in plastic wrap and put it in the fridge for at least half an hour.
6. At that point, take out the puff pastry, position it in front of you with the opening on the right and repeat the rolling out and folding operation. Before wrapping it again in plastic wrap and repositioning it in the fridge, mark it twice to remember that you have made the second fold.
7. The whole procedure should be repeated two more times, for a total of four folds.
8. In the end, the dough must be left to rest in the fridge for at least 1 hour. If you do not intend to use it immediately, you can leave it in the plastic wrap and freeze it.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★★
Preparazione: 130'
Dosi: 1 kg di pasta circa

name: Chocolate Fondant
text:
Recipe ID: chocolate_fondant_merged
Name: Chocolate Fondant

Ingredients:
- Whole eggs 24 n°
- Granulated sugar 800
- Chocolate 67% or 70% 1000
- Butter 800

Steps:
1. Combine the sugar with the eggs by hand, making sure not to incorporate air, and let the mixture rest, removing impurities from it 3 times.
2. Melt the chocolate and butter in a bain-marie (double boiler).
3. When the eggs and sugar are free of impurities, gently combine with the chocolate using a rubber spatula.
4. Line small molds in three steps: butter, sugar, and cocoa powder.
5. Bake at 175°C (347°F) with ½ fan speed for 8-10 minutes.

Other details:
Ricetta di: Aprea Andrea

name: Jabugo
text:
Recipe ID: jabugo_en_parsed
Name: Jabugo

Ingredients:
- 3 kg of ham bones.
- 8 white potatoes.
- 14 carrots.
- 1 pig's trotter.
- 3 cow's feet.
- 1kg of knee bones.

Steps:
1. a) We scald the ham bones 3 times, once scalded we select and check the salt level.
2. b) We blanch the knee bones and trotters.
3. c) Let it cook over medium heat for 8 hours covered.
4. d) Then 4 hours uncovered.
5. e) Strain and reduce until the desired texture is achieved.

name: Smoked Duck Breast
text:
Recipe ID: smoked_duck_breast_merged
Name: Smoked Duck Breast

Ingredients:
- CANE SUGAR 500
- ORANGES 3 PCS
- FEMALE DUCK BREAST 4 PCS

Steps:
1. PEEL THE ORANGES, KEEPING ONLY THE ZEST WITHOUT THE WHITE PART, AND LEAVE TO DRY UNDER A LAMP FOR 3 HOURS. BLEND THE CANE SUGAR AND DRIED ZEST IN A BLENDER UNTIL YOU OBTAIN A FINE POWDER (VEIL).
2. LINE THE BASE OF A LARGE POT WITH ALUMINUM FOIL, COVER WITH THE SUGAR OBTAINED, AND INSERT A WIDE-MESH SIEVE. PLACE THE DUCK BREASTS IN THE SIEVE, WRAP EVERYTHING IN PLASTIC WRAP, AND PLACE ON THE STOVE. ONCE THE SUGAR STARTS TO BURN, LOWER THE FLAME AND LEAVE COVERED FOR 8/9 MINUTES.

Other details:

name: Pumpkin Brioche
text:
Recipe ID: pumpkin_brioche_merged
Name: Pumpkin Brioche

Ingredients:
- 750g flour
- 75g pumpkin puree
- 15g pumpkin powder
- 15g salt
- 60g sugar
- 30g yeast
- 200ml pumpkin juice
- 270g whole eggs
- 225g softened butter

Steps:
1. Weigh all drys in mixing bowl and set aside
2. Combine milk and eggs
3. Using the hook attachment, slowly incorporate the wets with the drys until a dough is formed
4. Gradually add the butter until it is all incorporated into the dough
5. Cling film dough and rest for 24hrs

name: Passion Base
text:
Recipe ID: passion_base_merged
Name: Passion Base

Ingredients:
- 1KG CATSER CUGAR
- 400ML WATER
- 1.5L FRESH PASSIONFRUIT JUICE
- 2L PASSIONFRUIT BOIRON PUREE
- 180G CORNFLOUR

Steps:
1. ADD A LITTLE OF THE PASSIONFRUIT JUICE TO THE CORNFLOUR
2. TO MAKE A SLURRY
3. BRING CASTER SUGAR AND WATER TO 121C
4. ADD WARMED PASSIONFRUIT JUICE TO THE SUGAR AND WHISK TOGETHOR
5. RETURN TO THE BOIL ADD THE CORFLOUR MIX AND RETURN TO THE BOIL ONCE AGAIN
6. STIRRING WITH A WHISK CONSTANTLY
7. PASS INTO A DEEP GASTRO AND COVER WITH PARCHMENT SO AS NOT TO FORM A SKIN
8. COOL TO ROOM TEMP
9. BEAT WITH A WHISK TO SMOOTH IT OUT AGAIN
10. WEIGH INTO SMALL SOUS VIDE BAGS AT 200G

name: Taro
text:
Recipe ID: taro_parsed
Name: Taro

Ingredients:
- Taro
- Salt
- Water

Steps:
1. a) Boil water with salt
2. b) Clean the taro with skin and put to cook for 15/20 minutes

name: Basil Pesto
text:
Recipe ID: pesto_di_basilico_merged
Name: Basil Pesto

Ingredients:
- Blanched basil 100 GRAMS
- Grated Parmesan cheese 80
- Toasted pine nuts 100
- Grated Pecorino cheese 20
- Olive oil 80

Steps:
1. Previously blanch the basil in plenty of salted water. Cool it in ice water, drain it, and squeeze out all the excess water well. Once you have obtained 100 grams of “blanched basil,” add all the other ingredients to a bowl, mixing everything well. Fill a Pacojet container and blast freeze to – 20. Pacotize everything. Blast freeze again. Shortly before using, pacotize a second time and pass it through a fine-mesh sieve with the help of a spatula. Store the emulsion in a pastry bag.

name: Rice Fermented Veg
text:
Recipe ID: rice_fermented_veg_parsed
Name: Rice Fermented Veg

Ingredients:
- 1 kg Chilli
- 50 g Salt
- 500 g Congee liquid

Steps:
1. Cook congee in ratio of 1:10= rice:water
2. Leave congee to cool down to 30C
3. Wash the mustard green with cold water
4. Drain properly and salt the leaves
5. Lightly rub the salt into the leaves and line them in glass jar
6. Once the congee is cool down to around 30C (need to be luke warm still)
7. Pass through the warm congee through a sieve and pour into the veg to cover, discard the rice grain
8. Let it ferment for at least a week. May take up to 10days
9. Taste and refridgerate

Other details:
Total Time: nan min
Notes: ['Try whole veg', 'Try chopped up veg', 'Try cheap Jasmin rice', 'Keep an eye on how long it take to fermente', 'Try fermenting in vac pack bags']

text:
Recipe ID: turbot_with_sea_herbs_merged
Name: Turbot With Sea Herbs

1. 1. Using a hand blender, dissolve the salt in the water

14. Blend together

name: Malviz Broth
text:
Recipe ID: caldo_de_malviz_merged
Name: Malviz Broth

Ingredients:
- The broth:
- 800 g. malviz bones
- 850 g. caramelized onion
- 70 g. brandy
- 60g. olive oil
- 50g. Iberian bacon
- 2l. poultry broth
- 1l. broth of the day
- 160 g. hazelnuts
- 160g. almonds
- 7g. parsley

Steps:
1. PREVIOUS NOTE:
2. The malvices should be plucked very carefully, trying to preserve the integrity of the breast skin as much as possible.
3. Eviscerate, keeping the intestines intact to avoid contamination.
4. Both the viscera, head, legs, and wing tips will be discarded.
5. The breasts will be used for the dish while the bones will be used for the broth.
6. ELABORATION:
7. The broth:
8. Heat the olive oil and Iberian bacon in a pot.
9. Brown the malviz bones in the fat.
10. Add the caramelized onion and deglaze with the brandy.
11. Add the poultry broth and the broth of the day.
12. Reduce until you get a volume of 1 l.

Other details:

steps: PREVIOUS NOTE:
The malvices should be plucked very carefully, trying to preserve the integrity of the breast skin as much as possible.
Eviscerate, keeping the intestines intact to avoid contamination.
Both the viscera, head, legs, and wing tips will be discarded.
The breasts will be used for the dish while the bones will be used for the broth.
ELABORATION:
The broth:
Heat the olive oil and Iberian bacon in a pot.
Brown the malviz bones in the fat.
Add the caramelized onion and deglaze with the brandy.
Add the poultry broth and the broth of the day.
Reduce until you get a volume of 1 l.

name: Ledbury Lemon Tart
text:
Recipe ID: ledbury_lemon_tart_merged
Name: Ledbury Lemon Tart

Ingredients:
- 1040G FRESH LEMON JUICE
- ZEST OF ABOVE LEMONS
- 1560G CASTER SUGAR
- 24G LEMON VERBENA
- 1200G UHT CREAM
- 360G LIQUID EGG YOLKS
- 16 WHOLE EGGS
- 1360 SUGA
- 30G BERGAMOT
- 400 Youk
- 1200 UHT
- 16 EGGS

Steps:
1. LEMON FILLING MAKES 6 TARTS (BST MOULD)
2. WEIGH LEMON ZEST, JUICE, VERBENA AND SUGAR TOGETHOR
3. ADD TO YOLKS AND WHOLE EGGS IN BAIN MARIE
4. STIR TOGETHOR GENTLY WITH A MAURICE
5. WARM CREAM TO 65C
6. ADD TO YOLK MIX AND COOK UNTIL 65C GENTLY
7. STIRRING ALL THE TIME WITH A MAURICE
8. PASS THROUGH A FINE CHINOIS, SKIM OFF ANY SCUM
9. BAKE AT 94C FAN 3 FOR 45MIN-1HR
10. REST, TEST AND PRAY

name: Sablés
text:
Recipe ID: sablés_merged
Name: Sablés

Ingredients:
- X50pcs.
- 220g flour
- 120g butter
- 225g sugar
- 1 egg white
- Flavoring
- Sablés 2^
- x50pcs.
- 225g butter
- 320g flour
- 110g powdered sugar
- Flavoring
- Sablés 3^
- X50pcs
- 260g flour
- 40g cocoa
- 250g butter
- 120g sugar
- 1 egg white
- 2g salt
- Sablés
- X75pcs
- 400g puff pastry
- 500g sugar
- 10g coffee
- Vanilla

Steps:
1. Beat butter + sugar + eggs + the rest and the chocolate and walnuts into small pieces. Make cylinders 6-7cm in diameter. Refrigerate for 6 hours. Cut into 1cm thick discs. Bake at 170°C for 12 minutes.
2. Whip butter + sugar + egg white + everything else and the flour. Pipe and streak and bake at 170°C for 15 minutes.
3. Knead like a plum cake, shape into cylinders 3 cm in diameter. Refrigerate for 6 hours. Cut into 1 cm thick slices. Bake at 170°C for 15-18 minutes.
4. Roll out puff pastry to 3mm thick. Spread the other ingredients on top. Make cylinders 4cm in diameter. Freeze, cut into 3mm thick discs and bake. Bake on low. Bake at 220°C for 6-7 minutes.

- 2 beef shanks
- 1 beef foot
- 1 beef snout
- 20 white onions
- 15 tomatoes
- 12 carrots
- 6 leeks
- 2 liters of red wine

4. d) Strain, degrease, and reduce by half.

name: Pickled Shiitake Mushrooms
text:
Recipe ID: shitakes_encurtidos_es_parsed
Name: Pickled Shiitake Mushrooms

Ingredients:
- 1200g dried shiitake mushrooms
- 420g chopped ginger
- 1500g sherry vinegar
- 1500g shiitake mushroom water
- 900g sugar
- 60g fish sauce

Steps:
1. a) In a pot, combine the dried shiitake mushrooms and water and cook until the shiitake mushrooms are hydrated and tender.
2. b) Let cool in the pot to further concentrate the shiitake mushroom flavor.
3. c) Reserve the water to make the marinade with the remaining ingredients, bringing it to a boil to homogenize the mixture.
4. d) Add the shiitake mushrooms, cut into julienne strips, and let marinate for a minimum of 24 hours before use.
5. e) If the shiitake mushrooms are still dry when cut, cook them in the finished marinade.

Other details:

name: Cordon Bleu
text:
Recipe ID: cordon_bleu_merged
Name: Cordon Bleu

Ingredients:
- FOR THE CORDON BLEU
- Chicken (breast): 4 thick slices (about 120 g each)
- Cooked ham: 4 slices (about 100 g)
- Smoked Scamorza cheese, sliced: 80 g
- FOR THE BREADING
- Eggs: 2-3
- Breadcrumbs: as needed
- Salt: as needed
- FOR FRYING
- Vegetable oil: as needed

Steps:
1. Cut each slice horizontally, leaving 3 of the 4 sides joined; you will get a pocket, which you will fill with 20 g of sliced scamorza, on which you will place a slice of cooked ham (about 20-25 g).
2. When you have finished stuffing all 4 slices, dedicate yourself to the breading, passing the cordon bleu first in the beaten egg with salt (and pepper, if desired) on both sides and then in the breadcrumbs, making sure that they are perfectly covered with the egg and that the breadcrumbs adhere well and evenly to the entire surface. Remember to keep the edges of the meat tightly closed, so that they seal and nothing comes out during frying. If you want, you can help seal the pocket with toothpicks.
3. Then place the cordon bleu in a pan with plenty of hot (not boiling) oil and fry them over low heat, so as to obtain a nice crispy and golden crust on the outside and a perfect cooking on the inside. Once ready, remove them from the heat, dry the excess oil with paper towels and serve.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★★
Preparazione: 20'
Cottura: 10'
Dosi: 4 persone

name: Smoked Mussel Cream
text:
Recipe ID: smoked_mussel_cream_merged
Name: Smoked Mussel Cream

Ingredients:
- 250g mussel meat
- 1 ½ bronze gelatin leaf
- 100g mussel stock
- 50g grape seed oil
- 100mi whipping cream

Steps:
1. Rinse mussels under cold water for 30 mins.
2. De-beard and grade into small and large.
3. Use a roundel with a lid, heat until quite hot then add the large mussels and 60ml water.
4. The pan should instantly steam. Cover with lid and steam for 3 minute, check that the mussels have opened, then tip into a colander and reserve cooking liquor (mussel stock
5. Repeat above with small mussels.
6. Once all mussels are cooked, remove from shell and check for any debris and lay out flat around colander.
7. Wrap the top of the colander with foil.
8. Add a small handful of hay to the bowl under the colander and set alight.
9. Quickly place the colander over the flame and wrap with foil to seal in smoke.
10. Smoke for 5 minutes.
11. Soak gelatine in ice water. When soft, dissolve in the mussel stock
12. Place 250g mussels in vita prep.
13. Blend with mussel stock and gelatin. Blend long enough to ensure a smooth puree is formed, then ada 50g grapeseed oil and then cream.
14. Poss through chinois and chili over ice.
15. Season with salt and lemon juice and allow to set over ice then whisk smooth for service.

Other details:

name: Anchovy Air
text:
Recipe ID: anchovy_air_merged
Name: Anchovy Air

Ingredients:
- Fish stock 500
- Shallot 50
- White wine 150
- Pantelleria capers n°5
- Salted anchovies (desalted and boned) n°9
- Olive oil 100

Steps:
1. Sweat the shallot with a little oil, add the anchovies and capers, brown so they don't fall apart, then deglaze with the white wine and reduce. Finally add the fish stock and reduce to 50%, and filter everything through a chinois, and emulsify with the oil.
2. Before using, add 1 gram of soy lecithin.

name: Creamy Sour Potato Veil
text:
Recipe ID: velo_cremoso_de_patata_ácida_merged
Name: Creamy Sour Potato Veil

Ingredients:
- (For 4 people.)
- The potato kombucha:
- 800 g Monalisa potatoes
- 1500 ml water
- 1 kombucha scoby
- 500 g kombucha tea
- 500 g sugar
- The mashed potatoes:
- 800 g Monalisa potatoes
- 20 g butter
- 20 g cream
- 1.2 g salt
- For the cheese powder:
- 40 g Parmesan cheese
- The finish:
- 4 kombucha mother
- 40 g mashed potatoes
- q.s. cheese powder

Steps:
1. ELABORATION:
2. The potato kombucha:
3. Peel and cut the potatoes into irregular pieces and cook in water until overcooked. Once cooked, blend with a hand blender and let cool to room temperature (not exceeding 40°C). Add the sugar and the starter.
4. Disinfect a gastronorm tray and pour in the mixture, reserve for 48 hours at room temperature.
5. When a mother layer of about 5 mm appears, it will be ready. Wash the mother and reserve in gastronorm with film between the mothers.
6. The mashed potatoes:
7. Cook the potatoes with their skin. Peel and blend in a Thermomix with the remaining ingredients at speed 5 at 80°C for ten minutes.
8. The cheese powder:
9. Blend at trem- speed
10. FINISHING AND PRESENTATION
11. Cut the kombucha mother into 5x5 pieces with a cookie cutter. At the time of serving, put a little mashed potato on top of the powder and gratinate in a salamander.

- salt

- 2 tbsp of Kasuri Methi
- 3 cup of milk
- cubed paneer
- fresh coriander

Steps:
1. Boil 500 gr of Spinach in soda water
2. Make onion paste by blending 5 small red onion and 6 garlic cloves
3. Heat oil in a pan, then add 1 tbsp of cumin seed, 4 bay leaves ,toast everything properly and add  the onion paste and cook the water off, after 10 minutes add 15 gr of butter, and carry on cooking it until dry and golden brown.
4. Add 2 tbsp of chilli and ginger paste and salt
5. Blend the cook spinach for obtain a green paste
6. Add it back into the pan, with some coriander, keep cooking it.
7. After 5 minutes add 2 tbsp of Kasuri Methi and 3 cup of milk
8. In a separate pan pour oil for fry cubed paneer, once the oil reach temperature fry Paneer until slightly golden brown, and finish it by put it  in water for take off the excess oil
9. Strain the paneer from the water properly, and put it in the spinach and onion paste in the pan
10. Finish with fresh coriander

name: Tomatoes
text:
Recipe ID: tomatoes_merged
Name: Tomatoes

Ingredients:
- Fermented tomato
- Ripe tomatoes
- Salt

Steps:
1. PREVIOUS NOTE:
2. Conserva Cruda di Pomodoro. The Art of Fermentation, Sandor Ellix Katz, page 117.
3. Fermented tomato has been a way to preserve tomatoes and have them available throughout the year for hundreds of years. It is prepared in the season with the most tomato production.
4. ELABORATION:
5. The fermented tomato:
6. Wash the tomatoes, remove any rotten parts. Squeeze them with your hands in a tall gastromorm.
7. Fermentation will begin spontaneously, it will be quite similar to the fermentation of cabbage. A liquid will appear on the surface (in an acidified medium and promoted by lactic acid bacteria and molds.
8. The gastromorm should not be completely full and must always be covered with a cheesecloth to keep the tomatoes submerged.
9. The gas bubbles will push the solids to the surface, which will appear covered by a white mold.
10. Stir 2 times a day, mixing the mold with the fermenting tomato.
11. After 4 or 5 days, depending on the temperature, the gas will stop the "boiling" completely, we will stop seeing the gas so that solids float on the surface and then to remove the solids that float and the seeds.
12. Remove the solids that float and the seeds.
13. Serve the resulting puree in a clean linen cloth, squeeze it and leave it hanging in the refrigerator for 24 hours to drain all the liquid.
14. The linen cloth may appear covered by a white mold. Remove it by "peeling" it off with a spoon and discarding it.
15. Collect the tomato paste from the inside of the linen cloth. Serve it on a linen cloth or a gastromorm with holes and press it with a weight for a couple of days until the dough has the consistency of a firm dough.
16. Tomatoes in oil:
17. 1 ripe tomato.
18. Extra virgin olive oil
19. 1 head of garlic
20. Mix the dough with 25-30% salt. After a few hours, animate it, preserving it vacuum-packed or in a glass jar. It does not need refrigeration, although it will be stored in the refrigerator.
21. The yield is 8% with respect to the initial weight of tomatoes.
22. The fermented tomato dough is very salty and concentrated, so it should be used sparingly.
23. Tomatoes in oil: crush them by hand. Submerge them and polar the head of garlic for 2 days.
24. Macerate everything in oil for 2 days.
25. Take out the tomatoes and cut them naturally into slices about 4 cm thick and about 60 g.
26. Osmotize the tomatoes with the raw garlic oil.
27. FINISH AND PRESENTATION
28. Drain the tomatoes from the garlic oil bath on a clean linen cloth.
29. Spread the fermented tomato paste on a cutting board.
30. Serve.
31. The fermented tomato

2. Bacetti are a sin of gluttony, a fabulous idea for a party, a cuddle for a snack: you can make them the size you prefer and fill them with whatever you want, always being amazed by the fact that you are perfectly capable of competing (a ruthless competition) with supermarket snacks...

Add the sifted flour and yeast and mix gently, with movements from the bottom up. When the flour is blended, fill a pastry bag with the mixture and on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, form discs of dough equal to each other (with a diameter of about 3 cm: you should get about 60). Bake for about 12 minutes at 190°C (375°F). When the cakes begin to swell and brown at the edges, remove them and let them cool.

name: Civet. Civet Sauce
text:
Recipe ID: civet_salsa_de_civet_en_parsed
Name: Civet. Civet Sauce

Ingredients:
- 40 purple onions
- 20 carrots
- 1kg of garlic
- 1kg of ginger
- 18 packs of extra smoked Bacon (Oscar Mayer)
- 2 liters of white wine
- 480g of curry paste
- 4 tablets of palm sugar
- 12 liters of coconut milk
- 4 liters of chicken broth
- 50ml of Bacon essence (sosa)

Steps:
1. a) Cut all the vegetables into thin julienne strips.
2. b) Brown all the vegetables in a large pot.
3. c) Add the curry paste and fry it.
4. d) Deglaze with the wine and let it evaporate.
5. e) Add the chicken broth and coconut milk along with the palm sugar and a little salt.
6. f) Bring to a boil over high heat, once boiling, let it cook for 5 hours over medium heat.
7. g) To finish the sauce, blend it together with the vegetables in the Thermomix and then strain it through a fine-mesh sieve.
8. h) Chill.

Other details:

name: Sambher Gravy
text:
Recipe ID: sambher_gravy_parsed_2
Name: Sambher Gravy

Ingredients:
- 1 cup of Tura Dal soaked in water
- 25 gr of kashimiri chilli ( clean and de seeded)
- 3 ladle of sunflower oil
- 2 tbsp of mustard seeds
- 1 tbsp of cumin seed
- 1\2 tbsp of fenugreek seed
- 2 tbsp of White Urad Dal
- 2 tbsp of Tur Dal
- 2 tbsp of Chana Dal
- 15 curry leaves
- 2 tsp of Hing
- 1\2 tsp turmeric
- shaved coconut
- 1 handful of tamarind
- 2 red onion
- 2 big tomato
- 3 tbsp of Sambher masala powder
- 2 inch 3 Drumstick
- 250gr of pumping
- 250 gr of aubergine
- 1 cup of water
- salt to taste
- 1 tsp mustard
- 1 tsp cumin seed
- 1\2 tsp of fenugreek seed
- 1 tbsp Sambher powder
- 1 tsp chili powder
- 1 tsp of hing
- 2 green chilli
- 10 curry leaves
- 4 chilli chopped

Steps:
1. Open coconut and shave the pulp
2. 25 gr of kashimiri chilli ( clean and de seeded)  slightly toasted in 3 ladle of sunflower oil in a hot pan, remove the chili and set it aside
3. In the same pan toss 2 tbsp of mustard seeds, 1 tbsp of cumin seed and 1\2 tbsp of fenugreek seed, after the spice are popping add 2 tbsp of White Urad Dal,2 tbsp of Tur Dal and 2 tbsp of Chana Dal, 15 curry leaves 2 tsp of Hing and 1\2 tsp turmeric. Toast everything and add all the shaved coconut mix and toast everything well for 2 minute, then set aside to cool down
4. Wash well the Tura dal precedently soaked and put’em in the pressure pot with 2 finger of water above the dal level
5. Soak 1 handful  of tamarind in water for 10 minutes and strain for obtain the tamarind water
6. Dice 2 red onion and 2 big tomato and put everything in the pressure pot, close it and place it over medium heat fire, cook until 2 whistle and turn off fire and let it rest in the close pressure pot
7. Meanwhile place the coconut mixture in the blender with 3 tbsp of Sambher masala powder, the Kashmiri chili and little bit of water enough to make a smooth and thick paste (GRAVY)
8. Peel and cut 2 inch 3 Drumstick, cube 250gr of pumping and wedge 250 gr of aubergine soak all in water, and put the vegetable with 1 cup of water  in the pressure pot after have been crush slightly the lentil in the pressure pot, without the lid mix well and simmer until vegetable are tender ( but not overcook and mushi)
9. Add 1 ladle of gravy and salt to taste ( approx 3 spoon) and tamarind water
10. In a little pot toast in 2 ladle of oil the spices in this order: 1 tsp mustard, 1 tsp cumin seed and 1\2 tsp of fenugreek seed toast 30 second and then add 1 tbsp Sambher powder, 1 tsp chili powder, 1 tsp of hing, 2 green chilli and 10 curry leaves.
11. Once everything it’s nicely toasted add it         all back in the Sambher gravy, and for Mr jai add 4 chilli chopped

Other details:
servings: 2 people

Classifications:
- Dish Type: Component / Base (Stocks, Sauces, Pastes, Garnishes, Doughs)
- Cuisine: Indian
- Difficulty: Level 2 (Intermediate / Professional)
- Main Ingredient: Vegetable-Forward
- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Vegan, Vegetarian, Nut-Free
- Occasion: Family Style / Sharing
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

name: Grandma'S Cake
text:
Recipe ID: grandma's_cake_merged
Name: Grandma'S Cake

Ingredients:
- FOR THE SHORTCRUST PASTRY
- Butter: 200 g
- 00 Flour: 400 g
- Salt: 1 pinch
- Egg yolks: 4
- Icing sugar: 150 g
- FOR THE PASTRY CREAM
- Flour: 80 g
- Fresh milk: 1 liter
- Egg yolks: 8
- Vanilla: 1 pod
- Sugar: 250 g
- FOR THE TOPPING
- Pine nuts: 120 g
- Vanilla-flavored icing sugar: as needed

Steps:
1. For the shortcrust pastry, refer to the procedure on page 284. When it is ready, shape it into a low loaf, wrap it in plastic wrap and let it rest in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour.
2. In the meantime, prepare the pastry cream according to the procedure illustrated on page 280, adhering to the doses of this recipe.
3. While the cream is cooling in the refrigerator, remove the shortcrust pastry, take about two thirds and roll it out into a disc that can line a cake tin with a diameter of about 24-26 cm, including the sides. Butter and flour the cake tin and cover it with the pastry. Remove the excess by passing the rolling pin over the edges of the cake tin, or by cutting it flush with a small knife with a smooth blade, then prick the pastry with the tines of a fork. Finally, pour in the pastry cream.

Other details:

Cottura: 50'
Preparazione: 30'
Dosi: 10 persone

Finally, pour in the pastry cream.

name: Potato Frittata
text:
Recipe ID: potato_frittata_merged
Name: Potato Frittata

Ingredients:
- Eggs: 6
- Potatoes: 500 g
- Grated Parmigiano Reggiano: 100 g
- Parsley: 1 sprig
- Nutmeg: 1 pinch
- Oil: 4-5 tablespoons
- Salt and pepper: to taste

Steps:
1. Peel the potatoes and cut them into slices about 1 cm thick. Meanwhile, put a saucepan of salted water on the heat; when it comes to a boil, add the potatoes and cook them for about ten minutes, draining them before they fall apart.
2. In the meantime, beat the eggs in a large bowl, where you will add the Parmesan cheese, nutmeg and chopped parsley. Season with salt and pepper, then add the lukewarm potatoes. Mix.
3. Cover the bottom of a pan with a diameter of about 24-26 cm with oil. When it is hot, pour in the mixture and cook for 10-15 minutes over medium heat, covering with a lid. When the frittata comes off the bottom, it will be ready to be turned. Cook for 10-15 minutes on the other side as well, this time without a lid.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Cottura: 45'
Preparazione: 20'
Dosi: 6 persone

name: Thai Souquet
text:
Recipe ID: souquet_thai_en_parsed
Name: Thai Souquet

Ingredients:
- 12 shallots
- 100g garlic + 400g garlic
- 100g ginger
- 8 tomatoes
- 1l white wine
- 1kg small rice shrimp
- 1kg parrot fish
- 1lt coconut milk
- saffron
- water
- 300g almonds
- 200g tom yum

Steps:
1. a) Confit 400g of peeled garlic until golden brown in olive oil. Save.
2. b) Fry almonds until golden brown. Save.
3. c) Peel 100g of garlic, the ginger, and the shallots. Cut into julienne strips and sauté in a pot. Clean the tomatoes, cut into julienne strips and add the tomato once the sauté is golden.
4. d) Brown the shrimp and parrot fish in the oven. When the sauté is ready, add the shrimp and parrot fish and continue cooking.
5. e) When it is ready, add the tom yum and a few strands of saffron. Continue cooking, and once homogeneous, pour in the wine and reduce.
6. f) Then add the water and coconut milk, simmer covered for 20 minutes and uncovered for 25 minutes.
7. g) Strain the broth, and in a Thermomix, blend the broth with the confit garlic, fried almonds, and a couple of strands of saffron until the desired texture is achieved.

Other details:
original_language: es

Classifications:
- Dish Type: Main Course
- Cuisine: Thai / SE Asian
- Difficulty: Level 2 (Intermediate / Professional)
- Main Ingredient: Fish & Seafood
- Dietary: Dairy-Free, Gluten-Free, Pescatarian, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Fine Dining / Plated
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

d) Brown the shrimp and parrot fish in the oven. When the sauté is ready, add the shrimp and parrot fish and continue cooking.
e) When it is ready, add the tom yum and a few strands of saffron. Continue cooking, and once homogeneous, pour in the wine and reduce.

name: Wantou Pasta
text:
Recipe ID: pasta_wantou_es_parsed
Name: Wantou Pasta

Ingredients:
- Wantou pasta
- Water
- Oil
- Salt
- Ice

Steps:
1. a) Bring a pot of water to a boil with salt and oil.
2. b) Remove the pasta from the freezer 3 minutes before use. It is very important to cook it all; do not defrost it from one day to the next.
3. c) Introduce the sheets for 10 seconds and transfer them to the bowl with ice to stop the cooking process.
4. d) Arrange them on a gastronorm tray with parchment paper and oil for later use.
5. e) There are 4 sheets per plate.

name: Chocolate Salami
text:
Recipe ID: salame_di_cioccolato_merged
Name: Chocolate Salami

Ingredients:
- Dry biscuits: 300 g
- Butter: 150 g
- Dark chocolate: 200 g
- Rum: 2 tablespoons
- Eggs: 2
- Sugar: 100 g

Steps:
1. 1 Roughly crumble the dry biscuits into a large bowl. Melt the dark chocolate in a bain-marie, mixing until it is creamy and without lumps, then let it cool.
2. 2 Meanwhile, work the butter at room temperature with a mixer until it becomes a cream, into which you will gradually incorporate the sugar, eggs, cooled chocolate, and rum. Pour the mixture into the bowl where you crumbled the biscuits and mix well. You have obtained the dough for the chocolate salami: now it's up to you to decide whether you want to use it all to make one large salami, or divide it into several smaller salamis.
3. 3 To give the dessert the classic cylindrical shape, roll the dough in a sheet of parchment paper, which you will then wrap in aluminum foil. After 3 hours in the refrigerator, the salami will be ready to be served, sliced or whole.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Preparazione: 30'
Dosi: 8 persone

name: Genoese Focaccia
text:
Recipe ID: focaccia_genovese_merged
Name: Genoese Focaccia

Ingredients:
- Flour 300w 6000
- Water 3180
- Brewer's Yeast 220
- Salt 120
- Extra Virgin Olive Oil 420
- Malt (or 2 tbsp. fo-30) 60
- Extra Virgin Olive Oil 750
- Hot Water 600
- Salt 75
- Sun-dried Tomatoes 1000
- Capers 100
- Anchovies 50
- Taggiasca Olives 100
- Taggiasca Olive Oil 200

Steps:
1. Knead all the ingredients together. Form dough portions of 1670g and let rest for 30 minutes. Stretch the dough and let it rise on a 60x40 cm baking sheet for about 60 minutes. Bake at a temperature of 250°C with a little steam for about 20-25 minutes.
2. Emulsify all the ingredients together and pour over the focaccia before baking.
3. Place all the ingredients in a cutter until a homogeneous cream is obtained and spread over the focaccia before baking.

name: Fermented Black Rice Cake With Koshu
text:
Recipe ID: fermented_black_rice_cake_with_koshu_merged
Name: Fermented Black Rice Cake With Koshu

Ingredients:
- INGREDIENTS:
- (For 4 people.)
- The Fermented Black Rice:
- 250 g Yurina Nishiki Rice
- 270 ml water
- 2 g koji-kin
- INGREDIENTES:
- (Para 4 personas.)
- El arroz fermentado
- negro:
- 250 g. Arroz Yurina Nishiki
- 270 ml. agua
- 2 g. koji-kin

Steps:
1. PREPARATION:
2. The Fermented Black Rice:
3. Clean the rice in cold water 8 times, changing the water each time, until the water runs clear. Then drain the rice for 30 minutes.
4. Drain the rice for 30 minutes.
5. Add the rice to the automatic rice cooker, adding the water.
6. Cook for 37 minutes in the “White Rice” program.
7. Disinfect the trays that will be used for this preparation: rice on a wide tray, which recovers the excess water that the rice breathes. Sprinkle the koji-kin evenly on top of the warm rice. Toss the rice with the koji-kin 3 times very carefully so that all the grains are impregnated with the koji.
8. Soak to remove dirt and drain the water and serve the rice on a tray with holes and about 1.5 cm thick, without pressing.
9. Cover the trays with film and make holes in the film.
10. Ferment the rice in a hot cabinet at 29°C-30°C for 12 hours. Remove the film and let it breathe for 1 hour. Cover again with film and make holes and ferment for 24 to 36 hours at 29°C-30°C, making sure that the rice does not change its opaque color.
11. After time, store the rice wrapped on the plate and on the trays with perforated film in refrigeration to stop fermentation.
12. ELABORACIÓN:
13. El arroz fermentado negro:
14. Limpiar el arroz en agua fría 8 veces, cambiando el agua cada vez, cuando el agua salga blanca. Dejar escurrir después el arroz durante 30 minutos.
15. Escurrir después el arroz durante 30 minutos.
16. Servir el arroz en el cocedero automático añadiendo el agua.
17. Cocer durante 37 minutos en programa “White Rice”.
18. Desinfectar las bandejas que se utilizaran para esta elaboración: arroz sobre una bandeja ancha, que recupera el exceso de agua que respire el arroz. Espolvorear el koji-kin de forma uniforme encima del arroz tibio que tienes el arroz con con el koji-kin 3 veces y con mucho cuidado para que todos los granos estén impregnados del koji.
19. Remojar para quitar la suciedad y escurrirla de agua y servir el arroz sobre una bandeja con agujeros y tenga alrededor de 1,5 cm de grosor, sin presionar.
20. Cubrir las bandejas con film y hacer agujeros a este film.
21. Fermentar el arroz en armario caliente a 29°C-30°C durante 12 horas. Retirar el film y dejar respirar 1 hora. Volver a cubrir con film y hacer agujeros y fermentar entre 24 a 36 horas a 29°C-30°C vigilando que el arroz no cambie su color opaco.
22. Pasado el tiempo, guardar el arroz envuelto en el plato y en las bandejas con film agujereado en refrigeración para detener la fermentación.

Other details:

Classifications:
- Dish Type: Component / Base (Stocks, Sauces, Pastes, Garnishes, Doughs)
- Cuisine: Japanese
- Difficulty: Level 2 (Intermediate / Professional)

- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Vegan, Vegetarian, Dairy-Free, Nut-Free, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Fine Dining / Plated

- Seasonality: Year-Round

The Fermented Black Rice:
Clean the rice in cold water 8 times, changing the water each time, until the water runs clear. Then drain the rice for 30 minutes.
Drain the rice for 30 minutes.
Add the rice to the automatic rice cooker, adding the water.
Cook for 37 minutes in the “White Rice” program.

Disinfect the trays that will be used for this preparation: rice on a wide tray, which recovers the excess water that the rice breathes. Sprinkle the koji-kin evenly on top of the warm rice. Toss the rice with the koji-kin 3 times very carefully so that all the grains are impregnated with the koji.
Soak to remove dirt and drain the water and serve the rice on a tray with holes and about 1.5 cm thick, without pressing.
Cover the trays with film and make holes in the film.

Ferment the rice in a hot cabinet at 29°C-30°C for 12 hours. Remove the film and let it breathe for 1 hour. Cover again with film and make holes and ferment for 24 to 36 hours at 29°C-30°C, making sure that the rice does not change its opaque color.
After time, store the rice wrapped on the plate and on the trays with perforated film in refrigeration to stop fermentation.
ELABORACIÓN:
El arroz fermentado negro:
Limpiar el arroz en agua fría 8 veces, cambiando el agua cada vez, cuando el agua salga blanca. Dejar escurrir después el arroz durante 30 minutos.
Escurrir después el arroz durante 30 minutos.
Servir el arroz en el cocedero automático añadiendo el agua.
Cocer durante 37 minutos en programa “White Rice”.

Desinfectar las bandejas que se utilizaran para esta elaboración: arroz sobre una bandeja ancha, que recupera el exceso de agua que respire el arroz. Espolvorear el koji-kin de forma uniforme encima del arroz tibio que tienes el arroz con con el koji-kin 3 veces y con mucho cuidado para que todos los granos estén impregnados del koji.
Remojar para quitar la suciedad y escurrirla de agua y servir el arroz sobre una bandeja con agujeros y tenga alrededor de 1,5 cm de grosor, sin presionar.
Cubrir las bandejas con film y hacer agujeros a este film.

Fermentar el arroz en armario caliente a 29°C-30°C durante 12 horas. Retirar el film y dejar respirar 1 hora. Volver a cubrir con film y hacer agujeros y fermentar entre 24 a 36 horas a 29°C-30°C vigilando que el arroz no cambie su color opaco.
Pasado el tiempo, guardar el arroz envuelto en el plato y en las bandejas con film agujereado en refrigeración para detener la fermentación.

name: Rustic Apple Cake
text:
Recipe ID: torta_rustica_di_mele_merged
Name: Rustic Apple Cake

Ingredients:
- Golden Apples: 700 g
- Butter: 100 g
- Sugar: 200 g
- Flour: 200 g
- Eggs: 2
- Baking powder: 1 packet
- Lemons: 1 (zest and juice)
- Cinnamon: 1 teaspoon
- Vanillin: 1 packet
- Salt: as needed
- FOR SPRINKLING
- Cinnamon: as needed
- Powdered sugar: as needed

Steps:
1. Preheat the oven to 200°C. Divide the dough into two equal parts and use a rolling pin to roll it out lengthwise (like a rectangle) on a floured cloth. You should try to obtain a thin sheet like a sheet of paper. Once you have stretched it to the maximum, use your hands to widen it until it is almost transparent. Grease a sheet with half of the melted butter, leaving a 2-3 cm border all around, and sprinkle it with half of the toasted breadcrumbs previously toasted in butter, on which you will place half of the apple mixture. Roll the strudel from the longest side, using the cloth to avoid breaking the dough. Seal it also on the sides, so that the filling does not come out during cooking.
2. Place the roll in a greased baking pan (or on parchment paper) with the seam facing down and, before baking it at 200°C for about 40 minutes, drizzle it with a little melted butter. When cooked, sprinkle it with powdered sugar and serve it still warm, sliced.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Preparazione:: 20'
Cottura:: 60'
Dosi:: 8 persone

name: Potato Frittata
text:
Recipe ID: frittata_di_patate_merged
Name: Potato Frittata

Ingredients:
- Eggs: 6
- Potatoes: 500 g
- Parmigiano reggiano cheese, grated: 100 g
- Parsley: 1 sprig
- Nutmeg: 1 pinch
- Oil: 4-5 tablespoons
- Salt and pepper: to taste

Steps:
1. Peel the potatoes and cut them into slices about 1 cm thick. Meanwhile, put a pot of salted water on the stove; when it boils, add the potatoes and cook for about ten minutes, draining them before they fall apart.
2. In the meantime, beat the eggs in a large bowl, where you will add the Parmigiano reggiano cheese, nutmeg, and chopped parsley. Season with salt and pepper, then add the slightly cooled potatoes. Mix.
3. Cover the bottom of a pan with a diameter of about 24-26 cm with the oil. When it is hot, pour in the mixture and cook for 10-15 minutes over medium heat, covered with a lid. When the frittata comes loose from the bottom, it will be ready to be flipped. Cook the other side for 10-15 minutes as well, this time without a lid.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Cottura: 45'
Preparazione: 20'
Dosi: 6 persone

name: Villi
text:
Recipe ID: villi_merged
Name: Villi

Ingredients:
- The Villi:
- 1 liter raw liquid cream
- 250 g villi starter

Steps:
1. For the success of the recipe, cleaning the surfaces where we are going to manipulate is crucial. Everything must be used and disinfected with ethanol.
2. We are working with a living microorganism, the Villi, so its living conditions: temperature, humidity, pH and availability of nutrients will largely determine its growth in many unpredictable occasions.
3. PREPARATION:
4. The Villi: heat the raw milk until it reaches 80ºC (pasteurization temperature), once it reaches 80ºC let it cool very well, incorporate the villi starter and mix again.
5. Pour the mixture into a previously disinfected glass jar and close with a sheet of paper that finishes closing the glass jar for 24 hours between 20ºC - 25ºC.
6. Once the time has passed and the texture of the villi is compact, refrigerate for 6 hours before using.
7. After the time has passed, the villi can be consumed. Reserve 60 g of villi, which will act as the new "starter" to reproduce more quantity.
8. (repeat the complete preparation with the reserved "starter" villi and ferment in another glass jar).

Other details:

We are working with a living microorganism, the Villi, so its living conditions: temperature, humidity, pH and availability of nutrients will largely determine its growth in many unpredictable occasions.
PREPARATION:

Pour the mixture into a previously disinfected glass jar and close with a sheet of paper that finishes closing the glass jar for 24 hours between 20ºC - 25ºC.
Once the time has passed and the texture of the villi is compact, refrigerate for 6 hours before using.

name: Fruit Skewers
text:
Recipe ID: fruit_skewers_merged
Name: Fruit Skewers

Ingredients:
- Bananas: 2
- Strawberries: 12
- Kiwi: 1
- Melon: ½
- Mint: 8 small leaves
- FOR COATING
- Dark chocolate: 100 g
- Chopped hazelnuts: 8 teaspoons
- Fresh cream: 80 ml

Steps:
1. Wash and clean the fruit, obtaining small pieces of each type to be skewered on the skewers. With the appropriate tool, then, hollow out the pulp of the melon obtaining balls, cut the kiwi into cubes and the ba-nana into rather thick slices. Leave the strawberries whole, only removing the green stem.
2. Reduce the chocolate into small pieces and melt it in a double boiler with the cream: the result will be a den-se and creamy sauce that you can use both warm and cold.
3. Compose the skewers alternating pieces of different fruit and ending with a mint leaf. Place 2 skewers on each plate, drizzle with chocolate sauce and sprinkle with a teaspoon of chopped hazelnuts.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Preparazione: 15'
Dosi: 4 persone

name: Squid, Pork And Mushroom
text:
Recipe ID: squid,_pork_and_mushroom_merged
Name: Squid, Pork And Mushroom

Ingredients:
- For the Pickle juice
- 250g Brown sugar
- 250g light soy sauce
- 250g Rice vinegar
- 250g Rice vinegar
- 50g Grated ginger
- For the Pickled mushroom
- 200g of girolles
- 200g of the pickle juice(see above)
- For the Squid
- For Iberico pork jowel
- Extras

Steps:
1. 1. Add all the ingredients in a pan.
2. 2. Bring to the boil the remove from heat.
3. 3. Leave 20 minute to infuse the pass.
4. 1. Clean and trim the girolles
5. 2. Heat the pickle juice and pour over the mushrooms.
6. 3. Leave to cool in the pickle juice.

name: Halibut, Toasted Rice
text:
Recipe ID: halibut,_toasted_rice_merged
Name: Halibut, Toasted Rice

Ingredients:
- For the Halibut
- 80g Halibut
- 500g Neutral oil
- For the lemon thyme oil
- 100g lemon thyme
- 200g grape seed oil
- For the Fish stock
- 2kg Fish bones
- 200g White wine
- 3l Water
- For Toasted rice stock
- 750g Fish stock
- 50g Dry rice
- 2 Dry kombu
- 10g Burnt garlic
- 1g Long pepper

Steps:
1. PROCESS
2. 1. Warm the oil in the circulator to 62C.
3. 2. Temper the fish then place in the oil.
4. 3. Bring the fish up to 42C.
5. 4. Remove from the oil and torch the skin with a blowtorch.
6. 1. Pick all the leaves off of the branches.
7. 2. Bring oil to 70 degrees in thermo.
8. 3. Blanch, squeeze water out and add to oil.
9. 4. Blend until smooth and pass through muslin onto ice.
10. 1. Roast the fish bones until golden brown in the oven.
11. 2. Transfer to large pan and add the white wine and reduced cover with the water.
12. 3. Out for 20 minutes then pass.
13. 4. Cook for 20 minutes then pass.
14. 5. Reduce until desired flavour is reached.
15. 1. Toast the rice at 170C for 30 minutes until golden brown.
16. 2. Warm the stock and add the rest of the ingredients and Infuse 15 minutes.

name: Rustic Apple Tart
text:
Recipe ID: rustic_apple_tart_merged
Name: Rustic Apple Tart

Ingredients:
- Golden Apples: 700 g
- Butter: 100 g
- Sugar: 200 g
- Flour: 200 g
- Eggs: 2
- Baking powder: 1 packet
- Lemons: 1 (zest and juice)
- Cinnamon: 1 teaspoon
- Vanilla extract: 1 packet
- Salt: as needed
- FOR SPRINKLING
- Cinnamon: as needed
- Powdered sugar: as needed

Steps:
1. Preheat the oven to 200°C (392°F). Divide the dough into two equal parts and roll it out lengthwise (like a rectangle) on a floured cloth. You should try to obtain a thin sheet like a sheet of paper. Once you have stretched it to the maximum, use your hands to widen it until it is almost transparent. Grease one sheet with half of the melted butter, leaving a border of 2-3 cm (approx. 1 inch) all around, and sprinkle it with half of the toasted breadcrumbs previously toasted in the butter, on which you will place half of the apple mixture. Roll up the strudel from the longest side, using the cloth to avoid breaking the dough. Seal it on the sides as well, so that the filling does not come out during cooking.
2. Place the roll in a greased baking pan (or on parchment paper) with the seam facing down, and before baking it at 200°C (392°F) for about 40 minutes, drizzle it with a little melted butter. When cooked, sprinkle with powdered sugar and serve still warm, sliced.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Preparazione:: 20'
Cottura:: 60'
Dosi:: 8 persone

name: Lemon Condiment
text:
Recipe ID: lemon_condiment_merged
Name: Lemon Condiment

Ingredients:
- 100g fresh lemon juice
- 130g sugar
- 150g water
- 40g chardonnay vinegar
- 10g salt
- 2g ascorbic acid
- 550g fennel shaved on mandolin
- 95g lemon juice
- 4g gelespressa
- 50g grapeseed oil

Steps:
1. in a small pan bring the 95g lemon juice, sugar, water vinegar to the boil
2. reserve
3. warm a small rondel on the induction
4. add the grapeseed oil then add the fennel and quickly sweat down, keep it moving as you cannot allow any colouring
5. add the salt and ascorbic acid and mix through
6. add the lemon juice mix and bring to the boil
7. tightly wrap the pan in clingfilm and turn the induction down to 2
8. cook at 2 for 10 mins
9. remove clingfilm and add the gelespressa, sieve it in so as not to get any lumps
10. transfer to a bowl over ice and chill
11. add fresh lemon juice
12. for service chop 2 parts lemon condiment and 1 part pickled lemon zest, season with olive oil

Other details:
method: None

name: Hot Mayonnaise
text:
Recipe ID: mayonesa_caliente_en_parsed
Name: Hot Mayonnaise

Ingredients:
- 5 eggs
- 200ml sunflower oil
- 50ml olive oil
- 30ml Modena vinegar
- 2g salt
- 1g sugar
- 1g white pepper
- 1ml lemon juice

Steps:
1. a) Beat the eggs together with the vinegar and lemon juice.
2. b) Mix the oils and slowly drizzle them into the previous mixture.
3. c) Finally, add the sugar and salt and adjust seasoning.
4. d) Strain and pour into a one-liter siphon and charge with two cartridges.
5. e) Place in a sous vide (roner) at 60 degrees Celsius.

Other details:

name: Italian Meriengue
text:
Recipe ID: italian_meriengue_parsed
Name: Italian Meriengue

Ingredients:
- 216 g sugar
- 130 g egg whites

Steps:
1. Place the egg whites in a stand mixer and place the sugar in a pot with a splash of water.
2. Place the sugar on the stove to come to temperature.
3. When the sugar hits 100°C you can begin whipping the egg whites.
4. Let the sugar continue to climb to 121°C and then pour it into the egg whites, which at this point should be aerated, but not a firm meringue.
5. Let the mixture continue whipping until the temperature drops to 40°C.
6. Remove the mixture from the bowl and reserve.

Other details:

final_temperature: 40°C

raw.name: Italian Meriengue
steps: Place the egg whites in a stand mixer and place the sugar in a pot with a splash of water.
Place the sugar on the stove to come to temperature.
When the sugar hits 100°C you can begin whipping the egg whites.
Let the sugar continue to climb to 121°C and then pour it into the egg whites, which at this point should be aerated, but not a firm meringue.
Let the mixture continue whipping until the temperature drops to 40°C.
Remove the mixture from the bowl and reserve.

name: Armagnac De Liebre
text:
Recipe ID: armagnac_de_liebre_merged
Name: Armagnac De Liebre

Ingredients:
- INGREDIENTS:
(For 1.8 l.)
- 5 pcs. hares or rabbits, without viscera.
- 1.5 l. Red wine.
- 250 g. Port wine.
- 250 g. Soy sauce.
- 250 g. Manzanilla sherry.
- 100 g. Celery.
- 150 g. Carrot.
- 200 g. Onion.
- 5 g. Thyme.
- 25 pcs. Black peppercorns.
- 500 g. Pork blood.
- 60 g. Bas Armagnac.

Steps:
1. ELABORATION:
2. Mise en Place
3. - Chop the vegetables into regular pieces.
4. - Cut the hare into small, regular portions.
5. - Place the hares, vegetables, pepper, and thyme in a tall pot. Moisten with the wines and soy sauce (not the Armagnac).
6. - Marinate in refrigeration for 1 night.
7. Development
8. - Separate the hare meat from the marinade.
9. - Separate the vegetables from the marinade.
10. - Strain the marinade liquid to remove the peppercorns.
11. - In a very hot pan or tilting skillet, brown the hare meat until it acquires a brown crust. Deglaze the pan with a little water to release the browned protein that adheres to the bottom of the pan.
12. - In a hot pan, brown the vegetables until completely caramelized.
13. - When the hare meat is well caramelized, combine them and moisten with the marinade.
14. - Bring to a gentle boil and cook for 20 minutes, add the pork blood and cook for another 20 minutes.
15. - Strain the broth using a chinois strainer. Do not filter ANYTHING!!
16. - In a pot, place the strained remains of the previous broth and re-moisten with:
17. 1.5 l. Red wine
18. 250 g. Port wine
19. 250 g. Manzanilla sherry
20. 250 g. Soy sauce
21. 4 l. Water
22. - Bring to a heat and cook for 40 minutes at a gentle boil.
23. - Strain using a chinois strainer. Discard the strained remains and take the liquid to the filtering phase.

Other details:
original_language: es
translated_from: es

- Dish Type: Main Course
- Cuisine: Other
- Difficulty: Level 3 (Advanced / Michelin)
- Main Ingredient: Meat (Beef, Pork, Lamb, Game)
- Dietary: Dairy-Free, Nut-Free, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Fine Dining / Plated
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Autumn

raw.name: Armagnac De Liebre
ingredients: 5 pcs. hares or rabbits, without viscera.
steps: ELABORATION:
Mise en Place
- Chop the vegetables into regular pieces.
- Cut the hare into small, regular portions.
- Place the hares, vegetables, pepper, and thyme in a tall pot. Moisten with the wines and soy sauce (not the Armagnac).
- Marinate in refrigeration for 1 night.
Development
- Separate the hare meat from the marinade.
- Separate the vegetables from the marinade.
- Strain the marinade liquid to remove the peppercorns.
- In a very hot pan or tilting skillet, brown the hare meat until it acquires a brown crust. Deglaze the pan with a little water to release the browned protein that adheres to the bottom of the pan.
- In a hot pan, brown the vegetables until completely caramelized.
- When the hare meat is well caramelized, combine them and moisten with the marinade.
- Bring to a gentle boil and cook for 20 minutes, add the pork blood and cook for another 20 minutes.
- Strain the broth using a chinois strainer. Do not filter ANYTHING!!
- In a pot, place the strained remains of the previous broth and re-moisten with:
1.5 l. Red wine
250 g. Port wine
250 g. Manzanilla sherry
250 g. Soy sauce
4 l. Water
- Bring to a heat and cook for 40 minutes at a gentle boil.
- Strain using a chinois strainer. Discard the strained remains and take the liquid to the filtering phase.

name: Cream With Strawberries
text:
Recipe ID: nata_con_fresas_merged
Name: Cream With Strawberries

Ingredients:
- INGREDIENTS:
- (for 4 people)
- The cream kombucha:
- 700 ml. of cow's cream
- 200 ml. of whole milk
- 50 ml. of active liquid from kombucha mother
- 6 units. of egg yolks
- 100 g. of sugar
- The Kombucha cure:
- 2 l. cured kombucha
- Cream kombucha
- The strawberry inulin:
- 100 g. of strawberry
- 60% inulin
- 30% water
- The finishing:
- 120 g. of strawberry inulin
- INGREDIENTS:
- (for 4 people.)
- The cream kombucha:
- 700 ml. of cow's cream
- 200 ml. of whole milk
- 50 ml. of active liquid from kombucha mother
- 6 units. of egg yolks
- 100 g. of sugar
- The Kombucha cure:
- 2 l. cured kombucha
- Cream kombucha
- The strawberry inulin:
- 100 g. of strawberry
- 60% inulin
- 30% water
- The finishing:
- 120 g. of strawberry inulin

Steps:
1. ELABORATION
2. The cream kombucha:
3. In a tray, serve all the ingredients, mix with the help of a whisk and cover with a cloth, let ferment for 7 to 10 days.
4. The Kombucha cure:
5. Carefully wash the kombucha mothers from the tray and wash in a gentle stream of water.
6. Arrange between layers of sugar, one on top of the other, let cure for 2 days.
7. Once cured, wash under a gentle stream of water until all the excess sugar is removed, portion into 7 x 7 cm squares. Reserve.
8. The strawberry inulin:
9. Grind all the strawberry together with a splash of strawberry, add the inulin and a little water and reserve.
10. Grind the strawberry smoothie with the inulin in a Vitamix for 4 minutes at medium speed and reserve in a gastro-hucha.
11. With a spatula, smear the kombucha mother with the strawberry inulin.
12. Plate casually. Like a canvas with volumes.
13. PREPARATION
14. The cream kombucha:
15. In a tray, serve all the ingredients, mix with the help of a whisk and cover with a cloth, let ferment for 7 to 10 days.
16. The Kombucha cure:
17. Carefully wash the kombucha mothers from the tray and wash in a gentle stream of water.
18. Arrange between layers of sugar, one on top of the other, let cure for 2 days.
19. Once cured, wash under a gentle stream of water until all the excess sugar is removed, portion into 7 x 7 cm squares. Reserve.
20. The strawberry inulin:
21. Grind all the strawberry together with a splash of strawberry, add the inulin and a little water and reserve.
22. Grind the strawberry smoothie with the inulin in a Vitamix for 4 minutes at medium speed and reserve in a gastro-hucha.
23. With a spatula, smear the kombucha mother with the strawberry inulin.
24. Plate casually. Like a canvas with volumes.
