name: Malt Soil
text:
Recipe ID: malt_soil_parsed
Name: Malt Soil

Ingredients:
- 50 g hazelnut flour
- 20 g malt powder
- 50 g OO flour
- 50 g sugar
- 5 g bilberry powder
- 50 g melted butter
- brown cheese

Steps:
1. Break up the brown cheese into small chunks using your hands or a knife.
2. Place it into a dehydrator set to 60°C until almost dry. (the cheese will retain fat and as such will never dry out completely).
3. When the cheese is dry, remove it from the dehydrator, let it cool to room temperature and give it a quick, roughly 10 second pulse in the thermomix to break it up.
4. In a mixing bowl, combine all the dry ingredients.
5. Melt the butter and mix it in as well.
6. Transfer the mixture to an oven set to 140°C and bake it on a parchment lined tray for 10 minutes.
7. Take the soil out of the oven, let it cool slightly and then chill it in the fridge.
8. Once chilled, transfer it to a mortar and pestle, in order to pound down any lumps.
9. Combine the cooked soil crumble and dried brown cheese 1:1.

Other details:
temperature: 140°C
cook_time: 10 minutes

12. - Place the peeled peppers in large bowls (maximum 8 peppers per bowl), a spoonful of water and cover with 3 layers of film.

name: Tomatoes
text:
Recipe ID: tomates_merged
Name: Tomatoes

Ingredients:
- Fermented Tomato
- Ripe Tomatoes
- Salt

Steps:
1. PREVIOUS NOTE:
2. Conserva Cruda di Pomodoro. The art of fermentation, Sandor Ellix Katz, pag. 117.
3. Fermented tomato has been a way to preserve tomatoes for hundreds of years and have it available throughout the year. It is prepared in the season with the most tomato production.
4. PREPARATION:
5. The fermented tomato:
6. Wash the tomatoes, removing any rotten parts. Crush them with your hands in a tall gastromorm.
7. Fermentation will begin spontaneously, it will be quite similar to the fermentation of cabbage. A liquid will appear on the surface (in an acidified medium and promoted by lactic acid bacteria and molds.
8. The gastromorm should not be completely full and should always be covered with a cheesecloth to keep the tomatoes submerged.
9. Gas bubbles will push the solids to the surface, which will appear covered by a white mold.
10. Stir 2 times a DAY, mixing the mold with the fermenting tomato.
11. After 4 or 5 days, depending on the temperature, the gas will completely stop "boiling", we will stop seeing the gas so that solids float on the surface and then to remove the floating solids and seeds.
12. Remove the floating solids and seeds.
13. Serve the resulting puree in a clean lito, squeeze it and leave it hanging in the refrigerator for 24 hours so that all the liquid drains.
14. The lito may appear covered by a white mold. Remove it by "peeling" it with a spoon and discarding it.
15. Collect the tomato paste from inside the lito. Serve it on a lito or a gastromorm with holes and press it with a weight for a couple of days until the dough has the consistency of a firm dough.
16. Tomatoes in oil:
17. 1 ripe tomato.
18. Extra virgin olive oil
19. 1 head of garlic
20. Mix the dough with 25-30% salt. After a few hours, encourage it by preserving it vacuum-packed or in a glass jar. No refrigeration is required, although it should be stored in the refrigerator.
21. The yield is 8% with respect to the initial tomato weight.
22. The fermented tomato paste is very salty and concentrated, so it should be used in moderation.
23. Tomatoes in oil: crush them with your hand. Submerge them and peel the head of garlic for 2 days.
24. Macerate everything in oil for 2 days.
25. Remove the tomatoes and cut them naturally into slices about 4 cm thick and about 60 g.
26. Osmotize the tomatoes with the raw garlic oil.
27. FINISH AND PRESENTATION
28. Drain the tomatoes from the garlic oil bath onto a clean lito.
29. Spread the fermented tomato paste on a cutting board.
30. Serve.
31. The fermented tomato

name: Bacetti Golosi
text:
Recipe ID: bacetti_golosi_merged
Name: Bacetti Golosi

Ingredients:
- FOR 30 BACETTI
- WITH JAM
- Flour: 180 g
- Sugar: 150 g
- Baking powder: ½ sachet (8 g)
- FOR THE FILLING
- Strawberry jam: 250 g
- Fresh cream: 250 ml
- WITH CHOCOLATE
- Dark chocolate: 150 g
- Fresh cream: 100 ml
- FOR DECORATING
- Dark chocolate: 50 g

Steps:
1. I know, the "deliciousness" is already in the name, but I can't help but tell you at least a little about the symphony of flavors that you will taste millimeter by millimeter, as the sponge cake melts, releasing its secret prelude of jam and whipped cream or dark chocolate.
2. Bacetti are a guilty pleasure, a fabulous idea for a party, a treat for a snack: you can make them the size you prefer and fill them with whatever you want, always being amazed by the fact that you are perfectly capable of competing (fiercely competing) with supermarket snacks...
3. 1. Preheat the oven to 190°. Beat the eggs with the sugar using an electric mixer for at least 10 minutes at medium speed: you will obtain a light and fluffy mixture. Add the sifted flour and baking powder and mix gently, with movements from the bottom up. When the flour is mixed in, fill a pastry bag with the mixture and on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, form discs of dough that are the same size (about 3 cm in diameter: you should get about 60). Bake for about 12 minutes at 190°. When the cakes begin to swell and brown at the edges, remove them and let them cool.
4. 2. For the jam filling, cover the flat part of the 30 discs with a level spoonful of strawberry jam each, add the whipped cream and close with another disc of dough.
5. 3. If you prefer to fill them with chocolate, heat 150 ml of fresh cream in a saucepan, when it has almost reached boiling point, add the chopped dark chocolate

Other details:

Cottura: 12'
Preparazione: 60'
Dosi: 30 bacetti circa

- The oysters:
- 4 units Hossegor variety oyster
- The garlic oil:
- 10 g peeled garlic
- 40 g olive oil
- The rice sourdough:
- 80 g rice sourdough
- 1 sheet of obulato
- q.s. sunflower oil
- The finishing:
- 4 units Hossegor oyster
- 20 ml oyster water
- 100 ml 3% brine
- q.s. chopped parsley stems
- 4 units broken sheets of 20 g sourdough

Steps:
1. PREPARATION:
2. The oysters:
3. Keep the oysters refrigerated until serving.
4. The garlic oil:
5. Confit the olive oil for 4 hours. Strain and reserve the oil for finishing.
6. The rice sourdough:
7. The R&D department will take care of the fermentation of the rice sourdough.
8. Broken sheets of sourdough:
9. Brush sunflower oil on a silpat.
10. Spread a sheet of obulato on the fat and stretch the sourdough.
11. Dehydrate at 50°C for 3 hours or until dry and broken. Store in a warm cupboard.
12. FINISHING AND PRESENTATION
13. Heat the griddle to maximum temperature for 10 minutes. After this time, place the oysters on top of the griddle, pour a little water, and cover them with a bell or bowl to steam open. Cover for 4 minutes.
14. After this time, carefully open the oysters and reserve the oyster water. Once opened, clean them in a warm brine to remove dirt.
15. In a bowl, place the hot oyster. In a lunchbox, mix the parsley stems, garlic oil and oyster water and place on top of the oyster. Cover with the rice sourdough.

name: Chocolate Profiteroles
text:
Recipe ID: profiterol_al_cioccolato_merged
Name: Chocolate Profiteroles

Ingredients:
- FOR 60 CREAM PUFFS
- Water: 200 ml
- Butter: 100 g
- Flour: 130 g
- Salt: 1 pinch
- Eggs (medium): 4
- Sugar: 5 g
- FOR THE CHANTILLY CREAM
- Fresh cream: 500 ml
- Powdered sugar: 2 level tablespoons
- FOR THE COATING
- Dark chocolate: 400 g
- Fresh milk: 20 ml
- Fresh cream: 500 ml

Steps:
1. 1 To make the cream puffs, follow the recipe on page 286, using the quantities indicated here. When they are cold, make a hole on the base of each with a thin object (the handle of a teaspoon will work well) so that the pastry bag nozzle can penetrate it.
2. 2 Then prepare the Chantilly cream by placing 500 ml of very cold fresh cream in a large bowl; whisk with an electric mixer until the cream sticks to the beaters without dripping, then add the powdered sugar and beat for a few more seconds. Fill all the cream puffs and close them in an airtight container that you will put in the refrigerator.
3. 3 In the meantime, dedicate yourself to the chocolate coating: put the cream and milk in a saucepan, when they are about to boil add the chopped chocolate. Stir until you get a smooth and lump-free mixture that you will thicken in the refrigerator. At that point, dip the cream puffs, one at a time, in the cream, cover them completely, let them drain and arrange them on a plate.
4. 4 If you do not want to enjoy the profiteroles immediately, put them in the refrigerator and take them out 15 minutes before serving.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★★
Cottura: 30'
Preparazione: 60'
Dosi: 12 persone

name: Kadi
text:
Recipe ID: kadi_parsed_3
Name: Kadi

Ingredients:
- 800 gr of yogurt
- 800 gr of water
- 1 and ½ tsp of salt
- ½ tbsp of sugar
- ginger and chilli paste
- 5 tbsp gram flour
- 1 tbsp of ghee
- 1 ½ mustard seeds
- 1 ½ tsp of cumin seed
- ½ fenugreek seed
- 3 kerala chilli
- 1 tbsp of hing
- 20 curry leaves
- 4 green chilli cut in half
- fresh coriander

Steps:
1. MIx all well together : 800 gr of yogurt, 800 gr of water, 1 and ½ tsp of salt, ½ tbsp of sugar,  ginger and chilli paste, 5 tbsp gram flour
2. Heat a pan with 1 tbsp of ghee and toast the ingredients in this oder: 1 ½ mustard seeds, 1 ½ tsp of cumin seed, ½ fenugreek seed, 3 kerala chilli, 1 tbsp of hing and add 20 curry leaves
3. Fro Mr. Jay insert in this phase 4 green chilli cut in half
4. Once toasted everything add the Kadi mixture in the pan and proceed to cook for 10 minutes until combined and finish with fresh coriander

Other details:
servings: 6 to 8 people
cook_time: 10 minutes

name: Cuttlefish Mix
text:
Recipe ID: cuttlefish_mix_merged
Name: Cuttlefish Mix

Ingredients:
- 2X Bag Frozen Prawns
- 2X Bag Frozen Squid
- 200 G CornFlour prawns
- 40 G Garlic powder
- 100 Tsp Squid Ink
- 2 Tsp Sugar
- 20 ml Rice Vinegar
- 20 ml Sesame Oil
- 4 Egg White

Steps:
1. Defrost the Prawns and Squid.
2. Dice down the squid to help it blend.
3. Blend the prawns and squid down in batches with a little water if needed to help get it smooth.
4. Place a little amount of the puree back into the with the rest of the ingredients apart from the egg whites with a little more puree on top to help blend.
5. Place everything into Kitchen aid mixing bowl and mix until combined.
6. More squid ink may need to be aded to get the right Colour

name: Scallop Cream
text:
Recipe ID: scallop_cream_merged
Name: Scallop Cream

Ingredients:
- 100g turbot bones or skirt
- 400g scallop skirts
- 4g Malden
- 1500g whipping cream

Steps:
1. in a small roundel
2. add the bones and skirts and warm up until all the liquid comes out of the skirts
3. boil and reduce the liquid until almost dry
4. add cream and salt and bring to the boil
5. gently boil for 4 mins then remove from heat and strain
6. cream should now weigh 1300g
7. chill

name: Tropical Pina Colada (Terrazza 2010)
text:
Recipe ID: tropical_pina_colada_(terrazza_2010)_merged
Name: Tropical Pina Colada (Terrazza 2010)

Ingredients:
- Canned pineapple
- Diced pineapple 1
- Water 1000
- Sugar 500
- Cinnamon stick 1
- Vanilla half
- Star anise 3
- Almond milk foam
- Almond milk 200
- Cream 100
- Milk 100
- Passion fruit jelly
- Passion fruit puree 350
- Glucose 150
- Water 80
- Sugar 5
- Gelatin 2
- Water 25

Steps:
1. Bring water, sugar, cinnamon, vanilla, and star anise to a boil. Add the pineapple and let it steep until completely cooled.
2. Mix all the ingredients together, pass through a chinois strainer, put in a siphon and charge with two gas charges.
3. Bring the puree, glucose and water to a boil. Add the agar agar and bring back to a boil, add the gelatin, filter everything and blast chill. Store at -18°C.

Other details:

name: Tomato Sauce With Kimchi
text:
Recipe ID: salsa_de_tomate_con_kimuchi_en_parsed
Name: Tomato Sauce With Kimchi

Ingredients:
- 12 kg ripe vine tomatoes, chopped
- 7 carrots
- 25 white onions
- 1 kg garlic cloves
- 5 liters chicken stock
- 4 palm sugar cakes (pastilles)
- 300g sherry vinegar

Steps:
1. a) Cut all the vegetables into small dice.
2. b) Add in this order: garlic, onion, carrot and finally the tomatoes (sauté the vegetables well).
3. d) When everything is well sautéed, add the chicken stock + palm sugar + sherry vinegar and let it simmer over medium heat for 2 hours.
4. e) Finally, add the kimchi and adjust the salt. Then, blend in a Thermomix for 3 minutes until a fine sauce is obtained (adjust seasoning).

name: Hazelnut Cream Roll
text:
Recipe ID: hazelnut_cream_roll_merged
Name: Hazelnut Cream Roll

Ingredients:
- FOR THE SPONGE CAKE
- Flour: 120 g
- Sugar: 120 g
- Eggs: 4
- Vanilla extract: 1 sachet
- Butter q.s. (as needed)
- FOR THE HAZELNUT CREAM
- Milk: 250 ml
- Butter: 125 g
- Sugar: 100 g
- Egg yolks: 2
- Gelatin sheets: 10 g
- Hazelnut cream: 3 generous spoons
- Dark chocolate: 50 g
- Fresh cream: 250 ml
- FOR DECORATING
- Chopped hazelnuts or chocolate chips q.s. (as needed)

Steps:
1. Here is a recipe with a thousand variations: the roll can be filled with absolutely anything. Here I propose it with a delicious hazelnut cream, which also requires a certain dedication in the preparation, but trust me: it will absolutely be worth it.
2. The only real difficulty with this recipe lies in the cooking time of the sponge cake: since you will have to roll it on itself, it must remain moist, so do not leave it in the oven for more than 9 minutes. With this precaution and a certain delicacy when packaging, you can't fail!
3. To prepare the hazelnut cream, soak the gelatin sheets in cold water. While they soften, place the egg yolks, milk and sugar in a saucepan, and bring to the point of simmering. Add the squeezed gelatin sheets, mix and add the hazelnut cream and diced butter. Always boil while stirring and turn off the heat when the mixture begins to thicken. That is the time to melt the knife-chopped dark chocolate in the still hot cream. Pour the cream into a wide and low container, cover it with plastic wrap and let it cool in the refrigerator for at least 3 hours.
4. Then move on to the sponge cake. Separate the yolks from the whites and beat the latter until stiff; when they are white and fluffy, add the sugar a little at a time, continuing to whisk. Incorporate the yolks one by one, obtaining a frothy mixture in which you will insert the sifted flour and vanilla extract. 
5. Line a 25x35 cm baking tray with parchment paper, then spread the mixture with the help of a spatula, leveling it gently so as not to deflate it. Bake at 220°C (428°F) for 9 minutes.
6. Remove the roll from the oven and turn it over onto a sheet of plastic wrap that you have spread on the work surface, so that the part attached to the parchment paper remains facing upwards; you must remove it and roll the pastry with the plastic wrap, being careful not to break it. Seal the plastic wrap and let the roll cool at room temperature. At that point, unroll the dough and fill it with the hazelnut cream, which you will have passed through a mixer and in which you will have incorporated the whipped cream. Spread a third of the cream evenly over the roll and rewind it following its fold.
7. Then proceed with the decoration: fill a pastry bag with the remaining cream and make tufts all around. Finally, garnish with chopped hazelnuts or small chocolate chips.

Other details:
Difficoltà:: ★★★
Preparazione:: 120'
Cottura:: 20'
Dosi:: 1 rotolo (6 persone)

Remove the roll from the oven and turn it over onto a sheet of plastic wrap that you have spread on the work surface, so that the part attached to the parchment paper remains facing upwards; you must remove it and roll the pastry with the plastic wrap, being careful not to break it. Seal the plastic wrap and let the roll cool at room temperature. At that point, unroll the dough and fill it with the hazelnut cream, which you will have passed through a mixer and in which you will have incorporated the whipped cream. Spread a third of the cream evenly over the roll and rewind it following its fold.
Then proceed with the decoration: fill a pastry bag with the remaining cream and make tufts all around. Finally, garnish with chopped hazelnuts or small chocolate chips.

name: Sake Ice Cream
text:
Recipe ID: sake_ice_cream_merged
Name: Sake Ice Cream

Ingredients:
- 300g sushi rice
- 1 litre water
- 1200g whole milk
- 300g whipping cream
- 120g yolks
- 100g sugar
- 50g trimoline
- 75g milk powder
- 250g cooked rice
- 250g sake (white bottle)

Steps:
1. Cook the rice and water in a pressure cooker on high for 8 minutes
2. Take off heat and let stand for 10 minutes
3. Chill rice down
4. Warm milk, cream, and milk powder to 50 Celsius
5. Blend yolks, sugar, trimoline
6. Pour warm milk into the eggs and return to the pan whisking all the time
7. Return to the heat and bring to 72 Celsius and hold for 10mins
8. Cool down over ice
9. Add the rice and sake
10. Blend on high
11. Pass through a chinois

name: Manhatten Pigs Tails
text:
Recipe ID: manhatten_pigs_tails_merged
Name: Manhatten Pigs Tails

Ingredients:
- For the pigs tails
- 10kg Iberico tails
- Disposable razors

Steps:
1. PROCESS
2. 1. Cut the safe guard off the top of the razors to expose the blade.
3. 2. Shave the hair off the tails and clean
4. 3. Place one layer of tails in each vacuum bag and seal fully.
5. 4. Cook for 8 hours or overnight at 92 degrees on full steam
6. To prepare
7. 1. Remove from the vacuum bags the following day and drain the excess liquid
8. 2. Using a palette knife score the skin and flesh to gently pull apart to open the tail out. Remove the bones keeping the meat on the skin
9. 4. Place the tails between greaseproof paper and vacuum once chilled.
10. 5. Cook portion and store in fridge/freezer
11. To cook
12. 1. Place pigs tail between grease proof paper in a pan
13. 2. Put a weight on top to press the tail flat against the pan
14. 3. Cook on a low heat until crispy
15. 4. Trim, season and serve
16. For the Manhattan sauce
17. 1. Take from the lab and reduce by half
18. 2. Vacuum and store in the freezer like this. One by one
19. 3. To heat for service defrost fully. Add 4,5% kuzu
20. 4. Dissolve and heat slowly until above 85 degrees and translucent
21. 5. Store keep above 65 degrees in the oven or circulator

Other details:


Classifications:
- Dish Type: Main Course
- Cuisine: Other
- Difficulty: Level 2 (Intermediate / Professional)
- Main Ingredient: Meat (Beef, Pork, Lamb, Game)
- Dietary: Dairy-Free, Gluten-Free, Nut-Free, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Fine Dining / Plated

- Seasonality: Year-Round

2. Shave the hair off the tails and clean
3. Place one layer of tails in each vacuum bag and seal fully.
4. Cook for 8 hours or overnight at 92 degrees on full steam
To prepare
1. Remove from the vacuum bags the following day and drain the excess liquid
2. Using a palette knife score the skin and flesh to gently pull apart to open the tail out. Remove the bones keeping the meat on the skin
4. Place the tails between greaseproof paper and vacuum once chilled.
5. Cook portion and store in fridge/freezer
To cook
1. Place pigs tail between grease proof paper in a pan
2. Put a weight on top to press the tail flat against the pan
3. Cook on a low heat until crispy
4. Trim, season and serve
For the Manhattan sauce
1. Take from the lab and reduce by half
2. Vacuum and store in the freezer like this. One by one

5. Store keep above 65 degrees in the oven or circulator

name: Rice Roll
text:
Recipe ID: rice_roll_parsed
Name: Rice Roll

Ingredients:
- 90 g Rice Flour
- 30 g Potato Starch
- 10 g Tapioca Starch
- 1 pinch Salt
- 260 g Water
- Veg Oil

Steps:
1. Mix everything together well
2. Brush a thin layer of veg oil over the tray
3. Pre steam the oven
4. Pour the mixture onto the tray
5. Put in to steam for a 30sec once it's slightly set
6. Take out and tilt the tray until it's evenly spread
7. Steam for another a minute until it's fully set
8. Take out the rice film gently onto an oiled surface
9. Cut to 8cm strips
10. Roll and store in a container with baking sheet layers
11. Cling film to cover. Touching the rice roll
12. Store in fridge until service to reheat

name: Cantucci And Vin Santo
text:
Recipe ID: cantucci_and_vin_santo_merged
Name: Cantucci And Vin Santo

Ingredients:
- Butter: 100 g
- 00 Flour: 500 g
- Baking powder: 4 g
- Unpeeled almonds: 250 g
- Salt: 1 pinch
- Eggs: 4 + 1 yolk
- Granulated sugar: 280 g
- FOR BRUSHING
- Eggs: 1

Steps:
1. Turn on the oven to 190°C (375°F). When it has reached the temperature, toast the almonds arranged on a baking sheet for 3-4 minutes, then remove them and let them cool.
2. Then proceed with the dough: put the 4 whole eggs and the yolk with a pinch of salt and granulated sugar in the planetary mixer (or in a large bowl) and whisk. Add the melted and lukewarm butter and mix, then incorporate the flour sifted with the baking powder. Mix all the ingredients: the result will be a crumbly and soft mixture to which you will add the almonds. Transfer everything to a floured pastry board and compact it by hand to obtain a uniform ball.
3. Divide the dough into 2-3 balls of the same size and work them to obtain loaves about 30 cm (12 inches) long (with 2 you will get larger biscuits, with 3 smaller biscuits), which you will place on a baking sheet covered with baking paper. Brush them with the lightly beaten egg and bake for 20 minutes in a hot oven at 190°C (375°F). Then take out the loaves, let them cool for a few minutes and cut them diagonally, obtaining biscuits about 1-1.5 cm (1/2 inch) thick. Arrange them also on the baking sheet and put everything back in the oven at 170°C (340°F) for about 15 minutes. At that point, once cold, the cantucci will be ready to be enjoyed, naturally accompanied by a good glass of Vin Santo.

Other details:
Difficoltà: Difficoltà: **
Cottura: Cottura: 35'
Preparazione: Preparazione: 25'
Dosi: Dosi: 10 persone

- 350gr / 400gr of maltosec

name:
Basic Recipes
Choux Pastry
text:
Recipe ID: ricette_di_base
pasta_choux_merged
Name: Basic Recipes
Choux Pastry

Ingredients:
- Water: 200 ml
- Butter: 100 g
- Flour: 130 g
- Eggs: 4
- Sugar: 1 teaspoon (for sweet preparations)
- Salt: 1 pinch

Steps:
1. Pour the 200 ml of water into a saucepan (you can also combine 100 ml of water and 100 ml of milk) with the butter, sugar and a pinch of salt. As soon as the water begins to boil, remove the saucepan from the heat and pour in the sifted flour, mixing well and quickly to avoid the formation of lumps.
2. Put the saucepan back on the heat and, over low heat, stir vigorously. The mixture will soon become thick and soft and will detach from the sides, forming a ball. When a white film has formed on the bottom of the pan, remove the dough from the heat and let it cool completely on a plate. To speed up this process, you can immerse the saucepan in cold water.
3. Break the eggs - which in the meantime you will have taken out of the fridge and therefore will be at room temperature - and add them to the mixture one at a time, waiting for each egg to be completely absorbed by the dough before moving on to the next. This operation can be carried out by hand, with a wooden spoon, or in a stand mixer fitted with a paddle attachment.
4. When you have finished incorporating the eggs into the dough, the choux pastry will be ready. It is perfect if, when letting it fall from a wooden spoon, it forms a kind of ribbon.
5. Put it in a pastry bag with a smooth nozzle, grease the baking sheet with butter (or cover it with baking paper) and form small round mounds of dough (for cream puffs), or 5 centimeter long sticks (for éclairs).
6. Bake for 15 minutes at 220°C in a static oven (with a fan oven keep the temperature at 200°C), avoiding opening it, then lower the temperature to 190°C and cook for another 10 minutes, after which you can turn off the oven and let the cream puffs rest inside for another 15 minutes with the door slightly open, to let out any humidity. You can also pierce them with a toothpick, so that the air dries them internally, avoiding seeing them soften once removed from the oven. When they are completely cold you can use them for your preparations.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Cottura: 25-30'
Preparazione: 30'
Dosi: 60 bignè

name: Rice Fermented Veg
text:
Recipe ID: rice_fermented_veg_merged
Name: Rice Fermented Veg

Ingredients:
- Mustard Green
- Salt
- Congee liquid

Steps:
1. Cook congee in ratio of 1:10= rice:water
2. Wash mustard, cool down to 30C
3. Wash the mustard in the congee water
4. Drain properly and salt the leaves
5. Lightly rub the salt into the leaves and line them in glass jar
6. Once the congee is cool down to around 30C (need to be luke warm still)
7. Pass through the warm congee through a sieve and pour into the veg to cover, discard the rice grain
8. Let it ferment for at least a week. May take up to 10days
9. Taste and refrigerate

Other details:
Total Time: min
Amount: 500 g
Time: None
Step: None
Process: None
NOTES: 1) Try whole veg
2) Try chop up veg
3) Try cheap Jasmin rice
4) Keep an eye on how long il take to fermente
5) Try fermenting in vac pack bags

name: Parmesan Puff
text:
Recipe ID: parmesan_puff_merged
Name: Parmesan Puff

Ingredients:
- 1000g water
- 300g sushi rice (from JFC)
- 150g finely, grated Parmesan cheese
- 150g egg whites
- 60g corn flour

Steps:
1. RICE BASE
2. Using pressure cooker on induction hob
3. Add rice and water
4. Bring up to full pressure, then turn induction down to 2
5. Cook for 8 mins
6. Turn heat off and rest for 10 mins to reduce pressure, remove lid
7. Some further cooking will be required, on induction, number 2, and stirring until all water is absorbed
8. Remove from heat and allow to cool for 30 mins, stirring occasionally, this helps to make the rice mix more glutinous resulting in a better rise.
9. Rice should now weigh. 1100g
10. Blend in robo coupe with corn flour and then chill in fridge until cold
11. PUFF MIX
12. 1050g chilled rice base from above
13. Blend in robo coupe with grana padano and egg whites
14. Now rest in fridge for 24 hrs

Other details:


Classifications:
- Dish Type: Component / Base (Stocks, Sauces, Pastes, Garnishes, Doughs)
- Cuisine: Other
- Difficulty: Level 2 (Intermediate / Professional)
- Main Ingredient: Starch/Grain (Pasta, Rice, Bread)
- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Nut-Free, Vegetarian, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Fine Dining / Plated
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

name: Curry Paste
text:
Recipe ID: pasta_de_curry_en_parsed
Name: Curry Paste

Ingredients:
- 20 units of lemon grass
- 1 head of garlic
- 4 shallots
- 2 bulbs of ginger
- 4 galangal
- 20g of keffir lime
- 12 units of Basque chili peppers
- 24g of cilantro
- 10g of clove
- 24g white peppercorns
- 24g of cumin
- 12g of nutmeg
- 24g of dry mustard seeds
- 200g of coconut fat

Steps:
1. a) Toast the dry spices separately.
2. b) Hydrate the seedless Basque chili peppers.
3. c) Chop all the vegetables.
4. d) In the Thermomix, add the dry ingredients and make a powder.
5. e) Remove the spice powder from the Thermomix and add the vegetables.
6. f) Grind at maximum speed until a smooth mixture is obtained.

name: Mulled Wine
text:
Recipe ID: vin_brûlé_merged
Name: Mulled Wine

Ingredients:
- Full-bodied red wine: 1 liter
- Sugar: 200 g
- Oranges: 1 (zest)
- Lemons: 1 (zest)
- Cinnamon: 2 sticks
- Cloves: 8
- Nutmeg: ½

Steps:
1. Using a vegetable peeler, cut thin strips of lemon and orange zest, trying to exclude as much of the bitter, white part as possible. Then, place the peels in a steel saucepan with not-too-high sides with the sugar, spices, and lastly, the wine. Turn on the heat and slowly bring to a boil: without stopping stirring, boil for 5 minutes, until the sugar is completely dissolved.
2. At this point, ignite the wine using a long wooden skewer, which will ensure a safe distance: let the fire go out on its own. Then filter the mulled wine with a very fine-mesh sieve and serve it steaming hot.

Other details:
Difficoltà: ★
Cottura: 10'
Preparazione: 5'
Dosi: 4 persone

name: Lemon Puree
text:
Recipe ID: purea_di_limone_merged
Name: Lemon Puree

Ingredients:
- For the white part of the lemon
- Lemons with thick skin 400
- Water 1300
- Sugar 50
- For the lemon puree
- White part of lemon 150
- Heavy cream 50
- Lemon juice 20
- Butter 30
- Sugar 10

Steps:
1. Peel the lemons, extracting only the yellow part (zest), cut them into four pieces and extract the pulp from the inside
2. Immerse the white part of the lemon in boiling water for a few minutes, drain, cool in cold water and repeat the operation at least twice
3. Then cook the white part of the lemon in water and sugar for about 15-20 minutes, once cooked drain and set aside
4. For the puree:
5. In a Thermomix at 80 °C, combine the lemon pulp, a little water, and the sugar
6. For the lemon juice:
7. Grind and gradually add the hot cream and butter. It should have the texture of mashed potatoes

name: Duck Foie Gras
text:
Recipe ID: fegato_grasso_d'anatra_merged
Name: Duck Foie Gras

Ingredients:
- Duck Foie Gras
- Butter 1200
- Whole Eggs 400
- Pink Salt 960
- The Marinade
- Red Port 600
- Tawny Port 600
- White Port 300
- Brandy 200
- Very Finely Chopped Shallots 400
- Very Finely Chopped Garlic Cloves n. 4
- Thyme 1 Bunch

Steps:
1. INSTRUCTIONS:
2. 1. Place a tray for the bain-marie in the oven at 100°C.
3. 2. Clean the chicken livers and the foie gras, cut them into equal parts, add the pink salt, and place everything on a baking sheet with parchment paper and cover with film, keeping it in a warm place and mixing occasionally. The temperature of the livers should be around 40°.
4. 3. Melted butter and eggs at room temperature.
5. 4. Using a deep container and using an immersion blender, blend the livers with the reduction, then the eggs, and then the butter little by little until a homogeneous mixture is obtained.
6. 5. Blend in the Thermomix at 65° for 2 minutes at maximum power and then pass through a double sieve.
7. 6. Place the mixture in the terrines in the preheated tray, cook for 35-40 minutes and reach a core temperature of 62°/64°.
8. 7. Absolutely do not use the blast chiller and let it rest for 24 hours.

Other details:
original_language: it
translated_from: it

Classifications:
- Dish Type: Starter / Appetizer
- Cuisine: French
- Difficulty: Level 3 (Advanced / Michelin)
- Main Ingredient: Poultry (Chicken, Duck)
- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Nut-Free, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Fine Dining / Plated
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

raw.name: Duck Foie Gras
ingredients: Very Finely Chopped Garlic Cloves n. 4
steps: INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Place a tray for the bain-marie in the oven at 100°C.

2. Clean the chicken livers and the foie gras, cut them into equal parts, add the pink salt, and place everything on a baking sheet with parchment paper and cover with film, keeping it in a warm place and mixing occasionally. The temperature of the livers should be around 40°.
3. Melted butter and eggs at room temperature.
4. Using a deep container and using an immersion blender, blend the livers with the reduction, then the eggs, and then the butter little by little until a homogeneous mixture is obtained.
5. Blend in the Thermomix at 65° for 2 minutes at maximum power and then pass through a double sieve.
6. Place the mixture in the terrines in the preheated tray, cook for 35-40 minutes and reach a core temperature of 62°/64°.
7. Absolutely do not use the blast chiller and let it rest for 24 hours.

name: Colomba
text:
Recipe ID: colombe_merged
Name: Colomba

Ingredients:
- 1st refreshment
- panettone flour 323
- natural yeast 323
- water (47.5% on the flour) 153
- 2nd refreshment
- panettone flour 800
- natural yeast 800
- water 380
- 3rd refreshment
- panettone flour 2000
- natural yeast 2000
- water 950
- all the 1st dough
- panettone flour 5000 833 1000
- sugar 1900 316 380
- butter 1900 316 380
- egg yolks 1400 233 280
- water 1800 300 360
- salt 100 16.5 20
- vanilla 10 1.5 2
- colomba flavoring 3000 500 600
- diced orange 3000 500 600
- diced citron 1000 166 200

Steps:
1. Remove the skin from the natural yeast and use only the inner part. Knead the yeast, water, and flour. Form a loaf, make a cross-shaped cut on it, and place it in a suitable container, then cover with plastic wrap and place in a proofing chamber at 30°C for about 4 hours. It is ready when it has reached twice its original volume.
2. Remove the skin from the natural yeast and use only the inner part. Knead the yeast, water, and flour. Form a loaf, make a cross-shaped cut on it, and place it in a suitable container, then cover with plastic wrap and place in a proofing chamber at 30°C for about 4 hours. It is ready when it has reached twice its original volume.
3. Follow the procedure as for the 1st and 2nd refreshment, and weigh 3000 g of yeast and test a loaf on which a cross-shaped cut will be made and place in a proofing chamber at 30°C for about 4 hours. The remaining yeast must be wrapped in cloth and tied.
4. The amount of water depends on the flour's absorption. The amount of colomba flavoring depends on the % reported on the aroma's label itself, for example: 1X1000, that is, 1 g per 1000 g of product; 1X2000, that is, 1 g per 2000 g of product
5. Knead the 1st dough with flour, egg yolks, salt, vanilla, adding a little water with the colomba flavoring and sugar, which can be dissolved in the water or incorporated into the butter. Add water and butter little by little until finished, then let the dough work until a film of dough forms between buttered fingers; at this point, incorporate the fruit little by little at 1st speed. Place the dough on the buttered table and weigh the pieces, calculating 10% less than the desired weight after cooking, considering the weight of the glaze and the decoration with the granulated sugar. Form loaves and place them on boards without cloth. At the end, take the first loaves, cut them in half, and place them in the molds, forming the wings first and then the trunk. Place the molds in a proofing chamber at 25 - 28°C with humidity for 8 - 10 hours; the colomba is ready to bake when it reaches the edges of the mold. Spread with glaze, decorate with granulated sugar, whole raw almonds, and sprinkle with powdered sugar. Bake at a temperature of 180 - 200°C for about 25 minutes for 500 g pieces, while 40-45 minutes for 1000 g pieces.

Other details:

translated_from: it

It is ready when it has reached twice its original volume.

name: Entre Puebla Y Askola, Viii Con Crema De Aguacate
text:
Recipe ID: entre_puebla_y_askola,_viii_con_crema_de_aguacate_merged
Name: Entre Puebla Y Askola, Viii Con Crema De Aguacate

Ingredients:
- INGREDIENTS:
- (For 25 people.)
- The viii:
- 3 l. liquid cream
- 3 l. raw sheep's milk
- 250 ml. viii mother
- The avocado cream:
- 1 kg. avocado

Steps:
1. PREVIOUS NOTE:
2. For success, the cleanliness of the surface to be used and worked on is crucial. The manipulator's hands are going to be used, it is crucial. Everything must be disinfected with Ethanol.
3. We are working with a living microorganism, the viii, so its nutrient conditions will largely determine its growth in many often unpredictable.
4. ELABORATION:
5. The viii:
6. Heat the raw sheep's milk until it reaches 80°C (pasteurization temperature). Serve it in a clean and disinfected glass or plastic container and add the cream. Mix very well, add the viii mother and mix again.
7. Pour the mixture into a previously disinfected glass jar and cover it with cloth to allow it to be preserved.
8. Store for 24 hours at 20°C -25°C.
9. Once the time has passed and the texture of the viii is compact, refrigerate for 6 hours before using.
10. The avocado cream:
11. NOTE: the avocado paste made prior to serving
12. For the avocado paste we will need very ripe avocados.
13. Remove the avocado pulp and blend in a thermomix until a smooth and spreadable paste is obtained.
14. Strain the cream with a fine sieve and reserve in refrigeration, preserving with plastic wrap in contact. It is very important that the avocado does not oxidize.

Other details:
original_language: es
translated_from: es

Classifications:
- Dish Type: Component / Base (Stocks, Sauces, Pastes, Garnishes, Doughs)
- Cuisine: Other
- Difficulty: Level 2 (Intermediate / Professional)
- Main Ingredient: Dairy / Egg
- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Nut-Free, Vegetarian, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Fine Dining / Plated
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

raw.name: Entre Puebla Y Askola, Viii Con Crema De Aguacate
steps: PREVIOUS NOTE:
For success, the cleanliness of the surface to be used and worked on is crucial. The manipulator's hands are going to be used, it is crucial. Everything must be disinfected with Ethanol.
We are working with a living microorganism, the viii, so its nutrient conditions will largely determine its growth in many often unpredictable.
ELABORATION:
The viii:

Heat the raw sheep's milk until it reaches 80°C (pasteurization temperature). Serve it in a clean and disinfected glass or plastic container and add the cream. Mix very well, add the viii mother and mix again.
Pour the mixture into a previously disinfected glass jar and cover it with cloth to allow it to be preserved.
Store for 24 hours at 20°C -25°C.
Once the time has passed and the texture of the viii is compact, refrigerate for 6 hours before using.
The avocado cream:
NOTE: the avocado paste made prior to serving
For the avocado paste we will need very ripe avocados.
Remove the avocado pulp and blend in a thermomix until a smooth and spreadable paste is obtained.
Strain the cream with a fine sieve and reserve in refrigeration, preserving with plastic wrap in contact. It is very important that the avocado does not oxidize.

text:
Recipe ID: empanadilla_de_huevo_1_por_plato_es_parsed
Name: Egg Empanadilla (1 Per Plate)

Ingredients:
- The yolk of one egg
- Puff pastry dough
- Liquid egg yolk

Steps:
1. a) Separate the egg white from the yolk.
2. b) Roll out the puff pastry dough to the desired thickness.
3. c) Using a fork, poke holes in the dough.
4. d) Cut the dough into perfect squares.
5. e) Brush egg yolk on the edges of the dough and fold carefully, being careful not to break the yolk.
6. f) At the moment of service, fry in oil at approximately 180 degrees Celsius.
7. g) Be very careful not to overcook the yolk.

name: Sauerkraut
text:
Recipe ID: chucrut_merged
Name: Sauerkraut

Ingredients:
- The Sauerkraut:
- Curly Cabbage or Collard Greens
- 2% Coarse Salt
- The Sour Powder:
- Sauerkraut

Steps:
1. PREPARATION:
2. The Sauerkraut:
3. Cut the cabbage very thinly using a slicing machine.
4. Add 2% salt and massage the cabbage with the salt.
5. Serve on a tray, pressing weight on top.
6. Ferment for a minimum of 4 weeks.
7. The Sour Powder:
8. Remove the fermented cabbage and wash off excess salt with water if necessary.
9. Dry the sauerkraut for 48 hours at approximately 60ºC. Grind the dried sauerkraut in a Thermomix and sift until you obtain a sour and salty powder.

name: Yeast Sandwich
text:
Recipe ID: yeast_sandwich_merged
Name: Yeast Sandwich

Ingredients:
- For the toasted yeast puree
- 250g Fresh yeast
- 250g Unsalted butter
- For the yeast ice cream
- 625g Double cream
- 250g Yeast puree (previous recipe)
- 25g Egg white
- 4g Salt
- For harms on biscuit
- 300g Finely grated parmesan
- 150g Strong flour
- 250g Fresh egg whites
- 5g Salt

Steps:
1. Make light brown butter.
2. Add yeast and keep cooking. Whisk continuously until emulsified on medium heat to separate the water.
3. The mixture will split separating the butter fat.
4. Run off the excess fat. Repeat this process 3 times until the excess fat is removed.
5. Remove from heat and add a few drops of cof
6. Whisk until emulsified check it.
7. Once the yeast puree has emulsified season with salt and reserve in the fridge.
8. Warm the cream in a pan.
9. Add heated cream, xantana and blend using a hand blender.
10. Allow the mixture to cool and stir in the egg white.
11. Pass the mixture through a fine chinoix and fill 1l syphon gun.
12. Fill sifon gun with 2 charges the fill the rectangle moulder, let the bricks and freeze
13. Once frozen carefully dercalde and store in container in the freezer.
14. Blend all the ingredients together and pass through a drum sieve stencil of 3cm x 8cm spread the dough on the silpat using a spatula.
15. Bake in the oven at 160C with 60% humidity for 7 minutes until golden brown cool.
16. Once cool break into pieces.
17. Discard the broken or deformed biscuits.

Other details:
PROCESS:

- Dish Type: Amuse-bouche / Canapé
- Cuisine: Other
- Difficulty: Level 3 (Advanced / Michelin)
- Main Ingredient: Dairy / Egg
- Dietary: Nut-Free, Alcohol-Free
- Occasion: Fine Dining / Plated
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

name: Suckling Pork
text:
Recipe ID: suckling_pork_parsed
Name: Suckling Pork

Ingredients:
- Ginger
- Garlic
- Sichuan salt
- Peanut oil
- Butcher twins
- Rice vinegar
- Chinease red vineagar
- Lemon juice
- Kao Liang Wine
- Garlic 5 cloves
- Ginger 1/5 knob roughly choped
- Sichuan salt

Steps:
1. Wash ,Clean inside out . Split Suckling Pig in Half . Blow torced all hair away
2. Once is done Place pig over the sink on the rack and pour piping hot water all over the skin .
3. The hot water here is to break down the tissue inside the skin,so when roasting ,the skin dosen"t absorb
4. moisture from the meat itself. This is a traditional pekin pork process and its vital.
5. Pat pork dry from inside out ,Slit the knees and tide back with butcher strings
6. Spice rub :Garlic 5 cloves ,Ginger 1/5 knob roughly choped both and sichuan salt .
7. Use spice mix ,Sichuan salt rub outside the pork and inside ,inside the pork only use ginger and garlic rub
8. After marinated place pork flat skin side up and leave to marinade in walking fridge for 4 hours
9. Once is marinated ,Wipe the skin side only with jaycloth and brush with Vinegar liquore .
10. Aply ivenly everywhere on the skin and do 2 times .After place skin up on gastro tray and leave in blast chiller
11. untill skin will be dry and absored all mixture .
12. After service is over ,make sure your josper stay hot at 150 C . Brush off all spices ,ginger and garlic off
13. Place pig skin up in shalowed 1/1 gastro tray in josper at 160 C with door close
14. Next morning take pork out of the josper and place in rational oven at 290 C without head
15. Make sure you cooking pork untill skin gets nice brown colour and crispy.
16. Take pig from ratiuonal time to time to brush with peanut oil
17. Head : the ears and nose needs to be covered by foil

Other details:
Total Time: nan min
Crispy skin water ingredients: ['60gr Rice vinegar', '45 gr Chinease red vineagar', '15gr Lemon juice', '30gr Kao Liang Wine']

- Dietary: Gluten-Free, Dairy-Free, Nut-Free, Alcohol-Free

- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Year-Round

name: Italian Macarons
text:
Recipe ID: italian_macarons_merged
Name: Italian Macarons

Ingredients:
- Egg whites 360 gr
- Dried egg white powder 15 gr
- Sugar 1000 gr
- Water 240 gr
- Almond powder 1000 gr
- Icing sugar 1000 gr
- Egg whites 360 gr
- Food coloring Q.S. gr
- Lubeca almond paste 1000
- Cream 300
- Cocoa butter 100
- Blancs d'œuf 360 gr
- Blancs secs poudre 15 gr
- Sucre 1000 gr
- Eau 240 gr
- Poudre d'amande 1000 gr
- Sucre glace 1000 gr
- Blancs d'œuf 360 gr
- Colorant QS gr
- Pate d'amande Lubeca 1000
- Creme 300
- Beurre de cacao 100

Steps:
1. Mix the egg whites and dry egg white powder with a Bamix (immersion blender)
2. Whisk the egg whites slowly
3. Cook at 130°c and pour over the partially whipped egg whites
4. Mix to form a dough
5. Cook at 130° with two ventilation bars for 40min
6. Whip the egg whites and sugar at maximum speed
7. Mix all the ingredients together
8. In a food processor, mix the almond paste, cream and cocoa butter
9. Mixer au bamix les blancs et les blancs secs
10. Monter les blanc doucement
11. Cuire a 130°c et verser sur les blanc semi monter
12. Melanger pour former une pate
13. Cuire a 130° avec deux barres de ventilation 40min
14. Monter les blancs et sucre vitesse maximum
15. Melanger tout les ingredients
16. Au robot coupe mixer la pate d'amande la creme et le beurre de cacao

Other details:
Portions: 150 macaron fini
Note: Macaron
Cussen Gord Foun: 120°c 35-40mm
Masse amande: Remplacer la creme par une puree de fruits

name: Fried Shrimp Coral
text:
Recipe ID: gamba_frita_es_parsed
Name: Fried Shrimp Coral

Ingredients:
- 500g shrimp coral (extracted from the head)
- 200g tomato sauce
- 12g cornstarch
- salt

Steps:
1. a) Remove the coral from the heads of the shrimp, being careful to avoid extracting the black area.
2. b) Once we have all the coral, we place it in a saucepan and with the help
3. of a whisk, we emulsify the mixture and cook it so that the flavor
4. of the coral goes from raw to toasted, achieving a sweet flavor.
5. c) Add the tomato to reinforce the flavor and finally the cornstarch to
6. achieve the desired texture.
7. d) Finally, emulsify it with an immersion blender so that the mixture is perfectly
8. smooth.
9. e) Transfer to piping bags.

Other details:

text:

Ingredients:
- 1350g whole milk
- 600g whipping cream
- 14g fig leaf
- 150g trimoline
- 500g egg yolks
- 200g caster sugar

5. For HERB ICE CREAMS replace fig leaf with 45g blanched lovage or 90g of basil

text:
Recipe ID: brown_sugar_tart_merged
Name: Brown Sugar Tart

Ingredients:
- X3
- 1175g UHT CREAM
- 190G MOLASSES CANE SUGAR
- 345G LIQUID PASTURISED EGG YOLKS
- X1
- 392g UHT

- X4
- 1568G UHT CREAM

- X2
- 785g UHT
- 128g MOLASSES CANE SUGAR
- 230G LIQUID PASTURISED EGG YOLKS
- SX
- 1955G UHT CREAM
- 315G MOLASSES CANE SUGAR
- 575 LIQUID PASTURISED EGG YOLKS
- X7
- 2742g UHT
- 438g MOLASSES CANE SUGAR
- 705g LIQUID PASTERUISED YOLKS
- X6
- 2350 UHT CREAM
- 375 MOLASSES CANE SUGAR
- 690 LIQUID PASTURISED EGG YOLKS

Steps:
1. BOIL CREAM
2. CARAMELISE SUGAR IN A SEPARATE PAN WITH 10G OF WATER PER TART UNTIL THE STEAM JUST TURNS TO SMOKE AND LARGE BUBBLES APPEAR
3. ADD IMMEDIATELY TO CREAM, COOL TO 85C, STIRRING AS IT COOLS
4. TEMPER IN EGG YOLKS, PASS, TORCH THE BUBBLES AND USING THE DROPPER FILL TARTS 1CM FROM THE RIM, COOK AT 94C FAN3 FOR 35-45 MINUTES
5. ALLOW TO COOL TO ROOM TEMPERATURE BEFOR PLACING IN THE FRIDGE

name: Chocolate Chip Muffins
text:
Recipe ID: muffin_con_gocce_di_cioccolato_merged
Name: Chocolate Chip Muffins

Ingredients:
- Bicarbonate: ½ teaspoon
- Butter: 190 g
- Dark chocolate chips: 100 g
- Flour: 380 g
- Fresh whole milk: 200 ml
- Baking powder: 1 packet
- Salt: 1 pinch
- Eggs (medium): 3
- Vanillin: 1 packet
- Sugar: 200 g

Steps:
1. Turn on the oven to 180°. Soften the butter at room temperature, then beat it vigorously with the sugar (or using a food processor) for a few minutes, until the mixture is creamy. Then add the eggs one at a time, continuing to mix, and the lukewarm milk in a slow stream: the mixture will be ready when it is smooth, fluffy and homogeneous.
2. In a bowl, sift the flour and mix it with the baking powder, bicarbonate, vanillin and salt: add the powders to the mixture a little at a time, mixing until everything is creamy and without lumps. It is time to incorporate 80 g of chocolate chips.
3. Fill the paper liners with the dough up to the edge (using a muffin tin, if you have one), then distribute the 20 g of chocolate chips kept aside on the surfaces. Bake for about 20-25 minutes: when the muffins are golden, turn off the oven and let them rest for 5 minutes with the door open, then take them out and let them cool completely.

Other details:
Difficoltà: facile
Cottura: 25'
Preparazione: 15'
Dosi: 12 muffin

name: Jade (Green )Soup
text:
Recipe ID: jade_green_soup_parsed
Name: Jade (Green )Soup

Ingredients:
- 2tbsp White soy
- White pepper
- Corn flour
- Broccoli
- Chinease celery
- Flat leaf Parsley
- Organic carrot top(leaves )
- Master stock

Steps:
1. While you are boiling the dumplings ,prepare the Jade Soup .This green soup has the silky texture with corn flour as
2. as some Chinese soups has and its should be fresh and delicious by just drinking itself
3. Soup must be done portion by portion each time to order ,,,,You cannot prepare butch before service !!!
4. Also you must have very fine fresh master stock
5. First grate broccoli head on micro plane ,parsley cut very fine ,carrot and celery in to the fine bronoise an keep separate !
6. Heat up 200ml and 2tbs of white soy in to the sauce pan and to finish silky look you add 2tbs of cornstarch water
7. Add Broccoli tops 3tbs celery stalks 1.5 tbs carrot top 1.5 tbs cook for 1 minute and take off the heat !
8. When the dumplings get cooked ,place them in bowl , reheat soup add finelly 3tbs choped parsley and pinch of white pep
9. Mix well and pour over the dumplings. Serve very hot

Other details:

text:

Ingredients:
- 1350g milk
- 600g whipping cream
- 150g trimoline
- 500g egg yolks
- 200g sugar
- Zest of 2 clementines

text:

Ingredients:

- Black pepper sauce
- Egg yolk
- 120g rice
- 60g beef

name: Stew Sauce
text:
Recipe ID: stew_sauce_parsed
Name: Stew Sauce

Ingredients:
- 2.5 kg ginger
- 2.5 kg garlic
- 10 kg red onion
- 100 und tomato branch
- 3,600l water cooking
- 150gr fresh basil
- 32 pills palm sugar
- 8 cans of sweet basil chili paste
- 14 l meat stock
- 6LT Fino oloroso
- 3 tamarind packs
- Water

Steps:
1. a) Peel the garlic and ginger, slice and fry in a pan and oil
2. b) Peel the onion and cut into julienne strips. Once cooked garlic
and  ginger add the onion
3. c) wash the tomatoes and cut into julienne strips. Once poached golden onion
add the cut tomato and continue cooking the sauce
4. d) Once cooked all, add the palm sugar, tamarind cans
and the thai basil paste
5. e) After 10/15 min add the fino oloroso and reduce it. Finally add
the meat stock and 25l of water approximately.
6. f) Bring to a boil and let reduce about 8/10 hours approx, once achieved
the desired density, strain through fine sieve.

Other details:

name: Pear, Fig Leaf And Blackcurrant Sage
text:
Recipe ID: pear,_fig_leaf_and_blackcurrant_sage_merged
Name: Pear, Fig Leaf And Blackcurrant Sage

Ingredients:
- For The Whey
- 4 litres Whole Milk
- 5g Ascorbic Acid
- For the Pears
- 2kg Pears
- Ascorbic Acid
- 5% Calcium Hydroxide
- 5% Caster sugar
- For The Whey Sauce
- Whey (from 4 litres of milk)
- 5% sugar
- Pear trim
- For Fig Leaf Ice Cream
- 700g Whole Milk
- 700g Double Cream
- 200g Pasteurised Egg Yolk
- 175g Sugar
- 20% of total weight of anglaise base
- 100g Passion fruit puree
- 1g Agar agar

Steps:
1. PROCESS
2. For The Whey
3. 1. Heat milk to 93 degrees
4. 2. Add ascorbic acid and leave milk to split for twenty minutes
5. 3. Pass off whey and discard the curds
6. For the Pears
7. 1. Peel and trim pears keeping skin and trim separate in ascorbic acid bath for 2 hours
8. 2. After two hours, wash pears thoroughly and put into 4 litres of water with 400g sugar
9. 3. Reduce until pears become glassy and are soft in the middle
10. 4. Take out of water and dry for 8 hours until skin is dry
11. For The Whey Sauce
12. 1. Reduce whey, sugar and pear until thick toffee consistency
13. 2. Be careful not to over reduce, if unsure, let it cool to check consistency.
14. For Fig Leaf Ice Cream
15. 1. Mix milk, cream, egg yolk and sugar in thermomix with butterfly. Cook at 80 degrees, speed 4 for 11 minutes.
16. 2. Blanche fig leafs, chill in ice bath
17. 3. Blend fig leafs from thermomix and blitz leaves into base until base is green, check flavour
18. Return to heat and cook until sugar is completely caramelized.
19. Remove from the heat and spread out over silpat to cool.
20. Store in airtight container.

Other details:


Classifications:
- Dish Type: Dessert
- Cuisine: Other
- Difficulty: Level 3
- Main Ingredient: Fruit
- Dietary: Vegetarian, Nut-Free, Alcohol-Free, Gluten-Free
- Occasion: Fine Dining / Plated
- Prep Style: Make-Ahead / Prep-Heavy
- Seasonality: Autumn

raw.name: Pear, Fig Leaf And Blackcurrant Sage
ingredients: 20% of total weight of anglaise base
steps: PROCESS
For The Whey
1. Heat milk to 93 degrees
2. Add ascorbic acid and leave milk to split for twenty minutes
3. Pass off whey and discard the curds
For the Pears
1. Peel and trim pears keeping skin and trim separate in ascorbic acid bath for 2 hours
2. After two hours, wash pears thoroughly and put into 4 litres of water with 400g sugar
3. Reduce until pears become glassy and are soft in the middle
4. Take out of water and dry for 8 hours until skin is dry
For The Whey Sauce
1. Reduce whey, sugar and pear until thick toffee consistency
2. Be careful not to over reduce, if unsure, let it cool to check consistency.
For Fig Leaf Ice Cream
1. Mix milk, cream, egg yolk and sugar in thermomix with butterfly. Cook at 80 degrees, speed 4 for 11 minutes.
2. Blanche fig leafs, chill in ice bath
3. Blend fig leafs from thermomix and blitz leaves into base until base is green, check flavour
Return to heat and cook until sugar is completely caramelized.
Remove from the heat and spread out over silpat to cool.
Store in airtight container.

text:

Ingredients:

- 130 g Pork
- 40 g Lardo
- 10 g Sesame Oil
- 12 g Cornstarch
- 35 g Plain Flour
- 1 g White pepper
- 4 g Salt

1. Blitz all the ingredients a part from Spring Onion and Turnips until smooth and voluptous

4. on a tray lined with parchment paper, pipe into 18g cylinder of mix, you can cut the mix with a pair of scissors, think of this like churros
5. you can also do it straight into oil if needed.
6. You can freeze it now for a bit so it holds it shape, and then freeze it at 160C for 3-4 Minutes

on a tray lined with parchment paper, pipe into 18g cylinder of mix, you can cut the mix with a pair of scissors, think of this like churros
you can also do it straight into oil if needed.
You can freeze it now for a bit so it holds it shape, and then freeze it at 160C for 3-4 Minutes

name: Pineapple C-Ke Filling
text:
Recipe ID: pineapple_c-ke_filling_merged
Name: Pineapple C-Ke Filling

Ingredients:
- 350g Tinned crushed pineapple (drained)
- 150g Light brown sugar
- 250g Maltose
- 50g Unsalted butter
- 15g Lime juice
- 35g Glutinous rice flour

Steps:
1. Mix together the pineapple, brown sugars, maltose in a big pan.
2. Cook the mixture in high temperature until boiled.
3. Put down the fire, keep saute until see the white colour on the pineapple.
4. The mixture will be quite thick.
5. Add butter and lime juice until melted and boiled.
6. Add glutinous rice flour cooking in low temperature and mix it properly become very thick mixture.

name: Mole Mancho
text:
Recipe ID: mole_mancho_merged
Name: Mole Mancho

Ingredients:
- For the Mole
- 25g White onion
- 2g Garlic
- 200g Charred tomato fresh
- 75g Ancho
- 200g Date
- 200g Banana
- 100g Pear
- 100g Apple
- 50g Almonds
- 2g Oregano
- 1 Clove
- 5g Cinnamon bark
- 0.5g Cumin
- 2 litre chicken stock
- Extras

Steps:
1. 1. De-seed chilli and open out flat
2. 2. Burn on the hibachi yakitori or open flame (just blackening the skin)
3. 3. Hydrate in boiled water and leave to soak, keep the chilli water ( preferably one night)
4. 4. Toast spices in a pan
5. 5. Caramelize onion and garlic
6. 6. Cut the fruit and roast in the oven at 200 degrees for 20 minutes/ 0% humidity
7. 7. Blend all together in thermomix
8. 8. Cook out for 10 hours
9. 9. Pass in paco jet and spin three times (makes two paco)

Other details:

- Seasonality: Year-Round

raw.name: Mole Mancho
steps: 1. De-seed chilli and open out flat
2. Burn on the hibachi yakitori or open flame (just blackening the skin)
3. Hydrate in boiled water and leave to soak, keep the chilli water ( preferably one night)
4. Toast spices in a pan
5. Caramelize onion and garlic
6. Cut the fruit and roast in the oven at 200 degrees for 20 minutes/ 0% humidity
7. Blend all together in thermomix
8. Cook out for 10 hours
9. Pass in paco jet and spin three times (makes two paco)

text:

- 200gr of pineapple
- 100gr of beet
- 150gr of water

name: Paella Sofrito
text:
Recipe ID: sofrito_de_paella_en_parsed
Name: Paella Sofrito

Ingredients:
- 300gr garlic
- 300gr ginger
- 3 red bell peppers
- 3 green bell peppers
- 3 white onions
- 900 gr peeled frozen shrimp

Steps:
1. Chop the garlic and ginger separately in a Thermomix
2. Dice the red bell pepper, green bell pepper, and onion into brunoise (small dice)
3. Slowly sauté the ginger, then the garlic, and once golden, add the peppers and onion. Sauté until golden.
4. Dice the shrimp into brunoise and add them to the sofrito

name: Tupinambur Tiger'S Milk
text:
Recipe ID: tupinambur_tiger's_milk_merged
Name: Tupinambur Tiger'S Milk

Ingredients:
- 150 gr. Tupinambur base
- 150 gr. Ice
- 1 Unit Crushed ice
- 1 Unit Celery stalk
- 1 Unit Garlic
- 1 tsp. Ginger (same amount as garlic)
- 50 gr. Lime (juice of 1 unit approx.)
- ½ tsp. Xanthan gum
- 100 gr. Sunflower oil
- ½ tsp. Gelespesa

Steps:
1. Add all the ingredients to the blender (except the oil and xanthan gum) and mix until a homogeneous mixture is obtained.
2. Emulsify with the sunflower oil until creamy, add the xanthan gum and mix.
3. It should be light and creamy.
4. REPLACE EVERY 2 DAYS. CHECK THE TEXTURE AND FLAVOR DAILY
